  sponsor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the sponsoring organization for this agreement.']
  sponsor_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact person at the sponsoring organization.']
  sponsorship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sponsorship agreement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|negotiation|active|suspended|completed|terminated|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sponsorship_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of sponsorship level indicating prominence and benefits package (title sponsor, presenting sponsor, or tiered levels).']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the sponsorship agreement becomes effective and sponsor benefits commence.']
  termination_clause varchar [note: 'Summary of conditions under which either party may terminate the sponsorship agreement early.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sponsorship record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Master record for sponsorship agreements tied to esports leagues, tournaments, or teams. Captures sponsor name, sponsorship tier (title/presenting/gold/silver), contracted value, in-kind value, activation type (jersey patch, stream overlay, naming rights, in-game branding), start/end date, deliverables list, exclusivity category, renewal option flag, and sponsorship status. Tracks esports-specific commercial partnerships distinct from general marketing campaigns.'
}

Table esports.broadcast_viewership {
  broadcast_viewership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for broadcast_viewership']
  broadcast_session_id varchar [note: 'The unique session identifier assigned by the broadcast platform for this specific live stream. Used for platform-level reconciliation and SLA validation.']
  cdn_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.cdn_node. Business justification: Broadcast streams are delivered via specific CDN nodes. Operations teams track which CDN nodes serve viewership for performance monitoring, cost allocation, capacity planning, and troubleshooting buff']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Viewership data collection subject to jurisdiction-specific privacy laws (GDPR consent requirements, COPPA restrictions, data localization rules). Required for data protection compliance assessment, c']
  match_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific esports match being broadcast. Links this viewership telemetry to the competitive event.']
  promotional_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Broadcasts feature promotional assets (sponsor overlays, team logos, event graphics). Tracking asset usage is required for sponsor deliverable verification, rights management compliance, and broadcast']
  ad_break_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a commercial ad break was active at the time of this measurement. Used for monetization analysis and viewer retention impact assessment.']
  average_watch_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'The average duration in minutes that viewers have watched the broadcast, calculated across all viewers at this measurement interval.']
  bitrate_kbps varchar [note: 'The average streaming bitrate in kilobits per second at this measurement interval. Technical metric for stream quality and CDN performance.']
  broadcast_language varchar [note: 'The primary language of the broadcast stream, represented as ISO 639-1 two-letter code. Supports multi-language broadcast strategy and regional targeting.']
  broadcast_platform varchar [note: 'The streaming platform where the esports event was broadcast (e.g., Twitch, YouTube Gaming, Facebook Gaming).']
  broadcast_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the live broadcast session began. Establishes the baseline for session duration and lifecycle analysis.']
  broadcast_status varchar [note: 'The operational status of the broadcast at the time of this measurement. Tracks stream health and continuity for SLA compliance.']
  buffering_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'The ratio of time spent buffering to total watch time, measured at this interval. Quality of Service (QoS) metric for viewer experience.']
  ccu varchar [note: 'The number of concurrent users watching the broadcast at the time of this measurement interval. Key metric for real-time audience engagement.']
  chat_message_volume varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of chat messages posted by viewers during the broadcast session up to this measurement timestamp. Indicates audience engagement level.']
  co_stream_count varchar [note: 'The number of authorized co-streams or watch parties running simultaneously for this event at this measurement interval. Amplification and reach metric.']
  console_viewer_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of viewers watching via gaming console devices at this measurement interval. Platform distribution metric for audience segmentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this viewership measurement record was created in the data system. Audit trail for data lineage and ETL processing.']
  desktop_viewer_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of viewers watching via desktop/PC devices at this measurement interval. Platform distribution metric for audience segmentation.']
  follower_viewer_count varchar [note: 'The number of channel followers watching at this measurement interval. Community engagement and retention metric.']
  geographic_distribution_snapshot varchar [note: 'A snapshot representation of viewer geographic distribution at this measurement interval, typically stored as JSON or delimited country codes with viewer counts. Supports regional audience analysis.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact timestamp when this viewership measurement was captured during the live broadcast. Represents the real-world event time of the telemetry snapshot.']
  mobile_viewer_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of viewers watching via mobile devices at this measurement interval. Platform distribution metric for audience segmentation.']
  new_viewer_count varchar [note: 'The number of new viewers who joined the broadcast since the previous measurement interval. Tracks audience growth and discovery during the event.']
  pcu varchar [note: 'The peak concurrent users recorded during this broadcast session up to the measurement timestamp. Represents the maximum simultaneous viewership achieved.']
  peak_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the peak concurrent users (PCU) was recorded during this broadcast session. Identifies the moment of maximum audience engagement.']
  raid_incoming_viewers varchar [note: 'The number of viewers who joined via a raid or host from another channel at this measurement interval. Cross-promotion and discovery metric.']
  sponsorship_overlay_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether sponsor branding or overlay graphics were displayed at the time of this measurement. Sponsorship fulfillment and impression tracking.']
  stream_quality_tier varchar [note: 'The predominant stream quality tier being consumed by viewers at this measurement interval. Indicates technical delivery performance and viewer experience quality. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: source|1080p60|1080p|720p60|720p|480p|360p|audio_only — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  subscriber_viewer_count varchar [note: 'The number of platform subscribers (e.g., Twitch subscribers, YouTube members) watching at this measurement interval. Monetization and loyalty metric.']
  unique_viewers varchar [note: 'The cumulative count of unique individual viewers who have watched any portion of the broadcast session up to this measurement point.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this viewership measurement record was last updated in the data system. Audit trail for data corrections and reprocessing.']
  viewer_drop_count varchar [note: 'The number of viewers who disconnected or stopped watching since the previous measurement interval. Indicates audience retention and engagement fluctuations.']
  vod_availability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a video-on-demand recording will be available after the live broadcast ends. Content strategy and rights management indicator.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing real-time and peak viewership telemetry for live esports broadcast events. Records match reference, broadcast platform, CCU (Concurrent Users) at each measurement interval, PCU (Peak Concurrent Users) for the session, total unique viewers, average watch time in minutes, chat message volume, peak timestamp, geographic distribution snapshot, and stream quality tier. Supports GaaS live operations monitoring, broadcast rights SLA validation, and post-event audience reporting.'
}

Table esports.tournament_registration {
  tournament_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tournament registration record. Primary key.']
  age_verification_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.age_verification_event. Business justification: Tournament registration triggers age verification for age-restricted tournaments and games requiring parental consent. Essential for eligibility verification, COPPA compliance, and regulatory audit tr']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Tournament entry fees generate invoices for payment processing, refund eligibility tracking, and financial reconciliation. Real esports platforms (ESL, FACEIT) link registrations to billing records fo']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual player if registrant_type is individual. Null for team-based registrations.']
  support_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.support_ticket. Business justification: Registration issues (payment failures, eligibility disputes, technical problems) commonly generate support tickets. Critical for tournament operations and player support tracking.']
  team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the team entity if registrant_type is team, duo, or squad. Null for individual registrations.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tournament or qualifier event for which this registration was submitted.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the registration was approved by tournament organizers. Null if not yet approved or if rejected/withdrawn.']
  check_in_status varchar [note: 'Status of pre-event check-in requirement: not_required (no check-in needed), pending (awaiting check-in), checked_in (confirmed attendance), no_show (failed to check in).']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the registrant completed check-in. Null if not yet checked in or check-in not required.']
  contact_discord varchar [note: 'Discord username or handle for tournament organizer communication. Null if not provided.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for tournament communications and notifications to the registrant.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this registration record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  disqualification_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for registration rejection or disqualification (e.g., ineligible player, incomplete roster, rule violation, payment failure). Null if not disqualified.']
  eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Status of eligibility verification process: verified (meets all requirements), pending (under review), failed (does not meet requirements), not_checked (verification not yet performed).']
  eligibility_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when eligibility verification was completed. Null if not yet verified.']
  entry_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Entry fee amount charged for tournament registration in USD. Zero for free-to-enter tournaments.']
  entry_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the entry fee (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments from the registrant or tournament organizer regarding this registration (e.g., special accommodations, roster changes, administrative notes).']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'External payment transaction reference or receipt number from payment processor. Null if no payment required or not yet paid.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Status of entry fee payment: not_required (free entry), pending (awaiting payment), completed (paid), failed (payment declined), refunded (payment returned).']
  platform varchar [note: 'Gaming platform the registrant will compete on (PC, PlayStation, Xbox, Nintendo, Mobile, Cross-Platform).']
  previous_tournament_wins varchar [note: 'Count of previous tournament wins by this registrant in the same game title or league. Used for seeding and eligibility.']
  region varchar [note: 'Three-letter geographic region code for the registrant (e.g., NAM for North America, EUR for Europe, ASI for Asia). Used for region-locked tournaments.']
  registrant_type varchar [note: 'Type of registrant submitting the entry: team (full roster), individual (solo player), duo (two-player team), or squad (small group).']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique registration confirmation number or code issued to the registrant upon submission.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the registration: pending (under review), approved (accepted into tournament), waitlisted (on standby), rejected (denied entry), withdrawn (registrant cancelled), cancelled (tournament organizer cancelled).']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the registration was submitted by the registrant. Principal business event timestamp.']
  roster_lock_status varchar [note: 'Status of roster changes: unlocked (changes allowed), locked (no further changes permitted), frozen (administratively locked by organizer).']
  roster_lock_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the roster was locked and no further changes are permitted. Null if roster not yet locked.']
  seed_assignment varchar [note: 'Seeding position assigned to the registrant for bracket placement. Lower numbers indicate higher seeds. Null if seeding not yet assigned or not applicable.']
  skill_tier varchar [note: 'Skill tier or rank of the registrant at time of registration (e.g., Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond, Master, Grandmaster). Used for tier-restricted tournaments.']
  substitution_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether player substitutions are permitted for this registration per tournament rules. True if allowed, False if not.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this registration record. Audit trail field.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this registration record was last modified. Audit trail field.']
  waiver_signed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the registrant has signed the tournament liability waiver and terms of participation. True if signed, False if not.']
  waiver_signed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the waiver was signed. Null if not yet signed.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this registration record. Audit trail field.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing a team\'s or player\'s registration for a tournament or qualifier. Records registrant type (team/individual), registration date, registration status (pending/approved/waitlisted/rejected/withdrawn), seed assignment, check-in status, entry fee payment reference, eligibility verification status, and any disqualification reason. Manages the intake workflow from open registration through roster lock.'
}

Table esports.match_incident {
  match_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the competitive integrity incident or rule violation record.']
  compliance_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_incident. Business justification: Serious match incidents (cheating, match-fixing, player misconduct, data breaches during events) may escalate to compliance incidents requiring regulatory notification, player data breach reporting, o']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league official or tournament administrator assigned to investigate and rule on the incident.']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the esports league under whose rules the incident is being adjudicated.']
  match_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the esports match during which the incident occurred.']
  primary_match_player_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual player who reported the incident, if applicable.']
  primary_match_reporting_team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the team that filed the protest or report, if applicable.']
  appeal_deadline timestamp [note: 'The deadline by which an appeal must be filed per league rules.']
  appeal_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an appeal has been filed against the ruling.']
  appeal_filed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the appeal was officially submitted.']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'The final decision on the appeal, if one was filed.']
  appeal_ruling_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the appeal decision was issued.']
  assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident was assigned to an administrator for review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident record was first created in the system.']
  disclosure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident details were publicly disclosed.']
  evidence_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of evidence artifact identifiers (video clips, screenshots, logs, replay files) supporting the incident report.']
  game_number varchar [note: 'The specific game number within the match series where the incident occurred (e.g., game 2 of a best-of-5).']
  in_game_time timestamp [note: 'The in-game timestamp or match clock time when the incident occurred (e.g., 23:45 game time).']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the incident, including context, actions, and circumstances.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique incident reference number for tracking and appeals (e.g., INC-2024-0001).']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the incident investigation and resolution process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reported|under_review|evidence_gathering|pending_ruling|resolved|dismissed|appealed|appeal_upheld|appeal_denied — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incident_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the incident occurred during the match (in-game or real-world time).']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the competitive integrity incident or rule violation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: disconnect|cheating_allegation|unsportsmanlike_conduct|technical_failure|rule_protest|equipment_malfunction|player_absence|unauthorized_substitution|communication_violation|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  match_result_affected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident ruling resulted in a change to the official match result.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified the incident record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional administrative notes, context, or follow-up actions related to the incident.']
  penalty_amount_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary fine amount assessed in United States Dollars, if applicable.']
  penalty_type varchar [note: 'The type of disciplinary penalty applied as a result of the incident ruling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|warning|fine|match_forfeit|game_forfeit|point_deduction|suspension|disqualification|prize_forfeiture|probation|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  public_disclosure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident and ruling have been publicly disclosed or remain confidential.']
  reporting_party_type varchar [note: 'The type of entity that initially reported or flagged the incident. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: team|player|referee|broadcast_observer|league_official|tournament_admin|automated_system|spectator|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  review_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the formal review or investigation of the incident began.']
  ruling_outcome varchar [note: 'The final decision or verdict rendered by the league official on the incident. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: upheld|dismissed|partial_upheld|no_fault_found|technical_issue_confirmed|rematch_ordered|forfeit_declared|warning_issued|inconclusive — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ruling_rationale varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the reasoning and evidence basis for the ruling outcome.']
  ruling_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the final ruling or decision on the incident was issued.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'The assessed severity or impact level of the incident on competitive integrity.']
  suspension_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the player or team is suspended from competition, if applicable.']
  suspension_end_date date [note: 'The date when the suspension period ends and eligibility is restored.']
  suspension_start_date date [note: 'The date when the suspension period begins.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the incident record.']

  Note: 'Operational record for a competitive integrity incident, protest, or rule violation raised during or after a match. Captures incident type (disconnect, cheating allegation, unsportsmanlike conduct, technical failure, rule protest), reporting party, match reference, game number, incident timestamp, description, evidence references, assigned admin, resolution status, ruling outcome, and penalty applied. Supports competitive integrity enforcement and appeals processes.'
}

Table esports.match_stat {
  match_stat_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for match_stat']
  champion_character_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific champion, hero, or character the player selected for this match. Enables meta-analysis and pick/ban trends.']
  game_result_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent game result record that this player performance belongs to. Links individual player statistics to the overall match outcome.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game title for which these statistics were recorded. Enables cross-title analytics and genre-specific reporting.']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the esports player who achieved these performance statistics. Enables player-level competitive analytics and scouting.']
  team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the team the player represented during this match. Supports team-level aggregation and roster analysis.']
  accuracy_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall shooting or ability accuracy expressed as a percentage. Measures mechanical precision across applicable game genres.']
  apm varchar [note: 'RTS-specific metric measuring the number of game actions executed per minute. Indicator of mechanical skill and multitasking ability.']
  assists varchar [note: 'Total number of assists credited to the player for contributing to enemy eliminations. Core component of KDA ratio.']
  clutch_rounds_won varchar [note: 'Number of rounds won by the player in outnumbered situations. Indicator of high-pressure performance and decision-making.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this performance record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  crowd_control_duration_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration in seconds that the player applied crowd control effects to enemies. Relevant for support and tank role evaluation.']
  cs_per_min decimal(8,2) [note: 'MOBA-specific metric tracking the rate of minion/creep kills per minute. Key farming efficiency indicator for carry and mid roles.']
  damage_dealt decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total damage output inflicted by the player during the match. Key offensive performance metric across multiple game genres.']
  damage_taken decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total damage absorbed by the player during the match. Relevant for tank and frontline role evaluation.']
  deaths varchar [note: 'Total number of times the player was eliminated during the match. Core component of KDA ratio and survival analysis.']
  economy_score varchar [note: 'Measure of the player\'s in-game economic performance including gold earned, resources gathered, or economy management efficiency.']
  first_blood_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the player achieved the first kill of the match. High-value early-game performance marker.']
  headshot_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'FPS-specific metric representing the percentage of shots that resulted in headshots. Precision and accuracy indicator.']
  healing_done decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total healing or health restoration provided by the player to teammates. Critical metric for support role performance.']
  in_game_name varchar [note: 'The player\'s display name or gamertag used during this specific match. May differ from official roster name for branding or sponsorship reasons.']
  items_purchased varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or JSON array of in-game items purchased by the player during the match. Supports build path and itemization analysis.']
  kills varchar [note: 'Total number of enemy eliminations achieved by the player during the match. Core component of KDA (Kills/Deaths/Assists) ratio.']
  match_format varchar [note: 'The competitive format of the match in which this performance occurred. Context for interpreting performance under different tournament structures.']
  match_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this match performance occurred. Principal business event timestamp for temporal analysis and trend tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this performance record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit requirements.']
  multikill_count varchar [note: 'Total number of multikill events (double kills, triple kills, etc.) achieved by the player during the match.']
  mvp_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the player was awarded MVP status for this match based on overall performance metrics.']
  objective_score varchar [note: 'Aggregate score representing the player\'s contribution to match objectives such as capturing points, securing objectives, or completing mission goals.']
  performance_rating decimal(6,2) [note: 'Composite performance score calculated from multiple in-game metrics. Standardized rating for cross-player and cross-match comparison.']
  player_role varchar [note: 'The competitive role or position the player fulfilled during this match. Role-specific KPIs are evaluated based on this classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: carry|support|mid|jungle|top|tank|dps|healer|flex|entry_fragger|awper|igl|rifler — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  side_played varchar [note: 'The map side or faction the player competed on during this match. Relevant for side-balance analysis and map-specific performance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: blue|red|ct|t|radiant|dire|attacker|defender — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  stat_source_system varchar [note: 'The system or method by which these performance statistics were captured. Supports data quality assessment and lineage tracking.']
  time_played_seconds varchar [note: 'Total duration in seconds that the player was active in the match. Used for per-minute stat normalization.']
  ultimate_ability_uses varchar [note: 'Number of times the player activated their ultimate or signature ability during the match. Tracks high-impact ability usage.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification state of the performance statistics. Ensures data integrity for official league standings and prize distribution.']
  vision_score varchar [note: 'MOBA-specific metric quantifying the player\'s contribution to map vision through ward placement and enemy ward denial.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing in-game performance statistics for an individual player within a specific game_result. Stores KDA (kills/deaths/assists), damage dealt, healing done, objective score, economy score, MVP flag, role-specific KPIs (e.g., CS/min for MOBA, headshot rate for FPS, APM for RTS), and game title-specific stat fields. Enables player-level competitive analytics and scouting data.'
}

Table esports.transfer {
  transfer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the esports player transfer transaction.']
  destination_team_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the team to which the player is transferring.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the player\'s agent or representative who negotiated the transfer terms.']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the esports league within which this transfer occurs, governing transfer window rules and roster lock compliance.']
  origin_team_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the team from which the player is transferring. Null for free agent signings.']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the esports player being transferred between teams.']
  window_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the official transfer window period during which this transfer was executed, ensuring compliance with league roster lock rules.']
  agent_commission_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Commission paid to the player\'s agent as part of the transfer transaction. Null if undisclosed or not applicable.']
  announcement_date date [note: 'The date on which the transfer was publicly announced by the teams or league.']
  announcement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp of the public transfer announcement, used for media tracking and fan engagement analytics.']
  buyout_clause_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The contractual buyout amount specified in the player\'s contract that was triggered. Null if no buyout clause was activated.']
  buyout_clause_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transfer was executed via a pre-existing buyout clause in the player\'s contract, bypassing negotiation.']
  contract_expiry_date date [note: 'The expiry date of the player\'s contract with the origin team at the time of transfer. Null for free agent signings.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transfer record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the player officially joins the destination team roster and becomes eligible to compete.']
  fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value paid by the destination team to the origin team for the player transfer rights. Zero for free agent signings. Null if undisclosed.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transfer fee (e.g., USD, EUR, KRW).']
  league_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the league officially approved the transfer. Null if approval is not required or still pending.']
  league_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transfer requires explicit approval from the league governing body before completion.']
  league_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of league approval process for this transfer.']
  loan_duration_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months for loan-type transfers. Null for permanent transfers and free agent signings.']
  loan_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fee paid by the destination team to the origin team for the loan period. Null for non-loan transfers.']
  loan_option_to_buy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the loan agreement includes an option for the destination team to purchase the player permanently at the end of the loan period.']
  medical_clearance_date date [note: 'The date on which the player received medical clearance for the transfer. Null if not required or not yet completed.']
  medical_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transfer is contingent upon the player passing a medical or health assessment.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified this transfer record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transfer record was last modified in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional internal notes or comments regarding special circumstances, conditions, or context for this transfer.']
  option_to_buy_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Pre-agreed purchase price if the destination team exercises the option to buy at the end of the loan. Null if no option exists.']
  performance_bonus_clause varchar [note: 'Description of any performance-based bonus payments owed to the origin team based on the player\'s achievements with the destination team (e.g., tournament wins, MVP awards).']
  public_announcement_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the official public announcement or press release for this transfer.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the league or parties for why the transfer was rejected or cancelled. Null if transfer was not rejected.']
  salary_responsibility_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the player\'s salary that the origin team continues to pay during a loan transfer. Null for permanent transfers.']
  sell_on_clause_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of any future transfer fee that the origin team is entitled to receive if the destination team sells the player to another team. Null if no sell-on clause exists.']
  social_media_engagement_count varchar [note: 'Aggregate count of social media engagements (likes, shares, comments) on the transfer announcement, used for marketing and fan sentiment analysis.']
  transfer_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for this transfer transaction, used in public announcements and league filings.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transfer: pending league approval, publicly announced, completed and roster-locked, cancelled by parties, or rejected by league.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transfer mechanism: permanent transfer, loan, free agent signing, buyout clause activation, or player trade.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this transfer record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for a player transfer between esports teams, including free agent signings, loans, and buyouts. Captures transfer type, origin team, destination team, transfer fee amount, loan duration, buyout clause triggered flag, transfer window reference, effective date, announcement date, and transfer status. Tracks the competitive roster movement market distinct from the player_contract which governs the ongoing employment terms.'
}

Table esports.team_asset_license {
  team_asset_license_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this team-asset licensing relationship. Primary key.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the digital asset being licensed to the team']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (legal, brand manager, or esports operations) who approved this license. Required for audit trail and IP rights management.']
  team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the esports team that holds usage rights for the asset']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the license was approved for team use. Used for compliance reporting and audit trails.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the license agreement. Pending licenses cannot be used in official competition. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the asset being licensed (logo, jersey, promotional material, in-game skin, sponsor asset). Determines usage restrictions and approval workflows. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this license record was created in the system.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicating whether this team has exclusive rights to this asset (true) or if the asset may be licensed to other teams (false). Critical for sponsor asset management.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this license record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit.']
  license_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fee paid by the team (or sponsor) for usage rights to this asset. May be zero for team-owned assets or sponsor-provided assets.']
  license_terms varchar [note: 'Legal terms and conditions governing the team\'s usage of this asset, including permitted contexts (competitive play, broadcast, social media), geographic restrictions, and exclusivity clauses. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  logo_asset_url varchar [note: 'URL or reference path to the teams official logo asset stored in the content delivery network (CDN) or digital asset management system. Used for broadcasts, websites, and marketing materials. [Moved from esports_team: This attribute (logo_asset_url) in esports_team represents a specific team-asset relationship and should be modeled as a license record in the association. A team may have multiple logo variants (primary, alternate, sponsor co-branded) with different usage rights and approval dates. Moving this to the association allows tracking of multiple logo licenses with proper versioning and rights management.]']
  usage_context varchar [note: 'Specific contexts where the team is permitted to use this asset (competitive play, broadcast, social media, merchandise, marketing). Enforces licensing restrictions.']
  usage_rights_end_date date [note: 'The date when the team\'s usage rights for this asset expire. Used to enforce license renewal workflows and prevent unauthorized usage. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  usage_rights_start_date date [note: 'The date when the team\'s usage rights for this asset become active. Critical for tournament eligibility and brand compliance. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Specific version of the asset that this license covers. Teams must use the licensed version to maintain brand consistency and compliance. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the licensing agreement between an esports team and a digital asset. It captures the legal rights, usage terms, and approval workflow for teams to use specific assets (logos, jerseys, promotional materials, in-game skins, sponsor assets) in competitive play, broadcasts, and marketing. Each record links one esports team to one asset with licensing terms, usage rights periods, approval status, and asset version tracking that exist only in the context of this IP rights relationship.. Existence Justification: In esports operations, teams actively manage licensing relationships for multiple digital assets (team logos, jersey designs, promotional graphics, in-game skins, sponsor-provided assets) with specific usage rights, approval workflows, and time-bound terms. Simultaneously, individual assets (particularly sponsor logos and shared promotional materials) are licensed to multiple teams under different terms. The business recognizes these as \'Team Asset Licenses\' that require active management for IP compliance, tournament eligibility, and brand governance.'
}

Table esports.tournament_promotion {
  tournament_promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this specific promotion-tournament activation record. Primary key.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the promotional pricing event activated for this tournament']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the esports tournament during which this promotion is active']
  activation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this promotion activation for this tournament: scheduled (not yet started), active (currently running), paused (temporarily disabled), completed (ended normally), cancelled (terminated early).']
  conversion_count varchar [note: 'Number of times players redeemed this promotion during this specific tournament. Tournament-specific conversion metric.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this promotion expired for this specific tournament. May differ from the promotion\'s global end_timestamp if the promotion is deactivated at different times for different tournaments.']
  impression_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this promotion was displayed to players during this specific tournament. Tournament-specific metric for ROI analysis.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Display priority ranking for this promotion within the context of this specific tournament. Determines ordering when multiple promotions are active during the same tournament event.']
  revenue_generated decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total gross revenue generated from this promotion during this specific tournament. Tournament-specific revenue attribution for promotional ROI analysis.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this promotion became active for this specific tournament. May differ from the promotion\'s global start_timestamp if the promotion is activated at different times for different tournaments.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Event between promotion and tournament. It captures the activation of a specific promotional pricing event within the context of a specific esports tournament, tracking performance metrics and timing for each promotion-tournament pairing. Each record links one promotion to one tournament with attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship.. Existence Justification: In gaming monetization operations, promotional pricing events are strategically activated across multiple esports tournaments to drive in-game purchase conversions during high-engagement competitive events. A single promotion (e.g., \'20% off battle pass\') can run concurrently across multiple tournaments (Spring Championship, Regional Qualifiers, Community Cup), and each tournament features multiple concurrent promotions targeting different player segments or product categories. The business actively manages these promotion-tournament activations as discrete marketing events, tracking performance metrics (impressions, conversions, revenue) per tournament to optimize promotional calendars and calculate tournament-specific ROI.'
}

Table esports.tournament_asset_usage {
  tournament_asset_usage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this tournament asset usage record. Primary key.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the digital asset being used in the tournament']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the tournament where the asset is used']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this usage record was created in the system.']
  display_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration in seconds that the asset is displayed or used during the tournament broadcast or event. Null for static assets without time constraints. Identified in detection phase.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this usage record was last modified.']
  placement_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the asset is placed (e.g., top-right overlay, center stage screen, pre-match intro, post-match recap). Identified in detection phase.']
  rights_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Status of rights clearance for using this asset in this specific tournament context. Critical for legal compliance and sponsor contract fulfillment. Identified in detection phase.']
  sponsor_attribution varchar [note: 'Sponsor or brand associated with this asset usage, used for sponsor deliverable tracking and reporting. Null if not sponsor-related. Identified in detection phase.']
  usage_context varchar [note: 'The specific context or purpose for which the asset is used in the tournament (e.g., broadcast overlay, sponsor logo placement, promotional video, stream graphic). Identified in detection phase.']
  usage_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this asset usage in the tournament lifecycle (planned, active during event, completed, cancelled, replaced by another asset).']
  usage_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset was deployed or first used in the tournament. Used for audit trail and sponsor reporting. Identified in detection phase.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created this tournament asset usage record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the usage relationship between tournaments and digital assets. It captures the operational tracking of which assets (logos, overlays, sponsor graphics, promotional videos) are deployed in which tournaments, including usage context, placement, duration, and rights compliance. Each record links one tournament to one asset with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific usage instance.. Existence Justification: In esports tournament operations, tournaments use multiple digital assets (logos, overlays, sponsor graphics, promotional videos) across different contexts (broadcast, venue, social media), and assets are reused across multiple tournaments. Tournament organizers actively manage asset usage as part of sponsor deliverable fulfillment, broadcast production, and rights compliance. This is an operational relationship with specific usage data (context, placement, duration, rights status) that belongs to neither the tournament nor the asset alone.'
}

Table esports.team_event_participation {
  team_event_participation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this team-event participation record. Primary key.']
  community_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the community event in which the team is participating']
  team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the esports team participating in the community event']
  appearance_fee decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary compensation paid to the team for participating in this specific community event. Varies by event, team popularity, and participation type. Identified in detection phase as compensation.']
  attendance_count varchar [note: 'The number of team members (players, coaches, staff) who attended this specific community event on behalf of the team. Identified in detection phase as engagement metric.']
  confirmation_date date [note: 'The date when the team confirmed their participation in this community event.']
  confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the team\'s participation commitment for this event. Tracks the lifecycle from invitation through completion.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this participation record was created in the system.']
  engagement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated metric representing the team\'s engagement quality at this event based on attendance, social media activity, fan interactions, and post-event metrics.']
  event_role varchar [note: 'The specific role or function the team fulfilled at this community event (e.g., \'Headliner\', \'Opening Act\', \'Panelist\', \'Demo Team\'). Identified in detection phase.']
  invitation_date date [note: 'The date when the team was invited to participate in this community event.']
  participation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the team is participating in the community event. Drives expectations, compensation structure, and engagement requirements. Identified in detection phase.']
  social_media_reach varchar [note: 'Total social media impressions generated by the team\'s participation in this specific community event across all platforms.']
  sponsorship_tier varchar [note: 'The sponsorship level at which the team is supporting this specific community event. Different from the team\'s general sponsorship_tier attribute. Identified in detection phase.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this participation record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the participation relationship between esports teams and community events. It captures the business arrangement when an esports team participates in, sponsors, or appears at a community-organized event. Each record links one esports team to one community event with attributes that describe the nature and terms of that specific participation.. Existence Justification: In gaming and esports operations, teams participate in multiple community events throughout the year (meet-and-greets, charity streams, fan tournaments, AMAs, signing events), and each community event typically features multiple teams to drive engagement and attendance. The business actively manages these participation arrangements as operational records with specific terms (appearance fees, participation type, roles) and tracks engagement outcomes (attendance count, social media reach, engagement scores) for each team-event combination.'
}

Table esports.league_influencer_partnership {
  league_influencer_partnership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for league_influencer_partnership']
  influencer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the influencer partner engaged for league promotion']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the esports league that is the subject of the influencer partnership']
  brand_safety_guidelines varchar [note: 'Specific brand safety and content guidelines the influencer must follow when creating content for this league.']
  compensation_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The total compensation amount paid to the influencer for this specific league partnership. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  compensation_currency varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the compensation amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  content_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the league/company requires pre-approval of influencer content before publication for this partnership.']
  content_deliverables varchar [note: 'Description of the specific content obligations the influencer must deliver for this league (e.g., \'2 livestreams per week during season\', \'5 social posts per month\', \'opening ceremony coverage\'). Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  exclusivity_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the exclusivity restrictions for this partnership (e.g., \'no competing FPS league coverage\', \'exclusive for this game title\', \'non-exclusive\'). Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  partnership_end_date date [note: 'The date when this league-influencer partnership agreement expires or terminates. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  partnership_start_date date [note: 'The date when this league-influencer partnership agreement becomes effective. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  partnership_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this league-influencer partnership (draft, active, completed, terminated, suspended).']
  performance_metrics_target varchar [note: 'Specific performance targets or KPIs agreed for this partnership (e.g., \'minimum 50K views per stream\', \'engagement rate >5%\', \'drive 1000 installs\').']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual partnership between an esports league and an influencer/content creator. It captures the business agreement governing content creation, promotional activities, and compensation for influencer coverage of league events. Each record links one league to one influencer with partnership-specific terms, deliverables, exclusivity scope, and financial arrangements that exist only in the context of this marketing relationship.. Existence Justification: In the gaming industry, esports leagues routinely engage multiple influencers to create content, drive viewership, and promote events across different platforms and audience segments. Simultaneously, successful influencers partner with multiple leagues (e.g., covering both League of Legends LCS and Valorant Champions Tour) to diversify their content portfolio and revenue streams. Each league-influencer partnership is a distinct contractual relationship with its own terms, deliverables, compensation, and exclusivity scope.'
}

Table esports.venue {
  venue_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for venue']
  parent_venue_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on venue (parent_venue_id)']
  accessibility_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the venue meets accessibility standards for individuals with disabilities.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the venue location.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as suite, floor, or building number.']
  broadcast_infrastructure_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the venues broadcast and streaming infrastructure capabilities for esports events.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the venue is located.']
  concession_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether food and beverage concessions are available at the venue.']
  contract_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current venue partnership or rental contract expires.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the venue is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the venue record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for financial transactions related to the venue.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for venue booking and event coordination.']
  last_renovation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent major renovation or upgrade to the venue facilities.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the venue location in decimal degrees.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the venue location in decimal degrees.']
  merchandise_store_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an official merchandise store or booth is available at the venue.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the venue record was last modified or updated.']
  name varchar [note: 'Official name of the esports venue or arena.']
  network_bandwidth_gbps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Available network bandwidth in gigabits per second for online tournament connectivity and streaming.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments about the venue including special requirements, restrictions, or operational details.']
  opened_date date [note: 'Date when the venue first opened for esports events and operations.']
  operator_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization responsible for day-to-day venue operations and event management.']
  owner_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or entity that owns the venue.']
  parking_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of parking spaces available at or near the venue for event attendees.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the venue operations team.']
  player_station_count varchar [note: 'Number of dedicated gaming stations or player setups available in the venue for competitive play.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the venue address.']
  practice_room_count varchar [note: 'Number of separate practice or warm-up rooms available for teams at the venue.']
  preferred_vendor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the venue is a preferred or priority partner for esports events.']
  rental_rate_per_day_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard daily rental rate for booking the venue for esports events in US dollars.']
  seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of spectators that can be accommodated in the venue for esports events.']
  standing_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of standing spectators allowed in the venue during esports events.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the venue is located.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the venue in the esports ecosystem.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the venue location used for event scheduling and broadcast timing.']
  total_capacity varchar [note: 'Combined maximum capacity including seated and standing spectators for esports events.']
  venue_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to uniquely identify the venue in tournament systems and broadcasts.']
  venue_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the venue based on its primary use and structure for esports events.']
  vip_suite_count varchar [note: 'Number of VIP suites or premium viewing areas available for sponsors and special guests.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL for the venue providing information and booking details.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for venue. Referenced by venue_id.'
}

Table esports.round {
  round_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for round']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game title being played in this round.']
  previous_round_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on round (previous_round_id)']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent tournament or league that this round belongs to.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical or virtual venue where this round is hosted.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the round concluded.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the round commenced, capturing the real-world event time.']
  advancement_count varchar [note: 'Number of teams or players that advance from this round to the next stage.']
  average_concurrent_users varchar [note: 'Average number of concurrent viewers/users throughout the duration of this round.']
  best_of_format varchar [note: 'Number of games in the best-of series for matches in this round (e.g., 1 for Bo1, 3 for Bo3, 5 for Bo5).']
  bracket_type varchar [note: 'Indicates which bracket this round belongs to in double-elimination or multi-bracket tournament structures.']
  broadcast_language varchar [note: 'Primary language(s) for broadcast coverage of this round (e.g., \'English\', \'Spanish\', \'Multi-language\').']
  broadcast_rights_holder varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or platform holding exclusive or primary broadcast rights for this round.']
  completed_matches varchar [note: 'Number of matches that have been completed in this round.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this round record was first created in the system.']
  elimination_count varchar [note: 'Number of teams or players that are eliminated from the tournament after this round.']
  game_version varchar [note: 'Specific version or patch of the game being used for this round (e.g., \'12.4.1\', \'Season 3 Patch 2\').']
  is_online boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this round is conducted online (true) or at a physical venue (false).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this round record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this round.']
  peak_concurrent_users varchar [note: 'Maximum number of concurrent viewers/users watching or participating during this round across all platforms.']
  primary_sponsor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary or title sponsor for this round.']
  prize_pool_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total prize money allocated specifically for this round, if applicable.']
  prize_pool_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the prize pool amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or territory where this round is conducted (e.g., \'North America\', \'Europe\', \'Asia-Pacific\', \'Global\').']
  round_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used for external identification and reporting (e.g., \'GS\', \'QF\', \'SF\', \'GF\').']
  round_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the round (e.g., \'Group Stage\', \'Quarterfinals\', \'Grand Finals\', \'Upper Bracket Round 1\').']
  round_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the position of this round within the tournament bracket structure (e.g., 1 for first round, 2 for quarterfinals).']
  round_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the round indicating its operational status.']
  round_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the round format indicating the bracket structure and elimination rules.']
  ruleset_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the tournament ruleset governing this round.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned date when the round is scheduled to conclude.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date when the round is scheduled to begin.']
  seeding_method varchar [note: 'Method used to seed teams or players into this round (e.g., based on ranking, regional performance, or random draw).']
  sponsor_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary sponsorship level for this round.']
  total_matches varchar [note: 'Total number of matches scheduled to be played in this round.']
  total_viewership varchar [note: 'Total unique viewers who watched any portion of this round across all broadcast channels and platforms.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for round. Referenced by round_id.'
}

Table esports.division {
  division_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for division']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title for which this division is organized (e.g., League of Legends, Counter-Strike, Valorant).']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent esports league to which this division belongs.']
  organizer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tournament organizer or entity responsible for operating this division (first-party or third-party).']
  parent_division_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on division (parent_division_id)']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the current or most recent competitive season associated with this division.']
  average_ccu varchar [note: 'Average number of concurrent viewers across all broadcast channels during division matches.']
  broadcast_rights_holder varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or entity holding exclusive broadcast rights for this division.']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the division for operational and reporting purposes (e.g., \'PREM\', \'CHAL\', \'REG_NA\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this division record was first created in the system.']
  current_team_count varchar [note: 'Current number of teams actively registered and participating in this division.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the division, including its purpose, competitive structure, and unique characteristics.']
  end_date date [note: 'Official end date when the division concludes or is scheduled to conclude. Null for ongoing divisions.']
  format varchar [note: 'Tournament or competition format used within this division (e.g., round robin, single elimination, Swiss system).']
  is_franchised boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this division operates under a franchised model with permanent team slots (true) or an open promotion/relegation system (false).']
  match_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Frequency and pattern of scheduled matches within this division.']
  minimum_age_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum age in years required for players to participate in this division.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this division record was last modified.']
  name varchar [note: 'Official name of the esports division (e.g., \'Premier League\', \'Challenger Division\', \'Regional Championship\').']
  peak_ccu varchar [note: 'Peak number of concurrent viewers recorded during any single division match or event.']
  prize_pool_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary prize pool allocated for this division, in the base currency.']
  prize_pool_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the prize pool amount (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\').']
  promotion_slots varchar [note: 'Number of teams that can be promoted from this division to a higher tier at the end of the season.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO code representing the geographic region this division serves (e.g., \'USA\', \'EUR\', \'KOR\', \'CHN\').']
  registration_close_date date [note: 'Date when team registration for this division closes.']
  registration_open_date date [note: 'Date when team registration for this division opens.']
  relegation_slots varchar [note: 'Number of teams that will be relegated from this division to a lower tier at the end of the season.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of division revenue shared with participating teams, if applicable.']
  ruleset_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the official ruleset governing competition in this division.']
  sponsorship_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary sponsorship level secured for this division.']
  start_date date [note: 'Official start date when the division becomes active and competition begins.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the division in its lifecycle.']
  team_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of teams that can participate in this division.']
  tier varchar [note: 'Competitive tier or level of the division indicating skill level and prestige (e.g., tier 1 for top-level professional, tier 2 for semi-professional, amateur for grassroots).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for division. Referenced by division_id.'
}

Table esports.transfer_window {
  transfer_window_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for transfer_window']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game title this transfer window applies to (e.g., specific MOBA, FPS, or battle royale title).']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the esports league this transfer window applies to.']
  previous_transfer_window_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on transfer_window (previous_transfer_window_id)']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the competitive season during which this transfer window occurs.']
  allows_free_agents boolean [note: 'Indicates whether teams are permitted to sign free agent players (not currently under contract) during this window.']
  allows_loans boolean [note: 'Indicates whether teams are permitted to loan players to other teams temporarily during this window.']
  allows_releases boolean [note: 'Indicates whether teams are permitted to release players from their contracts during this window.']
  allows_trades boolean [note: 'Indicates whether teams are permitted to trade players with other teams during this window.']
  announcement_date date [note: 'Date when the transfer window schedule and rules were officially announced to teams and the public.']
  approval_deadline_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours before window close by which transfer requests must be submitted for league approval consideration.']
  close_date date [note: 'Date when the transfer window officially closes and no further roster changes are permitted.']
  close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the transfer window closes, including time zone information for global coordination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transfer window record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the transfer window purpose, special rules, and any unique conditions or restrictions.']
  emergency_extension_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the league commissioner can grant emergency extensions to the window close time under exceptional circumstances.']
  max_roster_size varchar [note: 'Maximum number of players each team is allowed to have on their roster after transfers are completed.']
  max_transfers_per_team varchar [note: 'Maximum number of player transfers a single team is permitted to execute during this window.']
  min_roster_size varchar [note: 'Minimum number of players each team must maintain on their roster after transfers are completed.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the league administrator who last modified this transfer window record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transfer window record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal administrative notes and comments regarding this transfer window, including special circumstances or historical context.']
  open_date date [note: 'Date when the transfer window officially opens and teams can begin submitting roster change requests.']
  open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the transfer window opens, including time zone information for global coordination.']
  region varchar [note: 'Three-letter geographic region code where this transfer window applies (e.g., \'NAM\' for North America, \'EUR\' for Europe, \'KOR\' for Korea).']
  requires_league_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all transfers during this window must be reviewed and approved by league officials before becoming effective.']
  ruleset_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the transfer rules and regulations governing this window (e.g., \'2.1\', \'3.0.1\').']
  salary_cap_applies boolean [note: 'Indicates whether salary cap restrictions are enforced for transfers executed during this window.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the transfer window indicating whether teams can execute roster changes.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the transfer window schedule (e.g., \'America/Los_Angeles\', \'Europe/London\').']
  total_teams_participating varchar [note: 'Number of teams that executed at least one roster change during this transfer window.']
  total_transfers_executed varchar [note: 'Total number of player transfers successfully completed across all teams during this window.']
  transfer_fee_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether teams must pay transfer fees to acquire players from other teams during this window.']
  window_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the transfer window, used for external references and display purposes.']
  window_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the transfer window (e.g., \'Summer 2024 Transfer Window\', \'Mid-Season Roster Changes Q1 2024\').']
  window_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transfer window based on timing and purpose within the competitive calendar.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the league administrator who created this transfer window record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for transfer_window. Referenced by window_id.'
}

Table esports.organizer {
  organizer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for organizer']
  parent_organizer_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on organizer (parent_organizer_id)']
  average_annual_events varchar [note: 'Average number of esports events or tournaments organized per year, used for capacity and performance assessment.']
  broadcast_rights_holder boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organizer holds broadcast rights for the events they organize (true) or if broadcast rights are managed separately (false).']
  compliance_certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the organizer\'s compliance with industry standards, regulatory requirements, or internal certification programs.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the master contract or agreement governing the relationship with this organizer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this organizer record was first created in the system.']
  founded_date date [note: 'Date when the organizer entity was founded or established.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the organizer\'s tournament and event operations.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City where the organizer\'s headquarters or primary office is located.']
  headquarters_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the organizer is headquartered.']
  insurance_coverage_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total insurance coverage amount in USD that the organizer maintains for event liability, cancellation, and other risks.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the organizer\'s current insurance policy coverage.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance, financial, or operational audit conducted on the organizer.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the organizer entity as it appears in official business registration documents.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full legal or business name of the tournament organizer entity.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance, financial, or operational audit of the organizer.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or context about the organizer that do not fit structured fields.']
  organizer_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code assigned to the organizer for external reference and integration purposes.']
  organizer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the organizer based on their relationship to the business: first-party (operated internally), third-party (external professional organizer), partner (strategic partnership), independent (unaffiliated), or community (grassroots/amateur).']
  partnership_end_date date [note: 'Date when the business relationship or partnership with this organizer ended or is scheduled to end. Null for ongoing partnerships.']
  partnership_start_date date [note: 'Date when the business relationship or partnership with this organizer began.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days within which payments are due to the organizer per contractual agreement (e.g., Net 30, Net 60).']
  preferred_payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for disbursing payments to the organizer.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for business communication with the organizer.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for direct communication with the organizer.']
  reputation_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Quantitative reputation or quality score for the organizer based on past performance, community feedback, and operational excellence, typically on a scale of 0.00 to 5.00.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of revenue shared with the organizer from tournaments, events, or sponsorships they manage, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 15.50 for 15.5%).']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or commonly used short name for the organizer, used in displays and branding.']
  social_media_followers_count varchar [note: 'Total number of followers across the organizer\'s primary social media platforms, used as a measure of reach and influence.']
  social_media_twitter_handle varchar [note: 'Official Twitter/X handle of the organizer for social media engagement and communication.']
  specialization varchar [note: 'Primary game titles, genres, or esports categories that the organizer specializes in (e.g., FPS, MOBA, Battle Royale, specific game franchises).']
  sponsorship_management boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organizer manages sponsorship agreements and activations for their events (true) or if sponsorships are managed by the business (false).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the organizer in the system lifecycle.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number or equivalent business registration number for the organizer entity, used for financial and regulatory compliance.']
  tier varchar [note: 'Tier classification indicating the scale and prestige level of the organizer: tier 1 (major international), tier 2 (established regional), tier 3 (emerging), regional, or local.']
  total_prize_pool_distributed_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative total prize pool amount in USD that the organizer has distributed across all tournaments and events to date.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this organizer record was last modified in the system.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the organizer for public information and branding.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for organizer. Referenced by organizer_id.'
}

Table content.asset {
  asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for asset']
  age_rating_cert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.age_rating_cert. Business justification: Assets (textures, audio, models) are evaluated by rating authorities for content descriptors (violence, language, sexual content). Asset approval gates require rating impact assessment before producti']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Asset development costs are allocated to cost centers for budgeting, capitalization decisions, and studio P&L reporting. Finance tracks asset creation costs by cost center for variance analysis and ca']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Asset creation tracking is fundamental in game development for IP rights attribution, performance reviews, and audit trails. Every asset must have a creator tracked for legal and operational accountab']
  game_server_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.game_server. Business justification: Game servers load specific asset versions at runtime. Operations teams track which assets are deployed to which servers for performance troubleshooting, rollback coordination, and version mismatch dia']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the game development studio or team that created or owns the asset. Links to the Studio domain for organizational tracking.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the game title or project for which the asset was created. Links to the Game Title domain for product-level tracking.']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensed_ip. Business justification: Game assets (textures, models, audio) frequently embed licensed IP (middleware SDKs, music tracks, brand logos). IP clearance tracking is mandatory for platform certification, rating board submissions']
  parent_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent or base asset ID if this asset is a variant (LOD variant, locale variant, platform variant). Null for base assets.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the asset was approved for production use by QA (Quality Assurance) or content review team.']
  asset_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for the asset used across development tools and pipelines.']
  asset_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the asset including its purpose, visual or audio characteristics, and usage notes for developers and artists.']
  asset_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the digital asset as it appears in the content management system and game engine.']
