  asset_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the digital asset by its primary content type in the game development pipeline. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: art|audio|texture|mesh|animation|shader|vfx|ui_element|video|script — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  audio_bit_depth varchar [note: 'Bit depth for audio assets (e.g., 16, 24). Applicable only to audio asset types. Affects dynamic range and audio fidelity.']
  audio_channel_config varchar [note: 'Channel configuration for audio assets (mono, stereo, 5.1 surround, 7.1 surround, Dolby Atmos). Applicable only to audio asset types.']
  audio_middleware_format varchar [note: 'Audio middleware system format for the asset (Wwise, FMOD, Unity Audio, Unreal Audio, proprietary). Applicable only to audio asset types.']
  audio_sample_rate_hz varchar [note: 'Sample rate in Hertz for audio assets (e.g., 44100, 48000). Applicable only to audio asset types. Determines audio quality and file size.']
  cdn_url varchar [note: 'CDN (Content Delivery Network) URL or endpoint where the asset is hosted for distribution to players. Used for live-ops content delivery and patches.']
  classification_tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or pipe-separated tags for categorizing the asset by genre, art style, feature area, platform, sensitivity, or other business dimensions. Enables search and filtering.']
  content_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash (e.g., SHA-256) of the asset file content for integrity verification, deduplication, and change detection.']
  deprecated_date date [note: 'Date when the asset was marked as deprecated and scheduled for removal from active use.']
  download_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the asset has been downloaded by players or distributed via CDN (Content Delivery Network). Applicable for DLC (Downloadable Content) and live-ops content drops.']
  drm_protected boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the asset is protected by DRM (Digital Rights Management) technology (True) or is unprotected (False).']
  engine_compatibility varchar [note: 'Game engine(s) with which this asset is compatible (Unity, Unreal Engine, proprietary engine, or cross-engine).']
  feature_area varchar [note: 'Game feature or system area where the asset is used (e.g., combat, UI, environment, character, vehicle). Aligns with game design documentation.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the asset file in bytes. Critical for build optimization, CDN (Content Delivery Network) distribution planning, and platform certification compliance.']
  format varchar [note: 'File format or extension of the asset (e.g., PNG, FBX, WAV, MP4, JSON). Determines compatibility with game engines and platforms.']
  ip_rights_status varchar [note: 'Intellectual Property (IP) rights status of the asset indicating ownership or licensing arrangement (owned, licensed, third-party, public domain, pending clearance).']
  is_locale_variant boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this asset is a locale-specific variant (True) or a locale-agnostic base asset (False).']
  last_modified_date date [note: 'Date when the asset was last modified or updated in the content pipeline.']
  license_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date for licensed third-party assets. Null for owned or perpetual-license assets. Critical for compliance and content continuity.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the asset in its content lifecycle from creation through deployment to retirement.']
  lod_tier varchar [note: 'LOD (Level of Detail) tier indicating the quality/complexity variant of the asset for performance optimization across different hardware capabilities.']
  moderation_status varchar [note: 'Moderation review status for UGC (User-Generated Content) assets. Tracks whether the asset has passed content moderation and compliance checks.']
  perforce_depot_path varchar [note: 'Full depot path in Perforce Helix Core version control system where the asset is stored. Authoritative source location for the asset file.']
  platform_target varchar [note: 'Target platform(s) for which this asset variant is optimized (e.g., PC, PlayStation, Xbox, Nintendo Switch, iOS, Android). Multiple platforms may be pipe-separated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pc|playstation|xbox|nintendo_switch|ios|android|web|vr|ar — promote to reference product]']
  published_date date [note: 'Date when the asset was published to live game builds or made available for deployment.']
  resolution_spec varchar [note: 'Resolution or quality specification for visual and audio assets (e.g., 1920x1080, 4K, 48kHz/16-bit). Used for LOD (Level of Detail) management and platform targeting.']
  supported_locales varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or pipe-separated list of locale codes (e.g., en-US, fr-FR, ja-JP) for which this asset has localized variants or is applicable. Used for localization targeting.']
  ugc_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this asset is User-Generated Content (UGC) created by players (True) or first-party content created by the studio (False).']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the asset is referenced or used across game builds, levels, or scenes. Used for impact analysis and optimization decisions.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the asset following semantic versioning or internal versioning scheme. Tracks iterations and revisions.']
  creation_date date [note: 'Date when the asset was first created or ingested into the content management system.']

  Note: 'Master record for every digital asset in the game content pipeline — art, audio, texture, mesh, animation, shader, VFX, UI element, video, and scripted data. Tracks asset name, type, format, file size, resolution/bitrate specs, LOD tier, platform target, engine compatibility (Unity/Unreal), creation date, author, current lifecycle status (draft, review, approved, deprecated), Perforce depot path, content hash for integrity verification, classification tags (genre, art style, feature area, platform, sensitivity), audio-specific metadata (sample rate, bit depth, channel config, middleware format like Wwise/FMOD), supported locale list, and locale-specific variant flags. This is the authoritative SSOT for all creative deliverables managed in Perforce Helix Core, including locale reference data for asset-level localization targeting.'
}

Table content.asset_version {
  asset_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this immutable asset version record. Primary key.']
  age_rating_cert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.age_rating_cert. Business justification: Version changes to assets may alter content rating (e.g., blood texture update triggers re-certification). Certification teams track which asset versions were submitted for ESRB/PEGI approval.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent digital asset entity that this version belongs to. Links to the master asset record across all its revisions.']
  capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_project. Business justification: Asset versions are capitalized as part of game development projects. Finance needs version-to-project linkage to determine capitalization eligibility, track accumulated costs, and calculate amortizati']
  committed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Version control systems require tracking who committed each asset revision for code review workflows, accountability, and audit compliance. Essential for Perforce/source control integration and change']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the live-ops content drop, seasonal event, patch, or hotfix release that this asset version is part of. Null for non-scheduled commits.']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the development studio that created this asset version. Used for attribution and cross-studio asset sharing governance.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title that this asset version belongs to. Links asset to the product catalog.']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensed_ip. Business justification: Version control systems must track which licensed IP components (middleware versions, music stems) are embedded in each asset revision to support compliance audits, royalty reconciliation, and license']
  rollback_from_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the asset version that this version rolled back from during a live-ops incident. Null for normal forward progression. Used for incident analysis.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the asset type. Used for pipeline routing and build system processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: texture|model|audio|animation|level|script|localization|ui|vfx|shader — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  audio_duration_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Duration of audio assets in seconds. Null for non-audio assets. Used for memory and streaming bandwidth planning.']
  branch_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Perforce branch or stream where this version was committed. Examples: main, dev, release/1.5, feature/new-character.']
  build_error_message varchar [note: 'Error message from the build pipeline if processing failed. Null for successful builds. Used for troubleshooting and QA.']
  build_pipeline_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the automated build pipeline processing for this asset version. Tracks whether the asset has been successfully compiled, optimized, and packaged.']
  build_pipeline_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the build pipeline last processed this asset version. Null if not yet processed.']
  cdn_distribution_status varchar [note: 'Status of this asset versions distribution to CDN edge nodes for player download. Tracks global content propagation.']
  cdn_distribution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this asset version was successfully distributed to all CDN regions. Null if not yet distributed.']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Commit message provided by the author describing what was changed in this version. May include Jira ticket references, bug fix notes, or feature descriptions.']
  changelist_number varchar [note: 'Perforce Helix Core changelist number representing the atomic commit that introduced this asset version. Unique identifier for the commit transaction.']
  checksum_md5 varchar [note: 'MD5 hash of the asset file content for integrity verification and duplicate detection. Used to validate file transfers and detect corruption.']
  commit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when this asset version was committed to Perforce Helix Core. Represents the business event time of the version creation.']
  compression_format varchar [note: 'Compression algorithm or format applied to this asset version. Examples: BC7, ASTC, Vorbis, Opus, LZ4. Null for uncompressed assets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this asset version record was first created in the data lakehouse. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  depot_path varchar [note: 'Full Perforce depot path to the asset file including depot name, directory structure, and filename. Example: //depot/GameAssets/Characters/Hero/hero_model.fbx']
  deprecated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this asset version was marked as deprecated. Null if not deprecated.']
  drm_protected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset version is protected by DRM encryption. True for premium DLC and licensed content requiring protection.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the asset file in bytes for this specific version. Used for storage capacity planning and CDN optimization.']
  file_size_delta_bytes varchar [note: 'Change in file size compared to the previous revision in bytes. Positive for growth, negative for reduction. Null for first revision.']
  file_type varchar [note: 'Perforce file type classification indicating how the file is stored and merged. Examples: text, binary, binary+l (exclusive lock), utf16.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset version has been marked as deprecated and should no longer be used in new builds. Used for asset lifecycle management.']
  localization_language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (with optional ISO 3166-1 region) for localized assets. Examples: en, en-US, ja, pt-BR. Null for non-localized assets.']
  lod_level varchar [note: 'LOD variant level for this asset version. LOD0 is highest quality, higher numbers are progressively lower detail for performance optimization. None for non-LOD assets. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lod0|lod1|lod2|lod3|lod4|lod5|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  platform_target varchar [note: 'Target gaming platform for this asset version. Assets may be optimized differently per platform. Multi indicates cross-platform asset. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pc|ps5|ps4|xbox_series|xbox_one|switch|ios|android|web|multi — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  polygon_count varchar [note: 'Number of polygons (triangles) in 3D model assets. Null for non-model assets. Used for performance budgeting and LOD validation.']
  promoted_to_live_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this asset version was deployed to the live production environment and became visible to players. Null if not yet live.']
  promoted_to_staging_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this asset version was promoted to the staging environment for UAT. Null if not yet promoted.']
  promotion_stage varchar [note: 'Current deployment stage of this asset version in the content delivery lifecycle. Tracks progression from development through to live production.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for this asset file within its depot path. Increments with each commit to the same file.']
  stream_path varchar [note: 'Perforce stream path if using stream-based development model. Defines the stream hierarchy and inheritance. Null for classic branch-based workflows.']
  texture_resolution varchar [note: 'Resolution of texture assets in pixels (e.g., 2048x2048, 4096x4096). Null for non-texture assets. Used for memory budget tracking.']
  ugc_moderation_status varchar [note: 'Moderation review status for user-generated content assets. None for first-party studio assets. Used to enforce community standards and COPPA compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this asset version record was last modified in the data lakehouse. Audit trail for data lineage.']

  Note: 'Immutable version record for each committed revision of a digital asset in Perforce Helix Core. Captures changelist number, depot path, revision number, branch/stream, commit author, commit timestamp, change description, file size delta, checksum, build pipeline status, and promotion stage (dev, staging, live). Enables full asset lineage tracing from first creation through live deployment and supports rollback operations during live-ops incidents.'
}

Table content.asset_bundle {
  asset_bundle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset bundle. Primary key.']
  age_rating_cert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.age_rating_cert. Business justification: Bundles are the unit submitted to rating authorities for platform certification. TRC/TCR workflows require linking bundle builds to ESRB/PEGI submission records for audit trail and deployment gating.']
  capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_project. Business justification: Asset bundles represent deliverable units that trigger capitalization milestones and amortization start dates. Finance tracks bundle certification and release dates to determine when development costs']
  cdn_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.cdn_node. Business justification: Asset bundles are distributed via CDN nodes for player downloads. CDN operators track which bundles are cached on which nodes to optimize cache hit ratios, plan bandwidth allocation, and diagnose down']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_release. Business justification: Asset bundles are packaged for specific content releases (DLC packs, seasonal events, patches). This FK links the bundle to its parent release record. N:1 cardinality - many bundles can be part of one']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the development studio responsible for creating this asset bundle. Used for attribution and resource tracking.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the game title (product catalog) that this asset bundle belongs to. Links the content layer to the title domain.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.ip_agreement. Business justification: Asset bundles containing licensed middleware, music, or brand content must reference governing agreements to validate distribution rights, calculate per-download royalties, and enforce territory/platf']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Launch campaigns distribute specific asset bundles (DLC, seasonal content packs). Marketing tracks which bundles are featured in campaigns for attribution, performance analysis, and revenue reporting.']
  perforce_changelist_id varchar [note: 'Perforce Helix Core changelist number associated with this bundle version, enabling traceability to source control commits and asset pipeline history.']
  primary_asset_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the workforce user who created this asset bundle record. Used for accountability and audit trail.']
  server_fleet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.server_fleet. Business justification: Asset bundles are deployed to server fleets as atomic content units. Fleet operators track which bundle versions are active on each fleet to coordinate staged rollouts, diagnose version conflicts, and']
  asset_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual assets (textures, models, audio files, localization strings, etc.) packaged within this bundle.']
  build_configuration varchar [note: 'Build configuration type for the bundle: debug (internal testing with symbols), development (QA testing), shipping (release candidate), gold_master (final approved build for distribution).']
  bundle_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for the asset bundle used in distribution and tracking systems.']
  bundle_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the asset bundle (e.g., \'Winter Event 2024\', \'Patch 1.5.3\', \'Console Launch DLC\').']
  bundle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the asset bundle: draft (initial creation), in_development (active work), qa_testing (quality assurance review), approved (ready for release), published (live in production), deprecated (retired from use).']
  bundle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the asset bundle by delivery purpose: base game (initial release), DLC (Downloadable Content), hotfix (urgent fix), patch (scheduled update), seasonal event (limited-time content), or expansion (major content addition).']
  cdn_distribution_path varchar [note: 'URL or path on the CDN where the asset bundle is hosted for player download. Business-confidential to prevent unauthorized direct access.']
  compressed_size_mb decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total size of the asset bundle in megabytes after compression, used for CDN (Content Delivery Network) bandwidth planning and player download estimates.']
  compression_format varchar [note: 'Compression algorithm used for the bundle (e.g., zip, gzip, lz4, zstd, proprietary engine format).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset bundle record was first created in the content management system. Used for audit trail and lifecycle tracking.']
  deprecated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset bundle was deprecated and removed from active distribution. Null if still active.']
  download_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this asset bundle has been downloaded by players. Used for analytics and content performance tracking.']
  encryption_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset bundle is encrypted to protect intellectual property (IP) and prevent unauthorized access or piracy. Aligns with DRM (Digital Rights Management) requirements.']
  error_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of failed download attempts for this bundle, used to monitor CDN health and content delivery quality.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the asset bundle is scheduled to be removed or deprecated, typically for seasonal events or limited-time content. Null for permanent content.']
  localization_languages varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of language codes (ISO 639-1) for which localization strings are included in this bundle (e.g., \'en,es,fr,de,ja,zh\').']
  lod_variant_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bundle includes multiple LOD (Level of Detail) variants of assets for performance optimization across different hardware tiers.']
  manifest_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash (e.g., SHA-256) of the bundle manifest file, used for integrity verification and version control in Perforce Helix Core and distribution systems.']
  minimum_engine_version varchar [note: 'Minimum version of the game engine (Unity, Unreal Engine, or proprietary) required to load and run this asset bundle. Ensures compatibility with the game client.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset bundle record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, release notes, known issues, or special instructions related to this asset bundle. Used for internal communication and documentation.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority for this bundle release: critical (urgent hotfix), high (major patch or DLC), medium (seasonal event), low (minor update). Used for resource allocation and scheduling.']
  published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset bundle was published to production CDN and made available to players. Null if not yet published.']
  release_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when the asset bundle was or will be released to players. Used for live operations (live-ops) scheduling and content roadmap planning.']
  requires_player_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether downloading this bundle requires explicit player consent due to size, data usage, or content nature. Aligns with GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) and COPPA (Children\'s Online Privacy Protection Act) requirements.']
  target_platform varchar [note: 'Platform(s) this bundle is built for, pipe-separated if multi-platform (e.g., \'PC\', \'PlayStation 5\', \'Xbox Series X|S\', \'Nintendo Switch\', \'iOS\', \'Android\'). Aligns with first-party console manufacturer and mobile platform requirements.']
  ugc_content_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bundle includes UGC (User-Generated Content) that has passed moderation review. Important for content policy compliance and community management.']
  uncompressed_size_mb decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total size of the asset bundle in megabytes after decompression on the player\'s device, used for storage requirement planning.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of the asset bundle (e.g., \'1.5.3\', \'2.0.0\') used for tracking iterations and compatibility in Perforce Helix Core and CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment) pipelines.']

  Note: 'Logical grouping of assets packaged together for a specific delivery unit — a DLC pack, seasonal event drop, patch, or platform build. Stores bundle name, bundle type (base game, DLC, hotfix, seasonal event, patch), target platform(s), total compressed size, compression format, encryption flag, CDN distribution path, minimum engine version, and bundle manifest hash. Distinct from the title SKU catalog (owned by the title domain); this entity owns the physical content packaging layer.'
}

Table content.lod_variant {
  lod_variant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the LOD variant record. Primary key.']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game development studio that owns and maintains this LOD variant. Supports multi-studio asset governance.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title this LOD variant is associated with. Supports cross-title asset reuse tracking and licensing.']
  parent_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent asset for which this LOD variant is defined. Links to the master asset record in the content domain.']
  source_asset_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset_version. Business justification: LOD variants are generated from a specific source asset version (the high-res master). Currently lod_variant has parent_asset_id (asset-level) but no version-level FK. Adding source_asset_version_id e']
  audio_bitrate_kbps varchar [note: 'Audio bitrate in kilobits per second for audio LOD variants. Null for non-audio assets. Higher bitrate means better quality but larger file size.']
  checksum_hash varchar [note: 'SHA-256 or MD5 hash of the LOD variant asset file. Used for integrity verification, duplicate detection, and CDN cache validation.']
  compression_method varchar [note: 'Compression algorithm applied to this LOD variant (e.g., \'DXT5\', \'BC7\', \'ASTC\', \'Vorbis\', \'None\'). Affects runtime decompression performance and memory usage.']
  content_rating_impact varchar [note: 'Indicates if this LOD variant contains content that affects ESRB, PEGI, or other content rating classifications. Used for compliance and regional variant management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this LOD variant record was first created. Follows format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Supports asset lifecycle tracking.']
  deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current deployment lifecycle status of this LOD variant. Deployed variants are live in production builds; deprecated variants are scheduled for removal.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'File format of the LOD variant asset (e.g., \'FBX\', \'OBJ\', \'PNG\', \'DDS\', \'WAV\', \'OGG\'). Determines compatibility with game engine import pipeline.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the LOD variant asset file in bytes. Used for download size estimation, CDN bandwidth planning, and platform certification compliance (e.g., console TRC/TCR size limits).']
  first_deployed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this LOD variant was first deployed to a production build. Null if never deployed. Follows format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  generation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to create this LOD variant. Manual indicates artist-created; auto-generated indicates engine or tool-generated decimation; procedural indicates runtime generation.']
  generation_tool varchar [note: 'Name and version of the tool used to generate this LOD variant (e.g., \'Simplygon 9.1\', \'Unity LOD Generator 2021.3\', \'Manual in Blender 3.4\'). Supports asset pipeline traceability.']
  has_collision_mesh boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this LOD variant includes a simplified collision mesh for physics calculations. Null for non-geometry assets.']
  is_streaming_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this LOD variant supports runtime streaming (progressive loading). Used for large open-world games to manage memory.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this LOD variant record was last modified. Follows format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  license_restriction varchar [note: 'Any third-party licensing restrictions or intellectual property constraints on this LOD variant (e.g., \'Licensed from XYZ Corp\', \'Internal Use Only\', \'Royalty-Free\'). Confidential business information.']
  localization_variant varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code or region code if this LOD variant is specific to a localization (e.g., \'en-US\', \'ja-JP\', \'de-DE\'). Null for language-agnostic assets.']
  lod_level_index varchar [note: 'The LOD tier index (0-5) where LOD0 is highest quality and LOD5 is lowest quality. Used by the rendering engine to select the appropriate variant based on camera distance or device performance.']
  material_count varchar [note: 'Number of unique materials applied to this LOD variant. Null for non-geometry assets. Affects draw call count and rendering performance.']
  memory_footprint_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated runtime memory footprint of this LOD variant in megabytes when loaded into GPU or system memory. Critical for platform memory budget compliance.']
  perforce_changelist varchar [note: 'Perforce changelist number at which this LOD variant was last modified. Supports asset versioning and rollback.']
  perforce_path varchar [note: 'Full depot path in Perforce Helix Core where this LOD variant asset file is stored (e.g., \'//depot/GameAssets/Characters/Hero/LOD1/hero_lod1.fbx\'). Enables version control integration.']
  platform_certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of this LOD variant in platform-specific certification processes (e.g., console TRC/TCR compliance). Passed status is required for release.']
  polygon_count varchar [note: 'Number of polygons (triangles) in this geometry LOD variant. Null for non-geometry assets. Critical metric for rendering performance optimization.']
  quality_validation_status varchar [note: 'Current quality assurance status of this LOD variant. Approved variants are eligible for deployment; rejected variants require rework.']
  rendering_distance_max_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum camera distance in meters at which this LOD variant should be used. Beyond this distance, the next lower LOD or culling applies. Null if distance-based LOD is not applicable.']
  rendering_distance_min_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum camera distance in meters at which this LOD variant should be used. Null if distance-based LOD is not applicable. Used by LOD Group components in game engines.']
  target_fps_impact varchar [note: 'Estimated impact of this LOD variant on target frame rate performance. Used by technical artists to balance visual quality and performance.']
  target_platform_tier varchar [note: 'The device or platform performance tier this LOD variant is optimized for. Guides runtime LOD selection based on detected hardware capabilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: high|medium|low|mobile|console|pc|vr — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  texture_resolution varchar [note: 'Resolution of the texture in pixels (e.g., \'2048x2048\', \'1024x1024\', \'512x512\'). Null for non-texture assets. Affects memory footprint and visual quality.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this LOD variant is referenced across all game levels, scenes, and prefabs. Used for impact analysis before deprecation or modification.']
  uv_channel_count varchar [note: 'Number of UV texture coordinate channels in this geometry LOD variant. Null for non-geometry assets. Supports multi-texture workflows (diffuse, lightmap, detail).']
  validated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the QA engineer or technical artist who validated this LOD variant. Supports accountability and audit trail.']
  validated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this LOD variant was validated. Follows format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  validation_notes varchar [note: 'Quality assurance notes from the validation process. Captures issues found, approval rationale, or rework instructions.']
  variant_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name for this LOD variant (e.g., \'High_Detail\', \'Mobile_Low\', \'Console_Medium\'). Used for identification in asset pipeline tools and Perforce Helix Core.']
  vertex_count varchar [note: 'Number of vertices in this geometry LOD variant. Null for non-geometry assets. Complements polygon count for performance profiling.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the technical artist, engineer, or automated process that created this LOD variant. Supports accountability and audit trail.']

  Note: 'Level-of-Detail (LOD) variant record for a parent asset, defining the specific geometry, texture resolution, or audio bitrate tier used at a given rendering distance or device performance tier. Captures LOD level index (LOD0–LOD5), polygon count or texture resolution, target platform performance tier (high/medium/low), file size, generation method (manual/auto-generated), and quality validation status. Critical for optimizing game performance across console, PC, and mobile hardware tiers.'
}

Table content.localization_string {
  localization_string_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the localization string record. Primary key.']
  audio_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Localization strings with voice-over (audio_vo_required = true) reference an audio asset. This FK links the string to its recorded VO asset, enabling asset lifecycle tracking and referential integrity']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Localization workflow management requires tracking string authorship for content ownership, approval routing, and performance attribution. Critical for multi-language game releases and localization pr']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title that this localization string belongs to. Links to the game title master record.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Strings must comply with jurisdiction-specific content laws (China censorship, Germany violence restrictions). Localization QA validates jurisdiction compliance before deployment; this link enables co']
  music_sync_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.music_sync_license. Business justification: Subtitle and lyric strings for licensed music tracks must link to sync licenses to verify territory and platform usage rights, validate content rating impacts, and ensure compliance with PRO clearance']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the localization manager or lead who approved this string for final use. Null if not yet approved.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this string was approved for final use. Null if not yet approved.']
  audio_vo_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this string requires voice-over audio recording for localization. True for dialogue and narrative strings; false for UI labels and system messages.']
  character_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of characters allowed for this string to fit within UI constraints or subtitle timing. Null if no limit applies.']
  content_warning_flags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of content warning categories applicable to this string (e.g., \'violence,profanity,drug_reference\'). Used for age rating and content filtering.']
  context_notes varchar [note: 'Contextual information for translators describing where and how the string is used in the game (e.g., \'Button label on main menu\', \'NPC greeting when player enters village\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this localization string record was first created in the system.']
  deployment_date date [note: 'Date when this string was deployed to production. Null if not yet deployed.']
  deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current deployment lifecycle status: draft (in development), staged (ready for deployment), deployed (live in production), archived (deprecated and removed from active use).']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'Date when this string was marked as deprecated and scheduled for removal. Null if still active.']
  esrb_rating_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether this string contains content that may impact the game\'s ESRB rating (profanity, violence references, sexual content). Used for content compliance review.']
  feature_area varchar [note: 'The specific game feature or module this string belongs to (e.g., \'Main Menu\', \'Combat System\', \'Inventory\', \'Quest Log\'). Used for organizing and filtering strings by game area.']
  gender_variant_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this string requires gender-specific variants for languages with grammatical gender (e.g., French, Spanish, German).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this string is currently active and available for use in the game (true) or has been deprecated/archived (false).']
  is_placeholder boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this string is a temporary placeholder (true) or final content (false). Placeholder strings are used during development and must be replaced before release.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this string.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this localization string record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes or comments about this string for internal team reference.']
  pegi_rating_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether this string contains content that may impact the game\'s PEGI rating. Used for European market compliance.']
  perforce_changelist varchar [note: 'Perforce changelist number associated with the most recent update to this string. Used for tracking version history and rollback.']
  perforce_path varchar [note: 'File path in Perforce Helix Core version control system where this string is stored (e.g., //depot/GameTitle/Localization/Strings/UI/MainMenu.json).']
  plural_variant_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this string requires plural form variants for languages with complex plural rules (e.g., Russian, Arabic, Polish).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Translation priority level indicating urgency and importance. Critical strings are required for core gameplay; low priority strings may be deferred to post-launch updates.']
  source_language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (with optional ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code) for the original language of the string (e.g., en, en-US, ja-JP).']
  source_text varchar [note: 'The original text content in the source language. This is the master text from which all translations are derived.']
  string_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the string by functional area: UI (user interface labels), narrative (dialogue and story text), marketing (promotional copy), tutorial (instructional text), system (technical messages), achievement (achievement descriptions), store (in-game store copy), error (error messages). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ui|narrative|marketing|tutorial|system|achievement|store|error — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  string_key varchar [note: 'Unique programmatic identifier for the string used by the game engine to reference this text. Typically uppercase with underscores (e.g., UI_BUTTON_START, NPC_DIALOGUE_GREETING_01).']
  subtitle_timing_end decimal(10,3) [note: 'End time in seconds for subtitle display when this string is used as a subtitle. Null if not applicable.']
  subtitle_timing_start decimal(10,3) [note: 'Start time in seconds for subtitle display when this string is used as a subtitle. Null if not applicable.']
  supports_rich_text boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this string supports rich text formatting (bold, italic, color tags) as defined by the game engine\'s text rendering system.']
  supports_variables boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this string contains dynamic variables or placeholders that are replaced at runtime (e.g., {player_name}, {item_count}).']
  translation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the string in the localization workflow: not_started (no translation work begun), in_progress (being translated), translated (initial translation complete), reviewed (reviewed by linguist), approved (approved for use), rejected (needs rework), final (locked for release). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_started|in_progress|translated|reviewed|approved|rejected|final — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  variable_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of variable names used in this string (e.g., \'player_name,item_count,gold_amount\'). Null if no variables are present.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of this string (e.g., 1.0.0, 2.1.3). Incremented when the source text or translations are updated. Supports content versioning in Perforce Helix Core.']

  Note: 'Master record for every localizable text string used in-game — UI labels, dialogue, subtitles, tutorial text, NPC speech, achievement descriptions, and store copy. Stores string key, source language text, character limit, context notes for translators, string category (UI, narrative, marketing), game title reference, feature area, and current translation status per locale. The authoritative SSOT for all in-game text content managed across the localization pipeline.'
}

Table content.localization_translation {
  localization_translation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the localization translation record. Primary key for the translation entity.']
  audio_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Localization translations (locale-specific) may reference recorded audio assets for voice-over in that language. This FK links the translation to its recorded VO asset, enabling asset lifecycle tracki']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_release. Business justification: Localization translations are versioned and deployed as part of content releases. One translation is deployed in ONE content release; one content release contains MANY translations. This FK enables tr']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Translations are jurisdiction-specific deliverables; each locale maps to regulatory territory. Translation vendors deliver per-jurisdiction compliance; this link supports regulatory audit of localized']
  localization_string_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source localization string being translated. Links to the master string definition in the content management system.']
  music_sync_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.music_sync_license. Business justification: Translated lyrics and subtitles for licensed music require sync license validation per locale to confirm translation rights, territory restrictions, and cultural adaptation permissions mandated by mus']
  translator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: When translations are performed in-house (translator_type=\'internal\'), linking to employee enables cost allocation to projects, performance tracking, and workload management. Essential for internal lo']
  character_count varchar [note: 'Total number of characters in the translated text. Used for translation billing, UI layout validation, and quality assurance checks to ensure text fits within UI constraints.']
  context_notes varchar [note: 'Additional context provided to translators about where and how this string is used in-game (e.g., UI button, NPC dialogue, tutorial text). Helps ensure accurate and contextually appropriate translation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this translation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and translation workflow analytics.']
  cultural_adaptation_notes varchar [note: 'Documentation of cultural localization decisions made for this translation, such as idiom adaptation, humor localization, or culturally sensitive content modifications to meet regional standards (ESRB, PEGI, CERO, GRAC).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this translation version becomes active in production. Supports scheduled content drops, seasonal events, and live-ops patch deployment.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this translation version is retired or replaced. Nullable for evergreen content. Used for time-limited events and seasonal content lifecycle management.']
  glossary_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the translation adheres to the game-specific terminology glossary and style guide. True indicates compliance with brand and terminology standards.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this translation record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code combined with ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g., en_US, ja_JP, de_DE, fr_FR, es_MX, pt_BR, ko_KR, zh_CN). Identifies the target locale for this translation.']
  machine_translation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this translation was generated by machine translation (MT) engine. True indicates machine-generated content that may require human post-editing.']
  platform_certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of this translation in platform-specific certification processes (TRC/TCR for console manufacturers). Tracks compliance with first-party platform localization requirements.']
  qa_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator for translations requiring additional quality assurance attention due to complexity, cultural sensitivity, or previous issues. True indicates QA escalation needed.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation for why a translation was rejected during QA review. Nullable for approved translations. Used for translator feedback and quality improvement.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the translation review was completed. Nullable for translations that have not yet been reviewed.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the translation in the quality assurance workflow. Tracks progression from initial submission through review to final approval or rejection.']
  reviewer_identity varchar [note: 'Identifier of the QA (Quality Assurance) reviewer or linguistic expert who evaluated this translation. Nullable until review is assigned.']
  revision_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this translation has been revised and resubmitted. Used to track translation complexity and identify problematic strings requiring additional localization guidance.']
  source_text_hash varchar [note: 'Hash of the original source string at the time of translation. Used to detect when source content has changed and translation needs updating.']
  translated_text varchar [note: 'The translated string content in the target locale. Contains the localized text that will be displayed to players in-game or in platform interfaces.']
  translation_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost incurred for this translation in USD. Used for vendor billing, budget tracking, and localization ROI (Return on Investment) analysis. Nullable for internal or community translations.']
  translation_date date [note: 'Date when the translation was completed and submitted. Used for tracking translation velocity and vendor SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance.']
  translation_memory_match_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage match score from translation memory system (0-100). Indicates how closely this translation matched existing approved translations. Used for cost optimization and consistency.']
  translator_identity varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual translator, vendor company name, or machine translation service that produced this translation. Used for quality tracking and vendor performance analysis.']
  translator_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the translation source: internal localization team, external vendor agency, machine translation engine, community contributor, or hybrid (machine + human review).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this translation. Increments with each approved revision. Supports content versioning in Perforce Helix Core and live-ops content update tracking.']
  word_count varchar [note: 'Total number of words in the translated text. Used for translation billing (many vendors charge per word), workload estimation, and linguistic complexity analysis.']

  Note: 'Translation record linking a localization string to a specific locale, capturing the translated text, translator identity (internal team or vendor), translation date, review status (pending, reviewed, approved, rejected), reviewer identity, QA flag, character count, and machine-translation flag. Supports multi-locale game releases across PEGI, ESRB, CERO, and GRAC regions. One record per string-locale pair, enabling per-locale translation workflow tracking.'
}

Table content.level_map {
  level_map_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the level, map, zone, or world area. Primary key.']
  age_rating_cert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.age_rating_cert. Business justification: Levels are evaluated holistically for rating (violence density, fear elements). Rating submission includes level walkthroughs; this link tracks which certification covers each map for platform deploym']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_release. Business justification: Game levels and maps are released as part of content drops (new zones for DLC, seasonal event maps, expansion areas). This FK links the level to its release event. N:1 cardinality - many levels can be']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Level designers must be tracked for attribution in credits, performance reviews, IP rights documentation, and portfolio building. Essential for game development project management and talent recogniti']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensed_ip. Business justification: Level maps using licensed middleware (Havok physics, Wwise audio) or brand partnerships (real-world locations, sponsored venues) must track IP dependencies for platform certification submissions, roya']
  perforce_changelist_id varchar [note: 'Perforce Helix Core changelist identifier for the current version of this level, enabling traceability to source control commits.']
  accessibility_features varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of accessibility features implemented in this level (e.g., \'colorblind_mode,subtitle_support,reduced_motion,audio_cues\').']
  asset_bundle_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of asset bundle identifiers required for this level (textures, models, audio, effects). References to packaged content in the asset pipeline.']
  audio_ambience_profile varchar [note: 'Identifier for the audio ambience profile used in this level (e.g., \'forest_day\', \'urban_night\', \'dungeon_echo\'). References audio asset configuration.']
  build_status varchar [note: 'Current production status of the level: blockout (initial layout/greybox), art_pass (art assets applied), optimization (performance tuning), gold (production-ready), deprecated (no longer in use).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this level record was first created in the content management system.']
  day_night_cycle_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the level supports a dynamic day-night cycle affecting lighting and gameplay.']
  deprecated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this level was deprecated or removed from active rotation. Null if still active.']
  difficulty_rating varchar [note: 'Designed difficulty level of the level: easy (beginner-friendly), normal (standard challenge), hard (experienced players), expert (advanced), nightmare (maximum difficulty).']
  estimated_play_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Expected average time in minutes for a player to complete or experience this level under normal gameplay conditions.']
  game_mode_compatibility varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of game modes this level supports (e.g., \'deathmatch,capture_the_flag,team_deathmatch\'). Indicates which gameplay rulesets can be played on this map.']
  geometry_complexity_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the level\'s geometric complexity and polygon count: low (simple geometry), medium (moderate detail), high (detailed environments), ultra (maximum fidelity).']
  interactive_object_count varchar [note: 'Total number of interactive objects (doors, switches, loot containers, destructible elements) placed in the level.']
  known_bug_count varchar [note: 'Current count of known open bugs or issues logged in Jira for this level. Used for quality tracking and release readiness.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this level record was last updated or modified.']
  level_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code or identifier used in game engine and asset pipeline to reference this level (e.g., \'LVL_001\', \'MAP_TUTORIAL_01\').']
  level_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the level, map, or zone (e.g., \'Crimson Fortress\', \'Tutorial Island\', \'Arena of Champions\').']
  level_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the level based on gameplay purpose: tutorial (onboarding), PvP (Player versus Player) arena, PvE (Player versus Environment) dungeon, open world, hub (social space), lobby (matchmaking), boss arena, raid, or story mission. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tutorial|pvp_arena|pve_dungeon|open_world|hub|lobby|boss_arena|raid|story_mission — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lighting_model varchar [note: 'Lighting technique used in the level: baked (pre-computed lightmaps), dynamic (real-time lighting), mixed (combination of baked and dynamic).']
  localization_string_count varchar [note: 'Total number of localization strings (text, dialogue, UI labels) associated with this level across all supported languages.']
  memory_budget_mb varchar [note: 'Allocated memory budget in megabytes for this level\'s assets and runtime data. Used for performance optimization and platform compliance.']
  minimum_fps_target varchar [note: 'Minimum target frame rate (FPS - Frames Per Second) the level is optimized to maintain on supported platforms.']
  navmesh_complexity varchar [note: 'Complexity of the AI navigation mesh for NPC (Non-Player Character) pathfinding: simple (basic navigation), moderate (standard pathfinding), complex (advanced multi-layer navigation), none (no AI navigation).']
  npc_spawn_zones_count varchar [note: 'Number of designated zones where NPCs (Non-Player Characters) can spawn or patrol within the level.']
  objective_marker_count varchar [note: 'Number of objective markers or mission waypoints placed in the level for gameplay guidance.']
  physics_configuration varchar [note: 'Physics simulation complexity for the level: standard (default physics), high_fidelity (advanced physics interactions), simplified (reduced physics for performance), disabled (no physics simulation).']
  published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this level was published to production and made available to players. Null if not yet published.']
  qa_test_pass_count varchar [note: 'Number of QA (Quality Assurance) test passes completed for this level. Indicates testing maturity and quality validation.']
  seasonal_event_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier for seasonal or live-ops event variant of this level (e.g., \'halloween_2024\', \'winter_festival\'). Null if base version.']
  spawn_point_count varchar [note: 'Total number of player spawn points configured in the level for matchmaking and respawn mechanics.']
  streaming_strategy varchar [note: 'Memory and asset loading strategy for the level: full_load (entire level loaded at once), streaming_chunks (progressive streaming of level sections), hybrid (combination approach).']
  supported_platforms varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of platforms this level is certified for (e.g., \'PC,PS5,Xbox_Series_X,Nintendo_Switch,iOS,Android\'). Indicates platform compatibility.']
  target_player_count_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of players the level is designed to support for optimal gameplay experience.']
  target_player_count_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of players the level is designed to support for optimal gameplay experience.']
  ugc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this level supports user-generated content (UGC) modifications, custom maps, or player-created elements.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of the level build (e.g., \'1.0.0\', \'2.3.1\'). Tracks iterations and updates to the level design and assets.']
  vertical_layers_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct vertical layers or floors in the level (e.g., multi-story buildings, underground sections). Indicates vertical complexity.']
  weather_system_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dynamic weather effects (rain, snow, fog) are enabled for this level.']
  world_size_square_meters decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total playable area of the level measured in square meters. Indicates the physical scale of the game space.']

  Note: 'Master record for each game level, map, zone, or world area representing a discrete playable space. Captures level name, level type (tutorial, PvP arena, PvE dungeon, open world, hub, lobby), game mode compatibility, target player count, estimated play duration, geometry complexity tier, streaming strategy (full load vs. streaming chunks), lighting model, physics configuration, navmesh complexity, associated asset bundles, and current build status (blockout, art-pass, optimization, gold). Owned by the content domain as the authoritative creative deliverable definition; distinct from game mode configuration owned by platform/title domains.'
}

Table content.npc_definition {
  npc_definition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the NPC definition record. Primary key for the NPC definition master data.']
  content_pack_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the DLC or content pack that introduced this NPC. Null for base game content. Used for entitlement checks and content gating.']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_release. Business justification: NPC definitions (enemies, allies, quest NPCs) are released as part of content drops (new characters for seasonal events, DLC story NPCs, live-ops additions). This FK links the NPC to its release. N:1 ']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: NPC designers and scripters must be tracked for attribution, performance evaluation, and IP rights. Essential for game design project management and credits. Replaces text-based created_by_user field ']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the development studio that created this NPC definition. Used for attribution and cross-studio content sharing.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the game title product catalog entry that this NPC definition belongs to. Links NPC content to specific game SKU.']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensed_ip. Business justification: NPCs using licensed character IP, voice actor performances under SAG-AFTRA agreements, or middleware animation systems (e.g., Euphoria) require IP tracking for royalty obligations, union residuals, an']
  model_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: NPC definitions reference a 3D model asset. Currently stored as model_asset_path (STRING path). Normalizing to FK enables referential integrity, asset lifecycle tracking, and JOIN-based queries. N:1 c']
  aggro_range decimal(6,2) [note: 'Distance in game units within which this NPC will detect and engage hostile targets. Critical for encounter design and difficulty balancing.']
  ai_behavior_profile varchar [note: 'Reference to the AI behavior script or profile that governs this NPC\'s decision-making, combat tactics, patrol patterns, and interaction logic. Links to game engine AI system configuration.']
  animation_rig_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the skeletal animation rig asset used for this NPC. Links to the animation pipeline in Unity or Unreal Engine and defines available animation sequences.']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the lead designer or content director who approved this NPC definition for production deployment.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this NPC definition was approved for production deployment. Marks transition from draft to approved lifecycle status.']
  archetype_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the NPC by its primary gameplay role: enemy (hostile combatant), ally (friendly combatant), vendor (merchant), quest_giver (mission provider), boss (major encounter), or ambient (background character).']
  audio_asset_bundle varchar [note: 'Reference to the audio asset bundle containing voice lines, sound effects, and ambient audio for this NPC. Used by the game engine audio system.']
  base_attack_power varchar [note: 'The default attack damage value for this NPC archetype at base level. Used for combat balancing and player difficulty progression.']
  base_defense_rating varchar [note: 'The default defense or armor value for this NPC archetype at base level. Determines damage mitigation in combat encounters.']
  base_health_points varchar [note: 'The default health points value for this NPC archetype at base level. Part of the core stat block used for combat balancing and difficulty tuning.']
  base_movement_speed decimal(5,2) [note: 'The default movement speed for this NPC archetype measured in game units per second. Affects chase mechanics, patrol behavior, and encounter pacing.']
  collision_profile varchar [note: 'Physics collision profile that defines the NPC\'s physical boundaries and interaction with the game world. Determines hitbox size and collision detection behavior.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this NPC definition record was first created in the content management system. Follows ISO 8601 format.']
  deployed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this NPC definition was deployed to live game servers. Used for live operations tracking and content drop scheduling.']
  deprecated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this NPC definition was marked as deprecated and scheduled for removal from active content. Used for content lifecycle management.']
  design_notes varchar [note: 'Internal design notes and rationale for this NPC\'s creation, balancing decisions, and intended player experience. Used for knowledge transfer and iteration planning.']
  dialogue_tree_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the dialogue tree or conversation script asset used when players interact with this NPC. Links to narrative content and localization systems.']
  experience_reward varchar [note: 'Amount of experience points awarded to players upon defeating or completing interactions with this NPC. Used for player progression and leveling systems.']
  faction varchar [note: 'The faction or allegiance group to which this NPC belongs. Determines relationship dynamics with player and other NPCs in the game world.']
  is_quest_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NPC is essential to main story quest progression and cannot be permanently removed from the game world. Affects respawn logic and narrative continuity.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the NPC definition: draft (in development), approved (ready for deployment), active (live in game), deprecated (scheduled for removal), or retired (no longer in use).']
  localization_key_prefix varchar [note: 'Prefix identifier used to look up localized strings for this NPC across all supported languages. Links to the localization string database for dialogue, names, and descriptions.']
  lod_variant_count varchar [note: 'Number of LOD mesh variants available for this NPC to optimize rendering performance at different camera distances. Critical for performance optimization in large-scale scenes.']
  loot_table_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the loot table configuration that defines item drops when this NPC is defeated or interacted with. Used for reward balancing and economy management.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this NPC definition record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trails.']
  npc_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the NPC definition used across game engine, content management systems, and development tools. Typically used in Perforce Helix Core and Unity/Unreal Engine asset pipelines.']
  npc_definition_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the NPC including backstory, role in the game world, and design intent. Used for documentation and content review.']
  npc_name varchar [note: 'The display name of the NPC as shown to players in-game. This is the canonical identity label for the NPC archetype.']
  perforce_changelist varchar [note: 'Perforce Helix Core changelist number associated with the last commit of this NPC definition. Enables version control traceability and rollback capability.']
  respawn_cooldown_seconds varchar [note: 'Time in seconds before this NPC respawns after being defeated. Used for encounter pacing and resource farming balance.']
  respawn_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NPC can respawn after being defeated or removed. Affects world population management and farming mechanics.']
  spawn_rule_set varchar [note: 'Reference to the spawn rule configuration that defines where, when, and under what conditions this NPC appears in the game world. Includes location constraints, time-of-day rules, and event triggers.']
  texture_asset_path varchar [note: 'File path or asset reference to the texture map(s) applied to the NPC model. Includes diffuse, normal, and material maps stored in the content pipeline.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version number tracking iterations of this NPC definition through the content pipeline. Follows semantic versioning convention (vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH).']
  voice_actor_credit varchar [note: 'Name of the voice actor who provided voice-over performance for this NPC. Used for credits, royalty tracking, and talent management.']

  Note: 'Master definition record for every Non-Player Character (NPC) archetype in the game — enemies, allies, vendors, quest givers, bosses, and ambient characters. Stores NPC name, NPC archetype type, faction, base stat block, AI behavior profile reference, dialogue tree reference, loot table reference, spawn rules, animation rig reference, voice actor credit, localization key prefix, associated asset references (model, textures, animations), and lifecycle status (draft, approved, deprecated). The content domain owns NPC creative definitions as authored deliverables; runtime NPC instances and state are managed by game server infrastructure.'
}

  age_rating_cert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.age_rating_cert. Business justification: Each content drop may require rating re-evaluation if new content added (DLC, season pass). Platform certification workflow requires linking drops to ESRB/PEGI submissions for deployment approval.']
  capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_project. Business justification: Major content releases trigger capitalization completion and amortization start dates. Finance needs release-to-project linkage to determine when \'technological feasibility\' is achieved and when to be']
  cdn_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.cdn_node. Business justification: Content releases are distributed through CDN infrastructure. Release teams coordinate with CDN operators to pre-warm caches before go-live, monitor distribution completion across nodes, and diagnose r']
