Business justification: Release planning ownership must be tracked for accountability, project management, and coordination. Essential for live operations and content drop management. Identifies the release manager or produc']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Content drops consume dedicated marketing and development budgets. Finance tracks actual spend vs. budget by release for variance analysis, ROI calculation, and future release planning. Critical for l']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the development studio responsible for creating and delivering this content release. Used for accountability and resource planning.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the game title this content release belongs to. Links the content drop to the specific game product in the catalog.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.ip_agreement. Business justification: Content drops containing licensed DLC, brand partnerships, or middleware updates must validate agreement compliance (territory restrictions, exclusivity windows, revenue thresholds) before deployment ']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Live ops drops must comply with loot box disclosure laws, GDPR consent updates, age verification requirements. Release approval checklist includes regulatory compliance sign-off; this link enables obl']
  server_fleet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.server_fleet. Business justification: Content drops are deployed to specific server fleets in staged rollout patterns. Release managers track fleet-level deployment status for canary testing, rollback planning, and coordinating content av']
  actual_go_live_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the content release was deployed to production and became available to players. Null if not yet deployed.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Cross-functional approval status for the content release: pending (awaiting stakeholder sign-off), approved (greenlit for deployment), rejected (not approved, requires changes). Tracks executive and product leadership approval.']
  asset_bundle_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of asset bundle identifiers or Perforce Helix Core changelist numbers associated with this content release. Links the release to the underlying digital assets.']
  cdn_distribution_status varchar [note: 'Status of content distribution to CDN nodes: pending (awaiting distribution), distributing (in progress), completed (available on all CDN nodes), failed (distribution errors). Critical for global content availability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this content release record was first created in the system. Audit trail for release planning lifecycle.']
  critical_issues_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical bugs or issues reported by players or QA after this content release went live. Used for quality monitoring and hotfix prioritization.']
  deployment_status varchar [note: 'Technical deployment status of the content release: not_started (scheduled but not yet deployed), in_progress (deployment underway), completed (successfully deployed to all target platforms), failed (deployment encountered errors).']
  download_size_mb_console decimal(10,2) [note: 'Download package size in megabytes for console platforms (PlayStation, Xbox, Switch). Used for bandwidth planning and player communication.']
  download_size_mb_mobile decimal(10,2) [note: 'Download package size in megabytes for mobile platforms (iOS, Android). Critical for mobile data usage and app store optimization.']
  download_size_mb_pc decimal(10,2) [note: 'Download package size in megabytes for PC platform. Used for bandwidth planning and player communication.']
  drop_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the content release (e.g., \'Winter Wonderland Event\', \'Patch 2.4.1\', \'Season 3 Battle Pass\').']
  eligibility_rules varchar [note: 'Player eligibility criteria for accessing this content release (e.g., \'Level 10+\', \'Premium subscribers only\', \'All players\'). Used for access control and segmentation.']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when a seasonal or limited-time event concludes and is no longer available to players. Applicable for event-based releases.']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when a seasonal or limited-time event becomes active for players. Applicable for event-based releases.']
  event_theme varchar [note: 'Thematic description of seasonal or limited-time events (e.g., \'Winter Holiday\', \'Halloween\', \'Summer Festival\'). Applicable when release_type is seasonal_event or limited_time_event.']
  exclusive_rewards varchar [note: 'Description of exclusive rewards or items available only during this content release (e.g., \'Legendary Winter Sword,Exclusive Holiday Avatar\'). Used for player engagement and retention.']
  featured_bundles varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of featured in-game bundles or offers associated with this content release (e.g., \'Winter Skin Pack,Holiday Emote Bundle\'). Used for monetization and marketing alignment.']
  gold_master_date date [note: 'Date when the content release reached gold master status (final, production-ready build). Marks the completion of development and testing.']
  live_ops_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the live operations team member or product owner responsible for this content release. Used for accountability and cross-functional coordination.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether players are required to download and install this content release to continue playing. True for mandatory updates (e.g., critical security patches, breaking changes), False for optional content.']
  marketing_campaign_alignment varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the marketing campaign aligned with this content release (e.g., \'Holiday 2024 Campaign\', \'Season 3 Launch\'). Used for cross-functional planning and ROI tracking.']
  minimum_client_version varchar [note: 'Minimum game client version required to support this content release. Players on older client versions may be blocked or prompted to update. Follows semver format.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this content release record was last updated. Audit trail for tracking changes to release plans and status.']
  notes_url varchar [note: 'URL to the public-facing release notes or patch notes document for this content release. Used for player communication and transparency.']
  participation_mechanics varchar [note: 'Description of gameplay mechanics or activities players must complete to participate in the event or unlock rewards (e.g., \'Complete 10 daily quests\', \'Win 5 PvP matches\').']
  patch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of patch releases: major (significant feature additions or breaking changes), minor (incremental features or improvements), hotfix (emergency bug fixes). Applicable only when release_type is patch or hotfix.']
  player_feedback_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Aggregated player sentiment score for this content release, typically on a scale of 1.00 to 5.00. Derived from player surveys, reviews, and community feedback post-launch.']
  qa_sign_off_date date [note: 'Date when the QA team formally approved this content release for deployment. Indicates completion of quality assurance and playtesting.']
  recurrence_pattern varchar [note: 'Frequency pattern for recurring events: one_time (single occurrence), annual (yearly), quarterly (every 3 months), monthly, weekly. Applicable for seasonal events.']
  release_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the content release: planned (scheduled but not yet approved), approved (greenlit for development), in_qa (undergoing quality assurance testing), certified (passed platform TRC/TCR certification), live (deployed to production), rolled_back (reverted due to issues), cancelled (release aborted). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|approved|in_qa|certified|live|rolled_back|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  release_type varchar [note: 'Type discriminator for the content release: seasonal_event (recurring themed events), patch (scheduled version updates), hotfix (emergency fixes), battle_pass_season (new battle pass tier progression), dlc_drop (downloadable content expansion), limited_time_event (one-off special events).']
  rollback_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the content release was rolled back or reverted due to critical issues. Null if no rollback occurred.']
  scheduled_go_live_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the content release is scheduled to go live to players. This is the target deployment timestamp set during release planning.']
  target_platforms varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of platforms this content release targets (e.g., \'PC,PlayStation,Xbox,Mobile,Switch\'). Indicates which platforms will receive this content drop.']
  trc_tcr_certification_status varchar [note: 'Platform certification status for console releases: not_required (PC/mobile only), pending (submitted for certification), in_progress (under review by first party), passed (certified), failed (rejected, requires fixes). Required for PlayStation, Xbox, Switch releases.']
  version_string varchar [note: 'Semantic versioning identifier for patches and hotfixes (e.g., \'2.4.1\', \'3.0.0\'). Follows semver convention: major.minor.patch. Null for non-patch release types.']
  window varchar [note: 'Deployment strategy for the content release: soft_launch (limited geographic or player segment release for testing), hard_launch (full production release), phased_rollout (gradual deployment across regions or player cohorts), global_simultaneous (worldwide release at the same time).']

  Note: 'Unified live-ops content release record representing any scheduled or emergency delivery of new or updated content to live players. Covers all release types via type discrimination: seasonal events (with event theme, start/end timestamps, recurrence pattern, featured bundles, exclusive rewards, participation mechanics, eligibility rules), patches and hotfixes (with semver version string, patch type, mandatory flag, minimum client version, per-platform size, QA sign-off, gold master date, TRC/TCR certification status), battle pass seasons, DLC drops, and limited-time events. Also serves as the release calendar, capturing planned vs actual release dates, release window (soft/hard), marketing campaign alignment, and cross-functional approval status. Stores drop name, release type, scheduled go-live timestamp, actual go-live timestamp, rollback timestamp, target platforms, associated asset bundles, release notes reference, live-ops owner, and deployment status. The single operational heartbeat of GaaS content delivery — one entity to answer \'what is going live, when, and what does it contain\'.'
}

Table content.patch {
  patch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for patch']
  capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_project. Business justification: Post-launch patches may be capitalized (if adding functionality) or expensed (if maintenance). Finance needs patch-to-project linkage to apply correct accounting treatment per ASC 350-40 and track cos']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_release. Business justification: Patch is a specialization of content_release (release_type = \'patch\'). This FK links the patch detail record to its parent content_release record, enabling unified live-ops tracking. 1:1 or N:1 cardin']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Patch authorship must be tracked for accountability, release management, and audit compliance. Essential for platform certification (TRC/TCR) and post-launch support operations. Replaces text-based cr']
  game_server_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.game_server. Business justification: Patches are applied to individual game servers during maintenance windows. Server operators track per-server patch status for compliance verification, incident correlation (linking crashes to specific']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title this patch applies to.']
  perforce_changelist_id varchar [note: 'The Perforce Helix Core changelist identifier that represents the source control snapshot for this patch build. Used for asset and code version traceability.']
  platform_patch_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.patch_release. Business justification: Content patches map to platform-specific patch releases for distribution. Live ops teams coordinate content patch versions with platform patch releases for multi-platform deployment synchronization.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Patches may address regulatory violations (emergency patch to fix COPPA violation, loot box disclosure bug). Compliance incident remediation via patch deployment requires linking patches to obligation']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the team member who approved the patch for release (typically a lead producer, technical director, or QA manager).']
  build_number varchar [note: 'Internal build identifier assigned by the CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment) pipeline for this patch.']
  cdn_distribution_status varchar [note: 'Status of the patch distribution to CDN nodes globally: pending (queued for distribution), in_progress (actively replicating), completed (available on all CDN nodes), failed (distribution error occurred).']
  content_drop_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this patch includes a live-ops content drop (seasonal event, new maps, new characters, limited-time content). True if content drop is included, false otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the patch record was first created in the system.']
  deployment_date_console date [note: 'The date when the patch was deployed live to console platforms (PlayStation, Xbox, Switch).']
  deployment_date_mobile date [note: 'The date when the patch was deployed live to mobile platforms (iOS App Store, Google Play).']
  deployment_date_pc date [note: 'The date when the patch was deployed live to PC platforms (Steam, Epic Games Store, etc.).']
  dlc_included varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of DLC SKUs or identifiers that are bundled or enabled by this patch. Null if no DLC is associated.']
  drm_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the DRM protection applied to this patch, if applicable. Used to track anti-piracy measures and compatibility.']
  esrb_rating_impact varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this patch introduces content that may affect the game\'s ESRB rating: none (no impact), content_descriptor_added (new content descriptor required), rating_change_pending (rating re-evaluation in progress).']
  gold_master_date date [note: 'The date when the patch build was finalized and locked as the gold master version ready for platform submission and deployment.']
  internal_change_log varchar [note: 'Internal technical change log detailing code commits, asset changes, and engineering notes not intended for public release. Used for development team reference and audit.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether players are required to install this patch to continue playing (true) or if it is optional (false).']
  localization_languages varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of language codes (ISO 639-1) for which localized patch notes and in-game strings are provided (e.g., \'en,es,fr,de,ja,zh\').']
  minimum_client_version varchar [note: 'The minimum game client version required for this patch to be applied successfully (semver format).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the patch record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Detailed release notes describing bug fixes, balance changes, new content, performance improvements, and known issues addressed in this patch. Intended for player-facing communication.']
  patch_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or codename for the patch (e.g., \'Winter Festival Update\', \'Critical Stability Hotfix\').']
  patch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the patch: draft (in development), qa_testing (undergoing QA), trc_submission (submitted for platform certification), approved (passed TRC/TCR), deployed (live on platforms), rolled_back (reverted due to issues), deprecated (superseded by newer patch). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|qa_testing|trc_submission|approved|deployed|rolled_back|deprecated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  patch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the patch by scope and purpose: hotfix (urgent bug fix), minor (incremental improvements), major (significant feature release), balance (gameplay tuning), content (new assets/levels), security (vulnerability fix).']
  pegi_rating_impact varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this patch introduces content that may affect the game\'s PEGI rating: none (no impact), content_descriptor_added (new content descriptor required), rating_change_pending (rating re-evaluation in progress).']
  performance_improvement_notes varchar [note: 'Description of performance optimizations included in this patch, such as FPS (Frames Per Second) improvements, load time reductions, memory optimizations, or LOD (Level of Detail) enhancements.']
  qa_sign_off_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the QA test cycle or approval record that certified this patch as ready for release (e.g., Jira ticket, test plan ID).']
  rollback_date date [note: 'The date when the patch was rolled back or reverted due to critical issues discovered post-deployment. Null if no rollback occurred.']
  rollback_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the patch was rolled back, including the nature of the critical issue (e.g., \'Server crashes on login\', \'Game-breaking exploit discovered\').']
  security_fixes_included boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this patch includes security vulnerability fixes or anti-cheat improvements. True if security fixes are present, false otherwise.']
  size_console_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Size of the patch download for console platforms (PlayStation, Xbox, Switch) in megabytes. May vary by specific console; this represents a typical or average size.']
  size_mobile_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Size of the patch download for mobile platforms (iOS, Android) in megabytes.']
  size_pc_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Size of the patch download for PC platform in megabytes.']
  target_platforms varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of platforms this patch targets (e.g., \'PC,PlayStation5,XboxSeriesX,Switch,iOS,Android\'). Each platform may have separate certification and deployment workflows.']
  trc_approval_date date [note: 'The date when the patch received approval from platform certification, clearing it for deployment.']
  trc_submission_date date [note: 'The date when the patch was submitted to platform holders (Sony, Microsoft, Nintendo, Apple, Google) for technical certification review.']
  version varchar [note: 'Semantic version string for the patch following semver convention (e.g., 1.2.3, 2.0.0-hotfix1).']

  Note: 'Patch record for a versioned software update delivered to players, covering bug fixes, balance changes, performance improvements, and content additions. Stores patch version string (semver), patch type (hotfix, minor, major, balance), target platforms, patch size per platform, mandatory flag, minimum client version required, patch notes, QA sign-off reference, gold master date, and deployment status per platform. Bridges the content pipeline with platform certification (TRC/TCR) requirements.'
}

  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Essential for live service games and seasonal content operations. Replaces text-base']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Finance tracks event spend vs. budget and calculates event ROI for live-ops investment decision']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title in which this seasonal event is deployed. Links to the game catalog.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Events may be region-locked due to regulatory restrictions (gambling laws, content censorship). Geo-blocking configuration for compliance requires linking events to jurisdictions for deployment rule e']
  loot_box_disclosure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.loot_box_disclosure. Business justification: Events often introduce limited-time gacha mechanics requiring odds disclosure (Belgium, Netherlands, Japan laws). Event launch checklist includes loot box probability publication; this link enables co']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated marketing campaign driving player awareness and acquisition for this seasonal event.']
  matchmaking_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.matchmaking_pool. Business justification: Seasonal events often have dedicated matchmaking pools with event-specific rules and player eligibility. Live ops teams configure event pools, track participation metrics, and coordinate pool activati']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Events must comply with gambling laws, advertising restrictions (Belgium loot box ban, UK advertising standards). Legal review of event monetization mechanics requires linking events to applicable obl']
  title_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.title_season. Business justification: Seasonal events align with title seasons (e.g., Season 3 Halloween Event). Live ops scheduling, marketing campaign alignment, and battle pass integration require linking events to their parent season ']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.tournament. Business justification: Seasonal in-game events often tie to esports tournaments (e.g., World Championship event, playoff celebration content). This links content releases to the competitive calendar, enabling coordinated ma']
  actual_participation_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of unique players who participated in the event after completion. Null until event concludes and analytics are finalized.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the executive producer, studio head, or approver who authorized the event for production and deployment.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the seasonal event was formally approved for scheduling and release.']
  asset_bundle_path varchar [note: 'File system or repository path to the event asset bundle in Perforce Helix Core, Unity, or Unreal Engine asset pipeline (e.g., \'/content/events/seasonal/winter_2024/\').']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of first-party platform certification review (TRC/TCR compliance for console manufacturers): not_submitted, submitted, in_review, approved, rejected.']
  content_rating varchar [note: 'Age-appropriateness rating assigned to event content by governing bodies (e.g., \'ESRB: E10+\', \'PEGI: 12\', \'CERO: B\'). May differ from base game rating if event introduces new content themes.']
  content_version varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of the event content package in Perforce Helix Core or content management system (e.g., v1.2.0). Tracks asset versioning and deployment history.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the seasonal event record was first created in the content management system.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the seasonal event concludes and is no longer accessible to players. Marks the official close of the event window.']
  estimated_participation_count varchar [note: 'Projected number of unique players expected to participate in the event, based on historical data, player base size, and marketing reach. Used for capacity planning.']
  event_budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total allocated budget for event development, marketing, and operations (in USD or base currency). Includes content creation, QA, localization, and promotional spend.']
  event_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the seasonal event used across systems and marketing materials (e.g., WINTER_2024, HALLOWEEN_23, ANNIVERSARY_5YR).']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name of the seasonal event shown to players in-game and in marketing channels.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the seasonal event: draft (in planning), scheduled (approved and queued), active (live in-game), paused (temporarily suspended), completed (ended successfully), cancelled (terminated before completion).']
  event_theme varchar [note: 'Creative theme or narrative concept for the event (e.g., Winter Wonderland, Spooky Halloween, Summer Beach Party, Lunar New Year).']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the seasonal event by business purpose: holiday (calendar-based celebrations), anniversary (game/studio milestones), esports_tie_in (tournament promotions), promotional (marketing campaigns), seasonal (quarterly/seasonal content drops), community (player-driven events), limited_time_offer (flash sales/special offers). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: holiday|anniversary|esports_tie_in|promotional|seasonal|community|limited_time_offer — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  exclusive_cosmetic_reward_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of exclusive cosmetic item identifiers (skins, emotes, banners, avatars) available only during this event as participation rewards or purchasable items.']
  featured_content_bundle_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of content bundle identifiers associated with this event, including new maps, levels, missions, or story chapters released as part of the event.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who most recently updated the seasonal event record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the seasonal event record was most recently updated.']
  localization_status varchar [note: 'Current state of localization and translation work for event strings, UI text, and narrative content across supported languages.']
  min_player_level varchar [note: 'Minimum player level required to access and participate in the seasonal event. Null if no level restriction applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes, special instructions, lessons learned, or operational context related to the event planning, execution, or post-mortem analysis.']
  participation_mechanics varchar [note: 'Description of how players engage with the event: gameplay modes, quest chains, challenges, leaderboards, or special activities required for participation and reward earning.']
  platform_eligibility varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of platforms where the event is available (e.g., \'PC,Console,Mobile\' or \'PlayStation,Xbox,Switch\'). Defines cross-platform availability.']
  player_eligibility_rules varchar [note: 'Business rules defining which players can participate in the event, including level requirements, region restrictions, account age, previous event participation, or VIP status criteria.']
  qa_status varchar [note: 'Current state of quality assurance testing for the event: not_started, in_progress, passed (approved for release), failed (issues found), blocked (dependencies unmet).']
  recurrence_pattern varchar [note: 'Frequency pattern indicating if and how the event repeats: one_time (single occurrence), annual (yearly), quarterly (every 3 months), monthly, weekly, custom (irregular schedule defined separately).']
  recurrence_schedule varchar [note: 'Detailed schedule specification for recurring events, including specific dates, times, or calendar rules (e.g., \'Every December 15-31\', \'First weekend of each quarter\'). Null for one-time events.']
  reward_structure varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the reward distribution model: milestone-based, leaderboard rankings, participation tiers, daily login bonuses, or completion-based rewards.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the seasonal event becomes active and visible to eligible players in-game. Represents the go-live moment for the event.']
  supported_languages varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 639-1 language codes for which event content has been localized (e.g., \'en,es,fr,de,ja,zh,pt,ru\').']
  target_dau_lift_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage increase in Daily Active Users (DAU) expected during the event period compared to baseline. Key performance indicator for event success.']
  target_revenue_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target incremental revenue (in USD or base currency) expected from event-related in-app purchases (IAP), microtransactions (MTX), and battle pass sales during the event window.']

  Note: 'Seasonal or limited-time event definition record capturing the creative and operational parameters of a live-ops event — holiday events, anniversary events, esports tie-ins, and promotional events. Stores event name, event theme, start and end timestamps, recurrence pattern, featured content bundles, exclusive cosmetic rewards, participation mechanics, player eligibility rules, and marketing campaign reference. Owned by content as the creative deliverable layer; monetization domain owns the associated IAP/MTX pricing.'
}

Table content.asset_review {
  asset_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset review record. Primary key for the asset review entity.']
  asset_bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset_bundle. Business justification: Asset reviews can certify entire asset bundles (not just individual assets). One review certifies ONE bundle at a time; one bundle can have MANY reviews (iterations, re-submissions). This FK enables t']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital asset being reviewed (art asset, audio file, texture, level data, NPC definition, localization string, or UGC submission).']
  asset_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset_version. Business justification: Asset reviews are conducted on specific immutable asset versions (Perforce revisions). Currently asset_review has asset_id (asset-level) and asset_version (STRING). Adding FK to asset_version table en']
  compliance_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance checklist template used for this review, particularly for platform certification, legal clearance, and IP compliance reviews.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Asset approval pipelines must verify compliance with rating board requirements (ESRB, PEGI), platform certification rules (TRC/TCR), and IP usage restrictions from licensing agreements to prevent cert']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual or team member who conducted the review.']
  lod_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.lod_variant. Business justification: LOD variants require separate performance certification (polygon count, texture resolution, rendering distance validation). One review certifies ONE LOD variant; one LOD variant can have MANY reviews ']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_policy. Business justification: Asset approval workflows enforce compliance policies (IP clearance, content rating guidelines, accessibility standards). Review checklists reference policy documents; this link enables policy adherenc']
  previous_review_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior review record for this asset, creating a linked audit trail of iterative reviews and revisions through the pipeline.']
  accessibility_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset meets accessibility standards for inclusive design (true) or requires accessibility improvements (false), covering visual, auditory, and cognitive accessibility.']
  audio_duration_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'The duration of audio assets in seconds, applicable to sound effect, music, and voice-over reviews.']
  automated_check_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated validation checks (file format, naming convention, technical specs) passed (true) or failed (false) prior to manual review.']
  blocking_issues_count varchar [note: 'The number of critical issues identified that must be resolved before the asset can proceed to the next pipeline stage.']
  content_policy_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset violates community content policies (true) or is compliant (false), used in UGC moderation and legal clearance reviews.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this review record was first created in the system, representing the audit trail creation point.']
  decision varchar [note: 'The outcome of the review: approved (asset passes and may proceed), rejected (asset fails and must be reworked), revision-required (specific changes needed), conditional-approval (approved with minor follow-up), deferred (decision postponed), escalated (requires higher authority).']
  esrb_rating_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of how this asset may impact the games ESRB rating: none (no impact), mild (E/E10+ content), moderate (T content), mature (M content), ao-risk (potential AO rating risk).']
  feedback_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed textual feedback provided by the reviewer, including observations, recommendations, and specific issues identified during the review.']
  file_size_mb decimal(12,2) [note: 'The file size of the asset in megabytes at the time of review, used to assess memory budget compliance and optimization requirements.']
  ip_clearance_status varchar [note: 'The IP clearance status for the asset: cleared (approved for use), pending (under legal review), flagged (potential IP conflict), licensed (third-party IP with valid license), original (company-owned IP).']
  iteration_number varchar [note: 'The sequential iteration count for this asset at this pipeline stage, tracking how many review cycles have occurred (1 = first submission, 2+ = resubmissions after revisions).']
  jira_ticket_key varchar [note: 'The Jira ticket identifier associated with this review, linking the review to project management tracking, sprints, and bug reports.']
  localization_language_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 639-1 language code and ISO 3166-1 country code (e.g., en-US, ja-JP, de-DE) for localized assets, used in localization reviews.']
  lod_variant varchar [note: 'The LOD variant of the asset being reviewed: lod0 (highest detail), lod1-4 (progressive simplification), billboard (lowest detail sprite), used for performance optimization across platforms.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this review record was last modified, tracking updates to review decisions, feedback, or metadata.']
  non_blocking_issues_count varchar [note: 'The number of minor or advisory issues identified that should be addressed but do not prevent pipeline progression.']
  pegi_rating_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of how this asset may impact the games PEGI rating: none (no impact), mild (PEGI 3/7 content), moderate (PEGI 12/16 content), mature (PEGI 18 content), restricted (potential classification issues).']
  perforce_changelist varchar [note: 'The Perforce Helix Core changelist number containing the asset version under review, providing version control traceability.']
  pipeline_stage varchar [note: 'The stage in the content production pipeline where this review occurred: concept (initial ideation), blockout (rough layout), greybox (functional prototype), art-pass (visual polish), tech-art-review (technical validation), qa-review (quality assurance), certification (platform compliance), approved (production-ready), deprecated (retired from use). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concept|blockout|greybox|art-pass|tech-art-review|qa-review|certification|approved|deprecated — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  platform_target varchar [note: 'The target platform(s) for which this asset is being reviewed (e.g., PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X, PC, iOS, Android), relevant for platform-specific certification and technical requirements.']
  polygon_count varchar [note: 'The number of polygons in the 3D asset, applicable to mesh and model reviews, used to validate performance budgets and technical art standards.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The urgency classification for this review: critical (blocking release), high (near-term milestone), medium (standard pipeline), low (backlog or future content).']
  review_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The actual time spent by the reviewer conducting the review in minutes, used for workload analysis and process optimization.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the review record, formatted as REV-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX for external tracking and audit trail purposes.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the review was performed, representing the principal business event for this transaction.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'The functional discipline conducting the review: art-direction (creative approval), tech-art (technical feasibility), qa (quality assurance), legal-clearance (rights and licensing), ip-compliance (intellectual property validation), platform-certification (console/store requirements), localization (language and cultural adaptation), accessibility (inclusive design standards). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: art-direction|tech-art|qa|legal-clearance|ip-compliance|platform-certification|localization|accessibility — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  revision_request_details varchar [note: 'Specific instructions and requirements for asset revisions when decision is revision-required or conditional-approval, detailing what changes must be made before re-submission.']
  sign_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the reviewer formally signed off on the review decision, marking the completion of this stage-gate checkpoint.']
  sla_actual_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The actual elapsed time in hours from review assignment to review completion, used to measure SLA compliance and pipeline efficiency.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review was completed within the SLA target hours (true) or exceeded the target (false), used for pipeline performance monitoring.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'The target number of hours within which this review type and pipeline stage should be completed, as defined by the content production SLA policy.']
  texture_resolution varchar [note: 'The resolution of texture assets in pixels (e.g., 2048x2048, 4096x4096), used to validate visual quality and memory budget compliance.']
  ugc_moderation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this review is for user-generated content requiring moderation (true) or first-party content (false), triggering additional content policy checks.']

  Note: 'Review and approval record for an asset at a specific pipeline stage, capturing reviewer identity, review type (art direction, tech art, QA, legal clearance, IP compliance), pipeline stage name (concept, blockout, grey-box, art-pass, tech-art-review, QA-review, certification, approved, deprecated), review timestamp, decision (approved, rejected, revision-required), feedback notes, revision request details, SLA compliance flag, and sign-off timestamp. Provides the stage-gate audit trail for every asset moving through the content pipeline from creation to live deployment.'
}

Table content.content_deployment {
  content_deployment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the content deployment event. Primary key.']
  age_rating_cert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.age_rating_cert. Business justification: Deployment gates require valid certification for target platform/region. Platform holder certification validation before CDN push requires linking deployments to ESRB/PEGI records for automated approv']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who approved the deployment, if manual approval was required.']
  capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_project. Business justification: Deployments to production trigger critical accounting events: capitalization completion, amortization start, and asset-under-construction reclassification. Finance needs deployment-to-project linkage ']
  cert_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.cert_finding. Business justification: Deployments blocked by unresolved certification findings (deployment cannot proceed until cert finding resolved, deployment rollback due to cert finding). Deployment blocker tracking for platform comp']
  cicd_pipeline_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment) pipeline that executed this deployment.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Content deployments must pass compliance checks for territory restrictions, rating requirements, and platform certification mandated by licensing agreements to prevent distribution violations, regulat']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the content drop or asset bundle being deployed.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title receiving this deployment.']
  network_region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.network_region. Business justification: Content deployments are region-specific for data residency compliance and latency optimization. Release managers coordinate deployments by region to manage player impact windows, CDN pre-warming sched']
  patch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.patch. Business justification: Content deployments need to track which specific patch is being pushed to live environments. One deployment pushes ONE patch to a specific environment/region; one patch can have MANY deployments (mult']
  primary_content_deployer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or system user who initiated the deployment.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Deployments to certain jurisdictions trigger mandatory filings (Korea game rating board notification, China content approval). Post-deployment compliance reporting requires linking deployments to fili']
  release_gate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.release_gate. Business justification: Deployments require release gate approval before production (gate-to-deployment workflow, deployment blocked until gate passes). Gate approval tracking enables deployment authorization and audit trail']
  seasonal_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.seasonal_event. Business justification: Content deployments need to track which seasonal event is being activated in live environments. One deployment activates ONE seasonal event in a specific region; one seasonal event can have MANY deplo']
  server_fleet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.server_fleet. Business justification: Content deployments target specific server fleets. This is the primary operational link between content release management and infrastructure capacity planning, enabling fleet-level deployment trackin']
  test_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.test_plan. Business justification: Deployments execute against test plans for validation (deployment requires test plan sign-off, post-deployment validation against test plan). Deployment testing workflow links deployment to test plan ']
  validation_test_suite_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the QA (Quality Assurance) test suite executed for post-deployment validation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status indicating whether the deployment was reviewed and authorized before execution.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the deployment was approved for execution.']
  asset_bundle_size_mb decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total size of the deployed asset bundle in megabytes, representing the download size for players.']
  asset_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual assets (textures, models, audio files, localization strings, etc.) included in this deployment.']
  build_number varchar [note: 'The CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment) build number or version identifier for the deployed content package.']
  cdn_distribution_status varchar [note: 'Status of the content distribution across the CDN (Content Delivery Network) nodes: not started, distributing (in progress), distributed (fully propagated), failed (distribution error), or partial (some nodes updated).']
  cdn_endpoint_url varchar [note: 'The primary CDN (Content Delivery Network) endpoint URL where the deployed content is accessible to players.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the deployment was successfully completed or failed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp indicating when this deployment record was first created in the system.']
  deployment_method varchar [note: 'The technical approach used to deploy the content: full push (complete asset bundle), delta patch (incremental changes), hotfix (emergency fix), rollback (revert to previous version), staged rollout (phased deployment), or canary (limited test deployment).']
  deployment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier for this deployment event, used for tracking and audit purposes.']
  deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the deployment event.']
  deployment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the deployment was initiated to the live environment or CDN (Content Delivery Network) endpoint.']
  deployment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deployment by business purpose: content drop (scheduled release), patch (bug fix), hotfix (emergency fix), DLC (Downloadable Content), seasonal event (limited-time content), or live ops (ongoing service update).']
  downtime_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration in minutes that the game or service was offline for this deployment, if downtime was required.']
  downtime_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the deployment required taking the game or service offline (True) or was deployed with zero downtime (False).']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time in seconds from deployment initiation to completion or failure.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Technical error message or stack trace if the deployment failed, used for troubleshooting and root cause analysis.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp indicating when this deployment record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments from the deployer describing the deployment context, special instructions, or known issues.']
  perforce_changelist_number varchar [note: 'The Perforce Helix Core changelist number associated with this deployment, providing version control traceability.']
  player_impact_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of the expected impact on live players: none (backend only), low (minor changes), medium (noticeable changes), high (significant gameplay changes), or critical (major content update or fix).']
  post_deployment_validation_status varchar [note: 'Status of automated or manual validation checks performed after deployment to verify content integrity and functionality.']
  region_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of geographic region codes where this deployment was pushed (e.g., NAM, EUR, APAC, LATAM). Used for geo-specific content rollouts.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the deployment was retried after initial failure before succeeding or being abandoned.']
  rollback_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deployment was subsequently rolled back due to issues (True) or remains active (False).']
  rollback_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification or technical reason for rolling back the deployment (e.g., critical bug, performance degradation, player complaints).']
  rollback_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the deployment was rolled back, if applicable. Null if no rollback occurred.']
  target_environment varchar [note: 'The environment to which the content is being deployed: dev (development), qa (quality assurance), uat (user acceptance testing), staging (pre-production), production (live), or live (player-facing).']
  target_platform_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of platform codes targeted by this deployment (e.g., PC, PS5, XBOX, IOS, ANDROID, SWITCH). Represents the platforms receiving this content drop.']
  window_end timestamp [note: 'The scheduled end time of the deployment maintenance window.']
  window_start timestamp [note: 'The scheduled start time of the deployment maintenance window.']

  Note: 'Deployment event record capturing the act of pushing a content drop, patch, or asset bundle to a live environment or CDN endpoint. Stores deployment timestamp, deployer identity, target environment (dev, staging, live), target platform(s), CDN distribution status, deployment method (full push, delta patch, hotfix), rollback flag, rollback timestamp, deployment duration, and post-deployment validation status. The operational log of every content change reaching live players.'
}

Table content.asset_dependency {
  asset_dependency_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset dependency record. Primary key.']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the content release or live-ops content drop that introduced or modified this dependency. Used to track dependency changes across seasonal events, patches, and hotfixes.']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the studio responsible for managing this dependency relationship. Used for organizational reporting and access control.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title that owns both the source and target assets. Used to scope dependency analysis to specific titles in multi-game studios.']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensed_ip. Business justification: Dependency graphs must track licensed middleware and IP to calculate royalty obligations (per-seat, per-title, revenue-share), validate sublicensing permissions, and enforce build-time compliance with']
  parent_asset_dependency_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on asset_dependency (parent_asset_dependency_id)']
  perforce_changelist_id varchar [note: 'The Perforce Helix Core changelist number that introduced or last modified this dependency relationship. Used for version control traceability.']
  primary_asset_employee_id varchar [note: 'The user ID or system account that created this dependency record. May be a developer username, automated tool identifier, or system process name.']
  source_asset_id varchar [note: 'The asset that declares or originates the dependency relationship. This is the dependent asset that requires or references the target asset.']
  target_asset_id varchar [note: 'The asset that is being depended upon. This is the asset that the source asset requires, references, or overrides.']
  asset_bundle_inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether both source and target assets must be included in the same asset bundle for runtime resolution. If true, the build pipeline ensures co-location.']
  build_order_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used by the build pipeline to determine asset compilation order. Lower numbers are built first. Used to resolve dependency graphs and ensure targets are built before sources.']
  circular_dependency_chain varchar [note: 'If validation_status is circular, this field contains the chain of asset IDs forming the dependency loop (e.g., A->B->C->A). Used for debugging circular reference issues.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this dependency record was first created in the system.']
  dependency_path varchar [note: 'The file system or depot path expression that defines how the source asset references the target. May include relative paths, symbolic references, or Perforce depot paths.']
  dependency_strength varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the dependency is hard (required for build success) or soft (optional, build can proceed without it). Hard dependencies block builds if missing; soft dependencies generate warnings.']
  dependency_type varchar [note: 'The nature of the dependency relationship between source and target assets. Defines how the source asset relates to the target asset in the build pipeline.']
  deprecated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dependency has been marked as deprecated and should be removed in future builds. Used to track technical debt and plan refactoring.']
  deprecated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this dependency was marked as deprecated. Null if not deprecated.']
  discovered_by_tool varchar [note: 'The name and version of the tool or system that discovered this dependency (e.g., Unity Asset Analyzer 2.1, Unreal Header Tool 5.3, Custom Dependency Scanner).']
  discovery_method varchar [note: 'The method by which this dependency was identified. Static analysis scans code/assets automatically; manual declaration is explicitly defined by developers; runtime scan detects dependencies during execution.']
  incremental_build_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dependency participates in incremental build scope computation. If true, changes to the target asset will trigger rebuild of the source asset.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dependency is currently active and should be enforced by the build pipeline. Inactive dependencies are retained for historical tracking but not validated.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this dependency record was last modified.']
  last_validated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this dependency was last validated by the build pipeline or asset integrity checker.']
  lod_tier varchar [note: 'The LOD tier for which this dependency applies, if lod_variant_dependency_flag is true. Used for platform-specific asset stripping during packaging.']
  lod_variant_dependency_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dependency is specific to a particular LOD (Level of Detail) variant. If true, the dependency applies only to specific quality tiers and may be stripped for lower-tier builds.']
  memory_impact_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated memory footprint in megabytes when both source and target assets are loaded simultaneously. Used for memory budget planning and optimization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional context, technical notes, or special handling instructions related to this dependency. Used by developers and build engineers for documentation.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'If dependency_type is overrides, this field explains why the source asset overrides the target asset. Common reasons include platform-specific customization, localization variants, or performance optimization.']
  platform_specific_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dependency is platform-specific (true) or applies across all platforms (false). Platform-specific dependencies are used for conditional asset stripping during packaging.']
  runtime_load_order varchar [note: 'Specifies the order in which assets should be loaded at runtime to satisfy this dependency. Lower numbers load first. Used by streaming systems and level loading.']
  target_platform_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of platform codes for which this dependency applies (e.g., PC, PS5, XBOX, IOS, ANDROID). Empty if dependency applies to all platforms.']
  validation_error_message varchar [note: 'Detailed error message if validation_status is invalid, missing-target, circular, or unresolved. Provides diagnostic information for build failures.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current validation state of the dependency. Valid means target exists and is accessible; invalid indicates broken reference; missing-target means target asset not found; circular indicates dependency loop detected.']
  version_constraint varchar [note: 'Specifies version requirements for the target asset (e.g., >=2.0, ==1.5.3, ~>3.0). Used to ensure compatibility between dependent assets across versions.']

  Note: 'Directed dependency record between two assets capturing the relationship type (references, requires, overrides, variant-of) used by the build pipeline to resolve asset graphs, detect missing references, and compute incremental build scopes. Stores source asset, target asset, dependency type, dependency strength (hard/soft), discovery method (static analysis, manual declaration), and validation status. Critical for build integrity checks and platform-specific asset stripping during packaging.'
}

Table content.content_pack {
  content_pack_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for content_pack']
  approval_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this content pack for deployment. Null if not yet approved.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or content creator who authored this content pack.']
  parent_content_pack_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on content_pack (parent_content_pack_id)']
  title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title this content pack is associated with.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the content pack was approved for deployment. Null if not yet approved.']
  archive_date date [note: 'Date when the content pack was archived and moved to long-term storage. Null for active or deprecated content packs.']
  asset_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual digital assets contained within this content pack.']
  checksum_hash varchar [note: 'SHA-256 checksum hash of the content pack for integrity verification during download and deployment.']
  compression_algorithm varchar [note: 'Compression algorithm applied to the content pack for storage and distribution optimization.']
  content_rating varchar [note: 'Entertainment Software Rating Board (ESRB) content rating applicable to this content pack: E (Everyone), E10+ (Everyone 10+), T (Teen), M (Mature), AO (Adults Only).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the content pack record was first created in the system.']
  dependency_pack_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of content pack IDs that this pack depends on for proper functionality. Null if no dependencies exist.']
  deployment_environment varchar [note: 'Target deployment environment for this content pack: development, staging, production, or live-ops.']
  deployment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the content pack was deployed to the target environment. Null if not yet deployed.']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'Date when the content pack was marked as deprecated and scheduled for removal. Null for active content packs.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the content pack purpose, contents, and usage notes for content teams and developers.']
  download_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this content pack has been downloaded by players or deployed to game clients.']
  error_report_count varchar [note: 'Total number of error reports or bug reports associated with this content pack since deployment.']
  event_end_date date [note: 'End date for live-ops event content availability, if applicable. Null for non-event or permanent content packs.']
  event_start_date date [note: 'Start date for live-ops event content availability, if applicable. Null for non-event content packs.']
  live_ops_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this content pack is part of a live-ops event such as seasonal content, limited-time events, or special promotions.']
  localization_language_count varchar [note: 'Number of languages supported in this content pack for localization strings and audio.']
  lod_variant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this content pack includes multiple LOD (Level of Detail) asset variants for performance optimization.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the content pack record was last modified or updated.']
  pack_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the content pack, used in game manifests and deployment pipelines.']
  pack_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the content pack displayed to developers and content teams.']
  pack_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the content pack in the asset pipeline workflow.']
  pack_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the content pack by asset category: art assets, audio files, localization strings, level/map data, NPC definitions, or textures.']
  patch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the content pack by deployment type: major patch, minor patch, hotfix, content drop, or expansion.']
  perforce_changelist_number varchar [note: 'Perforce Helix Core changelist number tracking the version control commit for this content pack.']
  platform_target varchar [note: 'Target gaming platform for which this content pack is optimized: console, PC, mobile, or cross-platform.']
  quality_assurance_status varchar [note: 'Current quality assurance testing status of the content pack.']
  release_notes varchar [note: 'Release notes documenting changes, new features, bug fixes, and known issues for this content pack version.']
  total_size_mb decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total storage size of the content pack in megabytes, including all assets and metadata.']
  ugc_moderation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this content pack contains user-generated content requiring moderation review before deployment.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of the content pack following major.minor.patch convention.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for content_pack. Referenced by content_pack_id.'
}

Table community.forum {
  forum_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each community forum or sub-forum entity within the gaming platform. Primary key for all forum-related data products.']
  developer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.developer_account. Business justification: Developer accounts manage official forums for their titles. Community managers need to link forum moderation permissions, content policy enforcement, and developer portal access to developer accounts.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title this forum is associated with. Enables forum analytics to be segmented by game title for community health reporting.']
  parent_forum_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the parent forum when this record represents a sub-forum. Null for top-level forums. Supports hierarchical forum structures.']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the gaming platform (e.g., PC, console, mobile) this forum is scoped to. Supports platform-specific community segmentation.']
  allows_attachments boolean [note: 'Indicates whether players are permitted to attach files, images, or media to posts in this forum. Relevant for UGC (User-Generated Content) forums and bug report forums requiring screenshots.']
  allows_ugc_submissions boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this forum accepts formal User-Generated Content (UGC) submissions such as mods, maps, or fan art. Distinct from general attachments; triggers UGC review workflows.']
  archived_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forum was transitioned to \'archived\' status. Null if the forum has never been archived. Supports community lifecycle management and data retention compliance.']
  auto_lock_days_inactive varchar [note: 'Number of days of inactivity after which threads in this forum are automatically locked. Null means threads never auto-lock. Supports community hygiene and moderation efficiency.']
  banner_image_url varchar [note: 'URL to the forum\'s banner or header image asset hosted on the CDN (Content Delivery Network). Used for visual branding of the forum on the community portal.']
  content_rating varchar [note: 'Content rating assigned to this forum based on the nature of discussions permitted, aligned with ESRB and PEGI rating systems. Determines age-gating and parental control enforcement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: E|E10+|T|M|AO|RP|PEGI_3|PEGI_7|PEGI_12|PEGI_16|PEGI_18 — promote to reference product]']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary geographic market this forum serves. Used for regional compliance (GDPR, COPPA) and localized community management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forum record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and community lifecycle tracking.']
  csat_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Customer Satisfaction Score (CSAT) for this forum, typically on a 1–5 scale, aggregated from player feedback. Tracks community satisfaction as part of player care operations.']
  dau varchar [note: 'Count of unique players who engaged with this forum (viewed, posted, or reacted) on the most recent calendar day. Key community health KPI (Key Performance Indicator) aligned with platform-wide DAU tracking.']
  depth_level varchar [note: 'Integer indicating the nesting depth of this forum in the hierarchy. Level 0 is a top-level forum; level 1 is a direct sub-forum; higher values indicate deeper nesting.']
  forum_category varchar [note: 'Logical grouping category for the forum used to organize forums in the community portal (e.g., \'Game Discussion\', \'Technical Support\', \'Esports\', \'Off-Topic\', \'Announcements\', \'UGC Showcase\'). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: game_discussion|technical_support|esports|announcements|ugc_showcase|off_topic|bug_reports|feedback — promote to reference product]']
  forum_description varchar [note: 'Short descriptive text explaining the purpose, scope, and posting guidelines of the forum. Displayed to players on the forum landing page.']
  forum_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name of the forum or sub-forum as shown to players on the community platform (e.g., \'General Discussion\', \'Bug Reports\', \'Esports Strategy\').']
  forum_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the forum. \'active\' is open for posting; \'inactive\' is temporarily disabled; \'archived\' is read-only historical forum; \'locked\' prevents new posts but remains visible; \'pending_review\' is awaiting moderation approval before going live.']
  forum_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the operational purpose of the forum. \'standard\' is general discussion; \'announcement\' is read-heavy official news; \'support\' maps to Zendesk-integrated player support; \'esports\' is for competitive community; \'ugc\' is for User-Generated Content (UGC) sharing; \'guild\' is for guild/clan community spaces.']
  icon_image_url varchar [note: 'URL to the forum\'s icon or thumbnail image asset hosted on the CDN (Content Delivery Network). Used in forum listings and navigation menus.']
  is_age_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this forum requires age verification before access, in compliance with ESRB, PEGI, and COPPA regulations for mature content communities.']
  is_featured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this forum is promoted as a featured community space on the platform homepage or community hub. Featured forums receive elevated visibility for player acquisition and retention.']
  is_pinned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this forum is pinned to the top of its category listing. Pinned forums are prioritized in navigation to drive player engagement with key community spaces.']
  last_post_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent post made in this forum. Used to determine forum activity recency, drive auto-lock logic, and surface active forums in community portal rankings.']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'IETF BCP 47 language-region code specifying the primary language and locale of the forum (e.g., \'en-US\', \'fr-FR\', \'ja-JP\', \'ko-KR\'). Supports multi-language community segmentation and localization.']
  mau varchar [note: 'Count of unique players who engaged with this forum in the most recent 30-day rolling window. Used alongside DAU for community stickiness and engagement ratio analysis.']
  max_post_length_chars varchar [note: 'Maximum character count allowed per post in this forum. Enforces posting rules and prevents spam. Null means no character limit is enforced.']
  minimum_age_years varchar [note: 'Minimum player age in years required to access this forum. Enforced for age-restricted forums per ESRB, PEGI, COPPA, and regional rating body requirements. Null if no age restriction applies.']
  moderation_mode varchar [note: 'Defines the content moderation approach for this forum. \'pre_moderated\' requires approval before posts are visible; \'post_moderated\' reviews after publishing; \'auto_moderated\' uses automated tools; \'unmoderated\' has no active moderation.']
  nps_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Net Promoter Score (NPS) for this forum, ranging from -100 to 100, derived from player satisfaction surveys. Measures community sentiment and likelihood of players recommending the forum to others.']
  post_count varchar [note: 'Total cumulative number of posts ever created in this forum. Used as a community health and engagement metric. Updated by the platform\'s community analytics pipeline.']
  posting_permission varchar [note: 'Defines which player segments are permitted to create new posts in this forum. \'verified_only\' requires email-verified accounts; \'moderators_only\' restricts to community moderators; \'staff_only\' is for official announcements.']
  requires_account_verification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether players must have a verified account (email or identity verified) to post in this forum. Supports anti-spam and COPPA compliance for age-gated communities.']
  rules_url varchar [note: 'URL linking to the official posting rules and community guidelines document for this forum. Displayed to players before posting to ensure compliance with community standards.']
  slug varchar [note: 'URL-safe, lowercase hyphenated identifier used in the forum\'s web address. Ensures stable, human-readable permalinks for SEO and deep-linking purposes.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric display ordering position of the forum within its parent category or parent forum. Lower values appear first in the community portal navigation.']
  subscriber_count varchar [note: 'Total number of players who have subscribed to receive notifications from this forum. Indicates community reach and is used in player retention and K-Factor (Viral Coefficient) analysis.']
  thread_count varchar [note: 'Total cumulative number of discussion threads created in this forum. Distinct from post count; a thread is the parent topic while posts are replies. Key community engagement indicator.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this forum record (e.g., settings change, status update, moderation rule change). Supports audit trail and change data capture in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  visibility varchar [note: 'Controls who can view the forum. \'public\' is visible to all including unauthenticated visitors; \'private\' is hidden from non-members; \'members_only\' requires platform account; \'staff_only\' is restricted to internal community team.']
  zendesk_group_code varchar [note: 'External identifier linking this forum to its corresponding Zendesk support group for player support ticket routing. Enables seamless escalation from community forum posts to Zendesk tickets.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing community discussion forums and sub-forums within the gaming platform. Tracks forum identity, category, game title association, moderation settings, posting rules, visibility (public/private/members-only), language locale, post count, subscriber count, and active status. SSOT for all community forum structures across titles and platforms.'
}

Table community.forum_thread {
  forum_thread_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each forum thread or post record within the community forum system. Primary key for the forum_thread data product.']
  backlog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.backlog_item. Business justification: Community-driven backlog prioritization: high-visibility threads discussing bugs or feature requests spawn backlog items. Community managers link threads to track player sentiment and prioritize work.']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_release. Business justification: Community sentiment analysis aggregates forum discussion volume and sentiment by content_drop_id to measure release reception and identify issues. Marketing uses this for launch retrospectives and fut']
  forum_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent forum or sub-forum board in which this thread or post resides. Establishes the community section context (e.g., General Discussion, Bug Reports, Patch Notes).']
  gacha_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.gacha_pool. Business justification: Gacha pools generate extensive forum discussion (drop rate analysis, pull strategy, complaints about pity system). Community managers track pool-specific sentiment for monetization feedback and to ide']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game title this forum thread is associated with. Enables game-specific community analytics, moderation scoping, and cross-title engagement reporting.']
  influencer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.influencer. Business justification: Influencers host forum threads for AMAs, announcements, and community engagement as part of marketing partnerships. Direct link enables influencer engagement tracking, contractual deliverable verifica']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Threads are created for campaign announcements, influencer AMAs, and promotional events. Direct campaign attribution enables measurement of community engagement effectiveness and content performance p']
  offer_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.offer_campaign. Business justification: Players create forum threads discussing promotional offers (value analysis, eligibility questions, bugs). Community managers track campaign-related discussions for sentiment analysis and to identify o']
  primary_forum_player_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player who authored this thread or post. Links to the player master record for identity, account standing, and community reputation context.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.promotion. Business justification: Players discuss promotions in forums (value analysis, code sharing, eligibility bugs). Community managers track promotion-related threads for sentiment, identify configuration issues, and measure prom']
  seasonal_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.seasonal_event. Business justification: Community engagement metrics track forum activity per seasonal event for event success measurement. Live ops uses thread volume and sentiment to evaluate event reception and inform future event design']
  thread_root_forum_thread_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating thread-starter post (depth level 0) for all replies within the same conversation. Equals forum_thread_id for root posts. Enables efficient retrieval of all posts within a thread without recursive traversal.']
  author_display_name varchar [note: 'The in-game or forum display name (gamertag/username) of the post author at the time of posting. Stored as a snapshot to preserve historical display context even if the player later changes their display name. Classified as confidential PII as it is a pseudonymous identifier traceable to an individual.']
  author_post_count_at_time varchar [note: 'Snapshot of the author\'s total forum post count at the time this post was submitted. Provides community seniority context for moderation decisions and trust-scoring models without requiring a join to current player stats.']
  body_content varchar [note: 'Full text body of the forum post or reply, stored as plain text or lightweight markup (BBCode/Markdown). Primary content payload for community discourse, moderation review, and NLP-based sentiment analysis.']
  content_language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 region subtag) of the post body content (e.g., en, en-US, fr, de, ja, ko). Supports multilingual community moderation routing, localization analytics, and GDPR jurisdiction determination.']
  content_rating_category varchar [note: 'Community content maturity classification applied to this post, aligned with ESRB/PEGI rating vocabulary. Supports age-gating of forum content, COPPA compliance for underage players, and platform certification requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when this forum_thread record was first persisted to the data platform. May differ slightly from post_timestamp due to ingestion latency. Supports data lineage and Silver layer audit requirements.']
  csat_score varchar [note: 'Customer Satisfaction Score (CSAT) value (1–5) optionally collected from the post author following a community support interaction associated with this thread. Supports player care quality measurement and Zendesk integration reporting.']
  downvote_count varchar [note: 'Total number of downvotes (negative reactions) received by this post from community members. Used alongside upvote_count to compute net community sentiment score and content quality signals.']
  edit_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this post has been edited by the author or a moderator. Supports transparency indicators and moderation audit. High edit counts may signal content manipulation patterns.']
  flair_label varchar [note: 'Community-assigned or author-selected flair tag categorizing the thread topic (e.g., Bug Report, Feedback, Guide, Lore, Patch Discussion). Supports forum navigation, filtering, and content taxonomy analytics.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Soft-delete flag indicating the post has been removed from public view by the author or a moderator. Retains the record for audit, compliance, and potential restoration. Supports GDPR right-to-erasure workflows.']
  is_edited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the post body or title has been edited after initial submission. Displayed to community members as an edit indicator. Supports transparency and moderation audit requirements.']
