  external_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external or legacy systems to reference this forum category for integration and migration purposes.']
  featured_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for featuring this category on the homepage or community hub. Higher numbers indicate higher priority. Null if not featured.']
  icon_url varchar [note: 'URL path to the icon image representing this forum category in the user interface.']
  is_moderated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether posts in this forum category require moderator approval before being published.']
  is_private boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this forum category is private and requires special permissions or membership to access.']
  is_read_only boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this forum category is read-only, preventing new posts or replies from being created.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the primary language for content in this forum category.']
  last_post_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent post or reply made in this forum category.']
  max_attachment_size_mb varchar [note: 'Maximum file size in megabytes allowed for attachments in posts within this forum category.']
  meta_description varchar [note: 'HTML meta description tag content for search engine optimization of the forum category page.']
  meta_title varchar [note: 'HTML meta title tag content for search engine optimization of the forum category page.']
  minimum_player_level varchar [note: 'The minimum player level required to view or post in this forum category. Null if no level restriction applies.']
  moderation_level varchar [note: 'The intensity level of content moderation applied to posts and discussions in this forum category.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forum category record was last updated or modified.']
  name varchar [note: 'The display name of the forum category as shown to players in the community forums.']
  notification_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether players can subscribe to notifications for new posts and activity in this forum category.']
  platform_type varchar [note: 'The gaming platform this forum category is focused on or applicable to.']
  post_cooldown_minutes varchar [note: 'Minimum number of minutes a player must wait between consecutive posts in this forum category to prevent spam.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter geographic region code indicating the target region for this forum category.']
  seo_slug varchar [note: 'URL-friendly slug used for search engine optimization and clean URLs for this forum category.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the forum category indicating its availability and visibility to players.']
  total_posts varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of all posts and replies made in this forum category since inception.']
  total_threads varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of all discussion threads created in this forum category since inception.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the forum category by its primary purpose and content focus.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for forum_category. Referenced by forum_category_id.'
}

Table community.kb_category {
  kb_category_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for kb_category']
  archived_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who archived this category. Null if category is not archived.']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this category record.']

  parent_kb_category_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on kb_category (parent_kb_category_id)']
  archived_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this category was archived. Null if category is not archived.']
  article_count varchar [note: 'Total number of knowledge base articles currently assigned to this category.']
  banner_url varchar [note: 'URL path to the banner image displayed at the top of the category page.']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the category used in external systems and reporting.']

  category_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the knowledge base category by subject area.']

  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence for ordering categories in user interfaces and navigation menus.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this category is scheduled to be archived or deactivated. Null for indefinite categories.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this category becomes active and visible to users.']
  external_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for this category in external support systems such as Zendesk or other knowledge management platforms.']
  external_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external system where this category originates or is synchronized to.']
  game_title_filter varchar [note: 'Specific game title this category is associated with, or null for cross-game categories.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth level in the category hierarchy tree, with 1 being top-level.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Full path from root to current category using category codes or IDs, delimited by forward slashes.']
  icon_url varchar [note: 'URL path to the icon image representing this category in user interfaces.']
  is_featured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category is featured prominently on the knowledge base home page.']
  is_searchable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether articles in this category are included in knowledge base search results.']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'Language and region code (ISO 639-1 and ISO 3166-1) for localized category content.']
  moderation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether new articles in this category require approval before publication.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this category record was last updated.']
  platform_filter varchar [note: 'Gaming platform(s) this category applies to for platform-specific knowledge base content.']
  requires_authentication boolean [note: 'Indicates whether users must be logged in to access articles in this category.']
  search_keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of keywords and tags to improve category discoverability in search.']
  seo_description varchar [note: 'Meta description for search engine optimization and social media sharing.']
  seo_slug varchar [note: 'URL-friendly identifier used in the category page web address.']
  seo_title varchar [note: 'Optimized title for search engine indexing and display in search results.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the knowledge base category.']
  sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this category was last synchronized with external support systems.']
  view_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of times articles in this category have been viewed by users.']
  visibility_scope varchar [note: 'Access level determining which user roles can view articles in this category.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for kb_category. Referenced by category_id.'
}

Table community.kb_section {
  kb_section_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for kb_section']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user (support agent or content manager) who authored this section.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game title this section relates to. Null if the section applies to general platform or account topics.']
  kb_article_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent knowledge base article that this section belongs to.']
  parent_kb_section_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on kb_section (parent_kb_section_id)']
  replacement_section_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the kb_section that replaces this deprecated section. Used to redirect players to current content.']
  reviewer_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user who reviewed and approved this section for publication. Null if not yet reviewed.']
  archived_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this section was archived and removed from active player-facing knowledge base. Null if currently active.']
  average_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Average player rating for this section on a scale of 0.00 to 5.00. Aggregated from player feedback submissions.']
  canonical_url varchar [note: 'Canonical URL path for this section in the public-facing knowledge base. Used for SEO and direct linking from external sources.']
  content_body varchar [note: 'The main text content of the knowledge base section. May include formatted text, links, and embedded media references.']
  content_format varchar [note: 'Format specification for the content_body field. Determines how the content should be rendered in the player-facing interface.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this knowledge base section record was first created in the system.']
  deprecation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why this section was deprecated. Null if the section is not deprecated. Used for content audit trails.']
  difficulty_level varchar [note: 'Complexity level of the content. Helps players assess whether the section matches their technical proficiency.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence number determining the order in which sections appear within the parent article. Lower numbers appear first.']
  estimated_read_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated time in minutes for a player to read and understand this section. Calculated based on word count and content complexity.']
  feedback_comment_count varchar [note: 'Number of player feedback comments submitted for this section. Used to identify content requiring attention or clarification.']
  helpful_vote_count varchar [note: 'Number of players who marked this section as helpful. Used to measure content quality and relevance.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this section content is outdated and scheduled for removal or replacement. Used to manage content lifecycle.']
  is_featured boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this section is featured prominently in the knowledge base interface. Used to highlight important or frequently needed content.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (with optional ISO 3166-1 country code) indicating the language of the section content. Supports multi-language knowledge base.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this section was last updated. Used to track content freshness and trigger review workflows.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this section was last reviewed for accuracy and relevance. Used to schedule periodic content audits.']
  linked_ticket_category varchar [note: 'Support ticket category code that this section addresses. Used to suggest relevant KB articles during ticket creation and deflect support volume.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next content review cycle. Used to ensure knowledge base content remains current and accurate.']
  platform_applicability varchar [note: 'Gaming platform(s) to which this section content applies. Enables platform-specific help content filtering.']
  published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this section was first published and made visible to players. Null if never published.']
  related_section_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of kb_section_id values for related sections. Used to provide cross-references and improve player navigation.']
  requires_authentication boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether players must be logged in to view this section. Used for account-specific or sensitive support content.']
  search_keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of keywords and phrases used to improve search discoverability of the section. Includes common player terminology and synonyms.']
  section_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the section, used for cross-referencing and integration with support ticketing systems.']
  section_title varchar [note: 'The human-readable title of the knowledge base section. Used for navigation and display in help documentation.']
  section_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the section content type. Determines how the section is presented and indexed for player search.']
  seo_meta_description varchar [note: 'Meta description text for search engine optimization. Used when the knowledge base is publicly indexed by search engines.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the knowledge base section. Controls visibility to players and availability in search results.']
  unhelpful_vote_count varchar [note: 'Number of players who marked this section as not helpful. Indicates content that may need revision or clarification.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the section content. Incremented with each published revision to track content evolution.']
  video_url varchar [note: 'URL to an embedded video tutorial or demonstration related to this section content. Null if no video is associated.']
  view_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this section has been viewed by players. Used for content effectiveness analytics.']
  visibility_level varchar [note: 'Access control level determining which player segments can view this section. Used for tiered support content delivery.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for kb_section. Referenced by section_id.'
}

Table community.survey {
  survey_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for survey']
  follow_up_survey_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on survey (follow_up_survey_id)']
  anonymous_responses_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether players can submit responses anonymously without player identification.']
  archived_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the survey was archived and removed from active distribution.']
  average_csat_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Average Customer Satisfaction score calculated from survey responses (scale 1-5).']
  average_nps_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Average Net Promoter Score calculated from survey responses (scale 0-10).']
  category varchar [note: 'Business category of the survey focus area.']
  completion_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of players who completed the survey after starting it.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the survey record was first created in the system.']
  data_retention_days varchar [note: 'Number of days survey response data is retained before archival or deletion per privacy policy.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the survey purpose and scope.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the survey is distributed to players.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the survey closes and stops accepting responses.']
  estimated_completion_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated time in minutes for a player to complete the survey.']
  external_survey_url varchar [note: 'URL link to an external survey platform (e.g., SurveyMonkey, Qualtrics) if the survey is hosted externally.']
  game_title_filter varchar [note: 'Specific game title(s) for which this survey is targeted, or \'all\' for cross-game surveys.']
  incentive_description varchar [note: 'Description of the incentive offered for completing the survey (e.g., 100 gems, exclusive skin).']
  incentive_offered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an incentive (e.g., in-game currency, items) is offered for survey completion.']
  integration_platform varchar [note: 'Third-party platform integrated for survey distribution and response collection.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code for the survey (e.g., en, es, ja, zh-CN).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the survey record was last updated.']
  localization_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the survey is available in multiple languages.']
  max_responses_target varchar [note: 'Target maximum number of responses to collect before closing the survey.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes or comments about the survey for team reference.']
  owner_team varchar [note: 'Business team responsible for managing and analyzing this survey.']
  platform_filter varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of platforms where the survey is displayed (e.g., PC, console, mobile, all).']
  privacy_policy_version varchar [note: 'Version of the privacy policy acknowledged by players when taking the survey.']
  published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the survey was published and made active to players.']
  question_count varchar [note: 'Total number of questions in the survey.']
  region_filter varchar [note: 'Geographic region(s) where the survey is distributed (e.g., NA, EU, APAC, all).']
  response_count varchar [note: 'Current count of responses received for this survey.']
  response_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether survey completion is mandatory for the player.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the survey becomes active and available to players.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the survey.']
  survey_code varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique code for the survey (e.g., NPS_Q1_2024, CSAT_POST_MATCH).']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the survey type: Net Promoter Score (NPS), Customer Satisfaction (CSAT), Customer Effort Score (CES), general feedback, bug report, or feature request.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated tags for categorization and search (e.g., seasonal, event-driven, post-launch).']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Player segment targeted for this survey.']
  title varchar [note: 'The display title of the survey shown to players.']
  trigger_event varchar [note: 'The in-game or platform event that triggers the survey display (e.g., match_completion, level_up, support_ticket_closed).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the community manager or analyst who created the survey.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for survey. Referenced by survey_id.'
}

Table marketing.marketing_campaign {
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing campaign. Primary key.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the game title being promoted by this campaign.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.ip_agreement. Business justification: Marketing campaigns for licensed IP games must track governing license agreement for usage rights validation, royalty obligations, territorial restrictions, and content approval workflows before launc']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Marketing campaigns must comply with jurisdiction-specific advertising restrictions, age-gating requirements, and content regulations. Essential for legal review workflows and campaign approval gates ']
  server_fleet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.server_fleet. Business justification: Live-ops campaigns (events, tournaments, content drops) require coordination with infrastructure teams to provision adequate server fleet capacity. Campaign managers reference the target fleet to ensu']
  adjust_campaign_token varchar [note: 'External identifier or token from Adjust attribution platform linking this campaign to attribution records and performance metrics.']
  appsflyer_campaign_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier from AppsFlyer attribution platform linking this campaign to attribution records and performance metrics.']
  aso_optimized boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this campaign includes App Store Optimization (ASO) efforts for organic discovery.']
  attribution_link varchar [note: 'Full attribution tracking URL or deep link used for this campaign to measure installs and post-install events.']
  attribution_partner varchar [note: 'Name of the mobile attribution platform or partner used to track campaign performance (e.g., AppsFlyer, Adjust, Branch, Kochava, Singular, or internal tracking).']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total allocated budget for the campaign in the specified currency.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the campaign budget (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code or identifier for the campaign used in attribution systems and tracking URLs.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the marketing campaign used for identification and reporting.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign indicating its operational state.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the campaign by its primary objective: user acquisition (UA), retention, re-engagement, brand awareness, soft launch, or hard launch.']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Specific marketing channel or platform where the campaign runs (e.g., Facebook, Google Ads, TikTok, YouTube, Apple Search Ads, influencer network).']
  channel_subtype varchar [note: 'Granular channel subtype or ad format (e.g., paid_social, display, search, video, CTV/OTT, influencer, email, push notification).']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'High-level channel taxonomy classification: organic, paid, owned, or earned media.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this campaign record was first created in the system.']
  creative_theme varchar [note: 'Primary creative theme or messaging angle used in campaign assets (e.g., gameplay showcase, character spotlight, seasonal event, competitive PvP).']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign is scheduled to end or ended. Nullable for evergreen campaigns.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the campaign is currently active and running (true) or inactive/completed (false).']
  is_crm_lifecycle_campaign boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a CRM-driven lifecycle campaign (e.g., onboarding, retention, win-back) managed through Salesforce.']
  is_influencer_campaign boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this campaign involves influencer or affiliate partnerships.']
  is_soft_launch boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this campaign is part of a soft launch (limited market test) or hard launch (full market release).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this campaign record was last updated or modified.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual campaign manager or lead responsible for campaign execution and performance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the campaign, including special instructions, learnings, or context for future reference.']
  objective varchar [note: 'Detailed business objective or goal statement for the campaign, such as target installs, target ROAS (Return on Ad Spend), or target retention rate.']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the marketing team or business unit responsible for managing this campaign.']
  salesforce_campaign_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier from Salesforce CRM system linking this campaign to the source system record.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign is scheduled to begin or began running.']
  target_audience_segment varchar [note: 'Description or identifier of the target audience segment for this campaign (e.g., core gamers, casual players, lapsed users, whales).']
  target_cpi decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target cost per install (CPI) goal for user acquisition campaigns, representing the maximum acceptable cost to acquire one new player install.']
  target_platform varchar [note: 'Primary gaming platform targeted by the campaign: iOS, Android, PC, console, or cross-platform.']
  target_region varchar [note: 'Primary geographic region or country targeted by the campaign using three-letter ISO country codes (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN).']
  target_roas decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target return on ad spend (ROAS) goal for the campaign, expressed as a ratio (e.g., 3.0 means $3 revenue for every $1 spent).']
  utm_campaign varchar [note: 'UTM campaign parameter identifying the specific campaign name or promo code used in tracking URLs.']
  utm_content varchar [note: 'UTM content parameter used to differentiate similar content or links within the same campaign (e.g., A/B test variants).']
  utm_medium varchar [note: 'UTM medium parameter identifying the marketing medium (e.g., cpc, banner, email, social) used in campaign tracking URLs.']
  utm_source varchar [note: 'UTM source parameter identifying the traffic source (e.g., google, facebook, newsletter) used in campaign tracking URLs.']
  utm_term varchar [note: 'UTM term parameter identifying paid search keywords or targeting criteria used in the campaign.']

  Note: 'Master record for all player acquisition and retention marketing campaigns across channels (paid social, display, search, video, CTV/OTT, influencer, CRM lifecycle). Captures campaign name, type (UA/retention/re-engagement/brand), objective, status, budget allocation, start/end dates, target platform (iOS/Android/PC/console), target region, soft-launch vs hard-launch flag, owning team, channel taxonomy (organic/paid/owned), and UTM parameters. Single source of truth for campaign identity in the marketing domain. Sourced from Salesforce CRM campaign objects and AppsFlyer/Adjust campaign metadata. Referenced by attribution records, spend records, influencer engagements, and budget allocations.'
}

Table marketing.ad_creative {
  ad_creative_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the advertising creative asset. Primary key for the ad creative catalog.']
  age_rating_cert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.age_rating_cert. Business justification: Ad creatives must comply with age rating content descriptors and platform certification requirements. Enforces creative approval workflows and prevents regulatory violations from non-compliant adverti']
  creative_producer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Gaming studios require accountability tracking for creative assets. Creative producer is currently denormalized text; linking to employee enables performance reviews, workload analysis, asset portfoli']
  dlc_bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.dlc_bundle. Business justification: Ad creatives promote specific DLC/season passes ("Season 3 Battle Pass Now Live"). Performance marketing measures DLC attach rate and ROAS per creative. Creative compliance verifies DLC pricing and co']
  game_edition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.game_edition. Business justification: Ad creatives frequently promote specific editions ("Pre-Order Ultimate Edition"). Creative approval workflows verify edition-specific claims (bonus content, pricing tier). Performance marketing tracks']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title this creative is promoting. Links creative to the specific game product being advertised.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.ip_agreement. Business justification: Ad creatives using licensed IP (characters, music, brands) require agreement reference for legal review, trademark usage validation, content descriptor compliance, and approval workflows. Creative tea']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.league. Business justification: Ad creatives often feature league-specific branding, logos, and content requiring league context for rights compliance, brand guidelines, and audience targeting. Essential for esports marketing creati']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign this creative belongs to. Links creative to broader campaign strategy and budget allocation.']
  agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external creative agency that produced this asset, if applicable. Null for in-house creatives.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the creative was approved for use in campaigns. Null if not yet approved. Critical for compliance audit trails.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who approved this creative for deployment. Used for accountability and compliance tracking.']
  archived_date date [note: 'Date when the creative was archived and removed from active use. Null if still active or not yet archived.']
  body_text varchar [note: 'Body copy or description text used in the creative. Provides additional context and messaging detail.']
  call_to_action varchar [note: 'The call-to-action text or button label used in the creative. Examples: Install Now, Play Free, Download Today, Join the Battle.']
  coppa_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the creative complies with COPPA requirements for advertising to children under 13. Required for games with child audiences.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the creative record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and lifecycle tracking.']
  creative_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for the creative asset. Used for tracking and attribution across platforms.']
  creative_concept varchar [note: 'The creative concept, theme, or messaging strategy used in the asset. Examples: character showcase, gameplay highlight, story teaser, competitive feature.']
  creative_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the advertising creative asset. Used for identification and reference in campaign management systems.']
  creative_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the creative asset. Tracks approval workflow and active deployment state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|paused|archived|rejected — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  duration_seconds decimal(6,2) [note: 'Duration of video or playable creative in seconds. Null for static formats. Critical for platform compliance and user experience optimization.']
  esrb_rating_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the creative content complies with ESRB content rating guidelines for the associated game title. Critical for North American market compliance.']
  file_reference_uri varchar [note: 'URI or path reference to the creative asset file in the content delivery network or digital asset management system. Links to the actual media file.']
  file_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'File size of the creative asset in megabytes. Used for CDN (Content Delivery Network) optimization and platform compliance validation.']
  first_deployed_date date [note: 'Date when the creative was first deployed in a live campaign. Marks the start of the creative\'s active lifecycle.']
  format_type varchar [note: 'The format or media type of the advertising creative. Determines how the creative is displayed and interacted with by players. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: video|playable|static_banner|interstitial|rewarded_video|native|carousel|story — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  geo_target_countries varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes this creative is targeted for. Used for localization and regional compliance.']
  headline_text varchar [note: 'Primary headline or title text displayed in the creative. Used for messaging analysis and ASO (App Store Optimization) alignment.']
  height_pixels varchar [note: 'Height dimension of the creative asset in pixels. Used for platform compatibility validation and placement optimization.']
  is_store_listing_creative boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this creative is used in app store listings (Apple App Store, Google Play) as opposed to paid user acquisition campaigns. True for store assets, false for UA ads.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (with optional ISO 3166-1 country code) for the creative localization. Examples: en, en-US, ja, pt-BR.']
  last_deployed_date date [note: 'Date when the creative was most recently deployed in a campaign. Used for recency analysis and creative refresh planning.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the creative record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data freshness validation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the creative asset. Used for collaboration, context sharing, and historical documentation.']
  pegi_rating_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the creative content complies with PEGI content rating guidelines for the associated game title. Critical for European market compliance.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of creative performance based on KPI (Key Performance Indicator) benchmarks such as CTR (Click-Through Rate), CPI (Cost Per Install), and conversion rate. Updated periodically based on campaign analytics.']
  production_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost to produce this creative asset in USD. Used for ROI (Return on Investment) and ROAS (Return on Ad Spend) analysis.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the creative was rejected during approval workflow. Null if approved or pending. Used for quality improvement and compliance learning.']
  target_audience_segment varchar [note: 'The player audience segment this creative is targeting. Examples: core gamers, casual players, lapsed users, high-value whales, new installs.']
  target_platform varchar [note: 'The advertising platform or channel this creative is designed for. Determines technical specifications and compliance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ios|android|web|facebook|instagram|tiktok|youtube|snapchat|unity_ads|applovin|ironsource|cross_platform — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  thumbnail_uri varchar [note: 'URI reference to a thumbnail or preview image of the creative. Used for quick visual identification in campaign management dashboards.']
  width_pixels varchar [note: 'Width dimension of the creative asset in pixels. Used for platform compatibility validation and placement optimization.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all advertising creative assets used in UA and retention campaigns — video ads, playable ads, static banners, interstitials, and store listing creatives. Tracks creative name, format type, dimensions, file reference, game title association, creative concept/theme, A/B test variant flag, approval status, ESRB/PEGI content rating compliance flag, and creative lifecycle dates. Owned by the marketing domain as the authoritative creative asset registry distinct from the content domain\'s in-game asset pipeline.'
}

Table marketing.attribution_record {
  attribution_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the attribution record. Primary key for the attribution ledger.']
  ad_creative_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the specific ad creative (banner, video, playable) that the player interacted with before installing.']
  ad_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.ad_network. Business justification: Attribution records capture install/re-engagement events attributed to marketing sources. The network STRING field currently stores the ad network name as free text. This should be normalized to a FK ']
  ad_set_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the ad set (ad group) within the campaign that drove this install.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'The unique device identifier used for attribution (IDFA for iOS, GAID for Android, or other platform-specific identifiers). Considered PII and subject to privacy regulations.']
  first_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment. Business justification: Links player\'s first payment to their install attribution source, enabling true LTV-by-source calculation critical for ROAS optimization and UA budget allocation decisions in performance marketing ope']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Attribution data collection and retention governed by jurisdiction-specific privacy laws (GDPR, CCPA). Required for data retention policy enforcement, DSR fulfillment, and privacy impact assessments.']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the marketing campaign to which this install is attributed, as defined in AppsFlyer or Adjust.']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the player who installed or re-engaged with the game. Links to the player master record.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.tournament. Business justification: Attribution tracking for tournament-driven installs is critical for measuring event marketing ROI. Worlds viewership driving game downloads is a primary acquisition channel requiring direct tournament']
  ad_set_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the ad set (ad group) within the campaign.']
  app_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the game application in the attribution platform (e.g., bundle ID for iOS, package name for Android).']
  app_name varchar [note: 'The human-readable name of the game application for which this install was attributed.']
  attribution_platform varchar [note: 'The mobile attribution platform that generated this attribution record (AppsFlyer, Adjust, Branch, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AppsFlyer|Adjust|Branch|Kochava|Singular|Tenjin|internal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  attribution_platform_record_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this attribution record in the source attribution platform (AppsFlyer or Adjust). Used for reconciliation and deduplication.']
  attribution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the attribution platform processed and attributed this install event. May differ from install_timestamp due to processing latency.']
  attribution_type varchar [note: 'The method by which the install was attributed: click (last-click), view-through (impression-based), probabilistic (fingerprinting), deterministic (device ID match), or organic (no paid attribution).']
  attribution_window_hours varchar [note: 'The attribution window in hours used to attribute this install (e.g., 24 hours for click, 1 hour for view-through). Defines the maximum time between ad interaction and install for attribution.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The marketing channel through which the player was acquired (e.g., paid social, paid search, display, influencer, organic, referral).']
  click_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the player clicked on the ad, if attribution type is click-based. Null for view-through or organic installs.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the player at the time of install, as detected by the attribution platform.']
  cpi_usd decimal(10,4) [note: 'The cost per install in US dollars for this attributed install, as reported by the media source or calculated from campaign spend data. Used to calculate ROAS and marketing efficiency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this attribution record was first created in the lakehouse. Used for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device on which the game was installed (mobile, tablet, desktop, console, unknown).']
  fraud_reason varchar [note: 'The reason or category for fraud rejection if fraud_rejection_flag is True (e.g., click injection, click spam, install hijacking, bot traffic). Null if install is legitimate.']
  fraud_rejection_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this install was flagged as fraudulent by the attribution platforms fraud detection algorithms (True = fraud detected, False = legitimate install).']
  impression_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the ad impression was served to the player, if attribution type is view-through. Null for click-based or organic installs.']
  install_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the player installed the game or re-engaged, as reported by the attribution platform. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  install_type varchar [note: 'The type of install event: new_install (first-time install), re-install (player previously uninstalled and reinstalled), re-engagement (dormant player reactivated), or re-attribution (player attributed to a new campaign).']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address of the device at the time of install, used for geo-location and fraud detection. May be considered PII in some jurisdictions.']
  is_organic boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this install is organic (True = no paid attribution, False = attributed to a paid campaign). Organic installs have no associated CPI or campaign.']
  media_source varchar [note: 'The media source or advertising network that served the ad (e.g., Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, Unity Ads, ironSource).']
  os_name varchar [note: 'The operating system of the device on which the game was installed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: iOS|Android|Windows|macOS|Linux|PlayStation|Xbox|Nintendo|unknown — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  os_version varchar [note: 'The version of the operating system on the device at the time of install (e.g., iOS 16.3, Android 13).']
  platform varchar [note: 'The gaming platform or app store through which the game was installed (iOS App Store, Google Play, Steam, Epic Games Store, console platforms, web). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: iOS|Android|Steam|Epic|PlayStation|Xbox|Nintendo|Web|unknown — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  referrer_url varchar [note: 'The URL of the referring page or deep link that led to the install, if available. Used for web-to-app attribution and deep linking analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this attribution record was last updated in the lakehouse. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'The user agent string of the device browser or app at the time of install, used for device fingerprinting and attribution.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each player install or re-engagement event attributed to a marketing source, as reported by AppsFlyer or Adjust. Captures attributed player ID, install timestamp, attributed campaign, ad set, ad creative, channel, network, media source, country, device type, OS version, attribution type (last-click/view-through/probabilistic), attribution window, CPI, and fraud rejection flag. This is the authoritative install attribution ledger for the marketing domain.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_spend {
  campaign_spend_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign spend record. Primary key for daily transactional marketing spend records.']
  ab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ab_experiment. Business justification: Soft launch campaigns often vary spend by experiment arm to test creative/targeting strategies. Links spend data to experiment framework for cost-per-variant analysis and ROAS measurement by treatment']
  ad_creative_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific ad creative asset used in this campaign spend record.']
  ad_network_id varchar [note: 'FK to marketing.ad_network']
  ad_set_id varchar [note: 'The ad set or ad group identifier from the ad network. Used for granular performance analysis within a campaign.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: User acquisition spend must be allocated to cost centers (title, studio, or platform-specific) for P&L reporting and overhead allocation. Monthly cost allocation runs distribute marketing spend to app']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game title being promoted by this campaign spend.']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign associated with this spend record.']
  offer_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.offer_campaign. Business justification: UA campaigns drive installs that convert via in-game offers. Linking campaign spend to offer campaigns enables full-funnel ROI analysis from acquisition cost through monetization revenue, critical for']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.tournament. Business justification: Tournament-specific marketing spend tracking (e.g., Worlds activation budget, regional finals campaigns) is essential for event ROI analysis, budget planning, and sponsor reporting. Standard practice ']
  attribution_window_days varchar [note: 'The number of days used for install attribution lookback window (e.g., 7-day click, 1-day view).']
  channel varchar [note: 'The marketing channel category for this spend (e.g., paid social, paid search, display, video, influencer, affiliate).']
  clicks varchar [note: 'Total number of clicks on the ad on the specified date.']
  cohort_date date [note: 'The cohort date used for retention analysis (D1/D7/D30) tied to this spend record. Typically matches spend_date for acquisition campaigns.']
  conversion_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'Install conversion rate expressed as a decimal. Calculated as installs / clicks. Measures post-click conversion effectiveness.']
  cpc decimal(10,4) [note: 'Cost per click. Calculated as spend_amount / clicks.']
  cpi decimal(10,4) [note: 'Cost per install. Calculated as spend_amount / installs. Key metric for user acquisition efficiency.']
  cpm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Cost per thousand impressions. Calculated as (spend_amount / impressions) * 1000.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this campaign spend record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  ctr decimal(8,6) [note: 'Click-through rate expressed as a decimal. Calculated as clicks / impressions. Measures ad engagement effectiveness.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the spend amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The source system from which this spend data was ingested (e.g., AppsFlyer cost aggregation, Adjust cost module, ad network API, manual entry).']
  discrepancy_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The difference between ad network reported spend and attribution platform aggregated spend, if any. Null if reconciled.']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the geographic target of this campaign spend.']
  impressions varchar [note: 'Total number of times the ad was displayed to users on the specified date.']
  installs varchar [note: 'Total number of game installs attributed to this campaign spend on the specified date, as reported by attribution platform (AppsFlyer/Adjust).']
  is_organic boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this record represents organic (non-paid) installs tracked for comparison purposes. False for paid spend records.']
  launch_phase varchar [note: 'The game lifecycle phase during which this spend occurred (soft launch, hard launch, live operations, sunset).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this spend record, such as manual adjustments, special events, or data quality issues.']
  platform varchar [note: 'The gaming platform targeted by this campaign spend (iOS, Android, Web, Console, PC).']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of spend reconciliation between ad network reported costs and attribution platform aggregated costs.']
  spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total marketing spend amount incurred on the specified date for this campaign, ad set, and channel combination.']
  spend_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the marketing spend was incurred. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this campaign spend record was last updated in the lakehouse silver layer.']

  Note: 'Daily transactional record of marketing spend per campaign, ad set, and channel as reported by ad networks and reconciled via AppsFlyer/Adjust cost aggregation. Captures spend date, campaign reference, channel/network, ad set, impressions, clicks, installs, spend amount, currency, CPM, CPC, CPI, CTR, and data source. Enables ROAS calculation and budget pacing against campaign budgets. Granularity is campaign × channel × date.'
}

Table marketing.retention_cohort {
  retention_cohort_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the retention cohort record. Primary key for the retention cohort entity.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the game title for which this cohort was acquired. Links to the game title master record.']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that acquired this cohort. Links to the campaign master record in the marketing domain.']
  offer_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.offer_campaign. Business justification: Retention cohorts measure player behavior including response to monetization offers. Linking enables analysis of offer campaign effectiveness by acquisition cohort, ROAS optimization, and identificati']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the platform (iOS, Android, PC, Console) on which this cohort was acquired. Links to the platform master record.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.tournament. Business justification: Cohort analysis for players acquired during major tournaments measures event-driven retention and LTV. Critical for evaluating tournament marketing effectiveness and informing future event investment ']
  acquisition_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total marketing spend attributed to acquiring this cohort. Used to calculate Cost Per Install (CPI) and Return on Ad Spend (ROAS). Expressed in the base currency (typically USD).']
  attribution_channel varchar [note: 'The marketing channel through which players in this cohort were acquired (e.g., paid social, organic, influencer, affiliate, cross-promotion).']
  attribution_partner varchar [note: 'The mobile attribution platform or partner that provided the install and attribution data for this cohort (e.g., AppsFlyer, Adjust, Branch).']
  attribution_source varchar [note: 'The specific source or network within the attribution channel (e.g., Facebook, Google Ads, TikTok, organic App Store).']
  cohort_arppu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average revenue per paying user for this cohort, calculated as total revenue from the cohort divided by the number of paying users in the cohort. Expressed in the base currency (typically USD).']
  cohort_arpu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average revenue per user for this cohort, calculated as total revenue from the cohort divided by cohort size. Expressed in the base currency (typically USD).']
  cohort_ltv_estimate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated lifetime value per user for this cohort, projected based on observed revenue trends and retention curves. Expressed in the base currency (typically USD).']
  cohort_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the retention cohort, typically combining install date, campaign, and channel for easy identification.']
  cohort_segment varchar [note: 'Business-defined segment or classification for this cohort based on performance, strategic importance, or experimental grouping (e.g., high-value, test-group-A, organic-tier-1).']
  cohort_size varchar [note: 'Total number of unique players who installed the game on the install date and are included in this cohort.']
  cohort_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cohort record. Active cohorts are still being tracked for retention metrics; mature cohorts have reached the end of the tracking window; archived cohorts are historical; under_review cohorts have data quality issues being investigated.']
  conversion_to_payer_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of users in this cohort who converted from free players to paying users, calculated as paying_users_count divided by cohort_size. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0523 for 5.23%).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the geographic market where players in this cohort were acquired.']
  cpi decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average cost to acquire one user in this cohort, calculated as acquisition_cost divided by cohort_size. Expressed in the base currency (typically USD).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this retention cohort record was first created in the data platform. Follows format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  d1_retained_players varchar [note: 'Number of players from this cohort who returned to the game on Day 1 after install.']
  d1_retention_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of players from this cohort who returned on Day 1, calculated as d1_retained_players divided by cohort_size. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.4523 for 45.23%).']
  d30_retained_players varchar [note: 'Number of players from this cohort who returned to the game on Day 30 after install.']
  d30_retention_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of players from this cohort who returned on Day 30, calculated as d30_retained_players divided by cohort_size. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0823 for 8.23%).']
  d60_retained_players varchar [note: 'Number of players from this cohort who returned to the game on Day 60 after install.']
  d60_retention_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of players from this cohort who returned on Day 60, calculated as d60_retained_players divided by cohort_size. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0512 for 5.12%).']
  d7_retained_players varchar [note: 'Number of players from this cohort who returned to the game on Day 7 after install.']
  d7_retention_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of players from this cohort who returned on Day 7, calculated as d7_retained_players divided by cohort_size. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2145 for 21.45%).']
  d90_retained_players varchar [note: 'Number of players from this cohort who returned to the game on Day 90 after install.']
  d90_retention_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of players from this cohort who returned on Day 90, calculated as d90_retained_players divided by cohort_size. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0345 for 3.45%).']
  dau_contribution varchar [note: 'Average number of daily active users contributed by this cohort over the measurement period, representing the cohorts ongoing engagement impact.']
  install_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which players in this cohort first installed the game. This is the cohort anchor date used for retention calculations.']
  is_soft_launch boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this cohort was acquired during a soft launch phase (limited geographic or audience release) versus a hard launch (full public release).']
  measurement_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days since install date for which retention and revenue metrics have been measured for this cohort. Used to determine data maturity and projection confidence.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for marketing analysts to record observations, anomalies, or context about this cohort (e.g., impacted by holiday promotion, data quality issue on D7).']
  paying_users_count varchar [note: 'Total number of users in this cohort who have made at least one in-app purchase or microtransaction.']
  roas decimal(10,4) [note: 'Return on ad spend for this cohort, calculated as total_revenue divided by acquisition_cost. Expressed as a ratio (e.g., 2.5000 means $2.50 revenue per $1.00 spent).']
  total_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative revenue generated by all users in this cohort since install date. Expressed in the base currency (typically USD).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this retention cohort record was last updated with new retention or revenue metrics. Follows format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Attribution-perspective retention cohort master record grouping players by install date, attributed campaign, channel, platform, and country. Tracks cohort size, D1/D7/D30/D60/D90 retention rates, DAU contribution, ARPU, ARPPU, LTV estimate, and conversion-to-payer rate for each cohort slice. Owned by marketing as the authoritative UA-sourced retention view, distinct from the analytics domain\'s broader behavioral cohorts.'
}

Table marketing.aso_listing {
  aso_listing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ASO listing record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing team member or ASO specialist responsible for managing and optimizing this store listing.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title this ASO listing represents.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.ip_agreement. Business justification: Store listings for licensed IP games require agreement reference for content descriptor compliance, trademark usage validation, territorial availability restrictions, and rating board alignment. ASO t']
  active_experiment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this listing is currently part of an active A/B test or ASO experiment. True = experiment running; False = baseline or no experiment.']
  average_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'The average user rating for the game on this storefront, typically on a 1-5 scale. Critical ASO factor influencing conversion.']
  contains_ads_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the game displays advertising to users. Required disclosure on most storefronts.']
  content_rating varchar [note: 'Age-appropriateness rating assigned by the relevant rating board (ESRB, PEGI, USK, CERO, GRAC) based on game content. Format varies by region and platform.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ASO listing record was first created in the system.']
  current_version varchar [note: 'The current published version number of the game on this storefront. Follows platform-specific versioning conventions (e.g., semantic versioning).']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'The data classification level for this ASO listing record. Public = publicly visible store data; Internal = internal marketing data; Confidential = competitive strategy data; Restricted = highly sensitive business data.']
  experiment_end_date date [note: 'The date when the ASO experiment or A/B test concluded for this listing variant. Null if experiment is ongoing or not applicable.']
  experiment_start_date date [note: 'The date when the ASO experiment or A/B test began for this listing variant. Null if not part of an experiment.']
  experiment_variant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific A/B test variant this listing represents (e.g., control, variant_a, variant_b). Null if not part of an experiment.']
  feature_graphic_reference varchar [note: 'Asset reference or URL for the primary feature graphic or hero image used in store promotions and featured placements. Platform-specific dimension requirements apply.']
  historical_conversion_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The historical store page visit-to-install conversion rate for this listing, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1250 = 12.50%). Used to measure ASO effectiveness and compare experiment variants.']
  impression_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this store listing was displayed in search results or browse views. Key metric for ASO visibility tracking.']
  in_app_purchase_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the game offers in-app purchases (IAP) or microtransactions (MTX). Required disclosure on most storefronts.']
  install_count varchar [note: 'Total number of installs attributed to this store listing. Used to calculate conversion rate from page views.']
  keyword_set varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of keywords and phrases used for App Store Optimization (ASO) to improve search discoverability. Platform-specific keyword strategies apply.']
  last_metadata_update_date date [note: 'The date when the store listing metadata (title, description, keywords, screenshots) was last updated. Distinct from version release date.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ASO listing record was last modified in the system. Tracks metadata changes for audit and version control.']
  listing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the store listing. Draft = being prepared; Pending Review = submitted to platform for approval; Live = published and discoverable; Suspended = temporarily removed; Delisted = permanently removed; Archived = historical record.']
  listing_title varchar [note: 'The display title of the game as it appears on the storefront. Optimized for discoverability and conversion.']
  localization_locale varchar [note: 'The language and regional locale for this listing variant (e.g., en-US, ja-JP, de-DE, fr-FR). Each locale may have distinct metadata for ASO optimization.']
  long_description varchar [note: 'Full detailed description of the game including features, gameplay, story, and selling points. Displayed on the full product page.']
  marketing_url varchar [note: 'URL to the games official marketing website or landing page. Used for additional promotional information and community links.']
  monetization_model varchar [note: 'The primary monetization strategy for the game. Free-to-Play (F2P) = free download with in-app purchases (IAP); Premium = upfront purchase; Freemium = free with optional purchases; Subscription = recurring payment; Ad-Supported = free with advertising.']
  page_view_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times users viewed the full product page for this listing. Used to calculate click-through rate (CTR) from impressions.']
  price_tier varchar [note: 'The pricing tier or model for the game on this storefront (e.g., free, $0.99, $4.99, $9.99, premium). Platform-specific pricing tiers apply.']
  primary_category varchar [note: 'The main category classification for the game on the storefront (e.g., Action, Strategy, RPG, Puzzle). Platform-specific category taxonomies apply.']
  privacy_policy_url varchar [note: 'URL to the games privacy policy. Required by GDPR, COPPA, and platform policies for games that collect user data.']
  promo_video_reference varchar [note: 'Asset reference or URL for the promotional video or app preview displayed on the store listing. Platform-specific format and duration requirements apply.']
  review_count varchar [note: 'Total number of user reviews submitted for the game on this storefront. Review volume impacts social proof and conversion.']
  screenshot_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of asset references or URLs pointing to the promotional screenshots displayed on the store listing. Each platform has specific dimension and count requirements.']
  secondary_category varchar [note: 'Optional secondary category classification for the game. Not all platforms support secondary categories.']
  short_description varchar [note: 'Brief promotional description of the game, typically displayed in search results and browse views. Character limits vary by platform.']
  storefront_platform varchar [note: 'The digital storefront platform where this listing is published (Apple App Store, Google Play, Steam, Epic Games Store, Microsoft Store, PlayStation Store).']
  support_url varchar [note: 'URL to the player support or customer service page for the game. Required by most storefronts.']
  version_release_date date [note: 'The date when the current version was released or went live on the storefront.']

  Note: 'Master record for App Store Optimization metadata and experimentation per game title per storefront (Apple App Store, Google Play, Steam, Epic Games Store). Captures store listing title, short/long descriptions, keyword set, primary/secondary category, content rating, screenshots and feature graphic references, localization locale, current version, last updated date, ASO owner, active experiment flag, and historical conversion rate. Supports A/B test variant tracking for store listing optimization. Single source of truth for live store listing metadata managed by the marketing team.'
}

Table marketing.influencer {
  influencer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the influencer or content creator partner. Primary key for the influencer master record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing team member responsible for managing the relationship with this influencer, coordinating campaigns, and handling contract renewals.']
  affiliate_code varchar [note: 'The unique affiliate tracking code or referral code assigned to the influencer for tracking sales, installs, and conversions driven by their promotional content. Used in AppsFlyer/Adjust attribution and revenue-share calculations.']
  affiliate_program_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the influencer is enrolled in the company\'s affiliate marketing program, earning commission on game sales or in-app purchases driven through their unique affiliate links. True means enrolled; False means not enrolled.']
  audience_age_group varchar [note: 'The primary age demographic of the influencers audience. Critical for ESRB/PEGI compliance, COPPA compliance, and ensuring age-appropriate game promotion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: under-13|13-17|18-24|25-34|35-44|45-plus|mixed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  audience_gender_split varchar [note: 'The gender distribution of the influencers audience, typically expressed as a percentage (e.g., 60% male, 40% female). Used for targeted campaign planning and demographic alignment with game titles.']
  average_views_per_post varchar [note: 'The average number of views or impressions the influencer generates per content post on their primary platform. Used for campaign reach estimation and Cost Per Mille (CPM) calculations.']
  content_category varchar [note: 'The primary game genre or content category that the influencer focuses on. Used to match influencers with relevant game titles and campaigns. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FPS|RPG|mobile|esports|strategy|casual|indie|AAA|multi-genre — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  content_rating_suitability varchar [note: 'The ESRB content rating level that best matches the influencers typical content and audience (E=Everyone, E10+=Everyone 10+, T=Teen, M=Mature, AO=Adults Only, RP=Rating Pending). Used to ensure appropriate game-influencer matching.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'The date when the current influencer partnership contract expires. Nullable for open-ended or evergreen agreements. Used for renewal planning and budget forecasting.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'The date when the current influencer partnership contract became effective. Used for contract lifecycle management and renewal planning.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the influencers contractual relationship with the company. Active indicates an ongoing partnership; pending indicates negotiations in progress; expired or terminated indicates no current agreement.']
  coppa_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the influencer has a significant audience under 13 years of age, requiring COPPA (Children\'s Online Privacy Protection Act) compliance measures in campaigns. True means COPPA compliance required; False means standard compliance.']
  cpi_benchmark decimal(10,2) [note: 'The historical average Cost Per Install (CPI) achieved through campaigns with this influencer. Used for campaign planning, budget allocation, and influencer performance comparison.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this influencer record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and partnership lifecycle analysis.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the rate card amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency contract management and financial reporting.']
  engagement_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The average engagement rate (likes, comments, shares divided by followers) expressed as a percentage. Key metric for evaluating influencer effectiveness and campaign ROI.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the influencer is under an exclusive agreement that prohibits them from promoting competing game titles or publishers. True means exclusive partnership; False means non-exclusive.']
  follower_count varchar [note: 'The current number of followers or subscribers on the influencers primary platform. Updated periodically for campaign planning and performance tracking.']
  follower_count_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the influencer based on follower count: nano (<10K), micro (10K-100K), mid-tier (100K-500K), macro (500K-1M), mega (>1M). Used for campaign targeting and rate negotiation.']
  handle varchar [note: 'The public username or handle of the influencer on their primary platform (e.g., @gamerpro123). This is the primary business identifier used in campaigns and contracts.']
  language varchar [note: 'The primary language used by the influencer in their content (e.g., English, Spanish, Japanese). Uses ISO 639-1 two-letter codes or full language names.']
  last_campaign_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent marketing campaign or content collaboration with this influencer. Used for relationship health monitoring and re-engagement planning.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this influencer record was last updated. Used for data freshness monitoring, audit trails, and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for relationship managers to capture important notes about the influencer, including preferences, past issues, special requirements, or strategic considerations for future campaigns.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'The Net Promoter Score (NPS) rating provided by the influencer regarding their satisfaction with the partnership, on a scale of 0-10. Used to measure influencer satisfaction and identify partnership improvement opportunities.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The payment structure agreed upon in the influencer contract (e.g., per-post fee, per-campaign fee, monthly retainer, revenue-share, or hybrid model). Used for budget planning and payment processing.']
  preferred_contact_email varchar [note: 'The primary email address for business communications, contract negotiations, and campaign coordination with the influencer.']
  preferred_contact_phone varchar [note: 'The primary phone number for urgent communications with the influencer or their management team.']
  primary_platform varchar [note: 'The primary social media or streaming platform where the influencer has the largest following and engagement.']
  rate_card_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The standard rate or fee paid to the influencer per the agreed payment terms (e.g., per post, per campaign, or monthly retainer). Stored in the company\'s base currency (USD). Used for campaign budgeting and ROI analysis.']
  real_name varchar [note: 'The legal or real name of the influencer for contract and payment purposes.']
  region varchar [note: 'The primary geographic region or country where the influencer is based and has their largest audience (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN). Uses 3-letter ISO country codes.']
  roas_benchmark decimal(10,2) [note: 'The historical average Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) achieved through campaigns with this influencer, expressed as a ratio (e.g., 3.5 means $3.50 revenue per $1 spent). Used for influencer selection and campaign optimization.']
  total_campaigns_completed varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of marketing campaigns or content collaborations completed with this influencer since the partnership began. Used for partnership value assessment and performance trending.']

  Note: 'Master record for influencer and content creator partners engaged in game marketing programs. Captures influencer handle, real name, primary platform (YouTube/Twitch/TikTok/Instagram), follower count tier, content category (FPS/RPG/mobile/esports), region, language, engagement rate, preferred contact, contract status, exclusivity flag, COPPA compliance flag (for creators with under-18 audiences), and relationship manager. Owned by marketing as the authoritative influencer roster.'
}

Table marketing.influencer_campaign {
  influencer_campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the influencer campaign association record.']
  brand_partnership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.brand_partnership. Business justification: Influencer activations for brand partnership games must track partnership agreement for co-marketing obligations, content approval workflows, brand usage guidelines, exclusivity terms, and performance']
  influencer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the influencer or affiliate partner engaged in this campaign.']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign this influencer engagement is part of.']
  offer_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.offer_campaign. Business justification: Influencer activations promote specific in-game offers with unique promo codes. Linking enables attribution of influencer-driven monetization, measurement of offer redemption rates by influencer, and ']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.tournament. Business justification: Influencers are frequently activated for tournament-specific coverage, watch parties, and co-streaming. Tournament context is essential for content rights management, performance tracking, and influen']
  activation_type varchar [note: 'Type of influencer engagement or content activation (e.g., sponsored video, stream, post, review, affiliate referral, brand ambassador program).']
  agreed_deliverables varchar [note: 'Description of the contracted deliverables the influencer agreed to produce (e.g., 3 videos, 5 social posts, 2 live streams).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the influencer content was approved by the marketing team.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Content approval status by the marketing team (pending review, approved, rejected, revision requested).']
  attributed_installs varchar [note: 'Total number of game installs attributed to this influencer campaign engagement.']
  attributed_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total revenue attributed to players acquired through this influencer campaign engagement.']
  compensation_model varchar [note: 'Payment structure for the influencer engagement: flat fee, Cost Per Install (CPI), Cost Per Acquisition (CPA), revenue share, or hybrid model.']
  content_go_live_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date when the influencer content was published or went live.']
  content_platform varchar [note: 'Primary social media or streaming platform where the influencer content was published (YouTube, Twitch, Instagram, TikTok, Twitter, Facebook, Discord). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: youtube|twitch|instagram|tiktok|twitter|facebook|discord — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  content_url varchar [note: 'Direct URL to the published influencer content (video, post, stream recording).']
  contract_date date [note: 'Date when the influencer engagement contract was signed or agreed upon.']
  contracted_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed fee amount contracted for the influencer engagement, if applicable.']
  cookie_window_days varchar [note: 'Attribution window in days for tracking installs or conversions back to the influencer engagement.']
  cpa_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Cost per acquisition rate agreed for performance-based compensation, if applicable.']
  cpi_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Cost per install rate agreed for performance-based compensation, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this influencer campaign engagement record was first created in the system.']
  ctr decimal(5,4) [note: 'Click-through rate for the influencer content, calculated as clicks divided by impressions.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this engagement.']
  engagement_count varchar [note: 'Total engagement actions (likes, comments, shares, clicks) on the influencer content.']
  engagement_end_date date [note: 'End date of the influencer campaign engagement period.']
  engagement_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the influencer campaign engagement period.']
  engagement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the influencer campaign engagement (draft, contracted, active, completed, cancelled, suspended).']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the influencer has an exclusivity agreement preventing promotion of competing games during the engagement period.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this influencer campaign engagement record was last updated.']
  last_payout_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment made to the influencer for this engagement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments about the influencer campaign engagement, including special terms or performance observations.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status for the influencer compensation (pending, scheduled, paid, partially paid, disputed, cancelled).']
  payout_schedule varchar [note: 'Description of the payment schedule or milestones for influencer compensation (e.g., 50% upfront, 50% on completion).']
  performance_vs_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall performance percentage against contracted KPIs, calculated as actual divided by target.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of attributed revenue shared with the influencer under revenue-share compensation model.']
  target_engagement_count varchar [note: 'Contracted target number of engagement actions (likes, comments, shares) the influencer is expected to deliver.']
  target_installs varchar [note: 'Contracted target number of installs the influencer is expected to deliver.']
  target_views varchar [note: 'Contracted target number of views or impressions the influencer is expected to deliver.']
  total_payout_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount paid or payable to the influencer for this campaign engagement.']
  tracked_link_url varchar [note: 'Unique tracking URL or deep link provided to the influencer for attribution of installs and conversions.']
  views_delivered varchar [note: 'Total number of views or impressions delivered by the influencer content.']

  Note: 'Association and activation record linking an influencer or affiliate partner to a specific marketing campaign engagement. Captures influencer reference, campaign reference, activation type (sponsored video/stream/post/review/affiliate-referral), agreed deliverables, content go-live date, contracted fee or commission structure (CPI/CPA/revenue-share with rate and cookie window), payment status, tracked link or promo code, attributed installs, attributed revenue, views/impressions delivered, engagement count, payout schedule, and performance vs. contracted KPIs. Enables influencer ROI measurement, affiliate payout tracking, and partner program management.'
}

Table marketing.crm_campaign {
  crm_campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the CRM lifecycle marketing campaign record. Primary key.']
  ab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ab_experiment. Business justification: CRM lifecycle campaigns frequently A/B test messaging, timing, and incentives. Links campaign execution to experiment registry for measuring lift in retention, conversion, or re-engagement by variant.']
