  consent_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consent_policy. Business justification: CRM campaigns must enforce applicable consent policies before sending communications. GDPR/CCPA operational requirement for lawful marketing communications and consent withdrawal processing.']
  dlc_bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.dlc_bundle. Business justification: CRM campaigns target players for specific DLC purchases ("You haven\'t bought the Season Pass yet"). Lifecycle marketing drives DLC attach rate through personalized offers. Segmentation based on DLC ow']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing team member or user responsible for creating, managing, and monitoring this campaign.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game title this campaign promotes or relates to. Null for cross-title or brand-level campaigns.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.ip_agreement. Business justification: CRM campaigns promoting licensed IP titles need agreement reference for content approval, territorial distribution restrictions, trademark usage guidelines, and co-marketing obligation compliance. Lif']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Every CRM campaign defines a primary success metric (open rate, click-through, D7 retention lift). Links campaign to KPI registry for standardized measurement and cross-campaign performance comparison']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.league. Business justification: CRM lifecycle campaigns target league-specific fan segments (e.g., LCS viewer re-engagement, Worlds hype campaigns). League context enables personalized messaging, viewing reminders, and fan retention']
  message_template_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the message template or creative asset used for this campaign, stored in the CRM or marketing automation system.']
  offer_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.offer_campaign. Business justification: CRM campaigns (email, push notifications) promote specific in-game offers to drive conversion and re-engagement. Linking enables cross-channel attribution, measurement of CRM-driven monetization lift,']
  player_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player segment this campaign targets, defined by behavioral, demographic, or engagement criteria in the CRM system.']
  suppression_list_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the suppression list containing players who should be excluded from this campaign due to opt-out, frequency capping, or other business rules.']
  title_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.title_season. Business justification: CRM campaigns drive engagement for active seasons ("Season 5 ends in 3 days - claim rewards"). Retention marketing tied to seasonal content cadence. Re-engagement campaigns target lapsed players per s']
  ab_test_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this campaign includes A/B testing of message variants, subject lines, or creative assets.']
  ab_test_split_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of target audience allocated to each test variant. For example, 50.00 for a 50/50 split. Null if A/B testing is not enabled.']
  ab_test_variant_count varchar [note: 'Number of test variants in the A/B test configuration. Null if A/B testing is not enabled.']
  actual_send_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual timestamp when the campaign was executed and messages were sent. May differ from scheduled time due to system delays or manual intervention.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign was approved for execution by the campaign owner or marketing manager. Null if not yet approved.']
  campaign_budget_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Allocated budget for this campaign in USD, including creative production, platform fees, and incentive costs. Null for zero-cost campaigns.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code for the campaign used in external systems and tracking URLs.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the CRM lifecycle campaign for internal identification and reporting.']
  campaign_objective varchar [note: 'Primary business objective of the campaign: retention (keep active players), reactivation (bring back churned players), upsell (increase spending), cross_sell (promote other titles), engagement (increase activity), or education (onboard/inform).']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the campaign in its lifecycle: draft (being created), scheduled (ready to launch), active (currently running), paused (temporarily stopped), completed (finished), or cancelled (terminated).']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Execution pattern of the campaign: one_time (single send), recurring (scheduled repeats), triggered (event-based), or drip (multi-step sequence).']
  communication_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel through which campaign messages are delivered to players: email, push notification, SMS, in-game message, or web notification.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign execution was completed and all messages were sent. Null if campaign is not yet completed.']
  coppa_age_gate_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether COPPA age verification is required before sending campaign messages. True when targeting players who may be under 13 years old in the United States.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this campaign record was first created in the CRM system.']
  expected_send_volume varchar [note: 'Expected number of messages to be sent after applying suppression lists, consent filters, and other exclusion rules.']
  frequency_cap_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether frequency capping rules are applied to prevent over-messaging players within a defined time window.']
  frequency_cap_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of campaign messages a player can receive within the frequency cap window. Null if frequency capping is not enabled.']
  frequency_cap_window_days varchar [note: 'Time window in days over which the frequency cap limit is enforced. Null if frequency capping is not enabled.']
  gdpr_consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether explicit GDPR consent is required from players before sending campaign messages. True for EU/EEA players under GDPR jurisdiction.']
  incentive_type varchar [note: 'Type of incentive or reward offered in the campaign to drive player action: none, virtual currency, discount code, free in-game item, battle pass tier boost, or exclusive content unlock.']
  incentive_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Specific value or description of the incentive offered, such as amount of virtual currency, discount percentage, or item SKU. Null if no incentive.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'The player lifecycle stage this campaign targets: onboarding (new players), active (engaged players), at_risk (declining engagement), churned (stopped playing), dormant (inactive), or win_back (re-engagement).']
  localization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether campaign messages are localized into multiple languages based on player locale or region.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this campaign record was last modified in the CRM system.']
  personalization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether campaign messages include personalized content based on player data such as name, game progress, or preferences.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority of the campaign used for resource allocation and frequency capping decisions: low, medium, high, or critical.']
  recurrence_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which a recurring campaign is executed: daily, weekly, biweekly, monthly, or quarterly. Null for non-recurring campaigns.']
  scheduled_send_date date [note: 'The date when the campaign is scheduled to be sent or activated. For recurring campaigns, this represents the initial send date.']
  scheduled_send_time timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the campaign messages are scheduled to be sent, including time zone considerations for global player base.']
  supported_locales varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of locale codes (e.g., en_US, fr_FR, ja_JP) for which localized campaign content is available. Null if localization is not enabled.']
  target_audience_size varchar [note: 'Total number of players in the target segment eligible to receive this campaign at the time of campaign creation.']
  trigger_event_type varchar [note: 'The player behavior or system event that triggers this campaign for event-driven campaigns, such as \'player_inactive_7_days\', \'first_purchase\', or \'level_milestone\'. Null for non-triggered campaigns.']
  utm_campaign varchar [note: 'UTM campaign parameter for tracking, typically set to the campaign code or name for attribution in web analytics.']
  utm_medium varchar [note: 'UTM medium parameter for tracking campaign traffic, typically set to the communication channel such as \'email\', \'push\', or \'sms\'.']
  utm_source varchar [note: 'UTM source parameter for tracking campaign traffic in analytics platforms, typically set to \'crm\' or \'lifecycle_email\'.']

  Note: 'Master record for CRM-driven lifecycle marketing campaigns executed via Salesforce CRM targeting existing players for retention, re-engagement, upsell, or win-back. Captures campaign name, lifecycle stage (onboarding/active/at-risk/churned), target segment, communication channel (email/push/SMS/in-game), message template reference, send schedule, A/B test configuration, suppression list reference, GDPR consent requirement flag, COPPA age-gate flag, and campaign owner. Distinct from paid UA campaigns.'
}

Table marketing.player_segment {
  player_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the player segment. Primary key.']
  consent_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consent_policy. Business justification: Marketing segments must respect consent policy boundaries for lawful processing. Privacy-by-design requirement ensuring segments only include players with appropriate consent for intended use.']
  player_behavior_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.player_behavior_segment. Business justification: Marketing segments increasingly incorporate ML behavior scores for enrichment (e.g., rule-based segment + churn propensity). Enables hybrid segmentation where marketing rules are augmented with analyt']
  processing_activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.processing_activity. Business justification: Marketing segments represent data processing activities that must be documented in GDPR Article 30 records of processing activities. Required for regulatory audits and privacy impact assessments.']
  activated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment was first activated for operational use in campaigns or targeting. Marks the transition from draft to active status.']
  activation_channels varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of channels where this segment is activated: paid_ua (user acquisition ads), crm_email, crm_push, retargeting, lookalike, in_game_offers. Tracks operational usage.']
  actual_segment_size varchar [note: 'Exact count of unique players in the segment after last materialization. Reflects the precise audience available for activation.']
  att_opt_in_requirement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment requires iOS ATT opt-in consent for player inclusion. Critical for iOS 14.5+ attribution and targeting compliance.']
  average_arppu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average revenue per paying user for players in this segment. Calculated as total revenue divided by paying users only. Indicates monetization efficiency among payers.']
  average_arpu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average revenue per user for players in this segment. Calculated as total revenue divided by total users. Used for monetization benchmarking.']
  average_session_length_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average session length in minutes for players in this segment. Indicates engagement depth and informs content and monetization strategies.']
  churn_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average churn risk score (0.00 to 100.00) for players in this segment, if applicable. Higher scores indicate higher likelihood of player churn. Used for retention campaign prioritization.']
  conversion_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage (0.00 to 100.00) of players in this segment who have made at least one in-app purchase. Measures free-to-paid conversion effectiveness.']
  coppa_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether players under 13 (or jurisdiction-specific age threshold) are excluded from this segment to comply with COPPA and similar child privacy regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and segment lifecycle tracking.']
  d1_retention_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Day 1 retention rate percentage (0.00 to 100.00) for players in this segment. Measures the percentage of players who return the day after first session.']
  d30_retention_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Day 30 retention rate percentage (0.00 to 100.00) for players in this segment. Measures the percentage of players who return thirty days after first session.']
  d7_retention_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Day 7 retention rate percentage (0.00 to 100.00) for players in this segment. Measures the percentage of players who return seven days after first session.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Primary data source used to build the segment: attribution (AppsFlyer/Adjust), CRM (Salesforce), in-game events (GameAnalytics/Amplitude), analytics (BI tools), data warehouse (aggregated), or third-party (external data provider).']
  definition_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rule or SQL-like expression defining segment membership criteria. For rule-based segments, contains the logical conditions; for ML-based segments, references the model identifier and feature set.']
  definition_type varchar [note: 'Method used to define segment membership: rule-based (SQL/business logic), ML model (predictive algorithm), hybrid (rules + ML), or manual upload (curated list).']
  deprecated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment was deprecated or archived. Nullable for active segments. Used for historical analysis and segment lifecycle management.']
  estimated_segment_size varchar [note: 'Approximate count of unique players currently matching the segment criteria at last refresh. Used for campaign planning and audience sizing.']
  game_title_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of game titles or game IDs this segment applies to. Supports cross-title or single-title segmentation strategies.']
  gdpr_lawful_basis varchar [note: 'GDPR Article 6 lawful basis for processing player data in this segment: consent, legitimate interest, contract, legal obligation, vital interest, public task, or not applicable (non-EU players). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: consent|legitimate_interest|contract|legal_obligation|vital_interest|public_task|not_applicable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes defining the geographic scope of the segment (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN). Empty if global.']
  last_refresh_date date [note: 'Date when the segment membership was last recalculated and refreshed. Critical for ensuring targeting accuracy and data freshness.']
  lookalike_seed_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is approved for use as a seed audience in lookalike modeling for paid user acquisition campaigns.']
  ltv_tier varchar [note: 'Lifetime value tier classification for players in this segment: whale (top spenders), high, medium, low, or unknown. Supports value-based targeting strategies.']
  ml_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the ML model used for segment generation, if applicable. Links to model registry for versioning and reproducibility.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was last modified. Tracks changes to segment definition, criteria, or metadata.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form operational notes, change history, or special instructions related to this segment. Used for team collaboration and knowledge transfer.']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Name of the marketing team or business unit responsible for maintaining and activating this segment (e.g., User Acquisition, Lifecycle Marketing, Retention).']
  owning_team_contact varchar [note: 'Primary email contact for the team responsible for this segment. Used for operational coordination and issue escalation.']
  pci_dss_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment involves payment card data and must comply with PCI DSS requirements. Typically true for payer and whale segments.']
  platform_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of platforms this segment applies to: mobile, console, PC, web. Enables platform-specific targeting strategies.']
  player_segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the segment purpose, targeting strategy, and intended use cases. Provides context for marketing teams and stakeholders.']
  refresh_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which the segment is recalculated: real-time (streaming), hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, or on-demand (manual trigger).']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the segment used in ad platform integrations and CRM systems.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the player segment used for operational targeting and campaign identification.']
  segment_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for segment activation when a player qualifies for multiple segments. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for campaign orchestration.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment: active (in use for targeting), inactive (paused), draft (under development), archived (historical reference), or deprecated (replaced by newer segment).']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segment methodology: behavioral (in-game actions), demographic (age/geo), LTV tier (lifetime value bands), churn risk (propensity to leave), genre affinity (preferred game types), whale (high spenders), payer propensity (likelihood to convert), retention cohort (D1/D7/D30 groups), engagement tier (DAU/MAU patterns), or platform preference (mobile/console/PC). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: behavioral|demographic|ltv_tier|churn_risk|genre_affinity|whale|payer_propensity|retention_cohort|engagement_tier|platform_preference — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  suppression_list_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is used as a suppression list to exclude players from targeting (e.g., churned players, high-value players to avoid cannibalization).']

  Note: 'Marketing-owned player segmentation master record defining audience segments used operationally for paid UA lookalike seed audiences, CRM lifecycle campaign targeting, and retargeting suppression lists. Captures segment name, segment type (behavioral/demographic/LTV-tier/churn-risk/genre-affinity/whale/payer-propensity), definition criteria (rule-based or ML-model reference), estimated segment size, last refresh date, data source (attribution/CRM/in-game-events), platform scope, GDPR lawful basis for processing, COPPA exclusion flag, ATT opt-in requirement, and owning marketing team. This is the operational targeting segment registry — segments here are activated in ad platforms and CRM tools. Distinct from player domain\'s identity-resolution segments and analytics domain\'s exploratory behavioral cohorts.'
}

Table marketing.launch_event {
  launch_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the launch event record. Primary key.']
  capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_project. Business justification: Major game launches link to underlying capex projects for ROI analysis and capitalization decisions. Finance tracks launch marketing spend against the capex project to calculate total project cost and']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title being launched. Links to the game title master record in the Game Title domain.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.ip_agreement. Business justification: Game launches using licensed IP must track governing agreement for rating board submission requirements, territorial launch rights, marketing material approvals, press embargo terms, and brand usage g']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Launch events define upfront success criteria (D1 retention target, D7 ARPU goal). Links launch planning to KPI registry for post-launch evaluation and success/failure determination against pre-define']
  network_region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.network_region. Business justification: Launch events are region-specific (target_regions attribute exists). Infrastructure must be provisioned in those regions before launch. Linking to network_region enables launch readiness validation, e']
  primary_launch_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or team lead responsible for coordinating this launch event across marketing, studio, platform, and community domains. Links to workforce or employee master data.']
  release_gate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.release_gate. Business justification: Launch events are gated by release gate approvals. Launch managers verify gate status (TRC compliance, performance benchmarks, defect counts) before committing marketing spend and scheduling launch ac']
  server_fleet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.server_fleet. Business justification: Game launches require dedicated infrastructure provisioning. Launch managers coordinate with infrastructure teams to ensure fleet capacity matches day_one_install_target and can sustain projected CCU.']
  tertiary_launch_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that last modified this launch event record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  actual_launch_date date [note: 'The actual date the launch event went live. May differ from scheduled date due to delays, early releases, or other factors. Null if launch has not yet occurred.']
  actual_spend_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual marketing spend incurred for this launch event in US Dollars. Tracked against budget to monitor cost efficiency and calculate final ROAS.']
  aso_optimization_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score (0-100) representing the quality and completeness of ASO metadata (title, description, keywords, screenshots, videos, ratings) at launch. Higher scores correlate with better organic discoverability.']
  average_cpi_usd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average cost per install across all launch campaigns, calculated as total spend divided by total installs. Key efficiency metric for user acquisition performance.']
  average_ctr_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average click-through rate across all launch campaign creatives, expressed as a percentage. Measures ad creative effectiveness and audience engagement.']
  coppa_compliance_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether COPPA compliance requirements (parental consent, data minimization for users under 13) have been verified for US launch. Required for games targeting or accessible to children.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this launch event record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  day_one_actual_installs varchar [note: 'Actual number of installs recorded on the first day of launch. Compared against target to assess launch performance. Sourced from AppsFlyer/Adjust and platform analytics.']
  day_one_install_target varchar [note: 'Marketing and studio target for the number of installs to achieve on the first day of launch. Key performance indicator (KPI) for launch success.']
  day_seven_actual_retention_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual percentage of Day 1 players who returned on Day 7 post-launch. Null until D7 data is available. Used to assess FTUE (First-Time User Experience) effectiveness and early game engagement.']
  day_seven_retention_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of Day 1 players expected to return on Day 7 post-launch. Critical retention KPI for evaluating launch quality and player engagement.']
  esrb_rating varchar [note: 'ESRB content rating assigned to the game for North American markets. Impacts marketing messaging, target audience, and platform certification requirements.']
  gdpr_compliance_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether GDPR compliance requirements (player data consent, privacy policy, data subject rights) have been verified for EU launch regions. Required for launches targeting European markets.']
  influencer_embargo_lift_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when influencers and content creators are permitted to publish gameplay videos, streams, and sponsored content. May differ from press embargo to manage launch narrative.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this launch event record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record for audit and synchronization purposes.']
  launch_budget_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total marketing budget allocated for this launch event in US Dollars. Includes paid user acquisition, influencer partnerships, PR, events, and creative production. Used for ROAS (Return on Ad Spend) calculation.']
  launch_campaign_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of marketing campaign identifiers associated with this launch event. Links to campaign performance records in Salesforce CRM and attribution platforms for CPI, CTR, CPM, eCPM, and ROAS analysis.']
  launch_event_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the launch event, used for external reference and cross-functional coordination (e.g., APEX_SOFT_LAUNCH_Q1_2024).']
  launch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the launch event. Tracks progression from planning through execution to completion or cancellation.']
  launch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the launch event type. Soft launch targets limited markets for testing; regional launch covers specific geographies; hard launch is full global release; major update launch is for significant DLC or expansion; beta and early access are pre-release phases.']
  pegi_rating varchar [note: 'PEGI content rating assigned to the game for European markets. Mandatory for distribution in EU and impacts marketing compliance under European consumer protection regulations.']
  platform_certification_status varchar [note: 'Aggregate status of platform certification submissions (TRC/TCR compliance) across all target platforms. Must be approved before hard launch can proceed.']
  post_launch_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of the post-launch retrospective and performance review process. Completed reviews capture lessons learned, KPI achievement, and recommendations for future launches.']
  post_launch_review_summary varchar [note: 'Executive summary of the post-launch review, including key successes, challenges, KPI performance vs. targets, and strategic recommendations. Captured for organizational learning and future launch planning.']
  pre_registration_count varchar [note: 'Total number of players who pre-registered for the game prior to launch. Used to gauge initial interest and forecast Day 1 (D1) installs. Sourced from platform SDKs and marketing attribution systems.']
  press_embargo_lift_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when press and media outlets are permitted to publish reviews, previews, and coverage of the game. Critical for coordinating PR and marketing efforts.']
  projected_d30_ltv_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Projected lifetime value per user at Day 30 post-launch, based on early monetization data and cohort analysis. Used to assess launch cohort quality and long-term revenue potential.']
  scheduled_launch_date date [note: 'Planned date for the launch event to go live. This is the target date communicated to stakeholders and used for campaign planning.']
  store_featuring_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether and how the game is featured on digital storefronts (Steam, Epic Games Store, App Store, Google Play, console stores) at launch. Featured placement significantly impacts discoverability and install volume.']
  target_platforms varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of platform identifiers where the game will be available at launch (e.g., PC_STEAM,PC_EPIC,PS5,XBOX_SERIES_X,IOS,ANDROID,SWITCH). Aligns with platform certification requirements.']
  target_regions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of 3-letter ISO country codes representing the geographic markets targeted for this launch (e.g., USA,CAN,GBR,DEU,JPN). For global launches, may contain GLOBAL keyword.']

  Note: 'Master record for each game title\'s marketing launch event — soft launch, regional launch, hard launch, or major update launch. Captures game title reference, launch type, target regions, launch date, pre-registration count, day-one install target, actual day-one installs, launch campaign references, press embargo lift date, influencer embargo lift date, store featuring status, and post-launch review. Enables launch performance tracking and cross-functional coordination between marketing, studio, and platform domains.'
}

Table marketing.ad_network {
  ad_network_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the advertising network or media source. Primary key.']
  api_key_encrypted varchar [note: 'Encrypted API key or authentication token used for programmatic integration with the networks reporting and campaign management APIs. Stored in encrypted format for security.']
  attribution_window_click_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after a click during which an install or conversion event can be attributed to this network. Standard values range from 1 to 30 days.']
  attribution_window_impression_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after an ad impression during which an install or conversion event can be attributed to this network. Typically shorter than click window (1-7 days).']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Expiration or renewal date of the current contractual agreement with the network. Null for evergreen agreements.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the current contractual agreement or insertion order with the network.']
  coppa_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the network is certified compliant with COPPA requirements for advertising to children under 13 in the United States.']
  cost_reporting_method varchar [note: 'Primary granularity at which the network reports cost data for spend reconciliation. Impression = CPM model; Click = CPC model; Install = CPI model; Event = CPA/CPE model; None = no automated cost reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this network record was first created in the data product. Audit trail field.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the networks primary billing currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  data_sharing_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the legal data sharing agreement between the business and the network, governing the exchange of attribution and cost data.']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'Date when the network was deprecated or removed from active use. Null if network is still active.']
  fraud_protection_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the networks fraud prevention and validation capabilities. Certified = MMP-certified fraud protection; Standard = basic validation; Unverified = no formal fraud protection.']
  gdpr_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the network has certified GDPR compliance for handling player data in European Union jurisdictions.']
  integration_type varchar [note: 'Technical integration method used to connect the network with the MMP attribution stack. SDK = Software Development Kit embedded in app; S2S = Server-to-Server postback; API = Application Programming Interface; Manual = manual upload.']
  is_privacy_sandbox_compatible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the network supports Googles Privacy Sandbox attribution APIs for privacy-preserving measurement on Android.']
  is_san boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the network is a Self-Attributing Network that reports its own attribution data rather than relying solely on MMP attribution logic (e.g., Facebook, Google, Apple Search Ads, TikTok).']
  is_skan_compatible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the network supports Apples SKAdNetwork framework for privacy-preserving attribution on iOS 14.5+.']
  last_campaign_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent campaign activity or spend recorded for this network. Used to identify inactive networks.']
  monthly_spend_cap_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum monthly advertising spend budget allocated to this network in US Dollars. Used for budget pacing and alerts.']
  network_code varchar [note: 'Standardized network identifier code registered in the MMP system (AppsFlyer or Adjust) for attribution and reporting purposes.']
  network_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the advertising network or media source (e.g., Meta Ads, Google UAC, Apple Search Ads, ironSource, Unity Ads, AppLovin, TikTok Ads).']
  network_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the network in the marketing attribution stack. Active = live and running campaigns; Paused = temporarily disabled; Deprecated = phased out; Onboarding = integration in progress; Suspended = blocked due to fraud or policy violation.']
  network_tier varchar [note: 'Internal classification of the networks strategic importance and spend volume. Tier 1 = top strategic partners with highest spend; Tier 2 = secondary partners; Tier 3 = tactical/test partners; Experimental = trial/pilot networks.']
  network_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the advertising network by primary ad format or channel type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: display|video|search|social|affiliate|influencer|ctv_ott|dsp|exchange|rewarded|playable — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special configuration details, partnership notes, or operational reminders related to this network.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the network was first integrated and configured in the MMP attribution stack.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after invoice date that payment is due to the network (e.g., Net 30, Net 60).']
  postback_url varchar [note: 'Server-to-server postback URL endpoint provided by the network to receive attribution and conversion event notifications from the MMP.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the account manager or technical contact at the advertising network.']
  supported_platforms varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of platforms supported by this network (e.g., iOS, Android, PC, Console, Web). Indicates where campaigns can run.']
  supports_cost_api boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the network provides an API for automated cost data ingestion into the MMP for spend reconciliation.']
  supports_deep_linking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the network supports deep linking to specific in-app content or experiences from ad creative.']
  supports_retargeting boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the network supports retargeting campaigns to re-engage existing players or lapsed users.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this network record was last modified. Audit trail field.']

  Note: 'Reference master for advertising networks and media sources integrated with the marketing attribution and spend stack via AppsFlyer/Adjust MMP — including Meta Ads, Google UAC, Apple Search Ads, ironSource, Unity Ads, AppLovin, TikTok Ads, CTV/OTT partners, and DSPs. Captures network name, MMP-registered network code, integration type (SDK/S2S/API), supported platforms (iOS/Android/PC/console), cost reporting method (impression/click/install), attribution window configuration per network, fraud protection tier (certified/standard/unverified), SAN (Self-Attributing Network) flag, data sharing agreement status, SKAN/Privacy Sandbox compatibility, and active status. Serves as the authoritative network dimension for campaign spend reconciliation, attribution validation, and fraud analysis. Lifecycle: networks are onboarded, configured, monitored, and occasionally deprecated as partnerships change.'
}

Table marketing.marketing_budget {
  marketing_budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing budget record. Primary key.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title this budget is allocated for.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Marketing budgets are allocated against specific KPI targets (target CPI, target ROAS, target D7 retention). Links budget planning to measurement framework for budget-to-outcome tracking and ROI analy']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Marketing budgets are approved and tracked at legal entity level for statutory reporting and consolidation. Intercompany eliminations and transfer pricing require knowing which legal entity owns each ']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign this budget allocation supports.']
  primary_marketing_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or user who owns and is accountable for this budget allocation (typically a marketing manager or campaign lead).']
  quaternary_marketing_last_modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or user who last modified this budget record.']
  tertiary_marketing_created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or user who created this budget record.']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual amount spent to date from this budget allocation. Updated in near real-time from campaign spend data.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date this budget allocation was approved by finance.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status: not_submitted (draft, not yet submitted for approval), pending (awaiting finance review), approved (approved by finance), rejected (rejected by finance), revision_requested (sent back for changes).']
  budget_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying this budget allocation in financial systems and reporting.']
  budget_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for this budget allocation (e.g., Q1 2024 Mobile UA Budget - Game X).']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget allocation: draft (being prepared), pending_approval (awaiting finance approval), approved (approved but not yet active), active (currently in use), paused (temporarily suspended), closed (completed or cancelled).']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget purpose: acquisition (player acquisition), retention (lifecycle marketing), brand (awareness campaigns), influencer (influencer/affiliate programs), aso (App Store Optimization), event (esports/live events).']
  channel varchar [note: 'The marketing channel or platform this budget is allocated to (e.g., facebook, google, apple_search_ads, tiktok, unity_ads, ironsource, snapchat, twitter, youtube, influencer, organic, cross_promotion, other). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: facebook|google|apple_search_ads|tiktok|unity_ads|ironsource|snapchat|twitter|youtube|influencer|organic|cross_promotion|other — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of budget that has been committed or reserved for planned campaigns but not yet spent (e.g., insertion orders signed but campaigns not yet launched).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The finance cost center code this budget is charged to. Links marketing budget to general ledger cost accounting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this budget record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The currency in which this budget is denominated. 3-letter ISO 4217 currency code (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter this budget allocation applies to (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4). Null if budget spans multiple quarters or is annual.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year this budget allocation applies to (e.g., 2024).']
  geo_region varchar [note: 'The geographic region or country this budget targets. Use 3-letter ISO country codes (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN) or regional codes (GLOBAL, NA, EU, APAC, LATAM, MEA).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this budget record was last modified.']
  last_revision_date date [note: 'The date this budget allocation was last revised or amended (e.g., budget increase, reallocation, or adjustment).']
  launch_type varchar [note: 'The type of launch or campaign phase this budget supports: soft_launch (limited geo test launch), hard_launch (full global launch), live_ops (ongoing live operations marketing), seasonal (seasonal event or promotion), evergreen (continuous baseline marketing).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this budget allocation. Used for context, special instructions, or coordination notes.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the budget period (inclusive). Defines when this budget allocation expires.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the budget period (inclusive). Defines when this budget allocation becomes effective.']
  platform varchar [note: 'The gaming platform this budget targets: ios, android, pc, console, web, cross_platform.']
  remaining_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining unspent budget amount. Calculated as total_approved_budget_amount minus actual_spend_amount minus committed_amount.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of the reason for the last budget revision (e.g., campaign performance exceeded expectations, reallocated from underperforming channel, additional funding approved for soft launch).']
  target_cpi decimal(10,2) [note: 'The target Cost Per Install (CPI) benchmark for this budget allocation. Used for pacing and performance evaluation.']
  target_d7_retention_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target Day 7 retention rate (percentage) for players acquired under this budget allocation. Used to evaluate cohort quality.']
  target_roas decimal(10,2) [note: 'The target Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) for this budget allocation. Expressed as a ratio (e.g., 3.5 means $3.50 revenue per $1 spent).']
  total_approved_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total budget amount approved by finance for this allocation. This is the authorized spending limit.']

  Note: 'Master record for marketing budget allocations per campaign, channel, game title, and fiscal period. Captures budget period (monthly/quarterly/annual), game title scope, channel allocation, total approved budget, committed spend, actual spend to date, remaining budget, budget owner, approval status, and last revision date. Owned by marketing as the operational budget tracking entity — distinct from the finance domain\'s general ledger cost center records. Enables real-time budget pacing against campaign spend.'
}

Table marketing.consent_record {
  consent_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing consent record. Primary key.']
  consent_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consent_policy. Business justification: Each consent record implements a specific consent policy version. Direct policy enforcement link required for consent validity verification, withdrawal processing, and regulatory audit trails.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device (mobile, console, PC) from which the consent was provided. Used for cross-device consent tracking and attribution.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game title in which the consent was collected. Enables game-specific consent tracking and reporting.']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign that triggered the consent request or collection. Null if consent was collected outside of a specific campaign context.']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player who provided or withdrew consent. Links to the player master record.']
  player_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player segment or cohort to which this player belonged at the time of consent. Enables segment-level consent analysis and reporting.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.tournament. Business justification: Consent collected during tournament registrations, watch parties, and promotional activations requires tournament context for compliance auditing, data retention policies, and GDPR/COPPA documentation']
  age_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the players age has been verified at the time of consent collection. True if age verification was completed; False otherwise. Critical for COPPA compliance.']
  attribution_source varchar [note: 'The mobile attribution platform or system that recorded the consent event. Relevant for consent collected during install attribution flows.']
  consent_collection_context varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the context or scenario in which consent was collected (e.g., first-time user experience, account settings update, post-purchase prompt). Provides qualitative insight for consent UX optimization.']
  consent_granularity_level varchar [note: 'Defines the level of granularity at which consent was collected. All-marketing is a blanket consent; category-specific allows per-category control; channel-specific allows per-channel control; partner-specific allows per-partner control.']
  consent_language varchar [note: 'The language in which the consent prompt or preference center was presented to the player. Uses ISO 639-1 two-letter language codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: en|es|fr|de|ja|ko|pt|zh|it|ru|pl|nl|sv|tr|ar — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  consent_method varchar [note: 'The user interface mechanism through which consent was captured. Explicit opt-in requires active player action; implicit opt-in assumes consent unless declined; pre-checked box is generally non-compliant under GDPR.']
  consent_proof_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or reference to stored documentation proving consent was obtained (e.g., screenshot, signed form, email confirmation). Provides audit trail for regulatory inquiries.']
  consent_renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this consent requires periodic renewal or re-confirmation. True if renewal is required; False for perpetual consent until withdrawn.']
  consent_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the scope of the consent grant. Global applies across all games and platforms; regional applies to a specific geographic region; game-specific applies to a single title; platform-specific applies to one platform.']
  consent_source varchar [note: 'The channel or interface through which the player provided or modified their consent. Tracks the origin of the consent action for audit and compliance purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in-game-prompt|web-preference-center|app-store-ATT|mobile-app-onboarding|email-link|customer-support|API — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the consent. Granted indicates active permission; withdrawn indicates player has revoked consent; expired indicates consent has passed its validity period; pending indicates consent request awaiting player action; revoked indicates administratively cancelled consent.']
  consent_text_snapshot varchar [note: 'A snapshot or reference to the exact consent language and terms that were presented to the player at the time of consent. Provides audit trail for what the player agreed to.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the player granted consent for this specific marketing communication type. Represents the principal business event time for this consent record.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'The specific type of marketing communication or data processing activity for which consent is being recorded. Defines the scope of the consent grant or withdrawal.']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the consent language, terms, or privacy policy that was presented to the player at the time of consent. Enables tracking of consent under different policy versions.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of days this consent record and associated player data should be retained before deletion or anonymization. Supports GDPR right to erasure and data minimization principles.']
  double_opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consent was confirmed through a double opt-in process (e.g., email confirmation link). True if double opt-in was completed; False for single opt-in.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record expires and is no longer valid for marketing use. Null for consents without expiration.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the consent action was initiated. Used for fraud detection and geographic validation. May be considered PII in certain jurisdictions.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this consent record is currently active and should be honored for marketing operations. True if active; False if superseded, archived, or invalidated.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The legal jurisdiction or country under which this consent was collected, determining applicable privacy regulations. Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|GBR|DEU|FRA|JPN|KOR|CAN|AUS|BRA|MEX|ITA|ESP|NLD|SWE|POL|CHN — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'The lawful basis under GDPR Article 6 for processing the players data for this marketing activity. Typically consent for marketing communications, but may include legitimate interest for certain profiling activities.']
  parental_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental or guardian consent was obtained for players under the age of digital consent. True if parental consent was provided; False otherwise. Required for minors under COPPA and GDPR.']
  platform varchar [note: 'The gaming platform or operating system through which the consent was collected. Relevant for platform-specific consent requirements such as Apple ATT. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: iOS|Android|PC|PlayStation|Xbox|Nintendo-Switch|Web|Steam|Epic-Games-Store — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  preference_center_url varchar [note: 'URL of the preference center or consent management interface where the player can view or modify their consent settings. Provides transparency and easy access to consent controls.']
  privacy_policy_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the privacy policy that was in effect and presented to the player at the time of consent. Tracks consent under different policy iterations.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record was first created in the data system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record was last modified. Audit field for tracking consent state changes over time.']
  salesforce_contact_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier linking this consent record to the corresponding Contact record in Salesforce CRM. Enables synchronization of consent state with CRM for lifecycle campaigns.']
  third_party_processor varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the third-party data processor or advertising partner for whom consent was granted. Relevant for third-party-ad-sharing consent type.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'The browser or application user agent string from which the consent was submitted. Used for technical audit and fraud detection.']
  withdrawal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the player withdrew their previously granted consent. Null if consent has not been withdrawn.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each player\'s marketing-specific consent and communication preference state as captured by the marketing team for GDPR, COPPA, CCPA, and CAN-SPAM compliance in marketing communications. Captures player reference, consent type (email-marketing/push-notification/SMS/data-profiling-for-targeting/third-party-ad-sharing), consent status (granted/withdrawn/expired), consent timestamp, consent source (in-game-prompt/web-preference-center/app-store-ATT), withdrawal timestamp, age-verified flag, parental consent flag, jurisdiction, consent version, and legal basis reference. Scoped exclusively to marketing communication consent — distinct from player domain\'s account-level consent and platform domain\'s data processing agreements. Enables lawful basis documentation for all CRM sends, retargeting, and lookalike audience creation.'
}

Table marketing.audience {
  audience_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the audience definition. Primary key.']
  ad_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.ad_network. Business justification: Audiences are exported to specific ad networks/platforms for activation. The target_platform STRING field (e.g., \'Facebook\', \'Google\', \'TikTok\') should be normalized to a FK relationship to ad_network']
  consent_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consent_policy. Business justification: Audience sync to ad platforms requires consent policy validation before data export. Privacy compliance gate ensuring only consented players are shared with third-party advertising networks.']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this audience definition.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or team responsible for managing and maintaining this audience definition.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title this audience is associated with for targeting purposes.']
  last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this audience definition.']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.league. Business justification: Marketing audiences built from league viewership data (e.g., LCS viewers, Worlds watchers) for retargeting and lookalike modeling. League context enables precise audience segmentation and activation f']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign this audience is primarily associated with for user acquisition targeting.']
  player_behavior_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.player_behavior_segment. Business justification: Audiences for lookalike targeting are often seeded from ML-driven behavior segments (high-LTV, churn-risk). Distinct from rule-based player_segment; enables tracking which ML segments drive best acqui']
  player_ltv_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.player_ltv_segment. Business justification: Marketing audiences for UA platforms are built from LTV segments for lookalike modeling and value-based bidding. Linking enables audience sync from monetization data, high-value player targeting in pa']
  player_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player segment that serves as the seed or source population for this audience definition.']
  processing_activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.processing_activity. Business justification: Audience creation and sync to third-party ad platforms is a processing activity requiring GDPR Article 30 documentation. Required for data protection impact assessments and third-party processor manag']
  source_audience_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on audience (source_audience_id)']
  source_player_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to player.segment. Business justification: Audiences for ad platform activation (Facebook, Google, TikTok) are synced FROM player domain segments. Marketing teams export player.segment definitions to create matched audiences on ad networks. Es']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when this audience was first activated and synced to the target ad platform.']
  att_opt_in_requirement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this audience is restricted to iOS users who have granted ATT opt-in consent for tracking.']
  audience_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or short code for the audience used in external systems and reporting.']
  audience_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name for the audience segment (e.g., \'High-LTV Whales Q1 2024\', \'D7 Churned Non-Payers\').']
  audience_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audience: draft (being built), active (syncing to platforms), paused (temporarily disabled), archived (no longer used), expired (past validity window).']
  audience_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audience definition method: lookalike (modeled from seed), custom (manually defined), behavioral (action-based), demographic (attribute-based), value_based (LTV/ARPU tier), or retargeting (re-engagement).']
  average_d7_retention_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average day-7 retention rate (percentage) of players in the source segment. Used to assess audience quality for lookalike modeling.']
  average_ltv_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average lifetime value in USD of players in the source segment used to build this audience. Used for value-based lookalike modeling.']
  coppa_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether users under age 13 (or applicable age threshold) are excluded from this audience to comply with COPPA requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audience record was first created in the system.']
  data_retention_days varchar [note: 'Number of days this audience data will be retained on the ad platform before automatic deletion, per privacy policy and platform limits.']
  estimated_reach varchar [note: 'Ad platform\'s estimated reachable audience size based on the matched identifiers and platform\'s user base. Provided by platform API.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this audience definition expires and should no longer be used for targeting. Null if no expiration.']
  gdpr_lawful_basis varchar [note: 'Legal basis under GDPR for processing and exporting this audience data: consent, legitimate_interest, contract, legal_obligation, or not_applicable (non-EU audience).']
  geo_target_countries varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of 3-letter ISO country codes where this audience is targeted (e.g., \'USA,CAN,GBR\'). Empty if global.']
  hashing_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to hash PII identifiers before export (SHA256, MD5, or none for non-PII identifiers).']
  identifier_type varchar [note: 'Type of player identifier used for matching with ad platforms: email (hashed), phone (hashed), device_id, IDFA (iOS), GAID (Android), or internal player_id.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audience record was last modified or refreshed.']
  last_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful or attempted sync to the target ad platform.']
  lookalike_expansion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expansion factor for lookalike modeling (e.g., 1% = most similar, 10% = broader reach). Null for non-lookalike audiences.']
  lookalike_seed_size varchar [note: 'Number of seed users used to generate a lookalike audience. Null for non-lookalike audience types.']
  match_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of audience members successfully matched by the ad platform (matched_count / audience_size * 100). Key metric for audience quality and targeting effectiveness.']
  matched_count varchar [note: 'Number of player identifiers successfully matched and accepted by the ad platform during last sync.']
  next_scheduled_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the next automatic sync to the ad platform is scheduled to occur.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this audience definition, including business context, targeting strategy, or special handling instructions.']
  platform_audience_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by the ad platform (e.g., Facebook Custom Audience ID, Google Audience ID) after successful sync.']
  platform_scope varchar [note: 'Device platform scope for this audience: iOS, Android, web, console, or cross_platform (all platforms).']
  size varchar [note: 'Total number of unique player identifiers included in this audience definition at last refresh.']
  suppression_list_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a suppression list (e.g., opted-out users, existing players) has been applied to exclude certain identifiers from this audience.']
  sync_error_message varchar [note: 'Error message returned by the ad platform if the last sync attempt failed. Null if sync was successful.']
  sync_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which the audience is refreshed and re-synced to the ad platform: real_time, hourly, daily, weekly, or manual (on-demand only).']
  sync_status varchar [note: 'Current synchronization status with the target ad platform: pending (queued), syncing (in progress), synced (complete), failed (error), partial (some records failed).']

  Note: 'Lookalike and custom audience definitions exported to ad platforms (Facebook, Google, TikTok) for paid UA targeting, capturing audience source segment, platform sync status, and match rates.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_exposure {
  campaign_exposure_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each campaign exposure event. Primary key for the campaign_exposure association.']
  creative_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific creative asset shown to the player during this exposure. Used for creative-level performance analysis and A/B testing.']
  impression_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the ad impression from the attribution partner (AppsFlyer, Adjust) or ad platform. Used for reconciliation with ad platform reporting and fraud detection.']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the marketing campaign that the player was exposed to. Enables campaign-level reach, frequency, and conversion analysis.']
  placement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the ad placement or inventory position where the creative was shown. Used for placement-level performance optimization.']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the player account who was exposed to the campaign. Enables player-level campaign journey analysis and cross-campaign frequency capping.']
  attributed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this exposure received attribution credit for a conversion under the current attribution model (last-touch, first-touch, linear, time-decay, or data-driven). Used for campaign ROI calculation and budget allocation decisions.']
  click_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the player clicked on the campaign creative following this exposure. Nullable if exposure did not result in a click. Used for click-through rate calculation and engagement analysis.']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the player completed the target conversion action (install, registration, purchase, re-engagement) attributed to this exposure. Nullable if no conversion occurred within the attribution window. Used for conversion rate and time-to-conversion analysis.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device on which the player was exposed to the campaign. Used for device-level targeting and cross-device attribution analysis.']
  exposure_channel varchar [note: 'The specific marketing channel through which the player was exposed to the campaign. Used for channel-level attribution and cross-channel journey analysis.']
  exposure_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of times this player has been exposed to this specific campaign up to this exposure event. Used for frequency capping enforcement and diminishing returns analysis.']
  exposure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the player was exposed to the campaign creative. Used for time-to-conversion analysis, multi-touch attribution sequencing, and frequency capping windows.']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the player\'s location at the time of exposure. Used for geo-level campaign performance analysis.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the exposure event between player_account and campaign. It captures every instance when a player is exposed to a marketing campaign across any channel (paid social, display, search, video, influencer, CRM lifecycle). Each record links one player_account to one campaign with attributes that track exposure timing, channel, frequency, engagement (clicks), and attribution outcomes. This is the operational foundation for multi-touch attribution, incrementality testing, frequency capping, and campaign performance measurement in the marketing domain.. Existence Justification: In gaming marketing operations, players are exposed to multiple campaigns throughout their lifecycle (UA at acquisition, retargeting during dormancy, CRM campaigns during active play, re-engagement campaigns post-churn), and each campaign reaches millions of players across multiple channels. Marketing teams actively manage and query campaign exposure data as an operational entity for multi-touch attribution, incrementality testing, frequency capping enforcement, and cross-channel journey analysis. This is not an analytical correlation—exposure tracking is a core operational process in mobile attribution platforms (AppsFlyer, Adjust) and marketing analytics systems.'
}

Table marketing.creative_test_assignment {
  creative_test_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this creative-to-experiment assignment record. Primary key.']
  ab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the A/B experiment in which this creative is being tested']
  ad_creative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the advertising creative asset being tested in this experiment']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier for the A/B test variant if this creative is part of a multivariate test. Null if not part of a test. Used for performance comparison and optimization. [Moved from ad_creative: This attribute currently stores the variant label for a creative, but variant assignment is specific to a particular experiment. A creative can be control in one experiment and variant_A in another. This attribute belongs in the association, not on the creative master record.]']
  assigned_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the marketing team member who assigned this creative to this experiment variant. Used for accountability and workflow tracking.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date when this creative was assigned to this experiment variant in the test management system. Distinct from test_start_date (when it began serving).']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this creative assignment within the experiment. Tracks whether the creative is actively serving, paused, completed testing, or removed from the test.']
  impressions_allocated varchar [note: 'Number of ad impressions allocated to this creative within this experiment variant. Used for traffic allocation tracking and performance normalization.']
  is_ab_test_control boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this creative is the control variant in an A/B test. True if control, false if treatment or not part of a test. [Moved from ad_creative: This boolean flag indicates control status, but control designation is experiment-specific. The same creative can be control in one test and a treatment variant in another. This should be derived from variant_label in the association, not stored on the creative.]']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Categorical assessment of this creative\'s performance within the experiment (top_performer, average, underperformer, not_evaluated). Used for creative optimization decisions.']
  test_end_date date [note: 'Date when this specific creative stopped serving in this experiment variant. Null if still active. Used to track creative rotation and test lifecycle.']
  test_start_date date [note: 'Date when this specific creative began serving in this experiment variant. May differ from the overall experiment start date if creatives are rotated or added mid-test.']
  variant_label varchar [note: 'The experiment variant identifier for this creative (e.g., control, variant_A, variant_B, treatment_1). Identifies which arm of the experiment this creative represents.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the assignment of advertising creative assets to A/B experiment variants. It captures which creative serves as which test variant in which experiment, tracking performance allocation and test lifecycle dates. Each record links one ad_creative to one ab_experiment with attributes that exist only in the context of this experimental assignment.. Existence Justification: In gaming user acquisition, creative testing is a core operational process where marketing teams actively manage which advertising creatives serve as which variants in which A/B experiments. One creative asset can be tested across multiple experiments (different audience segments, different games, different time periods), and one experiment tests multiple creative variants simultaneously (A/B/C/D creative tests). The assignment of creative-to-variant-to-experiment is an operational record that marketing teams create, monitor, and update.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_promo_distribution {
  campaign_promo_distribution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this campaign-promo code distribution record. Primary key.']
  billing_promo_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the promotional discount code being distributed through this campaign']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing team member who created this campaign-promo distribution configuration.']
  crm_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the CRM lifecycle marketing campaign that distributes this promo code']
  target_segment_id varchar [note: 'The player segment targeted for this specific campaign-code pairing. Same code may target different segments when distributed through different campaigns (e.g., at-risk players in winback campaign, active players in upsell campaign).']
  activation_date date [note: 'The date when this promo code becomes active for distribution through this specific campaign. Allows staggered activation of the same code across multiple campaigns.']
  actual_distribution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual timestamp when this promo code was distributed through this campaign. Used for performance analysis and attribution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this campaign-promo distribution record was created.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The specific communication channel through which this promo code is distributed in this campaign. Same code may be distributed via different channels in different campaigns (e.g., SAVE20 via email in one campaign, via SMS in another).']
  distribution_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this campaign-code distribution. Allows independent lifecycle management of each campaign-code pairing.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'The performance or priority tier assigned to this campaign-code distribution for A/B testing, budget allocation, or VIP treatment. Same code may have different performance tiers when distributed through different campaigns.']
  redemption_cap varchar [note: 'The maximum number of redemptions allocated to this campaign-code pairing. Enables budget control and performance tracking per distribution channel. Sum of all campaign-specific caps may equal or be less than the promo code\'s global max_redemption_count.']
  redemption_count_from_campaign varchar [note: 'The number of redemptions of this promo code that are attributed to this specific campaign distribution. Enables campaign ROI measurement and channel performance comparison.']
  total_discount_attributed_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative discount amount in USD attributed to redemptions from this campaign-code distribution. Used for campaign cost tracking and ROI calculation.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the distribution relationship between CRM lifecycle marketing campaigns and promotional discount codes. It captures which promo codes are distributed through which campaigns, enabling multi-channel distribution of the same code and multi-code distribution within a single campaign. Each record links one campaign to one promo code with distribution-specific attributes including channel, target segment, activation timing, redemption caps, and performance tier that exist only in the context of this campaign-code pairing.. Existence Justification: Gaming marketing operations distribute promotional codes through multiple CRM campaigns with channel-specific and segment-specific configurations. A single promo code (e.g., SAVE20) can be distributed via email campaign, push notification campaign, and SMS campaign simultaneously, each targeting different player segments with different redemption caps and performance tracking. Conversely, a single winback campaign may distribute multiple tier-specific promo codes (BRONZE10, SILVER20, GOLD30) to different player segments. This is an operational distribution management relationship, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table marketing.ad_set {
  ad_set_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ad_set']
  ad_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.ad_network. Business justification: ad_set should link to ad_network to capture which advertising network/platform this ad set is configured for (Facebook, Google, TikTok, etc.). The existing \'platform\' column (STRING) is a denormalized']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent marketing campaign that contains this ad set.']
  parent_ad_set_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ad_set (parent_ad_set_id)']
  age_max varchar [note: 'Maximum age of the target audience for this ad set.']
  age_min varchar [note: 'Minimum age of the target audience for this ad set.']
  app_store_url varchar [note: 'Direct URL to the game or app in the app store (Apple App Store, Google Play Store) being promoted by this ad set.']
  attribution_window_click varchar [note: 'Number of days after a click during which conversions are attributed to this ad set.']
  attribution_window_view varchar [note: 'Number of days after a view (impression) during which conversions are attributed to this ad set.']
  bid_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum bid amount or target cost per optimization event in the ad set\'s currency.']
  bid_strategy varchar [note: 'Bidding strategy used for ad delivery and auction participation (e.g., lowest cost, cost cap, bid cap, target cost).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ad set record was first created in the system.']
  creative_rotation varchar [note: 'Strategy for rotating multiple ad creatives within the ad set (optimize for best performers or rotate evenly).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this ad set (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  daily_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum amount allocated to be spent per day for this ad set in the campaign currency.']
  deep_link_url varchar [note: 'Deep link URL that directs users to a specific location within the app after install or click.']
  device_platforms varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of device platforms targeted (e.g., mobile, desktop, tablet, iOS, Android).']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the ad set is scheduled to stop running. Null indicates no end date (continuous run).']
  external_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the external advertising platform (e.g., Facebook Ads Manager, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, Unity Ads) for this ad set.']
  frequency_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times an individual user should see ads from this ad set within a specified time period.']
  frequency_cap_period_hours varchar [note: 'Time period in hours over which the frequency cap is applied.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Gender targeting criteria for the ad set audience.']
  geo_locations varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or JSON array of geographic locations (countries, regions, cities, postal codes) targeted by this ad set.']
  is_autobid boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic bidding is enabled for this ad set (true) or manual bidding is used (false).']
  lifetime_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total maximum amount allocated to be spent over the entire lifetime of this ad set in the campaign currency.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name assigned to the ad set for identification and reporting purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this ad set for internal team reference and documentation.']
  objective varchar [note: 'Primary marketing objective that this ad set is optimized to achieve (e.g., app installs, brand awareness, conversions).']
  optimization_goal varchar [note: 'Specific metric or event that the ad delivery algorithm is optimizing for (e.g., install, click, impression, ROAS, value).']
  pacing_type varchar [note: 'Strategy for how the ad set budget is spent over time (standard pacing spreads budget evenly, accelerated spends as quickly as possible).']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether ad placements are automatically optimized by the platform or manually selected.']
  placements varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or JSON array of specific ad placement positions (e.g., feed, stories, in-stream, audience network, rewarded video).']
  promoted_object_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier of the specific object being promoted (e.g., app store ID, Facebook page ID, event ID).']
  promoted_object_type varchar [note: 'Type of object being promoted by this ad set (e.g., app, page, event, product catalog, offer).']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the ad set is scheduled to begin running and serving ads.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the ad set in its lifecycle.']
  targeting_spec varchar [note: 'JSON or structured text representation of audience targeting criteria including demographics, interests, behaviors, and custom audiences.']
  tracking_url_template varchar [note: 'Template URL with macros for third-party tracking and attribution (e.g., AppsFlyer, Adjust, Branch) to measure campaign performance.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the marketing team member who last updated this ad set.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ad set record was last modified or updated.']
