  loot_box_disclosure_url varchar [note: 'URL to the publicly accessible odds disclosure page for any randomized reward mechanics included in this pass. Required for regulatory compliance in jurisdictions mandating loot box transparency (FTC, EU DSA). Null when contains_loot_box is false.']
  max_tier_skips varchar [note: 'Maximum number of tiers a player may purchase to skip (advance instantly) within this battle pass. Governs the pay-to-progress ceiling and P2W (Pay-to-Win) mechanics compliance.']
  pass_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code or SKU that uniquely identifies this pass product across distribution platforms (e.g., Steamworks, console SDKs, app stores). Used in platform certification and store listings.']
  pass_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the battle pass product definition. \'draft\' = in design; \'scheduled\' = approved and awaiting season start; \'active\' = currently purchasable and progressing; \'expired\' = season ended, no longer purchasable; \'retired\' = permanently removed from catalog.']
  pass_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pass monetization model. \'progression_tiered\' = XP-gated tier unlock system; \'content_access\' = flat subscription-style content entitlement (season pass); \'hybrid\' = combines both tier progression and flat content access.']
  pegi_rating varchar [note: 'PEGI content rating applicable to this battle pass and its associated content (e.g., \'12\'). Required for European regulatory compliance and storefront listing.']
  platform_availability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of platforms on which this battle pass is available for purchase (e.g., \'PC,PS5,XBOX,MOBILE,SWITCH\'). Drives platform-specific store listing and certification via Steamworks/Console SDKs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PC|PS5|XBOX|MOBILE|SWITCH|STADIA — promote to reference product]']
  premium_plus_price_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Retail price in USD for the premium-plus or bundle upgrade tier of the battle pass, if applicable. Null when has_premium_plus is false.']
  premium_price_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base retail price of the premium battle pass in US Dollars. The principal quantitative monetary value of this pass product. Used for ARPU, ARPPU, and revenue forecasting.']
  premium_price_virtual_currency varchar [note: 'Price of the premium battle pass denominated in the game\'s primary virtual currency (e.g., V-Bucks, Coins). Supports virtual economy pricing analysis and IAP (In-App Purchase) conversion tracking.']
  premium_tier_count varchar [note: 'Number of tiers exclusively accessible on the premium (paid) track. Represents the incremental value proposition of the premium pass purchase.']
  reward_schedule_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document path pointing to the reward schedule definition for this battle pass (e.g., a Jira Epic key, Perforce asset path, or content management system ID). Links to the detailed tier-by-tier reward manifest.']
  season_label varchar [note: 'Branded or thematic label for the season associated with this pass (e.g., \'Season of the Void\', \'Chapter 4 Season 2\'). Used in marketing and player-facing UI.']
  season_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal number of the game season associated with this pass (e.g., Season 7). Used for chronological ordering and player-facing season labeling.']
  soft_launch_date date [note: 'Date on which the battle pass was made available in a limited set of markets or platforms prior to full global launch. Used for staged rollout tracking and pre-launch performance analysis.']
  start_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the battle pass becomes active and purchasable by players. Aligns with the season launch date in the live-ops calendar.']
  tier_count varchar [note: 'Total number of progression tiers available in this battle pass. Applicable to \'progression_tiered\' and \'hybrid\' pass types. Null for pure \'content_access\' passes.']
  tier_skip_price_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Price in USD to purchase a single tier skip within this battle pass. Used for MTX (Microtransaction) revenue modeling and P2W analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this battle pass product definition record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and Silver layer incremental processing.']
  upgrade_path_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the upgrade path available for this pass (e.g., free-to-premium, premium-to-premium-plus). Defines the upsell funnel structure for conversion rate analysis.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of this battle pass product definition record, supporting change management and audit trail for pass configuration updates (e.g., price changes, tier adjustments).']
  virtual_currency_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the in-game virtual currency used for the virtual currency price (e.g., \'VBUCKS\', \'COINS\', \'GEMS\'). Required for multi-currency virtual economy management.']
  xp_per_tier varchar [note: 'Amount of XP (Experience Points) required to advance one tier in the battle pass progression system. Governs the grind curve and player engagement pacing.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or system identifier of the person or process that created this battle pass product definition. Supports audit trail and accountability for monetization product configuration.']

  Note: 'Master definition of pass-based monetization products per game title and season, encompassing both progression-tiered battle passes and content-access season passes. Captures pass name, pass type (progression_tiered, content_access, hybrid), season identifier, start/end dates, tier count (if applicable), free vs. premium track structure, premium price, XP-per-tier thresholds, reward schedule reference, content entitlements included, max purchasable tier skips, upgrade paths, platform availability, and active status. The SSOT for all pass product definitions — distinct from individual player entitlements.'
}

Table licensing.battle_pass_entitlement {
  battle_pass_entitlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a player\'s battle pass entitlement record. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  ab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ab_experiment. Business justification: Battle pass pricing, tier structure, XP requirements, and reward schedules are commonly A/B tested. Linking entitlements to experiments enables conversion rate analysis and revenue impact measurement ']
  battle_pass_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the battle pass product definition (season, name, tier structure, reward catalog) that this entitlement is for.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Battle pass entitlements tracked by cost center for revenue attribution. Management accounting for seasonal content revenue tracking.']
  deferred_revenue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.deferred_revenue. Business justification: Battle pass purchases create contract liabilities recognized over season duration. Direct ASC 606 requirement for performance obligation tracking and revenue recognition.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title under which this battle pass is operated. Supports multi-title monetization reporting.']
  iap_transaction_id varchar [note: 'The platform-issued transaction identifier from the app store or console SDK for the premium battle pass purchase (e.g., Apple App Store receipt ID, Google Play order ID, PSN transaction ID). Used for receipt validation and refund processing.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Premium battle pass purchases generate invoices for revenue recognition and tax reporting. Gaming companies must link entitlements to invoices for financial compliance and refund processing workflows.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.ip_agreement. Business justification: Player entitlements to licensed battle pass content require agreement reference for territory restrictions validation, platform rights verification, and automated compliance checks against license ter']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.league. Business justification: Battle passes themed around esports leagues (e.g., \'Championship Series Pass\'). Common monetization model linking premium content to competitive seasons. Tracks league-specific battle pass sales and e']
  primary_battle_player_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player who owns this battle pass entitlement. Core party reference linking entitlement to the player account.']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the platform (console, PC, mobile) on which the battle pass was purchased and is being progressed.']
  acquisition_channel varchar [note: 'The marketing or distribution channel through which the player acquired this battle pass entitlement. Supports CPI, ROAS, and conversion funnel analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: organic|paid_ua|influencer|bundle|gifted|promotional_code|loyalty_reward — promote to reference product]']
  age_gate_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the player passed the required age verification check prior to purchasing this battle pass. Required for COPPA compliance for players in applicable jurisdictions.']
  auto_renew_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the player has opted into automatic renewal of the battle pass subscription at the start of the next season. Supports subscription retention and churn rate analytics.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the player\'s registered region at the time of entitlement creation. Used for regional revenue reporting, tax compliance, and GDPR/COPPA jurisdiction determination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this entitlement record was first created in the system, which may precede the purchase timestamp for free-track entitlements granted at season start.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the real-money amounts recorded on this entitlement (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency monetization reporting.']
  current_tier varchar [note: 'The highest tier the player has currently unlocked on the battle pass, spanning both free and premium tracks. Used for reward grant eligibility checks at runtime.']
  current_xp varchar [note: 'The player\'s current accumulated experience points (XP) within this battle pass season. XP determines tier progression and is the primary progression currency.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to the battle pass purchase (e.g., promotional discount, loyalty reward, bundle discount). Zero if no discount was applied.']
  entitlement_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-visible alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this entitlement record. Used in player support, receipts, and entitlement enforcement lookups.']
  entitlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the battle pass entitlement. Controls whether the player can access premium rewards and progress tiers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|expired|suspended|refunded|pending|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this battle pass entitlement expires and the player can no longer earn or claim rewards. Typically aligned to the end of the game season.']
  free_tier_unlocked varchar [note: 'The highest tier unlocked on the free reward track. Tracked separately from premium tier to support free-vs-paid conversion analytics and reward grant logic.']
  is_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the player has reached the maximum tier and fully completed the battle pass. Used for completion reward grants and engagement analytics.']
  is_gifted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this battle pass entitlement was received as a gift from another player rather than self-purchased. Supports gifting revenue segmentation.']
  is_premium_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the player has activated the premium battle pass track. True means the player paid for premium access; False means free track only. Core entitlement enforcement flag.']
  last_progression_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The most recent timestamp at which the player earned XP or advanced a tier on this battle pass. Used for engagement monitoring, lapsed-player identification, and re-engagement campaign targeting.']
  max_tier varchar [note: 'The total number of tiers available in this battle pass. Used to calculate completion percentage and determine if the player has fully completed the pass.']
  net_purchase_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The net real-money amount charged to the player after discounts (purchase_price_amount minus discount_amount). Used for revenue recognition and ARPU/ARPPU reporting.']
  parental_consent_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether verifiable parental consent was obtained before granting this battle pass entitlement to a minor player. Mandatory for COPPA-regulated accounts.']
  player_level_at_purchase varchar [note: 'The player\'s account level at the time of battle pass premium track purchase. Used for conversion funnel segmentation and LTV modeling by player maturity cohort.']
  premium_tier_unlocked varchar [note: 'The highest tier unlocked on the premium reward track. Null or zero if the player has not activated the premium track. Drives premium reward grant eligibility.']
  promotional_code varchar [note: 'The promotional or discount code applied at the time of battle pass purchase, if any. Used for campaign attribution and promotional effectiveness analysis.']
  purchase_price_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The gross real-money price paid by the player to activate the premium battle pass track, in the currency indicated by currency_code. Null for free-track-only entitlements.']
  purchase_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the player purchased or activated the premium battle pass track. Null for free-track-only entitlements. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  refund_status varchar [note: 'Current refund state for this battle pass entitlement purchase. Tracks whether a refund has been requested, processed, or denied. Supports financial reconciliation and player support workflows.']
  refund_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when a refund was processed for this battle pass entitlement. Null if no refund has been issued. Used for financial reconciliation and chargeback tracking.']
  rewards_claimed_count varchar [note: 'The total number of individual tier rewards the player has claimed from this battle pass entitlement. Used to measure reward engagement and identify players who earn but do not claim.']
  season_number varchar [note: 'The sequential season number associated with this battle pass entitlement. Enables cross-season analytics and cohort comparisons.']
  tier_skip_spend_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total real-money or virtual currency equivalent amount spent on tier skips for this entitlement. Contributes to ARPPU and LTV calculations for the monetization domain.']
  tier_skips_purchased varchar [note: 'The total number of tier skip purchases made by the player on this battle pass entitlement. Each skip advances the player one tier without earning XP. Key microtransaction (MTX) revenue signal.']
  track_type varchar [note: 'Indicates which reward track(s) the player has access to: free (default), premium (paid upgrade), or premium_plus (deluxe tier with bonus rewards). Drives reward grant eligibility at runtime.']
  unclaimed_rewards_count varchar [note: 'The number of rewards the player has unlocked but not yet claimed from this battle pass. High unclaimed counts may indicate engagement drop-off and trigger re-engagement nudges.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this entitlement record, including tier progression, XP updates, tier skips, or status changes.']
  virtual_currency_cost varchar [note: 'The amount of in-game virtual currency (e.g., V-Bucks, Coins, Gems) spent to purchase the premium battle pass track, if applicable. Null for real-money-only purchases.']
  xp_to_next_tier varchar [note: 'The remaining XP required for the player to advance to the next battle pass tier. Supports real-time progression display and engagement nudge triggers.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a player\'s ownership and progression state for a specific battle pass. Tracks player reference, battle pass reference, purchase timestamp, tier unlocked (free and premium), current XP, premium track activated flag, tier skip purchases, expiry date, and completion status. Enables entitlement enforcement and reward grant eligibility checks at runtime.'
}

Table licensing.gacha_pull {
  gacha_pull_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the gacha pull transaction. Primary key for this event log entity.']
  ab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ab_experiment. Business justification: Gacha mechanics (drop rates, pity thresholds, pricing, multi-pull discounts) are heavily A/B tested in F2P games. Experiment attribution is critical for measuring revenue impact and player satisfactio']
  build_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.build. Business justification: Gacha pulls occur within specific builds. Tracking build version is essential for regulatory compliance (disclosed drop rates must match build version), exploit detection (abnormal pull outcomes in sp']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Gacha pulls tracked by cost center for revenue attribution. Revenue tracking for gacha monetization by game/feature.']
  gacha_pool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific gacha pool or banner from which the pull was executed. Links to the gacha pool configuration entity.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the game title in which this gacha pull occurred. Links to the game title master entity.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Gacha pulls are revenue events that must post to GL. Some jurisdictions require specific gacha revenue tracking for regulatory compliance and disclosure.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Gacha pulls must track jurisdiction for loot box disclosure compliance, drop-rate transparency requirements, and age-gate enforcement. Critical for regulatory filings in jurisdictions with loot box re']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensed_ip. Business justification: Individual gacha pulls yielding licensed items require IP attribution for per-transaction royalty calculation, regulatory reporting in jurisdictions requiring loot box disclosure, and revenue attribut']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotional campaign or limited-time event associated with this gacha pull, if applicable. Null for standard pulls.']
  mtx_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary item granted as the outcome of this gacha pull. Links to the in-game item catalog.']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the player who executed this gacha pull. Links to the player master entity.']
  session_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the player session during which this gacha pull occurred. Used for session-level analytics and fraud detection.']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the platform on which this gacha pull was executed (iOS, Android, PC, Console). Links to the platform master entity.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.tournament. Business justification: Gacha events timed with tournaments (limited-time tournament-themed pools). Real business pattern for promotional gacha during esports events. Tracks tournament-driven gacha revenue and player engagem']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier generated by the game server for this gacha pull. Used for reconciliation, auditing, and player support investigations.']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier of the A/B test variant or experiment group the player was assigned to at the time of this gacha pull. Used for conversion rate optimization and feature testing.']
  age_gate_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the player passed age verification requirements for gacha participation in their jurisdiction. True if verified, false otherwise.']
  bonus_items_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether additional bonus items were granted alongside the primary outcome (e.g., first-time bonuses, promotional extras). True if bonuses granted.']
  client_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the game client software used to execute this gacha pull. Used for version-specific analytics and bug tracking.']
  conversion_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of conversion currency (e.g., duplicate shards, exchange tokens) granted if the outcome was a duplicate. Null if not a duplicate.']
  currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of virtual currency spent on this gacha pull. Denominated in the currency_type specified.']
  currency_type varchar [note: 'Type of virtual currency used for this gacha pull: premium (paid), soft (earned), free (promotional), or event-specific currency.']
  days_since_install varchar [note: 'Number of days elapsed between the players initial game install and this gacha pull. Used for cohort analysis and LTV modeling.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used to execute the gacha pull: mobile phone, tablet, PC, or console. Used for device-specific analytics.']
  disclosed_drop_rate_pct decimal(5,4) [note: 'The publicly disclosed drop rate percentage for the rarity tier of the outcome item at the time of pull. Required for regulatory compliance in multiple jurisdictions.']
  duplicate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outcome item was a duplicate of an item the player already owned. True if duplicate, false if new acquisition.']
  fraud_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Automated fraud detection score assigned to this gacha pull transaction at the time of execution. Higher scores indicate higher fraud risk. Range 0.00 to 100.00.']
  lifetime_gacha_spend_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative real-money equivalent amount the player has spent on gacha pulls across all time, up to and including this transaction. Used for whale segmentation and LTV analysis.']
  outcome_item_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of the primary outcome item granted to the player. Typically 1 for character/weapon pulls, may be higher for consumables.']
  outcome_rarity_tier varchar [note: 'Rarity classification of the primary item granted: common, uncommon, rare, epic, legendary, or mythic. Determines drop rate and player value perception.']
  pity_counter_after varchar [note: 'Value of the players pity counter immediately after this pull was executed. Reset to zero if pity was triggered.']
  pity_counter_before varchar [note: 'Value of the players pity counter immediately before this pull was executed. Tracks cumulative pulls without a guaranteed drop.']
  pity_triggered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pity mechanism was triggered for this pull, guaranteeing a high-rarity item. True if pity activated, false otherwise.']
  player_level_at_pull varchar [note: 'The players in-game level at the time this gacha pull was executed. Used for progression-based analytics and monetization funnel analysis.']
  pull_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of items pulled in this single transaction. Typically 1 for single pulls, 10 for multi-pulls.']
  pull_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the gacha pull was executed by the player. Primary business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  pull_type varchar [note: 'Type of gacha pull executed: single pull, multi-pull (10x), free pull, premium pull, or discounted pull. Affects cost and pity mechanics.']
  real_money_equivalent_usd decimal(18,4) [note: 'Equivalent real-world monetary value of the currency spent, converted to USD. Used for revenue recognition and ARPU calculations.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this gacha pull record was first written to the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  refund_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the currency spent on this gacha pull was subsequently refunded to the player. True if refunded, false otherwise.']
  refund_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was processed for this gacha pull, if applicable. Null if no refund occurred.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the regulatory jurisdiction of the player at the time of pull. Used for loot box disclosure compliance (Belgium, Netherlands, Japan, China).']
  server_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region of the game server that processed this gacha pull (e.g., NA-EAST, EU-WEST, ASIA-PACIFIC). Used for latency analysis and regional performance tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every loot box or gacha pull event executed by a player. Captures game title, gacha pool reference, pull timestamp, currency type and amount spent, pity counter at time of pull, outcome item(s) granted, rarity tier of outcome, guaranteed drop flag (pity triggered), and regulatory jurisdiction. Supports loot box disclosure compliance (Belgium, Netherlands, ESRB loot box labeling) and gacha economy balancing.'
}

Table licensing.gacha_pool {
  gacha_pool_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the gacha pool configuration. Primary key.']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_release. Business justification: New gacha pools deploy with content updates for version compatibility. Links pool to release for client version validation, asset availability checks, and rollback coordination.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Gacha pools developed by specific cost centers for cost tracking. Development cost attribution for content creation and maintenance.']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.dev_project. Business justification: Gacha pools are designed content requiring dev resources. Linking to dev_project supports content pipeline tracking, A/B test attribution, regulatory compliance documentation (drop rate disclosure per']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user who approved this gacha pool configuration for production deployment.']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Gacha pool design and content creation consume development budget. Content budget tracking for art, design, and implementation costs.']
  gaas_live_ops_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.live_ops_cycle. Business justification: Gacha events are time-limited live-ops campaigns (e.g., limited-time banners tied to content updates). Linking enables event-to-revenue attribution, live-ops planning coordination, and analysis of gac']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.game_studio. Business justification: Gacha pools are designed by specific studios. Tracking studio ownership supports regulatory compliance (studio-level drop rate disclosures in some jurisdictions), revenue attribution for studio P&L, a']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title this gacha pool belongs to.']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensed_ip. Business justification: Gacha pools containing licensed characters (Genshin Impact collaborations) must track IP for drop rate disclosure compliance, royalty attribution per pull, and licensor approval of probability mechani']
  mtx_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific item guaranteed at hard pity threshold, if applicable.']
  royalty_accrual_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.royalty_accrual. Business justification: Gacha pools with licensed characters generate royalty obligations. Licensed content accounting for IP collaboration gacha events.']
  title_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.title_season. Business justification: Gacha pools are seasonal limited-time banners (e.g., Summer Festival Pool). Business tracks which season each pool belongs to for lifecycle management, revenue attribution by season, and content plann']
  age_gate_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether age verification is required before accessing this gacha pool due to regional regulations.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this gacha pool configuration was approved for production deployment.']
  banner_image_url varchar [note: 'CDN (Content Delivery Network) URL to the promotional banner image for this gacha pool.']
  base_drop_rate_r decimal(5,4) [note: 'Base probability of obtaining an R (common rarity) item per pull, expressed as decimal (e.g., 0.9430 = 94.3%).']
  base_drop_rate_sr decimal(5,4) [note: 'Base probability of obtaining an SR (high rarity) item per pull, expressed as decimal (e.g., 0.0510 = 5.1%).']
  base_drop_rate_ssr decimal(5,4) [note: 'Base probability of obtaining an SSR (highest rarity) item per pull, expressed as decimal (e.g., 0.0060 = 0.6%).']
  collaboration_partner varchar [note: 'Name of external IP (Intellectual Property) or brand partner if this is a collaboration gacha pool.']
  cost_per_multi_pull decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of currency required for a multi-pull (typically 10-pull) from this pool, often discounted.']
  cost_per_single_pull decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of currency required for a single gacha pull from this pool.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this gacha pool configuration record was first created in the system.']
  currency_type varchar [note: 'Type of virtual currency required to perform a pull from this gacha pool.']
  duplicate_conversion_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether duplicate items from this pool are automatically converted to alternative currency or resources.']
  duplicate_conversion_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Amount of alternative currency or resource awarded per duplicate item, if conversion is enabled.']
  featured_item_boost_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Probability multiplier applied to featured items in this pool (e.g., 0.5000 represents 50% rate-up).']
  hard_pity_threshold varchar [note: 'Maximum number of pulls after which a guaranteed high-rarity item is awarded.']
  max_pulls_per_player varchar [note: 'Maximum number of pulls a single player can perform on this pool during its active window. Nullable for unlimited.']
  minimum_player_level varchar [note: 'Minimum player level required to access this gacha pool. Nullable if no restriction.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this gacha pool configuration record was last modified.']
  multi_pull_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of items granted in a single multi-pull transaction (typically 10).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal operational notes or comments about this gacha pool configuration for product and operations teams.']
  pity_counter_shared boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pity counter for this pool is shared with other pools or independent.']
  platform_availability varchar [note: 'Gaming platforms where this gacha pool is available.']
  pool_active_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this gacha pool is no longer available to players. Nullable for permanent pools.']
  pool_active_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this gacha pool becomes available to players.']
  pool_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for the gacha pool used in game systems and external reporting.']
  pool_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the gacha pool, including thematic elements and featured items for player communication.']
  pool_display_order varchar [note: 'Sort order for displaying this gacha pool in the game UI, lower numbers appear first.']
  pool_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the gacha pool (e.g., \'Summer Event Banner\', \'Standard Wish\').']
  pool_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the gacha pool configuration.']
  pool_theme varchar [note: 'Thematic classification of the gacha pool (e.g., \'Fantasy\', \'Sci-Fi\', \'Holiday\', \'Collaboration\').']
  pool_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the gacha pool indicating its business purpose and availability pattern.']
  pool_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this gacha pool configuration, incremented when drop rates or items are modified.']
  region_availability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this gacha pool is available (e.g., \'USA,JPN,KOR\'). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product if more than 6 regions]']
  regulatory_disclosure_text varchar [note: 'Full legal disclosure text displayed to players regarding drop rates and probabilities, required by regional regulations.']
  soft_pity_threshold varchar [note: 'Number of pulls after which drop rates for high-rarity items begin to increase incrementally.']
  total_pulls_lifetime varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of all pulls performed on this gacha pool since inception, for analytics and reporting.']
  total_revenue_lifetime decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative revenue generated by this gacha pool since inception, in base currency (USD).']

  Note: 'Master definition of each gacha or loot box pool configuration per game title. Defines pool name, associated game title, pool type (standard, event, limited), item drop table with probability weights, pity system parameters (soft pity threshold, hard pity threshold, guaranteed item), cost per pull (currency type and amount), pool active window, and regulatory disclosure text. The SSOT for gacha pool configurations.'
}

Table licensing.offer_campaign {
  offer_campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the monetization offer campaign. Primary key.']
  ab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent A/B test experiment this campaign variant belongs to. Null if not part of an A/B test.']
  ad_creative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.ad_creative. Business justification: Offer campaigns display visual creative assets in-game and on storefronts. Linking to ad_creative enables A/B testing of offer visuals, creative performance analysis, and unified asset management acro']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_release. Business justification: Promotional campaigns timed to content releases for launch marketing. Links campaign to release schedule for coordinated go-live, player acquisition, and new content monetization.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Offer campaigns are cost center activities for expense tracking. Marketing cost management for promotional campaign spending.']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.dev_project. Business justification: Promotional campaigns often tie to content releases or live-ops cycles managed by dev teams. Linking enables campaign-to-content attribution, cross-functional planning between marketing and dev, and a']
  featured_dlc_bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.dlc_bundle. Business justification: Offer campaigns promote DLC bundles (e.g., Season Pass launch campaign). Business needs to track featured bundles for storefront merchandising, campaign ROI measurement, and marketing attribution. Cri']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Offer campaigns have budget allocations tracked against actuals. Campaign ROI analysis and financial planning requirement.']
  gaas_live_ops_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.live_ops_cycle. Business justification: Promotional campaigns frequently align with live-ops events (season launches, content drops, tournaments). Linking supports cross-functional event planning, campaign-to-content attribution, and analys']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title this offer campaign is associated with.']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensed_ip. Business justification: Offer campaigns promoting licensed content must track IP for marketing approval requirements, brand usage guideline compliance, and revenue attribution when campaigns drive sales of licensed items sub']
  message_localization_string_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.localization_string. Business justification: Campaign messaging requires localization for regional targeting and compliance. Links offer to translated marketing copy for multi-region deployment and A/B testing.']
  parent_campaign_offer_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent campaign if this is a recurring instance or variant. Null if this is a standalone campaign.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_policy. Business justification: Promotional campaigns must comply with specific policies governing advertising to minors, discount disclosure requirements, and gambling-like mechanics restrictions. Required for campaign approval wor']
  primary_offer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user or system account that created this campaign record.']
  seasonal_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.seasonal_event. Business justification: Promotional campaigns synchronized with seasonal events for marketing synergy. Links offer timing to event schedule for coordinated launch, player targeting, and revenue optimization.']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the platform (console, PC, mobile) where this campaign is active.']
  tertiary_offer_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user who approved this campaign for launch. Null if not yet approved.']
  title_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.sku. Business justification: Offer campaigns target specific SKUs for promotional pricing (e.g., weekend sale on Deluxe Edition). Business needs this to determine storefront eligibility, apply discounts at checkout, and measure c']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.tournament. Business justification: In-game offers often tie to esports tournaments (e.g., Championship Bundle, Team Skins for playoffs). This links monetization campaigns to the competitive calendar, enabling revenue analysis for tourn']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'A/B test variant identifier for this campaign (e.g., \'A\', \'B\', \'control\'). Null if not part of an A/B test.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this campaign was approved for launch. Null if not yet approved.']
  campaign_budget decimal(15,2) [note: 'Allocated budget for this campaign including discounts, bonuses, and promotional costs. Null if no budget cap.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the campaign used in tracking and reporting systems.']
  campaign_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the offer campaign expires and is no longer available to players.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the offer campaign (e.g., \'Black Friday Flash Sale\', \'Whale Retention Offer Q4\').']
  campaign_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the campaign for internal reference and collaboration.']
  campaign_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the offer campaign becomes active and visible to players.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the offer campaign.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes from legal or compliance review regarding regulatory considerations or restrictions for this campaign.']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory and legal compliance review for this campaign (e.g., COPPA, gambling regulations, consumer protection).']
  conversion_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Calculated conversion rate (redemptions / impressions) for this campaign. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0523 = 5.23%).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this campaign record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for monetary values in this campaign (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY).']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Type of discount or bonus structure offered in the campaign.']
  discount_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric value of the discount or bonus. Interpretation depends on discount_type (e.g., 25.00 for 25% off or $25 fixed discount).']
  display_image_url varchar [note: 'URL to the promotional image or banner asset used to display this offer in-game.']
  eligible_sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SKU identifiers eligible for this offer campaign. Empty if all SKUs are eligible.']
  geo_restriction_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this offer is available. Empty indicates global availability.']
  impression_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times this offer can be displayed to eligible players. Null indicates no cap.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this campaign is part of a recurring series (e.g., weekly flash sales). True = recurring; False = one-time.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this campaign record was last modified.']
  offer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the offer campaign type based on its monetization strategy and target behavior.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for display when multiple campaigns are eligible for the same player. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  redemption_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times this offer can be redeemed across all players. Null indicates no cap.']
  redemption_cap_per_player varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single player can redeem this offer. Null indicates no per-player limit.']
  target_dau_min varchar [note: 'Minimum DAU threshold for player eligibility. Players with DAU below this value are excluded. Null indicates no minimum.']
  target_days_since_last_purchase varchar [note: 'Number of days since player\'s last purchase to qualify for this offer (used for lapsed player targeting). Null indicates no recency requirement.']
  target_ltv_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum player lifetime value threshold for campaign eligibility. Null indicates no minimum LTV requirement.']
  target_player_segment varchar [note: 'Player spending tier or behavioral segment targeted by this campaign. F2P (Free-to-Play) = non-payers; Minnow = low spenders; Dolphin = moderate spenders; Whale = high spenders.']
  total_impressions varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of how many times this offer has been displayed to players since campaign start.']
  total_redemptions varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of how many times this offer has been redeemed by players since campaign start.']
  total_revenue_generated decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative revenue generated from all redemptions of this offer campaign in the campaign\'s currency.']

  Note: 'Master record of time-limited monetization offer campaigns (flash sales, first-purchase bonuses, returning player offers, whale retention offers). Captures campaign name, offer type, targeted player segment (F2P, minnow, dolphin, whale), eligible SKUs, discount or bonus structure, campaign start/end timestamps, impression cap, redemption cap, A/B test variant, and conversion rate tracking reference. Distinct from marketing campaigns — these are in-game revenue-driving offers.'
}

Table licensing.ip_agreement {
  ip_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the intellectual property licensing agreement. Primary key.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.game_title. Business justification: IP agreements govern which titles can use licensed IP (middleware engines, music, brands). Essential for legal compliance, royalty calculation, and release approval workflows in gaming.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Agreements specify territorial scope; linking to jurisdiction enables automated compliance rule lookup (GDPR applicability, loot box regulations, age verification). Real process: legal review workflow']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensed_ip. Business justification: IP agreements should link to the licensed_ip catalog to identify which specific IP asset is being licensed. Currently uses licensed_ip_asset_reference (STRING) which should be normalized to a FK. This']
  primary_ip_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the licensor organization, such as DUNS number or tax identifier.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: License agreements must track applicable regulatory obligations (age ratings, loot box disclosure, GDPR compliance). Real process: compliance teams map obligations to agreements for audit readiness an']
  server_fleet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.server_fleet. Business justification: Fleet-level licensing agreements for game engines and middleware (e.g., Unreal Engine fleet license) define deployment scope, enable fleet-wide compliance monitoring, and support aggregated royalty re']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the licensing agreement, typically formatted with prefix and sequential number.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the licensing agreement indicating its operational state and enforceability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|terminated|expired|under_renewal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the licensing agreement by commercial structure: outbound engine (Unity/Unreal SDK licensing), inbound brand/franchise (licensed IP usage), middleware (physics, audio, anti-cheat, analytics SDKs), music synchronization, art asset licensing, or brand partnership. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: outbound_engine|inbound_brand|inbound_franchise|middleware|music_sync|art_asset|brand_partnership — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assigned_account_manager varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the internal account manager or business development representative responsible for managing this licensing relationship.']
  brand_owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the brand owner or franchise rights holder for brand partnership agreements. Nullable for non-brand-partnership agreements.']
  brand_partnership_type varchar [note: 'Type of brand partnership: co-marketing (joint promotional campaigns), co-development (collaborative game development), character licensing (use of brand characters), franchise adaptation (game based on existing IP), or cross-promotion. Nullable for non-brand-partnership agreements.']
  brand_usage_guidelines_url varchar [note: 'URL or document reference to brand usage guidelines, style guides, and trademark usage requirements for brand partnership agreements. Nullable for non-brand-partnership agreements.']
  co_marketing_obligations varchar [note: 'Description of co-marketing obligations, including minimum spend commitments, promotional activities, and joint campaign requirements for brand partnership agreements. Nullable for non-brand-partnership agreements.']
  compliance_rating_board_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific rating board compliance requirements imposed by the agreement, including ESRB, PEGI, USK, CERO, or GRAC rating restrictions or content guidelines.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this agreement record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the licensing agreement becomes legally binding and enforceable.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the license grants exclusive rights to the licensee within the defined territory and scope. True if exclusive, False if non-exclusive.']
  exclusivity_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the exclusivity terms, including any limitations by platform, genre, or use case (e.g., \'exclusive for mobile RPG games in North America\').']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the licensing agreement terminates or expires. Nullable for perpetual or open-ended agreements.']
  governing_law_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of the agreement (e.g., \'State of California, USA\' or \'England and Wales\').']
  ip_ownership_terms varchar [note: 'Description of intellectual property ownership terms, including whether derivative works, modifications, or improvements belong to licensor, licensee, or are jointly owned.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this agreement record was last updated or modified.']
  licensee_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the party receiving the intellectual property license (the entity authorized to use the IP).']
  licensor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the party granting the intellectual property license (the IP owner or rights holder).']
  middleware_license_type varchar [note: 'Type of middleware license granted: per-title (licensed per game), per-seat (per developer), revenue-share, enterprise (organization-wide), or perpetual. Nullable for non-middleware agreements.']
  middleware_product_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific middleware product or SDK covered by the agreement (e.g., \'Havok Physics Engine\', \'Wwise Audio Middleware\'). Nullable for non-middleware agreements.']
  middleware_product_version varchar [note: 'Version or release number of the middleware product licensed. Nullable for non-middleware agreements or version-agnostic licenses.']
  middleware_usage_scope varchar [note: 'Description of permitted usage scope for middleware, including platform restrictions, title limits, or development team size constraints. Nullable for non-middleware agreements.']
  middleware_vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the middleware vendor for middleware-type agreements. Nullable for non-middleware agreements.']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum guaranteed payment amount that the licensee must pay regardless of actual usage or revenue, typically in advance or over the agreement term. Nullable if no minimum guarantee applies.']
  music_composer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the composer or songwriter for music synchronization licenses. Nullable for non-music agreements.']
  music_media_usage_rights varchar [note: 'Description of permitted media usage rights for music synchronization, including platforms (console, PC, mobile, streaming), territories, and duration. Nullable for non-music agreements.']
  music_pro_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Status of clearance from performing rights organizations (ASCAP, BMI, SESAC, PRS, etc.) for music synchronization licenses. Nullable for non-music agreements.']
  music_publisher_name varchar [note: 'Name of the music publisher holding rights for music synchronization licenses. Nullable for non-music agreements.']
  music_track_title varchar [note: 'Title of the music track or composition for music synchronization licenses. Nullable for non-music agreements.']
  payment_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all financial terms in this agreement (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which royalty or license fee payments are due from the licensee.']
  renewal_negotiation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of renewal negotiations for agreements approaching expiry.']
  renewal_notice_deadline_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before expiry_date by which renewal notice must be provided to trigger automatic renewal or initiate renewal negotiations.']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to revenue or sales for revenue-share or per-unit royalty calculations. Nullable if not applicable to the royalty structure.']
  royalty_structure_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the royalty or payment structure: fixed fee (flat payment), revenue share (percentage of revenue), per-unit (per copy sold), per-seat (per developer license), tiered (rate changes by volume), or hybrid (combination).']
  termination_clause_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of termination conditions and notice requirements, including breach conditions, convenience termination rights, and wind-down obligations.']
  territory_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic territories or regions where the license is valid, specified as comma-separated ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or regional descriptors (e.g., \'USA,CAN,MEX\' or \'worldwide\').']

  Note: 'Master record for all intellectual property licensing agreements, covering outbound engine/technology licensing, inbound licensed IP usage, third-party middleware agreements (physics engines, audio middleware, anti-cheat SDKs, analytics), music synchronization licenses, and brand/franchise partnership deals. Captures agreement type (outbound engine, inbound brand/franchise, music sync, middleware, art, brand partnership), licensor and licensee identities, territory scope, exclusivity terms, effective and expiry dates, governing law jurisdiction, agreement status, licensed IP asset reference, renewal tracking (expiry date, renewal notice deadline, renewal negotiation status, assigned account manager), and agreement-type-specific metadata including: for middleware — vendor, product version, license type (per-title, per-seat, revenue-share), usage scope; for music sync — track title, composer/publisher, PRO clearance status, media usage rights; for brand partnerships — brand owner, partnership type, co-marketing obligations, brand usage guidelines. This is the single source of truth for ALL licensing contracts in the gaming domain regardless of IP type or commercial structure.'
}

Table licensing.licensee {
  licensee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the licensee entity. Primary key for the licensee master data product.']
  annual_revenue_range varchar [note: 'The estimated annual revenue range of the licensee organization in USD. Used for partner segmentation and deal sizing. Ranges: under $1M, $1M-$10M, $10M-$50M, $50M-$100M, $100M-$500M, over $500M.']
  compliance_tier varchar [note: 'The compliance risk tier assigned to the licensee based on their jurisdiction, business practices, and regulatory history. Tier 1 (high risk, enhanced due diligence), Tier 2 (medium risk, standard due diligence), Tier 3 (low risk, simplified due diligence), Tier 4 (trusted partner, minimal oversight).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this licensee record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum credit exposure allowed for this licensee across all active licensing agreements, expressed in USD. Used for financial risk management.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'The credit rating assigned to the licensee by a recognized credit rating agency (e.g., Moody\'s, S&P, Fitch). Used for financial risk assessment and payment term determination.']
  doing_business_as_name varchar [note: 'The trade name or DBA name under which the licensee operates, if different from their legal entity name. Used for brand recognition and marketing purposes.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'The 9-digit DUNS number assigned by Dun & Bradstreet for unique business entity identification. Used for credit checks and business verification.']
  employee_count_range varchar [note: 'The estimated number of employees at the licensee organization. Used for partner segmentation and capacity assessment. Ranges: 1-10, 11-50, 51-200, 201-500, 501-1000, over 1000.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the licensee based on their primary business function in the gaming ecosystem. Indie studio (small independent developer), AAA publisher (major game publisher), platform operator (console/store operator), middleware vendor (engine/tool provider), music publisher (audio IP owner), or brand owner (franchise IP holder).']
  first_contract_date date [note: 'The date when the first licensing agreement with this licensee was executed. Used for relationship tenure analysis and anniversary tracking.']
  headquarters_address_line1 varchar [note: 'The first line of the licensee\'s headquarters street address, including building number and street name.']
  headquarters_address_line2 varchar [note: 'The second line of the licensee\'s headquarters address, typically used for suite, floor, or building information.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'The city where the licensee\'s headquarters is located.']
  headquarters_country_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the licensee\'s headquarters location (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN).']
  headquarters_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code for the licensee\'s headquarters address.']
  headquarters_state_province varchar [note: 'The state, province, or administrative region where the licensee\'s headquarters is located.']
  is_publicly_traded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee is a publicly traded company (True) or privately held (False). Used for financial risk assessment and reporting requirements.']
  jurisdiction_of_incorporation varchar [note: 'The country or jurisdiction where the licensee entity is legally incorporated, using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN).']
  kyc_completion_date date [note: 'The date when the Know Your Customer due diligence process was completed and the licensee was approved for business. Required for compliance and audit purposes.']
  kyc_review_due_date date [note: 'The date when the next periodic KYC review is due for this licensee. Typically annual or biennial depending on compliance tier.']
  last_contract_renewal_date date [note: 'The date when the most recent contract renewal or amendment was executed with this licensee. Used for renewal cycle tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this licensee record was last updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data freshness monitoring.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the licensee organization as registered in their jurisdiction of incorporation. This is the official name used in all licensing contracts and legal documents.']
  licensee_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric business identifier assigned to the licensee for use in contracts, invoicing, and operational systems. Typically 4-12 characters.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for capturing additional notes, special considerations, or context about the licensee that doesn\'t fit in structured fields. Used for relationship management and institutional knowledge.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'The Net Promoter Score for this licensee based on relationship satisfaction surveys. Score ranges from -100 to +100, with higher scores indicating stronger partnership satisfaction.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the licensee in the onboarding and lifecycle management process. Prospect (initial contact), KYC in progress (Know Your Customer due diligence underway), approved (cleared for contracting), active (has active agreements), suspended (temporarily inactive), terminated (relationship ended).']
  preferred_currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the licensee\'s preferred transaction currency (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY). Used for invoicing and payment processing.']
  preferred_payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'The standard payment terms in days negotiated with this licensee for royalty payments or license fees (e.g., Net 30, Net 60). Used for accounts payable and receivable management.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'The primary email address for the licensee contact. This is the main channel for licensing communications, contract negotiations, and operational correspondence.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the primary business contact at the licensee organization responsible for licensing relationship management.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'The primary phone number for the licensee contact, including country code. Used for urgent communications and contract discussions.']
  primary_platform_focus varchar [note: 'The primary gaming platform(s) that the licensee focuses on (e.g., PC, Console, Mobile, VR, Cross-Platform). Free-text field to accommodate multiple platforms and emerging technologies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pc|console|mobile|vr|ar|xr|web|cross_platform — promote to reference product]']
  relationship_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates the direction of the licensing relationship. Licensee (they license FROM Gaming), licensor (they license TO Gaming), or bilateral (both directions active).']
  relationship_manager_name varchar [note: 'The name of the Gaming employee who serves as the primary relationship manager for this licensee. Responsible for contract negotiations, renewals, and ongoing relationship management.']
  specialization_area varchar [note: 'The primary area of specialization or expertise for the licensee (e.g., Game Engine Technology, Audio Middleware, Physics Engine, Animation Tools, Multiplayer Infrastructure, Analytics SDK, Monetization Platform, IP Licensing, Music Licensing). Free-text to accommodate diverse specializations.']
  stock_ticker_symbol varchar [note: 'The stock ticker symbol for the licensee if they are publicly traded. Includes exchange prefix if applicable (e.g., NASDAQ:ATVI, NYSE:EA).']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'The tax identification number or equivalent (EIN, VAT number, etc.) for the licensee in their jurisdiction of incorporation. Used for tax reporting and compliance.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'The primary website URL for the licensee organization. Used for research, verification, and business intelligence.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing external parties who hold licenses from Gaming or grant licenses to Gaming — studios, publishers, developers, platform operators, music publishers, middleware vendors, and brand owners involved in any licensing relationship. Captures legal entity name, entity type (indie studio, AAA publisher, platform operator, middleware vendor, music publisher, brand owner), jurisdiction of incorporation, primary contact, credit rating, compliance tier, onboarding status, and relationship direction (licensee, licensor, or both). This is the SSOT for all external licensing counterparties regardless of deal direction.'
}

Table licensing.licensed_ip {
  licensed_ip_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the licensed intellectual property asset. Primary key for the licensed IP catalog.']
  age_rating_cert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.age_rating_cert. Business justification: Licensed IP (game engines, middleware, branded content) carries age ratings constraining licensee usage. Real process: licensees verify rating compatibility before integration; licensor tracks certifi']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.franchise. Business justification: Franchises often license third-party IP (Marvel, Star Wars, sports leagues). Tracks which franchises depend on external IP rights for portfolio planning and renewal negotiations.']
  active_license_count varchar [note: 'Current number of active licensing agreements in force for this IP asset. Used for portfolio management and capacity planning.']
  asset_thumbnail_url varchar [note: 'URL path to the thumbnail image or visual representation of the IP asset stored in the Content Delivery Network (CDN) or digital asset management system.']
  cero_rating varchar [note: 'CERO content rating classification for Japan (A=All Ages, B=12+, C=15+, D=17+, Z=18+, or Not Rated).']
  content_descriptors varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of content warning descriptors from rating boards (e.g., \'Violence\', \'Language\', \'In-Game Purchases\', \'Online Interactions\'). Used for compliance and parental guidance.']
  coppa_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this IP asset and its associated content meet COPPA compliance requirements for children\'s privacy protection (applicable for content targeting users under 13).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this IP asset record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this IP asset record was first created in the licensing catalog system.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL path to the official licensing documentation, SDK documentation, or technical specifications for this IP asset.']
  drm_protection_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this IP asset includes or requires DRM (Digital Rights Management) protection mechanisms for distribution and licensing enforcement.']
  esrb_rating varchar [note: 'ESRB content rating classification for North America (Early Childhood, Everyone, Everyone 10+, Teen, Mature, Adults Only, Rating Pending, or Not Rated).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the IP registration or protection expires. Null for perpetual rights or unregistered IP.']
  gdpr_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this IP asset and its licensing terms comply with GDPR data protection requirements for EU markets.']
  grac_rating varchar [note: 'GRAC content rating classification for South Korea (All, 12, 15, 18, or Not Rated).']
  ip_category varchar [note: 'High-level category grouping for the IP asset (technology-based, creative content, brand/franchise, or hybrid).']
  ip_code varchar [note: 'Internal business identifier or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) code for the IP asset used in licensing agreements and royalty tracking.']
  ip_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the intellectual property asset, including its purpose, key features, and licensing value proposition.']
  ip_name varchar [note: 'The official name or title of the intellectual property asset (e.g., \'Unreal Engine 5\', \'Fortnite Brand\', \'Epic Soundtrack Collection\').']
  ip_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the IP asset type. Determines licensing model and royalty structure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: game_engine|franchise_brand|game_title|character|music_composition|art_asset_library|middleware_library|rating_approved_content_package — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_licensed_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent licensing agreement for this IP asset was executed. Null if never licensed.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this IP asset record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this IP asset record was last updated in the licensing catalog system.']
  license_model varchar [note: 'Primary licensing business model applied to this IP asset (royalty percentage, fixed fee, subscription, revenue share, hybrid, or not applicable for internal-only IP).']
  licensing_availability_status varchar [note: 'Current availability status of the IP asset for new licensing agreements. Indicates whether the IP can be licensed out and under what terms.']
  marketing_tagline varchar [note: 'Official marketing tagline or slogan associated with the IP asset, used in licensing promotional materials and partner communications.']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard minimum guarantee amount (in USD) required from licensees for this IP asset. Null if no minimum guarantee applies.']
  monetization_restrictions varchar [note: 'Description of any restrictions on how licensees can monetize products built with this IP (e.g., \'No Pay-to-Win mechanics allowed\', \'In-App Purchases (IAP) permitted\', \'Ad-supported only\'). Null if no restrictions.']
  ownership_entity varchar [note: 'The legal entity or studio that owns the intellectual property rights (e.g., \'Epic Games Inc.\', \'Subsidiary Studio LLC\').']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Nature of ownership rights held by the business (wholly owned, joint ownership with partner, licensed from third party, or acquired through M&A).']
  pegi_rating varchar [note: 'PEGI content rating classification for Europe (3, 7, 12, 16, 18, or Not Rated).']
  platform_compatibility varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of gaming platforms or operating systems this IP asset supports or is compatible with (e.g., \'PC, PlayStation, Xbox, Nintendo Switch, iOS, Android\'). Applicable primarily to game engines and middleware.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date when the intellectual property was officially registered with the governing authority.']
  registration_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The legal jurisdiction or territory where the IP is registered (e.g., \'USA\', \'EUR\', \'JPN\', \'Global\'). Uses 3-letter country codes or regional identifiers.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration or patent number assigned by governing IP authority (USPTO, EPO, WIPO, copyright office). May include trademark, patent, or copyright registration identifiers.']
  sdk_version varchar [note: 'Current version number of the SDK or game engine technology if this IP is a licensable technology platform (e.g., \'UE5.3\', \'Unity 2023.1\'). Null for non-technology IP.']
  standard_royalty_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Default royalty rate (as decimal, e.g., 0.0500 for 5%) applied to licensees for this IP asset. Actual rates may vary by agreement. Null if not royalty-based.']
  territory_restrictions varchar [note: 'Geographic territories where licensing of this IP is restricted or prohibited due to legal, regulatory, or contractual constraints. Comma-separated list of 3-letter country codes or \'None\' if unrestricted.']
  third_party_dependencies varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of third-party middleware, libraries, or licensed components embedded within this IP asset that licensees must also comply with (e.g., \'Havok Physics\', \'Wwise Audio\', \'SpeedTree\').']
  total_revenue_to_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative licensing revenue (in USD) generated from this IP asset since inception. Includes royalties, fixed fees, and minimum guarantees.']
  ugc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this IP asset supports or allows user-generated content creation and distribution by licensees or end users.']
  usk_rating varchar [note: 'USK content rating classification for Germany (0, 6, 12, 16, 18, or Not Rated).']

  Note: 'Catalog of all intellectual property assets that are subject to licensing — game engine SDKs (Unity/Unreal-equivalent), franchise brands, game titles, characters, music compositions, art asset libraries, middleware libraries, and rating-board-approved content packages. Captures IP asset name, IP type (engine, brand, music, art, middleware, franchise), ownership entity, registration numbers, territory restrictions, content rating classifications (ESRB, PEGI, USK, CERO, GRAC), and current licensing availability status.'
}

Table licensing.commercial_term {
  commercial_term_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for commercial_term']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent IP licensing agreement to which these commercial terms apply.']
  advance_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Upfront payment made by licensee, typically recoupable against future royalties.']
  advance_recoupment_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed terms describing how advance payments are recouped from future royalty payments (e.g., 100% recoupment, cross-collateralization rules).']
  audit_frequency_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum frequency at which licensor may conduct audits (e.g., once per calendar year, twice per contract term).']
  audit_rights_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing licensors rights to audit licensees books and records to verify royalty calculations and compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commercial term record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this term set (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY).']
  derivative_works_rights varchar [note: 'Specifies licensees rights to create derivative works based on the licensed IP (e.g., sequels, spin-offs, adaptations).']
  exclusivity_window_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months for which platform or territory exclusivity applies before rights expand.']
  indemnification_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed indemnification obligations of each party, including scope of liability, defense obligations, and limitations.']
  late_payment_penalty_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage penalty rate applied to overdue payments, typically expressed as annual percentage rate (APR).']
  liability_cap_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum liability amount either party may be held responsible for under the agreement, excluding certain carve-outs.']
  marketing_approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether licensee must obtain licensor approval for marketing materials, promotional campaigns, and brand usage.']
  marketing_spend_commitment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum marketing spend amount licensee commits to invest in promoting the licensed IP or product.']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum guaranteed payment amount that licensee must pay regardless of actual royalty calculations. Common in IP licensing deals.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commercial term record was last modified.']
  payment_due_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after period end or invoice date by which payment must be received.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which royalty or license fee payments are due (e.g., quarterly, annually).']
  performance_milestone_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed terms describing performance milestones that trigger payments, bonuses, or rights adjustments (e.g., sales thresholds, user acquisition targets).']
  platform_exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement grants exclusive rights to specific gaming platforms (console, PC, mobile).']
  platform_exclusivity_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of platforms covered by exclusivity (e.g., PlayStation, Xbox, Nintendo Switch, PC, iOS, Android).']
  quality_assurance_requirements varchar [note: 'Quality standards and certification requirements licensee must meet, including ESRB, PEGI, or platform Technical Requirements Checklist (TRC) compliance.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiration by which licensee must provide notice to exercise renewal option.']
  renewal_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether licensee has the option to renew the agreement under predefined terms.']
  renewal_option_periods varchar [note: 'Number of renewal periods available to licensee (e.g., two 2-year renewal options).']
  revenue_reporting_due_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after period end by which revenue reports must be submitted to licensor.']
  revenue_reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which licensee must provide revenue and usage reports to licensor for royalty calculation.']
  royalty_basis varchar [note: 'The financial or usage metric upon which royalty calculations are based (e.g., net revenue, gross revenue, units sold).']
  royalty_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to revenue or units to calculate royalty payments owed to licensor. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 5.00 for 5%).']
  sublicensing_permitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether licensee is permitted to sublicense the IP or technology to third parties.']
  sublicensing_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed terms governing sublicensing arrangements, including revenue sharing, approval requirements, and restrictions.']
  term_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this version of commercial terms becomes binding and enforceable.']
  term_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this version of commercial terms ceases to be effective. Nullable for open-ended terms.']
  term_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this commercial term version.']
  term_version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for this term set. Agreements may have multiple versioned term sets reflecting amendments over time.']
  termination_cure_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the breaching party has to cure a breach before termination becomes effective.']
  termination_for_cause_conditions varchar [note: 'Detailed conditions under which either party may terminate the agreement for cause (e.g., material breach, bankruptcy, failure to meet minimum guarantees).']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required for termination, either for cause or for convenience.']
  territory_expansion_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether licensee has the option to expand into additional territories under predefined terms.']
  territory_expansion_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed terms governing territory expansion options, including pricing, approval process, and timing.']
  territory_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of countries or regions where license rights apply, using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes.']

  Note: 'Detailed commercial and legal terms attached to each IP agreement, including royalty rate structures, minimum guarantee amounts, advance payment schedules, audit rights clauses, sublicensing permissions, derivative works rights, platform exclusivity windows, territory expansion options, renewal options, and termination-for-cause conditions. One agreement may have multiple versioned term sets reflecting amendments over time.'
}

Table licensing.royalty_schedule {
  royalty_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the royalty schedule record. Primary key.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent licensing agreement or contract that this royalty schedule governs.']
  superseded_by_schedule_royalty_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the royalty_schedule_id that supersedes this schedule when a new version is activated. Null if this is the current active schedule.']
  audit_frequency_months varchar [note: 'The frequency in months at which audits are required (e.g., 12 for annual, 24 for biennial). Null if audit_required_flag is False.']
  audit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this royalty schedule requires periodic third-party audit of reported figures (True/False).']
  base_rate_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The baseline royalty rate expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.0000 for 5%). Applicable when royalty_basis is revenue-based.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this royalty schedule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which royalty amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
