  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this royalty schedule ceases to be active. Null for open-ended schedules.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this royalty schedule becomes active and applicable to the agreement.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this royalty schedule is tied to an exclusive licensing arrangement (True) or non-exclusive (False).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this royalty schedule record was last updated.']
  late_payment_penalty_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The penalty rate (as a percentage per period) applied to overdue royalty payments. Null if no penalty applies.']
  maximum_cap_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum royalty payment cap per reporting period. Null if no cap applies.']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The minimum royalty payment guaranteed per reporting period, regardless of actual usage or revenue. Null if no minimum guarantee applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional business context, special terms, exceptions, or clarifications related to this royalty schedule.']
  payment_due_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the end of the reporting period by which royalty payment is due (e.g., 30 days, 60 days).']
  platform_deduction_rule varchar [note: 'Specifies whether platform fees (e.g., Apple App Store 30%, Steam 30%, console first-party fees) are deducted before or after calculating royalties.']
  platform_scope varchar [note: 'The gaming platforms or distribution channels to which this royalty schedule applies (e.g., \'Console\', \'PC\', \'Mobile\', \'All Platforms\', \'Steam\', \'Epic Games Store\', \'PlayStation\', \'Xbox\', \'Nintendo Switch\').']
  product_scope varchar [note: 'The specific game titles, SKUs (Stock Keeping Units), or product lines covered by this royalty schedule. May reference specific game IPs (Intellectual Property) or engine versions.']
  rate_structure varchar [note: 'Describes how the royalty rate is applied: flat (single rate), tiered (different rates at different breakpoints), progressive (rate increases with volume), regressive (rate decreases with volume), or custom formula.']
  recoupment_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether advance payments or minimum guarantees can be recouped against future royalty earnings (True/False).']
  reporting_period_cadence varchar [note: 'The frequency at which royalty calculations and payments are reported and settled (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annually).']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'The accounting method used to recognize royalty revenue or expense (accrual, cash basis, milestone-based, or usage-based).']
  royalty_basis varchar [note: 'The fundamental metric or basis upon which royalty amounts are calculated (e.g., net revenue after platform fees, gross revenue, units sold, Daily Active Users (DAU), Monthly Active Users (MAU), or fixed periodic fee). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_revenue|gross_revenue|units_sold|dau_based|mau_based|fixed_fee|hybrid — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  royalty_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the royalty schedule.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name or label for this royalty schedule (e.g., \'Unity Pro Tier 1\', \'Unreal Engine Standard\', \'IP License Q1 2024\').']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the royalty schedule based on the nature of the licensing arrangement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: outbound_engine_licensing|inbound_ip_licensing|middleware_licensing|music_licensing|art_asset_licensing|franchise_partnership|technology_sdk — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  territory_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic territories or regions to which this royalty schedule applies (e.g., \'Worldwide\', \'North America\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\', or specific country codes).']
  tier_1_rate_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The royalty rate percentage applicable to revenue or units within the first tier. Null if not applicable.']
  tier_1_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'The upper boundary value for the first royalty rate tier. Null if rate structure is flat or only one tier exists.']
  tier_2_rate_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The royalty rate percentage applicable to revenue or units within the second tier. Null if not applicable.']
  tier_2_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'The upper boundary value for the second royalty rate tier. Null if fewer than two tiers exist.']
  tier_3_rate_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The royalty rate percentage applicable to revenue or units within the third tier. Null if not applicable.']
  tier_3_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'The upper boundary value for the third royalty rate tier. Null if fewer than three tiers exist.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this royalty schedule, incremented with each amendment or revision to the terms.']
  withholding_tax_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of royalty payment subject to withholding tax, if applicable under the jurisdiction of the licensee or licensor. Null if no withholding applies.']

  Note: 'Defines the royalty calculation rules and rate tiers for each IP agreement — including royalty basis (net revenue, gross revenue, units sold, DAU-based, MAU-based), tiered rate breakpoints, currency, reporting period cadence (monthly, quarterly), applicable platform deductions, and effective date ranges. Supports both outbound engine licensing royalties collected from licensees and inbound royalties owed to IP rights holders.'
}

Table licensing.royalty_report {
  royalty_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the royalty report record. Primary key.']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.dev_project. Business justification: Royalty reports for licensed IP usage (middleware, music, brands) are calculated per-project for revenue attribution and payment reconciliation. Essential for financial reporting and budget variance a']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user or team responsible for reviewing and validating this royalty report.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.game_title. Business justification: Royalty reports track revenue per title for licensed IP (engine royalties, music sync fees). Essential for accurate royalty calculation and licensor reporting.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice generated from this royalty report. Links the report to the billing and accounts receivable process.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensing agreement under which this royalty report is submitted. Links to the master license agreement entity.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Royalty metrics (royalty per user, IP conversion rate, royalty yield) are tracked as business KPIs. Links licensing financial reporting to analytics KPI framework for executive dashboards and performa']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensed_ip. Business justification: Royalty reports should track which specific licensed IP asset generated the revenue. Currently uses product_line (STRING) which is ambiguous. Adding licensed_ip_id FK enables precise IP-level revenue ']
  licensee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party (studio, publisher, or third-party developer) submitting or subject to this royalty report.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Royalty reports may trigger regulatory filings (revenue disclosures, tax withholding reports, cross-border payment notifications). Real process: finance-to-compliance handoff for jurisdictions requiri']
  applicable_royalty_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The royalty rate percentage applied to the net revenue for this reporting period. May vary based on tiered thresholds, product type, or territory as defined in the license agreement.']
  attachment_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to supporting documentation attached to this royalty report (e.g., detailed sales breakdowns, platform reports, reconciliation files).']
  audit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this royalty report has been flagged for audit or detailed review due to discrepancies, thresholds, or compliance requirements.']
  calculated_royalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The royalty amount due to the licensor, calculated by applying the applicable royalty rate to the reported net revenue. This is the amount subject to invoicing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this royalty report record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this report (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or categorization of any dispute raised regarding this royalty report. Populated when report status is disputed or under review.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert foreign currency revenue to the reporting currency, if applicable. Used for multi-currency licensing agreements.']
  minimum_guarantee_credit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of minimum guarantee advance recouped or credited against this royalty payment. Tracks progress toward recoupment of upfront payments.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this royalty report record was last modified. Tracks updates, amendments, or status changes.']
  net_royalty_payable decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net royalty amount payable after applying minimum guarantee credits and any other adjustments. This is the actual payment due to the licensor.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, clarifications, or context provided by the licensee or internal reviewers regarding this royalty report.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the calculated royalty payment is due to the licensor, as specified in the license agreement terms.']
  platform varchar [note: 'Gaming platform or distribution channel for which revenue is being reported (e.g., PlayStation, Xbox, Steam, mobile app stores, PC).']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the royalty report, typically assigned by the licensee or licensing system. Used for external reference and audit trails.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the royalty report. Tracks progression from submission through review, acceptance, or dispute resolution.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the royalty report based on reporting frequency or purpose (e.g., quarterly, monthly, annual, ad-hoc, final settlement).']
  reported_deductions decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total deductions applied to gross revenue as permitted by the license agreement (e.g., platform fees, returns, refunds, taxes). Reduces the royalty base.']
  reported_gross_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross revenue reported by the licensee for the reporting period, before any deductions or adjustments. Basis for royalty calculation.']
  reported_net_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net revenue after deductions, representing the royalty-bearing revenue base. Calculated as gross revenue minus deductions.']
  reported_unit_sales varchar [note: 'Total number of units sold or distributed during the reporting period, as reported by the licensee. May include game copies, DLC, or licensed content units.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the period covered by this royalty report. Defines the end of the revenue or usage window being reported.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the period covered by this royalty report. Defines the beginning of the revenue or usage window being reported.']
  review_completed_date date [note: 'The date on which the internal review of this royalty report was completed and a status decision (accepted, disputed, rejected) was made.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the licensee submitted this royalty report to the licensor. Used to track timeliness and compliance with reporting deadlines.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'The channel or mechanism through which the royalty report was submitted (e.g., licensing portal, email, API integration, manual upload, FTP/SFTP).']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Geographic territory or region covered by this royalty report. May be a country code, regional grouping, or worldwide indicator as defined in the license agreement.']

  Note: 'Periodic royalty reporting records submitted by licensees or generated internally for outbound agreements. Captures reporting period, reported gross and net revenue, reported unit sales, applicable royalty rate tier applied, calculated royalty amount due, currency, submission date, submission method, and report status (submitted, under review, accepted, disputed). Feeds into royalty invoice generation and audit workflows.'
}

Table licensing.royalty_payment {
  royalty_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the royalty payment record. Primary key for the royalty payment entity.']
  iap_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.iap_transaction. Business justification: Royalty payments for revenue-based licenses require direct linkage to source IAP transactions for audit trail, dispute resolution, and reconciliation between reported revenue in royalty_report and act']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing IP licensing agreement under which this royalty payment is made. Links to the master licensing contract that defines royalty terms, rates, and obligations.']
  payee_licensee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensee. Business justification: Royalty payment payees are licensees (either receiving royalties for outbound licenses or paying royalties for inbound licenses). Currently tracked as payee_name (STRING) and payee_identifier (STRING)']
  royalty_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating royalty report that calculated the amounts due. This payment reconciles against the report\'s calculated royalty obligation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Internal approval workflow status for the royalty payment. Indicates whether the payment has been reviewed and authorized by appropriate stakeholders before execution.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who approved this royalty payment for execution. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the royalty payment was approved for execution. Part of the audit trail for payment authorization workflow.']
  bank_reference varchar [note: 'Bank transaction reference or confirmation number provided by the financial institution. Used for payment tracing and audit trail.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this royalty payment record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert the payment amount to the reporting currency, if applicable. Null if payment currency matches reporting currency.']
  late_payment_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this payment was made after the contractual due date. True indicates late payment, which may trigger penalty clauses or interest charges per the licensing agreement.']
  late_payment_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional penalty or interest amount charged or owed due to late payment, as defined in the licensing agreement. Null if payment was on time or no penalty applies.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this royalty payment record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  net_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final net amount paid or received after applying withholding taxes, fees, and any other adjustments. This is the actual cash flow amount.']
  payee_identifier varchar [note: 'External identifier for the payee party, such as a vendor number, licensee code, or partner ID. Used for cross-system reconciliation.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross monetary value of the royalty payment in the original transaction currency, before any adjustments or fees.']
  payment_amount_reporting_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The payment amount converted to the enterprise\'s standard reporting currency using the applied exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting and analytics.']
  payment_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Represents the currency in which the payment was originally denominated.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the royalty payment was executed or received. This is the business event date for cash flow recognition, distinct from audit timestamps.']
  payment_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this is an inbound payment (received from a licensee for use of our IP or game engine technology) or an outbound payment (paid to a rights holder for licensed content, music, art assets, or third-party IP).']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'The contractual due date by which this royalty payment was required to be made or received, as defined in the governing IP agreement. Used to track payment timeliness and late payment penalties.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The financial instrument or mechanism used to execute the royalty payment (e.g., wire transfer, ACH, check, PayPal, platform payout system).']
  payment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this royalty payment. May include special instructions, explanations of adjustments, or contextual information for finance and legal teams.']
  payment_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the reporting period covered by this royalty payment. Defines the conclusion of the usage or revenue period for which royalties are being settled.']
  payment_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the reporting period covered by this royalty payment. Defines the beginning of the usage or revenue period for which royalties are being settled.']
  payment_processor_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transaction fee charged by the payment processor or financial institution for executing the royalty payment. Deducted from the gross payment amount.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'External payment reference number or transaction identifier used for tracking and reconciliation. May be a bank transaction ID, wire transfer reference, or payment processor confirmation number.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the royalty payment. Tracks progression from initiation through clearing and reconciliation against the originating royalty report.']
  reconciliation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes explaining reconciliation variances, disputes, adjustments, or special circumstances related to this payment. Used for audit trail and dispute resolution.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of reconciliation between this payment record and the originating royalty report. Indicates whether the payment amount matches the calculated royalty obligation or if discrepancies exist.']
  reconciliation_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary difference between the payment amount and the expected amount per the royalty report. Positive values indicate overpayment, negative values indicate underpayment. Null if fully reconciled.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of tax withheld from the royalty payment as required by applicable tax jurisdiction regulations. Common for cross-border royalty payments.']
  withholding_tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage rate of withholding tax applied to this royalty payment, as mandated by tax treaty or local tax law. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15%).']

  Note: 'Records of actual royalty payments made or received against royalty reports. Captures payment direction (inbound from licensee, outbound to rights holder), payment amount, currency, exchange rate applied, payment date, payment method, bank reference, reconciliation status against the originating royalty report, and linkage to the governing IP agreement. This is the SSOT for royalty-specific cash flows within the licensing domain — distinct from the billing domain\'s general payment processing records. Supports royalty reconciliation, cash flow forecasting, and audit trail requirements.'
}

Table licensing.entitlement {
  entitlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the entitlement record. Primary key for the entitlement entity.']
  game_server_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.game_server. Business justification: Server-level entitlements for licensed technology (engine versions, middleware modules, DRM systems) enforce operational access control, enable per-server license verification, and support audit trail']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.game_title. Business justification: Entitlements specify which titles are covered by license terms (middleware seats, engine versions). Required for compliance audits and build certification processes.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent license agreement under which this entitlement is granted.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Entitlements are jurisdiction-specific (regional licensing, platform certifications, content restrictions). Real process: entitlement provisioning respects territorial restrictions; compliance teams v']
  licensee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party (organization or individual) who holds this entitlement.']
  server_fleet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.server_fleet. Business justification: Fleet-level entitlements define deployment scope for licensed IP (game engines, middleware stacks), authorize which fleets can use specific technology, and support compliance monitoring and capacity p']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the entitlement becomes active and the licensee can begin exercising the granted rights.']
  audit_frequency_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum frequency at which the licensor can conduct compliance audits (e.g., once per year, twice per contract term).']
  authorized_distribution_channels varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of distribution channels through which the licensee is authorized to distribute the licensed content (e.g., Steam, Epic Games Store, PlayStation Store, Xbox Store, retail, direct download).']
  authorized_platforms varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of gaming platforms on which the licensee is authorized to distribute or use the licensed IP or technology (e.g., PlayStation, Xbox, PC, Mobile, Nintendo Switch).']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entitlement automatically renews at the end of its term unless terminated by either party.']
  compliance_audit_rights boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensor retains the right to audit the licensee\'s usage and compliance with entitlement terms.']
  content_rating_requirements varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of required content ratings by territory and rating board (e.g., ESRB:M for USA, PEGI:18 for Europe, CERO:D for Japan) that must be obtained before distribution.']
  covered_territories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes representing the geographic territories where the entitlement is valid.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the entitlement record was first created in the system.']
  drm_activation_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of device activations permitted per end-user license under the DRM implementation requirements. Null if unlimited or not applicable.']
  drm_offline_grace_period_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours that the licensed content can be used offline without DRM re-authentication. Null if always-online or not applicable.']
  drm_provider varchar [note: 'The DRM technology provider that must be implemented for content distributed under this entitlement to protect intellectual property.']
  engine_version_scope varchar [note: 'Specific game engine versions or SDK versions covered by this entitlement (e.g., Unity 2021.x, Unreal Engine 5.x). Applicable for game engine technology licenses.']
  entitlement_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the entitlement, used in contracts and communications.']
  entitlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the entitlement indicating whether the licensee can exercise the granted rights.']
  entitlement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entitlement based on the type of intellectual property or technology being licensed.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement grants exclusive rights to the licensee within the specified scope (territories, platforms, or use cases).']
  exclusivity_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the scope of exclusivity if the exclusivity flag is true (e.g., exclusive for mobile platforms in North America, exclusive for first-person shooter genre).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the entitlement expires and the licensee can no longer exercise the granted rights. Null for perpetual licenses.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the entitlement record was last updated or modified.']
  max_install_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of installations or deployments permitted under this entitlement. Applicable for SDK, middleware, or technology licenses. Null if unlimited or not applicable.']
  max_seat_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of concurrent developer seats or user licenses permitted under this entitlement for SDK or tool licenses. Null if unlimited or not applicable.']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum guaranteed payment amount that the licensee must pay regardless of actual usage or revenue. Null if no minimum guarantee applies.']
  modification_restrictions varchar [note: 'Specific restrictions or conditions on modifications if modification rights are limited (e.g., no changes to core characters, must maintain brand guidelines, requires licensor approval).']
  modification_rights varchar [note: 'Level of rights granted to the licensee to modify, adapt, or create derivative works from the licensed IP or technology.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of each renewal period if auto-renewal is enabled. Null if not applicable.']
  revenue_threshold_for_royalty decimal(15,2) [note: 'Revenue threshold that must be exceeded before royalty payments commence. Null if royalties apply from the first dollar.']
  royalty_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether royalty payments are required under this entitlement based on usage, revenue, or other metrics.']
  royalty_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate royalty payments if applicable (e.g., percentage of revenue, per-unit fee, per-install fee, tiered structure).']
  royalty_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to the royalty base (e.g., gross revenue, net revenue) if royalty calculation is revenue-based. Null if not applicable.']
  sublicensing_conditions varchar [note: 'Specific terms, restrictions, or approval requirements that apply if sublicensing is permitted under this entitlement.']
  sublicensing_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee is permitted to grant sublicenses to third parties under this entitlement.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the entitlement before the expiry date.']
  territory_restrictions varchar [note: 'Specific restrictions or conditions applied to certain territories, such as digital-only distribution, physical and digital rights, streaming rights, or content modifications required for regional compliance.']
  trademark_usage_guidelines varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand guidelines document or specific requirements for trademark usage if permitted.']
  trademark_usage_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee is permitted to use the licensor\'s trademarks, logos, or brand assets in marketing and distribution.']
  usage_reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which usage reports must be submitted if usage reporting is required.']
  usage_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee is required to submit periodic usage reports (e.g., install counts, active users, revenue) to the licensor.']

  Note: 'Tracks the specific rights, usage entitlements, and territorial scope granted to a licensee under an IP agreement — including authorized platforms, covered territories and territory-specific restrictions (digital-only, physical+digital, streaming), permitted game titles or engine versions, maximum seat/install counts for SDK licenses, authorized distribution channels, sublicensing rights, DRM implementation requirements (provider, activation limits, offline grace period), and platform-specific content rating requirements per territory. Each entitlement record has its own activation date, expiry date, and current status (active, suspended, expired, revoked). This is the SSOT for what a licensee is permitted to do under their agreement.'
}

Table licensing.middleware_agreement {
  middleware_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the middleware licensing agreement record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal employee or team responsible for managing this middleware agreement.']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game development studio primarily using this middleware. Null for enterprise-wide agreements.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.game_title. Business justification: Middleware licenses (Unreal Engine, Havok Physics) are often title-specific with per-title royalties. Essential for royalty calculation and compliance reporting to middleware vendors.']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensed_ip. Business justification: Middleware products (physics engines, audio middleware, etc.) are licensed IP assets that should be cataloged in licensed_ip. This normalization removes redundant product identification fields and ena']
  licensee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party vendor or licensor providing the middleware technology.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the middleware licensing agreement, used in contracts and vendor communications.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the middleware licensing agreement.']
  attribution_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement requires public attribution or credit to the middleware vendor in game credits or documentation (True/False).']
  audit_rights varchar [note: 'Vendor rights to audit licensee usage and revenue reporting: none, annual scheduled audits, on-request, or quarterly.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement automatically renews at the end of each term (True) or requires explicit renewal action (False).']
  compliance_obligations varchar [note: 'Free-text description of specific compliance requirements passed through to game titles using this middleware (e.g., ESRB disclosure requirements, GDPR data handling, platform Technical Requirements Checklist (TRC) compliance).']
  contract_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the signed legal contract document in the document management system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this middleware agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this agreement (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the middleware licensing agreement expires or terminates. Nullable for perpetual or open-ended agreements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the middleware licensing agreement becomes legally binding and usage rights commence.']
  fixed_license_fee decimal(15,2) [note: 'One-time or recurring fixed fee for the middleware license. Null for revenue-share-only agreements.']
  governing_law_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction and governing law for dispute resolution (e.g., State of California, England and Wales).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this middleware agreement record was last updated.']
  license_type varchar [note: 'Licensing model governing usage rights: per-title (licensed per game), per-seat (licensed per developer), revenue-share (royalty-based), perpetual (one-time fee), subscription (recurring), or enterprise (unlimited internal use).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special terms, or internal comments about the middleware agreement.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required to terminate or opt out of renewal. Null if not applicable.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence of license fee or royalty payments to the middleware vendor.']
  permitted_platforms varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of gaming platforms where the middleware may be deployed (e.g., PlayStation, Xbox, PC, iOS, Android, Nintendo Switch).']
  post_termination_rights varchar [note: 'Description of rights and obligations after agreement termination (e.g., continued use in already-shipped titles, removal from future releases, wind-down period).']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary business contact at the middleware vendor.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary business contact at the middleware vendor for this agreement.']
  redistribution_rights varchar [note: 'Defines rights to redistribute middleware components: none (internal use only), embedded-only (within compiled game), limited (with restrictions), or full redistribution.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months for automatic renewal periods. Null if agreement does not auto-renew.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of game revenue owed to the middleware vendor under revenue-share licensing model. Null if not revenue-share based.']
  revenue_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum revenue threshold before revenue-share royalties apply (e.g., first $1M exempt). Null if no threshold exists.']
  source_code_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement grants access to middleware source code (True) or binary-only distribution (False).']
  support_entitlement varchar [note: 'Level of technical support and maintenance included with the middleware license (e.g., basic email support, premium 24/7 support).']
  termination_conditions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of conditions under which either party may terminate the agreement (e.g., breach of terms, non-payment, change of control).']
  update_entitlement varchar [note: 'Scope of software updates and upgrades included: patches only, minor version updates, major version updates, or all updates.']
  usage_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the breadth of permitted usage: single game title, multiple titles, specific studio, entire enterprise, or restricted to specific platforms (console, PC, mobile).']

  Note: 'Master records for third-party middleware licensing agreements — covering physics engines, audio middleware (FMOD, Wwise), anti-cheat SDKs, analytics SDKs, voice chat libraries, and other embedded third-party software components used in game development. Captures middleware vendor, product name and version, license type (per-title, per-seat, revenue-share), usage scope, renewal terms, and compliance obligations passed through to game titles.'
}

Table licensing.music_sync_license {
  music_sync_license_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the music synchronization license record.']
  age_rating_cert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.age_rating_cert. Business justification: Music licenses must comply with game\'s age rating (explicit lyrics restrictions for Teen vs Mature). Real process: content review ensures licensed music matches rating; legal teams verify compliance b']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.dev_project. Business justification: Music sync licenses are acquired for specific game projects. Project-level tracking enables budget allocation, milestone planning, and compliance verification for licensed music usage in development.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title in which the licensed music will be used.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent licensing agreement under which this sync license is granted.']
  licensee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party (composer, publisher, or label) that owns the synchronization rights to the music track.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether rights holder approval is required before each use of the licensed music (true) or not (false).']
  composer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the composer or songwriter who created the musical composition.']
  composition_rights_status varchar [note: 'Status of musical composition rights clearance (cleared, pending, not required, denied, or conditional).']
  content_rating_restrictions varchar [note: 'Game content rating restrictions for the licensed music (e.g., ESRB ratings, PEGI ratings). Pipe-separated list of allowed ratings.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this synchronization license record was first created in the system.']
  credit_attribution_required varchar [note: 'Specific credit or attribution text that must be displayed when the music is used (e.g., in game credits, promotional materials).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this license.']
  derivative_works_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee is permitted to create derivative works or remixes of the licensed music (true) or not (false).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the synchronization license expires or terminates. Null for perpetual licenses.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the synchronization license becomes effective and usage rights commence.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the synchronization license grants exclusive rights (true) or non-exclusive rights (false).']
  flat_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'One-time flat fee payment amount for the synchronization license, if applicable.']
  isrc_code varchar [note: 'International Standard Recording Code uniquely identifying the sound recording.']
  iswc_code varchar [note: 'International Standard Musical Work Code uniquely identifying the musical composition.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this synchronization license record was last updated or modified.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier or contract number for this music synchronization license.']
  license_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the synchronization license (draft, pending approval, active, suspended, expired, terminated, or renewed). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|expired|terminated|renewed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  licensed_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration of the music track or excerpt licensed for synchronization, measured in seconds.']
  master_recording_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the party (typically a record label or artist) that owns the master recording rights.']
  master_recording_rights_status varchar [note: 'Status of master recording rights clearance (cleared, pending, not required, denied, or conditional).']
  media_usage_rights varchar [note: 'Specific media contexts where the music may be synchronized (e.g., in-game background music, cinematic trailer, esports broadcast, streaming platform, promotional materials). Pipe-separated list.']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum guaranteed payment amount to the rights holder regardless of actual usage or sales.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special terms related to this synchronization license.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which royalty payments are made to the rights holder (one-time, monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual, or per milestone).']
  per_unit_royalty_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Royalty amount payable per unit sold (e.g., per game copy sold), if applicable.']
  platform_rights varchar [note: 'Gaming platforms for which synchronization rights are granted (e.g., console, PC, mobile, VR, cloud gaming). Pipe-separated list.']
  pro_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Status of clearance with the Performance Rights Organization for public performance rights (cleared, pending, not required, or denied).']
  pro_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Performance Rights Organization managing the performance rights (e.g., ASCAP, BMI, SESAC, PRS, SOCAN).']
  publisher_name varchar [note: 'Name of the music publisher that controls the publishing rights to the composition.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of revenue shared with the rights holder as royalty payment, if applicable.']
  royalty_type varchar [note: 'Type of royalty payment structure for the synchronization license (flat fee, per unit sold, per stream, revenue share, or hybrid).']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date when the synchronization license agreement was signed by all parties.']
  sublicensing_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee is permitted to sublicense the synchronization rights to third parties (true) or not (false).']
  sync_license_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the synchronization license based on duration and scope (perpetual, term-limited, project-specific, platform-specific, territory-specific, or buyout).']
  territory_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic territories or regions where the synchronization rights are granted (e.g., worldwide, North America, specific countries). Pipe-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or region names.']
  track_title varchar [note: 'The title of the music track or composition being licensed for synchronization.']
  usage_restrictions varchar [note: 'Specific restrictions or limitations on how the music may be used (e.g., no use in violent scenes, no association with certain content ratings, no use in competitive titles).']

  Note: 'Licensing records for music synchronization rights used in game soundtracks, trailers, and in-game audio. Captures music track title, composer/publisher rights holder, sync license type (perpetual, term-limited), territory scope, media usage rights (in-game, trailer, esports broadcast, streaming), licensed duration, master recording rights status, performance rights organization (PRO) clearance status, and royalty obligation per use.'
}

Table licensing.brand_partnership {
  brand_partnership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the brand partnership licensing agreement. Primary key.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.game_title. Business justification: Brand partnerships (Nike in FIFA, Porsche in racing games) are title-specific. Required for revenue share calculation, content approval workflows, and marketing rights management.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.ip_agreement. Business justification: Brand partnerships are a specialized type of IP licensing agreement. Linking to ip_agreement provides the master agreement framework (effective dates, status, governing law, termination clauses) while']
  licensee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensee. Business justification: Brand partnerships involve external brand owners who are licensees. The brand_owner_name and brand_owner_contact_email should be normalized to the licensee table. This FK enables joining to get full l']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Brand deals impose compliance requirements (advertising standards, age-appropriate placement, alcohol/tobacco restrictions). Real process: partnership approval workflow checks regulatory constraints b']
  server_fleet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.server_fleet. Business justification: Branded content partnerships (e.g., Marvel, Star Wars) deployed on specific fleets require operational tracking for partnership obligations, co-marketing requirements, usage reporting, and compliance ']
  approval_turnaround_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of business days the brand owner has to review and approve submitted materials. Null if no approval workflow.']
  approval_workflow_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether brand owner approval is required for content, marketing materials, or product releases (True) or not (False).']
  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the brand owner may conduct audits (annual for once per year, semi-annual for twice per year, quarterly for four times per year, on-demand for as needed, none if no audit rights).']
  audit_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the brand owner has the right to audit revenue, usage, or compliance (True) or not (False).']
  brand_usage_guidelines_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the brand owner\'s official usage guidelines, style guide, or brand standards documentation.']
  co_marketing_obligation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partnership includes mandatory co-marketing or cross-promotional activities (True) or not (False).']
  co_marketing_requirements varchar [note: 'Detailed description of co-marketing obligations (e.g., \'Minimum 3 social media posts per quarter\', \'Joint press release at launch\', \'Brand logo in all trailers\').']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory and legal compliance obligations specific to this partnership (e.g., \'COPPA compliance for child-targeted content\', \'GDPR data processing agreement\', \'PCI DSS for payment handling\').']
  contract_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the signed legal contract document in the document management system.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this brand partnership record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this brand partnership record was first created in the system (format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this partnership (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\').']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the brand partnership agreement expires or terminates. Null for perpetual or open-ended agreements (format: yyyy-MM-dd).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the brand partnership agreement becomes legally binding and rights become active (format: yyyy-MM-dd).']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partnership grants exclusive rights (True) or non-exclusive rights (False) to use the licensed brand assets.']
  exclusivity_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed description of exclusivity conditions, restrictions, and carve-outs (e.g., \'Exclusive for sports simulation genre only\', \'Non-exclusive with right of first refusal\').']
  fixed_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'One-time or periodic fixed payment amount for the partnership. Null if royalty-based model.']
  game_title_scope varchar [note: 'Specific game titles or product lines covered by this partnership (e.g., \'FIFA 24\', \'Marvel\'s Spider-Man Series\', \'All Mobile Titles\').']
  ip_ownership_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed terms regarding IP ownership of derivative works, new characters, or co-created content (e.g., \'All derivative IP owned by brand owner\', \'Joint ownership of new characters\').']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this brand partnership record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this brand partnership record was last updated in the system (format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  licensed_brand_assets varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific brand assets covered by the license (character names, logos, trademarks, visual designs, audio elements, storylines, etc.).']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum guaranteed payment to brand owner regardless of actual sales performance. Null if no MG applies.']
  partnership_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the brand partnership (e.g., \'FIFA World Cup 2024 License\', \'Marvel Cinematic Universe Character Pack\').']
  partnership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the brand partnership agreement (draft for initial creation, negotiation for under discussion, active for in force, suspended for temporarily paused, terminated for ended early, expired for naturally ended, renewed for extended). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|negotiation|active|suspended|terminated|expired|renewed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  partnership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the brand partnership agreement type (character license for fictional characters, franchise license for entire IP universes, sports league for official league/team rights, celebrity likeness for real person representation, music rights for soundtrack/score, art asset license for visual content).']
  performance_milestones varchar [note: 'Specific performance targets or milestones tied to the partnership (e.g., \'Launch within 12 months\', \'Achieve 1M DAU (Daily Active Users) in first quarter\', \'Minimum $5M revenue in year 1\').']
  platform_scope varchar [note: 'Gaming platforms covered by the license (e.g., \'Console, PC, Mobile\', \'PlayStation and Xbox Only\', \'Mobile Exclusive\').']
  rating_board_restrictions varchar [note: 'Content rating restrictions imposed by the brand owner (e.g., \'Must maintain ESRB E10+ or lower\', \'No M-rated content allowed\', \'PEGI 12 maximum\').']
  renewal_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partnership includes an option to renew or extend (True) or not (False).']
  renewal_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed description of renewal conditions, notice periods, and renegotiation terms (e.g., \'Auto-renew for 2 years with 90-day notice\', \'Right of first refusal on renewal\').']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'How often revenue, usage, or performance reports must be provided to the brand owner (monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, or annual).']
  revenue_share_model varchar [note: 'Financial structure of the partnership (fixed fee for flat payment, percentage royalty for revenue share, hybrid for combination, minimum guarantee plus royalty for MG+royalty structure).']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of revenue paid to brand owner as royalty (e.g., 5.00 for 5% of net revenue). Null if fixed fee model.']
  termination_clause varchar [note: 'Conditions under which either party may terminate the partnership early (e.g., \'Breach of contract\', \'Material adverse change\', \'180-day notice for convenience\').']
  territory_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic territories where the licensed brand assets may be used (e.g., \'Worldwide\', \'North America Only\', \'EU and APAC\').']

  Note: 'Master records for franchise and brand partnership licensing deals — covering co-branded game titles, licensed character integrations (e.g., sports leagues, film franchises, celebrity likenesses), cross-promotional campaigns, and esports sponsorship IP rights. Captures brand owner, partnership type, licensed brand assets, exclusivity terms, co-marketing obligations, revenue share structure, approval workflow requirements, and brand usage guidelines reference.'
}

Table licensing.compliance_obligation {
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the compliance obligation record. Primary key.']
  cdn_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.cdn_node. Business justification: CDN-level compliance obligations (data residency laws, content filtering requirements, regional restrictions) enforce where content can be cached and served, supporting GDPR compliance, rating board r']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.game_studio. Business justification: Studio-level compliance obligations (COPPA, GDPR, ISO27001, platform certifications) apply across all studio operations. Required for studio onboarding, contract renewals, and regulatory audits.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent IP agreement or licensing contract from which this compliance obligation arises.']
  linked_game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game title to which this compliance obligation applies, if applicable.']
  linked_platform_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the platform (console, PC, mobile) to which this compliance obligation applies, if platform-specific.']
  network_region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.network_region. Business justification: Region-specific compliance obligations (GDPR, COPPA, data sovereignty laws, rating board jurisdiction) determine operational constraints for each network region, guide deployment decisions, and ensure']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee responsible for ensuring this compliance obligation is fulfilled.']
  primary_compliance_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this compliance obligation record.']
  server_fleet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.server_fleet. Business justification: Fleet-level compliance obligations (regional certification requirements, platform-specific restrictions, content rating enforcement) define deployment constraints, guide capacity planning, and ensure ']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when the governing body or counterparty approved the submitted compliance deliverables.']
  audit_submission_requirement varchar [note: 'Details of audit reports, financial statements, or compliance documentation that must be submitted to the licensor or regulatory body.']
  completion_date date [note: 'The actual date when the compliance obligation was fulfilled and marked as complete.']
  content_restriction_clause varchar [note: 'Specific content restrictions imposed by the IP agreement or regulatory body (e.g., no violence, age-appropriate content, no gambling mechanics).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance obligation record was first created in the system.']
  data_handling_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific data privacy and handling obligations tied to licensed player-facing features (e.g., GDPR consent requirements, COPPA parental consent, data retention limits).']
  drm_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific DRM implementation requirements mandated by the IP agreement or platform (e.g., Denuvo, Steamworks DRM, platform-native DRM).']
  due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which this compliance obligation must be fulfilled to avoid breach or penalty.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date when this compliance obligation becomes active and enforceable under the governing agreement or regulation.']
  escalation_contact varchar [note: 'Email address of the internal or external contact to escalate to if this obligation is at risk of breach.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'The regulatory or industry body that mandates this obligation (e.g., ESRB, PEGI, GDPR Supervisory Authority, FTC, platform holder). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ESRB|PEGI|USK|CERO|GRAC|FTC|GDPR_Authority|COPPA|PCI_SSC|ISO_IEC|Apple|Google|Sony|Microsoft|Nintendo|Other — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_blocking_release boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this compliance obligation must be fulfilled before a game title can be released (true) or is non-blocking (false).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the jurisdiction where this obligation applies (e.g., USA, DEU, JPN, KOR).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance obligation record was last updated.']
  next_recurrence_date date [note: 'For recurring obligations, the date when the next instance of this obligation is due.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, clarifications, or internal comments about this compliance obligation.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique code identifying the compliance obligation type (e.g., ESRB_SUBMIT, GDPR_DPA, TRC_CERT).']
  obligation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the compliance obligation, including specific requirements, deliverables, and conditions that must be met.']
  obligation_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the compliance obligation (e.g., \'ESRB Rating Submission\', \'GDPR Data Processing Agreement\').']
  obligation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the compliance obligation: pending (not started), in progress (work underway), submitted (awaiting review), approved (accepted by governing body), rejected (failed review), completed (fulfilled), overdue (past due date), or waived (exempted). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|in_progress|submitted|approved|rejected|completed|overdue|waived — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Category of compliance obligation: rating board submission (ESRB, PEGI, USK, CERO, GRAC), content restriction clause, data privacy requirement (GDPR, COPPA), DRM implementation, platform certification (TRC/TCR), audit submission, royalty reporting, or other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rating_board_submission|content_restriction|data_privacy|drm_implementation|platform_certification|audit_submission|royalty_reporting|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'The department or business unit responsible for managing this compliance obligation (e.g., Legal, Compliance, Publishing, Platform Relations).']
  penalty_for_breach varchar [note: 'Description of penalties, fines, or consequences if this compliance obligation is not met (e.g., financial penalty, license termination, platform delisting).']
  platform_certification_requirement varchar [note: 'Platform-specific certification obligations (TRC for Sony, TCR for Microsoft, lotcheck for Nintendo) that must be met before release.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this obligation based on risk, contractual importance, and regulatory impact.']
  recurrence_frequency varchar [note: 'Indicates how often this obligation recurs: one-time, monthly, quarterly, annually, per game release, or event-driven (triggered by specific business events).']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Calculated risk score (0-100) representing the business impact and likelihood of breach for this obligation. Higher scores indicate higher risk.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date when deliverables or documentation for this obligation were submitted to the governing body or counterparty.']
  supporting_documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to supporting documentation, guidelines, or reference materials for this compliance obligation.']

  Note: 'Tracks specific compliance obligations arising from IP agreements — including rating board submission requirements (ESRB, PEGI, USK, CERO, GRAC), content restriction clauses, GDPR/COPPA data handling obligations tied to licensed player-facing features, DRM implementation requirements, platform certification obligations (TRC/TCR), and audit submission deadlines. Each obligation has an owner, due date, status, and linkage to the governing IP agreement.'
}

Table licensing.audit_event {
  audit_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the licensing audit or dispute event. Primary key.']
  claimant_party_licensee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party initiating the dispute claim (IP owner, licensor, or third party).']
  game_server_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.game_server. Business justification: License audits targeting specific servers (middleware usage verification, DRM compliance checks) require server-level scope tracking for investigation, evidence collection, and remediation of unauthor']
  infrastructure_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.infrastructure_incident. Business justification: License compliance incidents (DRM failures, unauthorized middleware usage, entitlement violations) trigger infrastructure incidents requiring coordinated response. Links audit events to operational in']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the license agreement being audited or disputed.']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific IP asset (game title, engine SDK, music track, art asset) that is the subject of the dispute.']
  licensee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensee party being audited or involved in the dispute.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Audits verify compliance with specific obligations (royalty reporting accuracy, usage rights adherence). Real process: audit scoping identifies obligations to test; findings reference violated obligat']
  respondent_party_licensee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party responding to the dispute claim (alleged infringer or licensee).']
  server_fleet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.server_fleet. Business justification: Fleet-level license audits (engine compliance, middleware usage verification) define audit scope, enable aggregated compliance reporting, and support dispute resolution for technology licensing agreem']
  audit_period_end_date date [note: 'The ending date of the period covered by the audit or dispute.']
  audit_period_start_date date [note: 'The beginning date of the period covered by the audit or dispute.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual auditor, audit firm, or legal counsel assigned to the event.']
  auditor_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or firm conducting the audit (e.g., Big Four accounting firm, law firm).']
  auditor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entity conducting the audit or investigating the dispute.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Official court or arbitration case number if the dispute has entered formal legal proceedings.']
  compliance_action_required varchar [note: 'Description of any corrective actions, process changes, or compliance measures required as a result of the audit findings.']
  confidentiality_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audit findings or settlement terms are subject to a confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit event record was first created in the system.']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount in dispute (underpaid royalties, claimed damages, settlement demand). Null for non-financial disputes.']
  disputed_amount_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the disputed amount (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY).']
  event_completed_date date [note: 'The date the audit was finalized or the dispute was resolved or closed.']
  event_initiated_date date [note: 'The date the audit was scheduled or the dispute claim was filed.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit or dispute event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|in_progress|findings_issued|disputed|in_mediation|settled|litigated|closed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit or dispute event. Distinguishes between audit types (financial, technical, compliance) and dispute types (infringement, underpayment, unauthorized use). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: financial_royalty_audit|sdk_usage_audit|content_compliance_audit|ip_infringement_claim|royalty_underpayment_dispute|unauthorized_territory_use|unlicensed_derivative_work|contract_breach_claim — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of audit findings, compliance gaps, or dispute allegations. Detailed findings are stored in separate audit detail records.']
  follow_up_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the follow-up audit, if applicable.']
  follow_up_audit_scheduled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up audit has been scheduled to verify compliance with corrective actions.']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'The body of law governing the license agreement and dispute resolution (e.g., California Commercial Code, UK Contract Law).']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and commentary for audit management and dispute tracking. Not shared externally.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The legal jurisdiction or territory governing the audit or dispute (e.g., USA-California, EU-Germany, JPN).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit event record was last updated.']
  legal_counsel_assigned varchar [note: 'Name of the legal counsel or law firm assigned to represent the company in the dispute.']
  litigation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute has escalated to formal litigation or court proceedings.']
  mediation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute is currently in or has entered formal mediation or arbitration.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this record.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date a settlement agreement was signed or a judgment was issued.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome classification of the audit or dispute event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: no_findings|minor_adjustments|material_breach_identified|settlement_reached|litigation_pending|judgment_for_claimant|judgment_for_respondent|dismissed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final agreed settlement or adjustment amount. Null if event is not yet settled.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the settlement amount.']
  settlement_terms_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of settlement terms, including payment schedules, license modifications, or non-monetary remedies. Detailed terms are stored separately.']
  trigger_reason varchar [note: 'The reason or mechanism that initiated the audit or dispute event.']

  Note: 'Records of formal licensing audits, compliance reviews, and IP dispute resolution events. For audits: captures audit type (financial royalty audit, SDK usage audit, content compliance audit), audit trigger (scheduled, for-cause, random), audit period covered, auditor identity (internal or third-party), findings summary, disputed amount, resolution status, and final settlement outcome. For disputes: captures dispute type (infringement claim, royalty underpayment, unauthorized territory use, unlicensed derivative work), claimant and respondent identities, disputed IP asset, claim amount, legal counsel assigned, dispute status (open, in mediation, settled, litigated), resolution date, and settlement terms. This is the single source of truth for all compliance events, audit findings, and IP dispute resolution within the licensing domain.'
}

Table licensing.agreement_amendment {
  agreement_amendment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the IP agreement amendment record. Primary key.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent IP agreement being amended. Links this amendment to the master licensing agreement.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date when the amendment was formally drafted or proposed. Represents the business event timestamp for the amendment creation.']
  amendment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the changes introduced by this amendment, including specific clauses modified, new terms added, or provisions removed. Supports legal and audit requirements for amendment history.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential or coded identifier for this amendment within the context of the parent agreement (e.g., AMD-001, AMD-002). Business identifier for the amendment.']
  amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the amendment: draft (being prepared), pending_approval (under review), approved (authorized but not yet signed), executed (fully signed and active), rejected (declined), or superseded (replaced by newer amendment).']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the amendment by its primary purpose: rate adjustment (pricing changes), territory expansion (geographic scope), term extension (duration), scope modification (deliverables/rights), royalty change (payment terms), or termination clause (exit conditions).']
  approval_chain varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of roles or individuals who must approve this amendment (e.g., Legal Counsel, CFO, VP Licensing, General Counsel). Tracks the approval workflow.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who provided final approval for this amendment. Nullable if amendment is not yet approved.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the amendment received final approval. Nullable if amendment is not yet approved.']
  compliance_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of the amendments impact on regulatory compliance obligations (ESRB, PEGI, GDPR, COPPA, etc.). Ranges from none (no compliance impact) to critical (significant compliance implications).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this amendment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for financial amounts in this amendment (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the physical or digital amendment document stored in the document management system. Supports audit trail and legal discovery.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the amendment terms become legally binding and operational. May differ from amendment_date if the amendment is executed with a future effective date.']
  executed_by_licensee varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the licensee representative who signed the amendment. Nullable if amendment is not yet executed.']
  executed_by_licensor varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the licensor representative who signed the amendment. Nullable if amendment is not yet executed.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date when the amendment was fully executed (signed by all parties). Nullable if amendment is not yet executed.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the amendment terms expire or are no longer applicable. Nullable for amendments that remain in effect for the life of the agreement.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of this amendment on the agreement value, expressed in the agreements base currency. Positive for revenue increases, negative for decreases. Nullable if financial impact is not quantified.']
  legal_review_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether legal review has been completed for this amendment. True if review is complete, False otherwise. Nullable if legal review is not required.']
  legal_review_date date [note: 'Date when legal review was completed. Nullable if legal review is not required or not yet completed.']
  legal_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment requires formal legal counsel review before execution. True if legal review is mandatory, False otherwise.']
  legal_reviewer varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the legal counsel who reviewed this amendment. Nullable if legal review is not required or not yet completed.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified this amendment record. Supports audit trail requirements.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this amendment record was last modified in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  new_minimum_guarantee decimal(15,2) [note: 'Updated minimum guarantee payment amount introduced by this amendment. Represents the floor payment obligation regardless of actual usage or sales. Nullable if amendment does not affect minimum guarantees.']
  new_royalty_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Updated royalty percentage or rate introduced by this amendment. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0500 for 5%). Nullable if amendment does not affect royalty rates.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or context about this amendment. Supports internal documentation and knowledge transfer.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date when formal notification of this amendment was sent to stakeholders. Nullable if notification has not been sent.']
  notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal notification of this amendment has been sent to all relevant stakeholders. True if notification sent, False otherwise.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the amendment was rejected, if applicable. Nullable if amendment was not rejected.']
  scope_change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of changes to the agreement scope, including new IP rights granted, restrictions added, or deliverables modified. Supports tracking of scope evolution.']
  superseded_term_version varchar [note: 'Identifier of the previous agreement version or amendment that this amendment replaces or modifies. Maintains lineage of contract evolution.']
  term_extension_months varchar [note: 'Number of months by which the agreement term is extended by this amendment. Positive integer for extensions, negative for reductions. Nullable if amendment does not affect term duration.']
  territory_added varchar [note: 'Geographic territories or markets added to the agreement scope by this amendment. Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or region names.']
  territory_removed varchar [note: 'Geographic territories or markets removed from the agreement scope by this amendment. Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or region names.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this amendment record. Supports audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Tracks formal amendments to IP agreements — capturing amendment number, amendment date, description of changes (rate adjustments, territory expansions, term extensions, scope modifications), approval chain, effective date of amendment, and the superseded term version. Maintains a complete amendment history for each IP agreement to support legal and audit requirements.'
}

Table licensing.usage_report {
  usage_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the usage report record. Primary key.']
  game_server_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.game_server. Business justification: Server-level usage reporting for licensed technology (CCU on middleware-enabled servers, engine runtime hours) provides granular data for royalty calculation, compliance verification, and operational ']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the license agreement under which this usage is being reported.']
  licensee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensee party submitting this usage report.']
  server_fleet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.server_fleet. Business justification: Fleet-level usage aggregation for licensed technology (total CCU, aggregate runtime, deployment scale) supports royalty reporting, license compliance verification, and capacity planning for middleware']
  audit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this usage report requires a formal audit due to variance, dispute, or contractual terms.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the licensee contact person for follow-up or clarification regarding this usage report.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the licensee contact person responsible for this usage report submission.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this usage report record was first created in the system.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'Date when the dispute was raised regarding this usage report.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the usage report is disputed, if applicable.']
  expected_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Expected or forecasted usage value for the reporting period based on historical trends or contractual projections.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this usage report record was last modified.']
  metric_type varchar [note: 'Type of operational consumption metric being reported (e.g., SDK active seat counts, DAU/MAU figures, impression counts, API call volumes). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active_seats|deployment_count|dau|mau|impression_count|play_count|api_calls — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments provided by the licensee or licensor regarding this usage report.']
  platform varchar [note: 'Gaming platform on which the licensed IP or technology was used during the reporting period. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: console|pc|mobile|web|vr|ar|cloud — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  prior_period_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reported value from the previous reporting period for variance comparison.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for this usage report, typically assigned by the licensee or system.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the usage report in the verification and acceptance workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|verified|disputed|accepted|rejected — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the usage report based on the type of licensed IP or technology being reported.']
  reported_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Numeric value of the usage metric reported by the licensee for the reporting period.']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'End date of the period covered by this usage report.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the period covered by this usage report.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the dispute or variance issue was resolved.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes explaining how the dispute or variance was resolved.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the licensee submitted this usage report to the licensor.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the usage report was submitted, including time zone information.']
  supporting_documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to supporting documentation provided by the licensee (e.g., analytics exports, SDK logs, platform reports).']
