  territory varchar [note: 'Geographic territory or market where the usage occurred, represented as ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code.']
  title_name varchar [note: 'Name of the game title or product in which the licensed IP or technology was deployed, if applicable.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the reported value is measured (e.g., seats, users, impressions, API calls). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seats|deployments|users|impressions|plays|calls|sessions|downloads — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  variance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the reported usage shows significant variance compared to prior periods or expected values.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance of the reported value compared to the prior period or expected baseline.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the usage report was verified by the licensor.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the licensor\'s verification process for the reported usage data.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or system that verified the usage report.']

  Note: 'Periodic usage reports submitted by licensees documenting actual consumption of licensed IP assets — including engine SDK active seat counts, title deployment counts by platform, DAU/MAU figures for GaaS titles using licensed IP, in-game branded asset impression counts, music play counts, and API call volumes for middleware. Captures reporting period, submission date, reported metric type, reported value, verification status, and variance flags against prior periods. Feeds royalty calculation engines and compliance monitoring workflows. Distinct from royalty_report which captures financial figures — usage_report captures operational consumption metrics.'
}

Table licensing.rating_submission {
  rating_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the rating submission record. Primary key for the rating submission entity.']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.dev_project. Business justification: Rating board submissions (ESRB, PEGI, CERO) are project-specific deliverables tracked alongside development milestones. Required for release gate status and platform certification workflows.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title being submitted for rating. Links to the game title master data.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensing agreement under which this rating submission is required. Links to the license agreement master data.']
  network_region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.network_region. Business justification: Rating board submissions (ESRB, PEGI, USK) determine which regions can legally serve content. Links submission to deployment region for operational enforcement, content delivery restrictions, and comp']
  publisher_licensee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensee. Business justification: Publishers submitting rating applications are licensees. Currently tracked as publisher_name (STRING). Normalizing to publisher_licensee_id FK enables linking to full licensee details (legal entity, c']
  appeal_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an appeal was filed challenging the assigned rating. True if appeal submitted, False otherwise.']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'The result of the rating appeal process. Upheld (original rating confirmed), overturned (rating changed), modified (rating adjusted), withdrawn (appeal voluntarily dropped). Null if no appeal filed or appeal pending.']
  assigned_rating varchar [note: 'The official rating assigned by the rating board (e.g., ESRB: E, E10+, T, M, AO; PEGI: 3, 7, 12, 16, 18). Null if rating not yet assigned or submission rejected.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting compliance considerations, special circumstances, rating board feedback, or internal observations related to this submission. Used for audit trail and knowledge management.']
  content_descriptors varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of content descriptors submitted to the rating board describing the nature of content in the game (e.g., violence, language, sexual content, drug reference, gambling). Descriptors vary by rating board.']
  content_modifications_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rating board mandated content modifications as a condition of the assigned rating. True if modifications required, False if content approved as-is.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rating submission record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  developer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the game development studio that created the title being rated.']
  expected_release_date date [note: 'The anticipated commercial release date for the game title. Used by rating boards to prioritize review timelines and ensure rating availability before launch.']
  interactive_elements varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of interactive elements disclosed in the submission (e.g., users interact, shares location, in-game purchases, unrestricted internet, digital purchases). Required by most rating boards for online-enabled games.']
  ip_ownership_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the submitter has confirmed ownership or licensing rights to all intellectual property (IP) contained in the game, including third-party assets, music, art, and franchise elements. Required for compliance with IP licensing terms.']
  modification_compliance_date date [note: 'The date by which mandated content modifications must be completed and verified. Null if no modifications required.']
  modification_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of content modifications mandated by the rating board. Includes specific scenes, dialogue, imagery, or gameplay elements that must be altered or removed. Null if no modifications required.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rating submission record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the submission fee was paid. Null if payment not yet received.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the submission fee payment. Pending (awaiting payment), paid (payment received), overdue (payment past due), waived (fee exempted), refunded (payment returned).']
  platform varchar [note: 'Target gaming platform(s) for which the rating is being requested. May include console (PlayStation, Xbox, Nintendo), PC, mobile (iOS, Android), or multi-platform designations.']
  rating_board varchar [note: 'The rating board organization to which this submission was made. ESRB (Entertainment Software Rating Board - North America), PEGI (Pan European Game Information - Europe), USK (Germany), CERO (Japan), GRAC (South Korea), ACB (Australia), DJCTQ (Brazil). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ESRB|PEGI|USK|CERO|GRAC|ACB|DJCTQ — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rating_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate or registration number issued by the rating board upon successful rating assignment. Used for compliance verification and platform certification.']
  rating_decision_date date [note: 'The date on which the rating board issued its rating decision. Null if rating not yet assigned.']
  rating_effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the assigned rating becomes valid and can be used in marketing and distribution. Typically the same as or shortly after the rating decision date.']
  rating_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the rating expires and must be renewed, if applicable. Some rating boards require periodic re-certification for live-service games. Null for ratings with no expiry.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the rating submission materials were formally submitted to the rating board. This is the principal business event timestamp for the submission lifecycle.']
  submission_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The fee charged by the rating board for processing this submission. Amount varies by rating board, submission type, and title complexity.']
  submission_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the submission fee (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  submission_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier for this rating submission, typically assigned by the submitting organization or rating board tracking system.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rating submission. Draft (being prepared), submitted (sent to board), under_review (board reviewing), pending_info (awaiting additional information), rated (rating assigned), rejected (submission denied), withdrawn (voluntarily pulled), expired (rating validity ended). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|pending_info|rated|rejected|withdrawn|expired — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Type of rating submission. Initial (first-time rating for new title), re-rating (resubmission after content changes), DLC (Downloadable Content rating), patch (content update rating), update (revised submission), appeal (challenge to assigned rating).']
  submitter_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact for this rating submission. Used for rating board correspondence and notifications.']
  submitter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or organization that submitted the rating application on behalf of the licensee or publisher.']
  submitter_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact for this rating submission.']
  third_party_content_disclosed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the submission disclosed the use of third-party licensed content (music, art assets, middleware, game engine components). Required for rating board transparency and IP compliance.']
  title_name varchar [note: 'The name of the game title or DLC being submitted for rating as it will appear on the rating certificate and in public-facing materials.']

  Note: 'Records of content rating submissions made to rating boards (ESRB, PEGI, USK, CERO, GRAC) for licensed game titles and DLC. Captures submission date, rating board, title name, submission type (initial, re-rating, DLC), content descriptors submitted, assigned rating received, rating certificate number, rating expiry (where applicable), and any required content modifications mandated by the rating board. Supports IP agreement compliance obligations and platform certification workflows.'
}

Table licensing.ip_dispute {
  ip_dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the IP dispute record. Primary key.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the license agreement related to this dispute, if applicable.']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific IP asset (game title, engine technology, trademark, patent, or licensed content) that is the subject of the dispute.']
  primary_ip_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal legal counsel or external law firm assigned to manage this dispute on behalf of the company.']
  primary_ip_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (individual or organization) filing the IP dispute claim.']
  actual_legal_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual total legal and administrative costs incurred in managing and resolving this IP dispute.']
  alleged_violation_date date [note: 'Date when the alleged IP infringement or violation is claimed to have occurred or begun.']
  claim_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount claimed by the claimant as damages or compensation for the alleged IP violation.']
  claimant_legal_counsel varchar [note: 'Name of the law firm or attorney representing the claimant in this dispute.']
  claimant_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the claimant party filing the IP dispute.']
  confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute and its resolution are subject to confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this IP dispute record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the claim amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  dispute_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the IP dispute, including the nature of the alleged infringement or violation and supporting facts.']
  dispute_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known case number or reference code assigned to this IP dispute for tracking and legal correspondence.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the IP dispute case indicating its progression through resolution stages.']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the IP dispute indicating the nature of the claim or violation.']
  disputed_ip_asset_name varchar [note: 'Name or title of the IP asset under dispute (e.g., game title, engine SDK, franchise brand).']
  document_repository_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the secure repository containing all legal documents, correspondence, and evidence related to this IP dispute.']
  estimated_legal_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total legal and administrative costs associated with managing and resolving this IP dispute.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date when the IP dispute claim was formally filed or initiated.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and commentary from legal, compliance, or business teams regarding the dispute strategy, risks, and progress.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction or court system under which the IP dispute is being adjudicated (e.g., US Federal Court, UK High Court, arbitration panel).']
  litigation_start_date date [note: 'Date when formal litigation or court proceedings were initiated, if the dispute escalated beyond mediation.']
  mediation_start_date date [note: 'Date when formal mediation or alternative dispute resolution proceedings began, if applicable.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this IP dispute record was last modified or updated.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next internal review or status update meeting regarding this IP dispute.']
  precedent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dispute case sets a legal or business precedent that may impact future IP licensing or dispute handling.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this dispute based on financial impact, strategic importance, and reputational risk.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the IP dispute was formally resolved, settled, or dismissed.']
  resolution_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the IP dispute was resolved (e.g., out-of-court settlement, court judgment, arbitration).']
  respondent_legal_counsel varchar [note: 'Name of the law firm or attorney representing the respondent in this dispute.']
  respondent_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the respondent party defending against the IP dispute.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Primary risk category associated with this IP dispute (e.g., financial loss, brand reputation damage, operational disruption).']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount agreed upon or awarded as part of the dispute resolution or settlement.']
  settlement_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed terms and conditions of the settlement agreement, including non-monetary provisions such as licensing adjustments, territory restrictions, or future royalty modifications.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the geographic territory where the alleged IP violation occurred.']

  Note: 'Tracks IP ownership disputes, infringement claims, and licensing violation cases — capturing dispute type (infringement claim, royalty underpayment, unauthorized territory use, unlicensed derivative work), claimant identity, respondent identity, disputed IP asset, claim amount, legal counsel assigned, dispute status (open, in mediation, settled, litigated), resolution date, and settlement terms. Supports legal and compliance oversight of the IP portfolio.'
}

Table licensing.sublicense {
  sublicense_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sublicense arrangement. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal employee or team responsible for managing and monitoring this sublicense arrangement.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary IP agreement that grants sublicensing permissions to the licensee.']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific IP asset being sublicensed (game engine SDK, middleware, franchise IP, etc.).']
  parent_sublicense_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sublicense (parent_sublicense_id)']
  primary_licensee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original licensee who is granting sublicense rights to the sublicensee under their master agreement.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the sublicense arrangement was formally approved by all required parties (primary licensee and original licensor).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the sublicense request, indicating whether the primary licensee and/or original licensor have granted permission.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or role who granted final approval for this sublicense arrangement.']
  audit_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the primary licensee or original licensor retains the right to audit the sublicensee\'s usage and revenue reporting.']
  compliance_monitoring_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sublicensee must submit regular compliance reports or undergo audits to verify adherence to sublicense terms.']
  contract_document_reference varchar [note: 'File path, URL, or document management system reference to the signed sublicense agreement and related legal documents.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sublicense record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this sublicense arrangement.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the sublicense arrangement expires or terminates, after which the sublicensee no longer has rights to the licensed IP.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the sublicense arrangement becomes legally binding and the sublicensee may begin exercising granted rights.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sublicensee has exclusive rights within their territory/platform scope, preventing the primary licensee from granting additional sublicenses.']
  fixed_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'One-time or recurring fixed payment amount for the sublicense, independent of usage or revenue.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sublicense record was most recently updated in the system.']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum payment amount the sublicensee must pay regardless of actual usage or revenue generated from the sublicensed IP.']
  modification_rights varchar [note: 'Level of permission granted to the sublicensee to modify, adapt, or create derivative works from the sublicensed IP.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional context, special conditions, or internal observations about this sublicense arrangement.']
  pass_through_royalty_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sublicense revenue that the primary licensee must pass through to the original licensor as per the parent agreement terms.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which the sublicensee must remit royalty or fixed fee payments to the primary licensee.']
  platform_scope varchar [note: 'Permitted gaming platforms and distribution channels for the sublicensed IP (console, PC, mobile, cloud gaming, etc.).']
  post_termination_obligations varchar [note: 'Requirements the sublicensee must fulfill after sublicense termination, such as ceasing use, destroying copies, or completing sell-off periods.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary business contact at the sublicensee organization for sublicense communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary business contact at the sublicensee organization responsible for managing this sublicense relationship.']
  rating_board_compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific content rating obligations the sublicensee must meet (ESRB, PEGI, CERO, etc.) when using the sublicensed IP in their products.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the sublicense request was rejected, if applicable, including specific terms or conditions that were not acceptable.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which the sublicensee must submit usage reports, revenue reports, or compliance documentation to the primary licensee.']
  revenue_share_model varchar [note: 'Financial structure governing how sublicense revenue is shared between the primary licensee, sublicensee, and original licensor.']
  source_code_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sublicensee is granted access to source code of the sublicensed SDK or game engine technology.']
  sublicense_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for this sublicense arrangement, used in contracts and communications.']
  sublicense_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the sublicense arrangement indicating whether it is pending approval, active, suspended, expired, terminated, or revoked.']
  sublicense_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sublicensing arrangement based on the nature of rights granted (SDK access for contractors, co-development partnerships, distribution rights, etc.).']
  sublicensed_ip_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific IP assets, SDK components, game engine features, or technology elements covered under this sublicense.']
  sublicensee_royalty_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of revenue that the sublicensee must pay to the primary licensee for use of the sublicensed IP.']
  sublicensing_permitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sublicensee is allowed to further sublicense the IP to additional downstream parties (cascading sublicense).']
  termination_conditions varchar [note: 'Specific circumstances under which the sublicense may be terminated early by either party, including breach conditions and cure periods.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the sublicense arrangement.']
  territory_restrictions varchar [note: 'Specific geographic limitations or exclusions where the sublicensee is prohibited from using the sublicensed IP, even if broader territories are granted.']
  territory_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic territories where the sublicensee is permitted to exercise sublicensed rights, specified as comma-separated ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or regional descriptors.']
  usage_restrictions varchar [note: 'Specific limitations on how the sublicensee may use the IP, including prohibited use cases, content restrictions, or technical constraints.']

  Note: 'Tracks sublicensing arrangements where a primary licensee grants downstream rights to third parties under sublicensing permissions in their IP agreement. Captures sublicensee identity, parent agreement reference, sublicensed IP scope, territory restrictions, revenue share pass-through terms, sublicense effective/expiry dates, approval status, and compliance monitoring requirements. Critical for gaming engine licensing where studios sublicense SDK access to contractors or co-development partners.'
}

Table licensing.economy_config {
  economy_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the virtual economy configuration record. Primary key.']
  ab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ab_experiment. Business justification: Virtual economy parameters (earn rates, sink rates, exchange rates, balance caps) are commonly A/B tested for game balance. Experiment attribution enables economy health analysis and monetization impa']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.dev_project. Business justification: Economy configurations are versioned game design artifacts managed within dev projects. Tracking provenance supports balance iteration analysis, regulatory audit trails (economy changes per release), ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user (game designer, economy manager) who approved this configuration for production use.']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.game_studio. Business justification: Economy configs are designed by specific studios. Tracking studio ownership supports multi-studio publishers managing different economy models per studio, studio-level economy health monitoring, and a']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title for which this economy configuration applies.']
  network_region_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the gaming platform (console, PC, mobile) for which this economy configuration applies. Null if configuration is cross-platform.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_policy. Business justification: Economy configurations must comply with policies governing virtual currency regulations, anti-money laundering controls, and minor spending protections. Required for economy design approval, regulator']
  superseded_economy_config_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on economy_config (superseded_economy_config_id)']
  title_season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game season or content release period associated with this economy configuration. Null for non-seasonal configurations.']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier for the A/B test variant this economy configuration represents. Used for experimentation and optimization. Null for non-test configurations.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this economy configuration was approved for production deployment.']
  base_earn_rate_primary decimal(18,6) [note: 'Default rate at which players earn primary currency per unit of gameplay activity (e.g., per match, per hour, per quest completion).']
  base_earn_rate_secondary decimal(18,6) [note: 'Default rate at which players earn secondary currency per unit of gameplay activity. Typically lower than primary currency earn rate.']
  config_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for this economy configuration, used for external reference and integration.']
  config_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name for this economy configuration (e.g., \'Season 5 Premium Economy\', \'Launch Week Economy\').']
  config_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the business rationale, design decisions, or special considerations for this economy configuration.']
  config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this economy configuration. Draft configurations are under development, active configurations are live in-game, suspended configurations are temporarily disabled, and archived configurations are historical records.']
  config_version varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of this economy configuration (e.g., 1.0.0, 2.1.3). Used for tracking configuration changes and rollbacks.']
  conversion_fee_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage fee charged when players convert between primary and secondary currencies. Creates friction and currency sink.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this economy configuration record was first created in the system.']
  currency_expiry_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after which unspent currency expires and is removed from player accounts. Only applicable when currency_expiry_enabled is true.']
  currency_expiry_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether currency balances have expiration dates. When true, unspent currency may expire after a defined period.']
  daily_earn_cap_primary varchar [note: 'Maximum amount of primary currency a player can earn in a single day through gameplay. Prevents exploitation and maintains economy health.']
  daily_earn_cap_secondary varchar [note: 'Maximum amount of secondary currency a player can earn in a single day through gameplay. Typically lower than primary currency cap.']
  deflation_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable deflation rate (as percentage) before automated controls trigger. Measured as rate of currency sink exceeding earn rate.']
  economy_health_score_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable economy health score (0-100 scale) before alerts are triggered. Health score measures balance between earn and sink rates, inflation, and player engagement.']
  economy_health_score_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target economy health score (0-100 scale) that represents optimal economy balance. Used for monitoring and automated adjustment decisions.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this economy configuration expires and is no longer active. Null for open-ended configurations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this economy configuration becomes active and begins governing in-game economy behavior.']
  exchange_rate_primary_to_secondary decimal(18,6) [note: 'Conversion rate from primary currency to secondary currency (e.g., 100 gold = 1 gem would be 0.01). Used for in-game currency conversion mechanics.']
  exchange_rate_secondary_to_primary decimal(18,6) [note: 'Conversion rate from secondary currency to primary currency. May differ from inverse of primary-to-secondary rate to create conversion friction.']
  inflation_control_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated inflation control mechanisms are active for this economy configuration. When true, system may dynamically adjust earn/sink rates.']
  inflation_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable inflation rate (as percentage) before automated controls trigger. Measured as rate of currency supply growth exceeding sink rate.']
  max_balance_cap_primary varchar [note: 'Maximum amount of primary currency a single player account can hold. Prevents hoarding and maintains economy balance.']
  max_balance_cap_secondary varchar [note: 'Maximum amount of secondary currency a single player account can hold. Typically higher than primary currency cap for premium currency.']
  primary_currency_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary virtual currency governed by this configuration (e.g., \'GOLD\', \'GEMS\', \'VBUCKS\').']
  real_money_to_primary_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Conversion rate from real-world currency (USD) to primary virtual currency for IAP (In-App Purchase) transactions. Used for monetization calculations.']
  real_money_to_secondary_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Conversion rate from real-world currency (USD) to secondary virtual currency for IAP transactions. Typically more favorable than primary currency rate.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country or region code where this economy configuration applies (e.g., USA, EUR, JPN). Null for global configurations.']
  secondary_currency_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the secondary or premium virtual currency governed by this configuration. Null if only one currency is used.']
  sink_rate_target_primary decimal(18,6) [note: 'Target rate at which primary currency should be removed from the economy through player spending (e.g., per player per day). Used for economy health monitoring.']
  sink_rate_target_secondary decimal(18,6) [note: 'Target rate at which secondary currency should be removed from the economy through player spending. Used for economy health monitoring.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this economy configuration record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Virtual economy configuration parameters per game title defining currency exchange rates, earn rates, sink rates, inflation controls, and economy health thresholds.'
}

Table licensing.promotion {
  promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotional pricing event. Primary key for the promotion entity.']
  ab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ab_experiment. Business justification: Promotional discount percentages, messaging, targeting criteria, and display timing are standard A/B test subjects in live ops. Experiment attribution enables promotion effectiveness analysis and incr']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_release. Business justification: Promotions coordinated with patch/DLC releases for launch incentives. Essential for release day sales strategy, player re-engagement, and new content adoption tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Promotions executed by specific cost centers for expense tracking. Marketing cost management and campaign cost attribution.']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.dev_project. Business justification: Promotions coordinate with content releases managed by dev teams. Dev teams need visibility into which promotions align with their release cycles for go-to-market planning, and monetization teams need']
  featured_dlc_bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.dlc_bundle. Business justification: Promotions discount DLC bundles (e.g., holiday bundle sale). Business validates bundle eligibility, applies discounts at checkout, and reports promotional revenue by bundle. Core monetization workflow']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Promotional campaigns consume marketing budget and require budget tracking for cost control and ROI analysis. Standard marketing finance integration.']
  gaas_live_ops_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.live_ops_cycle. Business justification: Promotions often launch alongside live-ops cycles (e.g., season pass discounts at season start). Tracking this relationship enables coordinated go-to-market execution, promotional performance analysis']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title this promotion applies to. Links promotion to specific game product.']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensed_ip. Business justification: Promotional campaigns featuring licensed IP require tracking for marketing rights compliance, licensor approval workflows, and accurate royalty calculation when discounts affect net revenue subject to']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent marketing campaign or offer campaign that this promotion is part of. Links to broader marketing initiatives.']
  message_localization_string_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.localization_string. Business justification: Promotional text needs translation for multi-region deployment. Essential for localized marketing campaigns, regional compliance, and culturally appropriate messaging.']
  promotion_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business user who approved this promotion for activation. Required for governance and compliance with promotional pricing policies.']
  promotion_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business user or system account that created this promotion record. Used for audit and accountability.']
  promotion_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business user or system account that last modified this promotion record. Used for audit and accountability.']
  seasonal_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.seasonal_event. Business justification: Time-limited promotions tied to in-game events (double XP weekends, event sales). Core live ops coordination for event participation incentives and monetization spikes.']
  superseded_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on promotion (superseded_promotion_id)']
  title_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.sku. Business justification: Promotions apply discounts to specific SKUs (e.g., 20% off Standard Edition). Business validates SKU eligibility and calculates final price at purchase. Core workflow for promotional pricing, revenue ']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier for the experimental variant if this promotion is part of an A/B test. Used to measure conversion rate optimization.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this promotion was approved for activation. Part of the promotional governance workflow.']
  banner_image_url varchar [note: 'URL path to the promotional banner image asset displayed in the game storefront and marketing materials.']
  conversion_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of redemptions to impressions, indicating the effectiveness of the promotion in driving purchase behavior. Calculated as total_redemptions / total_impressions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this promotion record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary discount values. Applicable when discount_type is fixed_amount.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the promotional discount is applied to the purchase price.']
  discount_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric value of the discount. Interpretation depends on discount_type: percentage (0-100), fixed amount (currency units), or bonus multiplier.']
  display_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking used to determine the order in which promotions are displayed in the storefront. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the promotion expires and is no longer available to players. Nullable for open-ended promotions.']
  geographic_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where promotion is available or restricted. Empty indicates global availability.']
  max_discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cap on the total discount value that can be applied to a single transaction. Prevents excessive discounts on high-value purchases.']
  min_purchase_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum transaction value required to qualify for the promotion. Nullable if no minimum threshold applies.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this promotion record was last updated. Audit trail for record changes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal free-text notes and comments about the promotion for business users. May include rationale, performance observations, or operational instructions.']
  platform_availability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of platform codes where this promotion is available (e.g., iOS, Android, Steam, PlayStation, Xbox). Empty indicates all platforms.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the promotion used in marketing materials and player-facing interfaces. Externally visible promotion reference.']
  promotion_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name of the promotional event shown to players in storefront and marketing channels.']
  promotion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the promotion indicating whether it is in planning, running, or concluded.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotional event type indicating the nature and structure of the offer.']
  redemption_limit_per_player varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single player can use this promotion. Nullable for unlimited redemptions.']
  requires_first_purchase boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotion is exclusively available to players making their first in-app purchase (F2P conversion incentive).']
  stackable_with_other_promos boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotion can be combined with other active promotions in a single transaction.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the promotion becomes active and eligible purchases can receive the discount. Represents the business event time when the promotional pricing takes effect.']
  target_player_segment varchar [note: 'Player segmentation criteria defining which players are eligible to see and redeem this promotion. May reference LTV segments, behavioral cohorts, or geographic regions.']
  total_discount_given decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary value of all discounts applied through this promotion. Represents the revenue foregone to drive conversion.']
  total_impressions varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of how many times this promotion was displayed to players across all channels and sessions.']
  total_redemption_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of total redemptions allowed across all players before the promotion automatically ends. Nullable for uncapped promotions.']
  total_redemptions varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of how many times players successfully used this promotion to complete a purchase.']
  total_revenue_generated decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative gross revenue (before discount) from all transactions that used this promotion. Measured in USD for reporting consistency.']

  Note: 'Time-limited promotional pricing events for in-game purchases including flash sales, bundle discounts, first-purchase bonuses, and seasonal pricing with eligibility rules and discount structures.'
}

Table licensing.catalog_offer {
  catalog_offer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this catalog-offer association record. Primary key.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user who added this catalog item to the campaign. Supports audit trail and campaign management accountability.']
  mtx_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the MTX catalog item included in this offer campaign']
  offer_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the offer campaign featuring this catalog item']
  ab_test_variant_assignment varchar [note: 'A/B test variant identifier for this specific catalog item within the campaign. Enables testing different item selections or pricing strategies within the same campaign framework. May differ from the campaign-level ab_test_variant when testing item-level variations.']
  added_to_campaign_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this catalog item was added to the campaign. Supports dynamic campaign composition where items are added/removed during the campaign lifecycle.']
  campaign_specific_discount_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'The discount or bonus value applied to this specific catalog item within this campaign. May override the catalog item\'s default promo_discount_pct for campaign-specific pricing strategies. Interpretation depends on the campaign\'s discount_type.']
  conversion_rate_for_item decimal(5,4) [note: 'Calculated conversion rate (redemption_count / impression_count) for this catalog item within this campaign. Tracks item-level performance to optimize future campaign composition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this catalog-offer association record was created in the system.']
  display_priority_in_campaign varchar [note: 'Ranking priority for displaying this catalog item within the campaign\'s offer UI. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used when campaigns feature multiple items and need to control presentation order.']
  impression_count_for_item varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of how many times this specific catalog item was displayed to players as part of this campaign. Used for campaign performance analytics and A/B testing evaluation.']
  is_featured_item boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this catalog item is featured or highlighted within the campaign\'s promotional materials. Featured items receive premium placement and visual emphasis.']
  redemption_count_for_item varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of how many times this specific catalog item was purchased by players through this campaign. Core metric for campaign effectiveness and item performance within campaigns.']
  removed_from_campaign_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this catalog item was removed from the campaign. Null if the item is still active in the campaign. Supports dynamic campaign management and performance-based item rotation.']
  revenue_generated_for_item decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total revenue generated from purchases of this catalog item through this campaign. Denominated in the campaign\'s currency_code. Used for ROI analysis and campaign optimization.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the inclusion of specific MTX catalog items within time-limited monetization offer campaigns. It captures the operational relationship between purchasable offerings and targeted promotional campaigns. Each record links one MTX catalog item to one offer campaign with campaign-specific pricing, display priority, and performance tracking attributes that exist only in the context of this campaign-item pairing. This enables dynamic campaign composition, A/B testing of item selection, and per-item performance analytics within campaigns.. Existence Justification: In F2P gaming monetization, offer campaigns routinely feature multiple catalog items (bundles, currency packs, cosmetics) to maximize conversion across player segments, and individual catalog items appear in multiple campaigns over time (seasonal sales, whale retention offers, new player promotions). The business actively manages campaign composition by adding/removing items, testing different item mixes via A/B testing, and tracking per-item performance metrics (impressions, conversions, revenue) within each campaign to optimize future campaign design.'
}

Table licensing.ip_compliance_obligation {
  ip_compliance_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this IP-obligation compliance relationship record. Primary key.']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the licensed intellectual property asset subject to this compliance obligation']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory obligation that applies to this IP asset']
  audit_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of findings from the most recent audit of this IP-obligation pair. Documents specific compliance gaps, control weaknesses, or validation results.']
  compliance_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or team responsible for ensuring this specific IP asset complies with this specific obligation. Assigns accountability at the IP-obligation level.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this specific IP asset with respect to this specific obligation. Tracks whether the IP meets the regulatory requirement.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this IP-obligation compliance relationship record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this regulatory obligation became applicable to this specific IP asset. May differ from the obligation\'s general effective date based on IP release date or territory expansion.']
  exemption_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the exemption expires and the IP must achieve full compliance. NULL if no exemption or exemption is permanent.']
  exemption_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this IP asset has been granted an exemption from this obligation by the governing body or internal compliance authority.']
  implementation_deadline date [note: 'Deadline by which this IP asset must achieve compliance with this obligation. May be driven by regulation enforcement date, IP launch date, or remediation plan.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit conducted for this IP-obligation pair. Tracks audit history at the granular IP-obligation level.']
  last_updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this compliance relationship record. Tracks when status, audit results, or other attributes were modified.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance review or audit of this IP asset against this obligation. Drives compliance calendar and audit scheduling.']
  remediation_plan_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the remediation plan document for addressing non-compliance of this IP with this obligation. NULL if compliant or no plan exists.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level for non-compliance of this specific IP with this specific obligation. Risk varies by IP-obligation combination based on IP revenue, territory exposure, and obligation severity.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance relationship between licensed intellectual property assets and regulatory obligations. It captures the specific compliance requirements, audit history, risk assessment, and implementation status for each IP-obligation pairing. Each record links one licensed IP asset to one regulatory obligation with attributes that track compliance posture, deadlines, and enforcement actions specific to that combination.. Existence Justification: In gaming operations, a single licensed IP asset (e.g., a game engine SDK) is subject to multiple regulatory obligations across jurisdictions (GDPR for EU players, COPPA for US children, PEGI rating requirements for Europe, loot box disclosure laws). Conversely, a single regulatory obligation (e.g., GDPR Article 30 record-keeping) applies to multiple IP assets (all engines with telemetry, all games with user accounts). Compliance teams actively manage a compliance matrix mapping each IP to applicable obligations, tracking compliance status, audit dates, risk levels, and remediation plans for each IP-obligation pair.'
}

Table licensing.catalog_promotion_eligibility {
  catalog_promotion_eligibility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each catalog item-promotion eligibility record']
  mtx_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the MTX catalog entry that is eligible for this promotion']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the promotional campaign that includes this catalog item']
  added_to_promotion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this catalog item was added to the promotion\'s eligible item list, supporting audit trail and promotional planning analysis']
  display_order_in_promotion varchar [note: 'Sequence number controlling the visual presentation order of this catalog item within the promotion\'s storefront display']
  effective_discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The actual discount percentage applied to this specific catalog item within this promotion, which may differ from the promotion\'s default discount based on item-specific rules or overrides']
  eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this item\'s eligibility within the promotion: active (currently eligible), paused (temporarily suspended), removed (no longer eligible), pending (scheduled for future activation)']
  eligible_sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of MTX catalog SKU codes that qualify for this promotion. Empty indicates all SKUs are eligible. [Moved from promotion: This STRING field containing comma-separated SKU codes is a denormalized representation of the M:N relationship. It should be removed from the promotion product as the association table now properly normalizes this relationship, enabling proper querying, indexing, and relationship-specific attribute tracking.]']
  featured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this catalog item is designated as a featured or hero item within this promotion, receiving prominent placement in promotional materials']
  item_specific_redemption_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of how many times this specific catalog item was purchased using this specific promotion, enabling item-level promotional performance analysis']
  item_specific_revenue_generated decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total gross revenue generated from purchases of this catalog item through this promotion, tracking the revenue contribution of each item-promotion combination']
  promotion_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking determining the display order and priority of this catalog item within the promotion\'s featured items list']
  removed_from_promotion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this catalog item was removed from the promotion (if applicable), enabling tracking of promotional composition changes during the campaign lifecycle']

  Note: 'This association product represents the promotional eligibility relationship between MTX catalog items and promotional campaigns. It captures which purchasable offerings are included in which time-limited promotional events, along with item-specific promotional performance metrics and display configuration. Each record links one MTX catalog entry to one promotion with attributes that track the effectiveness and presentation of that specific item within that specific promotional context.. Existence Justification: In gaming monetization, promotional campaigns are actively managed business events where merchandising teams strategically select which MTX catalog items to include in which promotions, track performance metrics per item-promotion combination, and adjust promotional mix based on conversion data. A single catalog item (e.g., a legendary skin) participates in multiple promotions over time (flash sale, seasonal event, bundle discount), and each promotion includes multiple catalog items with item-specific discount rates, display priority, and performance tracking. This is an operational M:N relationship with its own lifecycle and relationship-specific data.'
}

Table licensing.team_brand_license {
  team_brand_license_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each team brand licensing agreement record']
  mtx_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the microtransaction catalog item that features the licensed team brand']
  team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the esports team whose brand is licensed for this MTX item']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the brand licensing agreement. Teams must approve the use of their brand in MTX items before they can be sold.']
  contract_reference_code varchar [note: 'External reference to the legal contract document or master licensing agreement that governs the terms of this team brand license.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this team brand license record was created in the system.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this licensing agreement grants exclusive rights to feature this team in this item category or time period, preventing other teams from being featured in similar items during the exclusivity window.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this team brand license record was last modified, including sales count or revenue share updates.']
  licensing_end_date date [note: 'The expiration or termination date of the brand licensing agreement for this MTX item. Sales after this date do not accrue royalties unless the agreement is renewed.']
  licensing_start_date date [note: 'The effective start date of the brand licensing agreement for this MTX item. Sales before this date do not accrue royalties to the team.']
  revenue_share_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative revenue share amount (in USD) paid or owed to the esports team for this specific MTX item under this licensing agreement. Calculated as sales revenue × royalty_percentage.']
  royalty_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of net revenue from this MTX item that is paid to the esports team as a royalty for licensing their brand. Varies by team tier, item type, and negotiated contract terms.']
  sales_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of units sold for this specific MTX item attributed to this team brand license. Used for performance tracking and royalty calculation verification.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the licensing contract between an esports team and a microtransaction catalog item. It captures the commercial agreement where a team licenses their brand (logo, name, colors, identity) to be featured in purchasable MTX items such as team skins, emotes, banners, or bundles. Each record links one MTX catalog item to one esports team with attributes that govern the revenue sharing, royalty payments, licensing period, and sales performance tracking that exist only in the context of this commercial relationship.. Existence Justification: In the gaming industry, esports teams license their brand identity (logos, names, colors) to be featured in purchasable microtransaction items such as team skins, weapon wraps, emotes, banners, and all-star bundles. A single MTX item can feature multiple teams (e.g., an all-star bundle with skins for 5 different championship teams), and a single team\'s brand appears across multiple MTX items (team skin pack, team emote, team banner, seasonal variants). The business actively manages these licensing relationships with negotiated royalty percentages, revenue tracking per team-item pair, licensing periods, exclusivity clauses, and approval workflows.'
}

Table billing.invoice {
  invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the invoice. Primary key for the invoice entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue invoices are attributed to cost centers (studio/title) for internal P&L reporting and budget variance analysis. Gaming finance teams track revenue by cost center to measure title profitability']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Manual invoice creation (credits, adjustments, enterprise billing) requires audit trail of the billing operations employee who created it for SOX compliance, dispute resolution, and internal controls.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.ip_agreement. Business justification: Royalty invoices are issued under specific IP licensing agreements. Links invoice to governing agreement terms for royalty billing, reconciliation, and audit. Essential for royalty revenue recognition']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Invoices must track tax jurisdiction for VAT/sales tax filing and audit compliance. Gaming platforms operate globally with complex tax nexus rules. Replaces denormalized tax_jurisdiction_code with pro']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Invoices must be issued by a legal entity for tax compliance, VAT/sales tax reporting, and consolidated financial statements. Gaming companies operate multiple legal entities across jurisdictions; inv']
  payment_id varchar [note: 'External payment processor transaction identifier linking this invoice to the actual payment settlement. Used for payment reconciliation and chargeback tracking. Null if invoice is unpaid.']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the player to whom this invoice is issued. Links to the player master for customer billing and payment history.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address for the invoice recipient. Required for tax jurisdiction determination and payment processing compliance.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address (apartment, suite, unit number). Optional address component for complete billing address capture.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City component of the billing address. Used for tax jurisdiction determination and geographic revenue reporting.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the billing period covered by this invoice. Defines the close of the billing cycle for subscription and recurring charges.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the billing period covered by this invoice. Used for subscription billing cycles and recurring revenue recognition.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the billing address. Used for tax jurisdiction determination and payment fraud detection.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the billing address. Critical for US state sales tax and Canadian provincial tax calculations.']
  chargeback_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice was subject to a payment chargeback initiated by the cardholder or payment processor. True if chargeback occurred; false otherwise. Critical for fraud detection and payment risk management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice record was first created in the billing system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this invoice. Critical for multi-currency revenue reporting and foreign exchange reconciliation.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied to this invoice, including promotional discounts, volume discounts, early payment discounts, or platform-specific adjustments. Reduces the net amount due.']
  dispute_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the reason for invoice dispute if status is disputed (e.g., unauthorized charge, service not delivered, billing error, pricing discrepancy). Null if no dispute. Used for customer service escalation and fraud pattern analysis.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is expected per the agreed payment terms. Used for accounts receivable aging and overdue invoice identification.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was formally issued to the customer or platform partner. Serves as the baseline for payment terms and aging calculations.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique invoice number issued to the player or platform partner. Human-readable business identifier used for customer service, accounting reconciliation, and dispute resolution.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice. Tracks progression from draft through issuance, payment, or dispute resolution. Critical for accounts receivable aging and revenue recognition workflows.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of invoice document type. Standard invoices represent normal sales; credit memos represent refunds or adjustments; debit memos represent additional charges; proforma invoices are estimates; recurring invoices are for subscription billing cycles.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this invoice. May include special billing instructions, customer service annotations, or accounting adjustments. Used for audit trail and customer communication context.']
  paid_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was fully paid. Null if invoice is unpaid or partially paid. Used for cash flow analysis and Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculations.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Interface or channel through which the payment was initiated. Distinct from payment method; tracks the user experience touchpoint for conversion funnel analysis and channel-specific revenue attribution.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument type used or expected for this invoice. Identifies the financial instrument (credit card, PayPal, bank transfer, platform wallet, etc.) for PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard) compliance tracking and payment processing reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|paypal|bank_transfer|platform_wallet|gift_card|cryptocurrency — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual payment terms defining the period within which payment is due. Immediate means payment on receipt; net_X means payment due X days after invoice date.']
  platform_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total platform commission or distribution fee charged by the storefront (e.g., 30% Apple App Store fee, 12% Epic Games Store fee). Deducted from gross revenue to calculate net payout to the studio.']
  platform_partner_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the platform partner (Steam, Epic, console first-party, mobile app store) for B2B invoices related to revenue share settlements and payout reconciliation. Null for direct player invoices.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount refunded to the customer if this invoice was partially or fully refunded. Zero if no refund issued. Tracked separately from credit memos for chargeback and dispute analysis.']
  revenue_recognition_category varchar [note: 'Accounting classification determining when and how revenue from this invoice is recognized per ASC 606 / IFRS 15 standards. Immediate for one-time purchases; deferred for DLC (Downloadable Content) or season passes; subscription for recurring GaaS (Game as a Service) revenue; usage-based for consumption models; milestone for performance-based contracts.']
  storefront_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the digital storefront or distribution channel where the transaction originated. Critical for revenue attribution and platform-specific reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: STEAM|EPIC|PSN|XBOX|NINTENDO|APPLE|GOOGLE|DIRECT — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all line item amounts before taxes, fees, and discounts. Base amount for revenue recognition calculations.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to this invoice, including sales tax, VAT (Value-Added Tax), GST (Goods and Services Tax), or other jurisdiction-specific taxes. Sum of all line-level tax amounts.']
  tax_exemption_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating tax exemption status if applicable (e.g., non-profit organization, educational institution, tax-exempt jurisdiction). Null if no exemption applies.']
  tax_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Effective tax rate percentage applied to this invoice. Composite rate if multiple tax types apply (e.g., state + local sales tax, or VAT).']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final invoice total after applying subtotal, taxes, discounts, and fees. This is the net amount due from the customer or platform partner. Used for payment reconciliation and revenue recognition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice record was last modified. Tracks changes to invoice status, amounts, or payment information for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  voided_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why this invoice was voided (e.g., billing error, duplicate invoice, customer cancellation, fraud). Null if invoice has not been voided. Required for financial audit and compliance reporting.']
  voided_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice was voided or cancelled. Null if invoice has not been voided. Used for audit trail and revenue reversal tracking.']

  Note: 'Master billing document representing a formal request for payment issued to a player or platform partner. Captures invoice number, billing period, total amount, currency, tax breakdown, storefront origin, invoice status (draft, issued, paid, void, disputed), due date, payment terms, and embedded line-item detail (SKU reference, product type, quantity, unit price, line total, tax amount, discount applied, revenue recognition category). SSOT for all real-money billing documents and their granular line breakdowns across storefronts and subscription cycles.'
}

Table billing.invoice_line {
  invoice_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the invoice line item. Primary key for this entity.']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_release. Business justification: Invoice lines track which content drop/DLC release a purchase belongs to. Critical for launch revenue tracking, content drop P&L reporting, and measuring commercial success of major content releases a']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the development studio responsible for the product sold on this line. Used for studio-level revenue attribution and royalty calculations.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title or Intellectual Property (IP) this line item is associated with. Used for title-level revenue reporting and Profit and Loss (P&L) analysis.']
  iap_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.iap_transaction. Business justification: Invoice lines for IAP purchases need transaction linkage for refund reconciliation, entitlement revocation, and chargeback dispute resolution. Financial operations require tracing invoice lines to ori']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent invoice header that contains this line item. Links this line to the overall billing transaction.']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensed_ip. Business justification: Invoice lines itemize specific licensed IP usage (engine licenses, middleware components, music tracks, brand assets). Enables IP-specific revenue recognition, royalty calculation, and usage-based bil']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign that drove this purchase. Used to calculate Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) and campaign effectiveness. Null if purchase was organic.']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the player who made this purchase. Links billing transactions to player behavior analytics and Lifetime Value (LTV) calculations.']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.storefront. Business justification: Invoice lines itemize sales per storefront for multi-platform publishers. Required for storefront-specific revenue attribution, platform fee reconciliation, and regional tax compliance reporting.']
  storefront_order_id varchar [note: 'External transaction identifier provided by the platform or storefront (Steam order ID, Epic transaction ID, Apple receipt ID, Google order token). Used for reconciliation with platform payout reports.']
  chargeback_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount reversed due to chargeback initiated by the payment card issuer. Zero if no chargeback has occurred. Expressed in the invoice currency.']
  chargeback_date date [note: 'Date when the chargeback was processed against this line item. Null if no chargeback has occurred.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice line record was first created in the billing system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this line (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total discount applied to this line item, including promotional discounts, coupon redemptions, loyalty rewards, or platform sale pricing. Expressed in the invoice currency.']
  discount_code varchar [note: 'Promotional code, coupon identifier, or campaign code applied to this line item. Null if no discount code was used.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the invoice, used for ordering and referencing individual line items on the invoice document.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this invoice line item: active (valid charge), refunded (fully refunded), partially_refunded (partial refund issued), disputed (under dispute), or chargeback (payment reversed by card issuer).']
  line_total decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final total for this line item including all discounts and taxes: subtotal + tax_amount. This is the amount recognized for this line. Expressed in the invoice currency.']
  list_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Original catalog or storefront list price per unit before promotional discounts. Used to calculate discount percentage and promotional effectiveness.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice line record was last modified. Updated when refunds, chargebacks, or status changes occur. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  net_revenue decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net revenue after platform fees: line_total - platform_fee_amount. This is the amount the studio receives after platform revenue share. Expressed in the invoice currency.']
  platform_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fee charged by the distribution platform or storefront (e.g., 30% Apple/Google fee, Steam revenue share). Deducted from gross revenue to calculate net payout. Expressed in the invoice currency.']
  platform_fee_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Platform fee rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.3000 for 30% platform fee). Used for financial modeling and payout reconciliation.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the product or service sold on this line: game title, expansion pack name, subscription plan name, or virtual item bundle name.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Category of the product sold: base game purchase, Downloadable Content (DLC), season pass, subscription period, In-App Purchase (IAP), virtual currency, cosmetic item, or battle pass. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: base_game|dlc|season_pass|subscription|iap|virtual_currency|cosmetic|battle_pass — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of this Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) purchased on this line. Typically 1 for digital goods; may be greater than 1 for virtual currency bundles or multi-license purchases.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total amount refunded for this line item. Zero if no refund has been issued. Expressed in the invoice currency.']
  refund_date date [note: 'Date when the refund was processed for this line item. Null if no refund has been issued.']
  refund_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is eligible for refund based on platform policies (e.g., Steam 2-hour/14-day policy, Apple/Google refund windows). True if refundable, False if non-refundable.']
  refund_window_end_date date [note: 'Last date on which a refund can be requested for this line item per platform policy. Null if item is non-refundable or refund window has no expiration.']
  revenue_recognition_category varchar [note: 'Accounting treatment for revenue recognition: immediate (one-time purchase), deferred (season pass, pre-order), subscription (recurring), or usage-based (consumable virtual currency).']
  revenue_recognition_end_date date [note: 'Date when revenue recognition ends for this line item. Null for immediate recognition. For subscriptions or deferred items, marks the end of the recognition period.']
  revenue_recognition_start_date date [note: 'Date when revenue recognition begins for this line item. For immediate recognition, equals the invoice date. For deferred or subscription items, marks the start of the recognition period.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Product identifier representing the specific item sold: game title, Downloadable Content (DLC) pack, season pass, subscription tier, In-App Purchase (IAP) bundle, or virtual currency package.']
  subscription_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the subscription billing period for subscription line items. Null for non-subscription purchases.']
  subscription_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the subscription billing period for subscription line items. Null for non-subscription purchases.']
  subtotal decimal(18,4) [note: 'Line total after discounts but before taxes: (quantity × unit_price) - discount_amount. Expressed in the invoice currency.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax applied to this line item, including sales tax, Value-Added Tax (VAT), Goods and Services Tax (GST), or other jurisdiction-specific taxes. Expressed in the invoice currency.']
  tax_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic tax jurisdiction code or name where the tax was assessed (e.g., state, province, country). Required for multi-jurisdiction tax reporting.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Effective tax rate applied to this line item, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0750 for 7.5%). Used for tax reporting and reconciliation.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per single unit of the Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) before any discounts or taxes. Expressed in the invoice currency.']

  Note: 'Individual line items on a billing invoice, representing discrete chargeable units such as a game title purchase, DLC pack, season pass, subscription period, or IAP bundle. Captures SKU reference, quantity, unit price, line total, tax amount, discount applied, and revenue recognition category. Enables granular revenue breakdown per storefront transaction.'
}

Table billing.payment {
  payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment transaction. Primary key for the payment entity.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the game title for which the payment was made. Links to the game title master entity.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.ip_agreement. Business justification: Links payments to specific licensing agreements for advance payments, milestone payments, minimum guarantees, and royalty payments. Enables agreement-specific payment tracking, recoupment calculation,']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Payments are received by specific legal entities; critical for cash management, bank reconciliation, and intercompany settlement. Gaming companies must track which legal entity received each payment f']
  licensee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensee. Business justification: Tracks payments from/to licensees for royalties, license fees, minimum guarantees, and revenue shares. Essential for licensee payment reconciliation, cash application, and accounts receivable/payable ']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_instrument. Business justification: Payment transactions should reference the payment instrument used for the transaction. While payment captures point-in-time card details (card_last_four_digits, card_brand, expiry dates) as historical']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the player who initiated the payment. Links to the player master entity.']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the subscription if this payment is part of a recurring subscription billing cycle. Nullable for one-time purchases.']
  amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The gross payment amount received from the player before any fees, taxes, or adjustments. Represents the total real-money value of the transaction.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'The authorization code returned by the payment gateway or issuing bank confirming that the payment was approved. Used for reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the billing address associated with the payment method. Used for tax calculation and fraud detection.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code of the billing address. Used for address verification (AVS) and fraud prevention.']
  card_brand varchar [note: 'The brand of the payment card used (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, American Express). Applicable only when payment method type is credit or debit card. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visa|mastercard|amex|discover|jcb|diners_club|unionpay — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  card_expiry_month varchar [note: 'The expiration month of the payment card (1-12). Used for subscription renewal validation and payment method expiry alerts.']
