  tax_engine_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the tax calculation engine or tax rate database used at the time of calculation. Supports reproducibility and audit.']
  tax_exemption_certificate_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the tax exemption certificate on file, if the exemption was granted based on a certificate provided by the customer. Nullable if no certificate applies.']
  tax_exemption_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction qualified for a tax exemption. True if exempt, False if tax was applied normally.']
  tax_exemption_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or regulatory basis for the tax exemption, if applicable (e.g., educational institution, government entity, below de minimis threshold, reverse charge mechanism). Nullable if no exemption applies.']
  tax_invoice_number varchar [note: 'The invoice number on which this tax was reported to the customer. Links the tax record to the customer-facing invoice document. Nullable if no formal invoice was issued.']
  tax_jurisdiction_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country-level tax jurisdiction where the tax obligation arises (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU, JPN).']
  tax_jurisdiction_municipality varchar [note: 'City, county, or local municipality name where the tax obligation arises. Nullable for jurisdictions without municipal-level tax.']
  tax_jurisdiction_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or first-level administrative subdivision code where the tax obligation arises (e.g., CA for California, ON for Ontario). Nullable for countries without state-level tax.']
  tax_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The tax rate applied to the taxable amount, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.200000 for 20% VAT, 0.0875 for 8.75% sales tax). Supports up to six decimal places for precision in complex jurisdictions.']
  tax_remittance_date date [note: 'The date on which the collected tax was (or will be) remitted to the tax authority. Nullable if remittance has not yet occurred.']
  tax_remittance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of tax remittance to the tax authority. Pending: collected but not yet remitted; Remitted: paid to authority; Deferred: scheduled for future remittance; Disputed: under audit or dispute; Refunded: returned to customer.']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'Type of tax applied to the transaction. VAT (Value-Added Tax) for EU and other jurisdictions, GST (Goods and Services Tax) for Canada/Australia/India, sales_tax for US state/local, digital_services_tax for DST regimes, withholding_tax for cross-border payments, consumption_tax for Japan.']
  taxable_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The base amount subject to tax, in the transaction currency. This is the pre-tax subtotal on which the tax rate is applied. For VAT, this is the net amount; for sales tax, this is the gross sale amount.']
  vat_moss_scheme_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction was reported under the EU VAT Mini One Stop Shop scheme (True) or through standard VAT registration in each member state (False). MOSS simplifies VAT compliance for digital services sold to EU consumers.']
  vat_registration_number varchar [note: 'The VAT registration number of the entity responsible for remitting the tax (either the publisher or the platform holder). Format varies by jurisdiction (e.g., GB123456789, DE123456789). Nullable if not applicable.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of tax calculated, collected, and remitted on a billing transaction. Captures tax jurisdiction (country, state/province, municipality), tax type (VAT, GST, sales tax, digital services tax, withholding tax), tax rate applied, taxable amount, tax amount collected, tax exemption flag, exemption certificate reference, remittance status, and filing period reference. Supports multi-jurisdiction tax compliance for global digital game sales including EU VAT MOSS, US state nexus rules, and platform-specific tax collection responsibilities across Steam, console, and mobile storefronts.'
}

Table billing.revenue_recognition {
  revenue_recognition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for revenue_recognition']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue recognition must be attributed to cost centers for title/studio P&L reporting. Gaming finance tracks recognized revenue by cost center to measure performance against budget and allocate develo']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title (product) for which revenue is being recognized. Links to the specific game, DLC, or season pass.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice that triggered this revenue recognition event. Links to the billing invoice that contains the underlying transaction.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Revenue recognition events generate GL journal entries; this link provides audit trail from billing transactions to financial statements. Required for ASC 606/IFRS 15 compliance and external audit sup']
  original_recognition_event_revenue_recognition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original revenue recognition event being reversed, if reversal_flag is True. Null for non-reversal events.']
  performance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific performance obligation being satisfied. Relevant for contracts with multiple deliverables (e.g., game + season pass + in-game currency).']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player (customer) for whom revenue is being recognized. The counterparty in the revenue transaction.']
  primary_revenue_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that created this revenue recognition record. Supports audit and compliance requirements.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue recognition drives profit center reporting for title performance analysis and portfolio management. Gaming companies use profit centers to track title-level profitability and make investment d']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution platform (Steam, Epic Games Store, PlayStation Store, Xbox Store, App Store, Google Play) where the transaction occurred.']
  storefront_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying financial transaction (purchase, subscription charge, DLC sale, MTX) that generated revenue to be recognized.']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player contract or subscription agreement under which this revenue is recognized. Relevant for GaaS and subscription-based revenue streams.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (YYYY-MM or YYYY-QX) to which this revenue recognition is allocated. Used for period-end close and financial reporting.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate transaction price to multiple performance obligations. Standalone_selling_price for observable prices; residual for unobservable prices; adjusted_market_assessment for market-based estimates.']
  contract_modification_date date [note: 'Date the contract was modified, if contract_modification_flag is True. Null for unmodified contracts.']
  contract_modification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this recognition event resulted from a contract modification (e.g., subscription upgrade, refund, chargeback, price adjustment). True if modified; False if original contract terms.']
  contract_modification_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for contract modification (e.g., \'Player upgraded subscription tier\', \'Refund issued\', \'Chargeback processed\', \'Price adjustment applied\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this revenue recognition record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  deferred_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of revenue deferred to future periods. Represents unearned revenue for services not yet delivered (e.g., remaining months of a subscription, unreleased DLC in a season pass).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert transaction currency to reporting currency at the time of recognition. Used for multi-currency revenue consolidation.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this revenue is recognized. Supports multi-year revenue recognition for long-term GaaS contracts.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which this revenue is posted. Links to the chart of accounts for financial reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context for this revenue recognition event (e.g., special accounting treatment, manual adjustments, auditor comments).']
  performance_obligation_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the performance obligation being satisfied (e.g., \'Deliver base game\', \'Provide 12 months of live service\', \'Grant access to DLC content\').']
  recognition_date date [note: 'The date on which revenue was recognized in the general ledger. The principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  recognition_event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for this revenue recognition event. Format: RRE-XXXXXXXXXX.']
  recognition_method varchar [note: 'The method used to recognize revenue per ASC 606. Point-in-time for one-time purchases (game sales, DLC); over-time for subscriptions and GaaS; proportional for bundled offerings; milestone for performance-based contracts; usage-based for consumption models.']
  recognition_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total transaction amount recognized in this event. Used for proportional and milestone-based recognition. Range: 0.00 to 100.00.']
  recognition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the revenue recognition event. Tracks whether revenue has been recognized, is still deferred, or has been adjusted.']
  recognized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of revenue recognized in this event. For point-in-time recognition, equals total_transaction_amount. For over-time, represents the portion recognized in this period.']
  remaining_deferred_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative deferred revenue balance remaining after this recognition event. Tracks the liability for unearned revenue.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of revenue. Product for one-time sales; service for ongoing subscriptions/GaaS; hybrid for bundled offerings.']
  revenue_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the revenue stream. Game_sale for base game purchases; DLC for downloadable content; season_pass for bundled future content; subscription for recurring GaaS fees; MTX/IAP for in-game microtransactions; licensing for engine/IP licensing; advertising for ad-supported F2P models. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: game_sale|dlc|season_pass|subscription|mtx|iap|licensing|advertising — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this event reverses previously recognized revenue (e.g., due to refund, chargeback, or accounting correction). True if reversal; False if original recognition.']
  service_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the service period for over-time revenue recognition (e.g., subscription end date, GaaS service period end). Null for point-in-time recognition.']
  service_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the service period for over-time revenue recognition (e.g., subscription start date, GaaS service period start). Null for point-in-time recognition.']
  total_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total transaction price allocated to this performance obligation. Gross amount before recognition adjustments.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'The date the underlying transaction (purchase, subscription charge) occurred. May differ from recognition_date for deferred revenue scenarios.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this revenue recognition record was last modified. Audit trail for record changes.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the recognition of deferred or earned revenue per ASC 606 / IFRS 15 accounting standards. Captures recognition date, recognized amount, deferred amount, recognition method (point-in-time vs. over-time), performance obligation reference, contract modification flag, and accounting period. Enables compliant revenue recognition for game sales, DLC, season passes, and GaaS subscriptions where revenue may be deferred over service periods.'
}

Table billing.promo_code {
  promo_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for promo_code']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Promotional campaigns are budgeted and tracked by cost center for marketing spend analysis and ROI measurement. Gaming finance allocates promo discount costs to title/studio cost centers to measure cu']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.ip_agreement. Business justification: Promotional codes issued as part of partnership agreements, co-marketing obligations, or brand licensing deals. Links promotional campaigns to governing agreements for compliance tracking and partners']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Promo codes often have jurisdiction-specific restrictions for tax treatment, gambling regulations, or regional compliance. Gaming platforms must enforce offer eligibility by jurisdiction. Replaces den']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign that issued this promotional code.']
  primary_promo_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user or system account that created this promo code record.']
  seasonal_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.seasonal_event. Business justification: Promotional codes are frequently tied to seasonal events (Halloween sale, Winter event discounts). Marketing teams track promo performance by event to measure event-driven monetization lift and optimi']
  applicable_sku_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the scope of products to which this promo code applies: all (any purchase), specific_skus (listed SKUs only), category (product category), platform (specific platform purchases).']
  attribution_channel varchar [note: 'The marketing channel through which this promo code was distributed, used for campaign performance attribution and ROAS (Return on Ad Spend) analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|social_media|paid_search|organic|affiliate|in_game|customer_support — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  auto_apply_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promo code is automatically applied at checkout for eligible players without requiring manual entry. True if auto-applied, False if manual entry required.']
  code_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotional code based on redemption limits: single_use (one redemption total), multi_use (limited redemptions), unlimited (no cap).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this promo code record was first created in the billing system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for fixed_amount discounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null for percentage or free_trial discounts.']
  current_redemption_count varchar [note: 'The current total number of times this promo code has been successfully redeemed. Incremented with each successful redemption.']
  deactivated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this promo code was manually deactivated or suspended. Null if never deactivated.']
  deactivation_reason varchar [note: 'The reason why this promo code was deactivated: fraud (fraudulent use detected), abuse (terms violation), campaign_ended (promotional period concluded), business_decision (strategic change), error_correction (code issued in error).']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'The mechanism by which the discount is applied: percentage (e.g., 20% off), fixed_amount (e.g., $5 off), free_trial (e.g., 30 days free), bundle_discount (special bundle pricing).']
  discount_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The numeric value of the discount. For percentage discounts, this is the percentage (e.g., 20.00 for 20%). For fixed_amount, this is the currency amount. For free_trial, this is the number of days.']
  first_time_purchaser_only_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promo code is restricted to players making their first real-money purchase. True if restricted to first-time buyers, False otherwise.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal business notes about this promo code, such as campaign objectives, special handling instructions, or approval references. Not visible to players.']
  issuing_source varchar [note: 'The origin or channel through which this promo code was issued: marketing_campaign, partner_promotion, influencer, customer_support (service recovery), loyalty_program, event_giveaway.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this promo code record was last updated.']
  max_redemption_count varchar [note: 'The total number of times this promo code can be redeemed across all players. Null for unlimited redemption codes.']
  maximum_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum discount amount that can be applied when using this code, used to cap percentage-based discounts. Null if no cap applies.']
  minimum_purchase_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum order subtotal required for this promo code to be applicable. Null if no minimum is required.']
  partner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external partner or influencer associated with this promo code, if applicable. Used for co-marketing and attribution tracking.']
  per_player_redemption_limit varchar [note: 'The maximum number of times a single player can redeem this promo code. Typically 1 for single-use-per-player codes.']
  platform_scope varchar [note: 'The gaming platform(s) on which this promo code is valid. Restricts redemption to specific storefronts or first-party platforms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all|steam|epic|playstation|xbox|nintendo|mobile_ios|mobile_android — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  promo_code_code varchar [note: 'The alphanumeric promotional code string that players enter at checkout to receive the discount. Must be unique across all active codes.']
  promo_code_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promo code: active (available for redemption), inactive (not yet active or manually disabled), expired (past valid_until_date), depleted (max_redemption_count reached), suspended (temporarily disabled by admin).']
  public_description varchar [note: 'Player-facing description of the promotional offer associated with this code, displayed in marketing materials and at checkout. Example: \'Get 20% off your next purchase!\'']
  sku_filter_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SKU identifiers or category codes to which this promo code is restricted. Null if applicable_sku_scope is \'all\'.']
  stackable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promo code can be combined with other promotional codes in a single transaction. True if stackable, False if exclusive.']
  terms_and_conditions_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the full terms and conditions governing the use of this promo code, ensuring compliance with consumer protection regulations.']
  total_discount_amount_applied decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative total discount amount (in the promo code\'s currency) that has been applied across all redemptions of this code. Used for financial reconciliation and ROI (Return on Investment) analysis.']
  total_revenue_attributed decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative gross revenue (before discount) from all orders that used this promo code. Used to measure the revenue-driving impact of the promotion.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this promo code becomes active and can be redeemed. Part of the promotional window.']
  valid_until_date date [note: 'The date after which this promo code expires and can no longer be redeemed. Null for codes with no expiration.']

  Note: 'Master record of promotional discount codes and vouchers applicable to real-money purchases, including their redemption history. Captures promo code string, discount type (percentage, fixed amount, free trial), discount value, applicable SKU scope, maximum redemption count, per-player redemption limit, valid date range, issuing campaign reference, current redemption count, and embedded redemption log (player reference, order reference, redemption timestamp, discount amount applied, redemption channel, redemption status). Distinct from in-game virtual currency promotions — this owns real-money discount instruments and their utilization tracking.'
}

Table billing.promo_redemption {
  promo_redemption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotional code redemption transaction. Primary key.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game title for which the promotional code was redeemed, if the promo is title-specific.']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice_line. Business justification: Promotional discounts are applied at the line-item level on invoices. While promo_redemption already links to storefront_order, the actual discount application happens on invoice_line (which represent']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign that issued this promotional code, enabling ROI tracking and campaign performance analysis.']
  platform_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.platform_sku. Business justification: Promo codes apply to specific platform SKUs. Required for promotional effectiveness tracking, discount attribution by product, and marketing campaign ROI analysis at SKU level.']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player who redeemed the promotional code.']
  promo_code_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional code definition that was redeemed.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.promotion. Business justification: Billing promo redemptions tied to monetization promotions enable campaign attribution, discount validation, and ROI analysis. Marketing operations require linking redemptions to promotions for perform']
  storefront_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order or transaction where the promotional code was applied.']
  transaction_storefront_order_id varchar [note: 'The external transaction identifier from the payment processor or platform SDK, used for reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  attribution_source varchar [note: 'The marketing attribution source that led to this promotional code redemption (e.g., email campaign, social media ad, influencer partnership, organic search).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this promotional redemption record was first created in the billing system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the discount amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the discount applied to the purchase in the transaction currency. For percentage discounts, this is the calculated dollar amount saved.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount applied if the promo code is percentage-based (e.g., 10.00 for 10% off). Null for fixed-amount discounts.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'The type of promotional discount applied: percentage off, fixed amount off, free shipping, bonus virtual currency, free item, or bundle discount.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the promotional code at the time of redemption, captured for historical tracking and compliance.']
  final_order_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total order amount after the promotional discount was applied.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this redemption was flagged as potentially fraudulent by automated or manual review.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The fraud risk score assigned to this redemption transaction by the fraud detection system (0.00 = no risk, 100.00 = high risk).']
  is_first_purchase boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this redemption was associated with the players first-ever purchase (true) or a repeat purchase (false).']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this promotional code was allowed to be combined with other promotions on the same order.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this promotional redemption, typically used for customer service annotations or special handling instructions.']
  original_order_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total order amount before the promotional discount was applied.']
  partner_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the external partner or affiliate who distributed the promotional code, enabling partner payout tracking.']
  redemption_channel varchar [note: 'The storefront or platform where the promotional code was redeemed (e.g., Steam, Epic Games Store, console first-party stores, mobile app stores, in-game store, web store). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: steam|epic_games_store|playstation_store|xbox_store|nintendo_eshop|mobile_app_store|in_game_store|web_store|retail_partner — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  redemption_code varchar [note: 'The actual promotional code string entered or applied by the player (e.g., SUMMER2024, WELCOME10).']
  redemption_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the geographic location where the redemption occurred.']
  redemption_device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device used to redeem the promotional code (PC, console, mobile, tablet, web browser).']
  redemption_ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the promotional code redemption was initiated, used for fraud detection and geographic analysis.']
  redemption_platform varchar [note: 'The operating system or gaming platform on which the promotional code was redeemed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: windows|macos|linux|playstation|xbox|nintendo_switch|ios|android|web|unknown — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  redemption_region varchar [note: 'The geographic region or market where the promotional code was redeemed (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific).']
  redemption_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotional redemption indicating whether the discount was successfully applied, reversed due to refund, or failed validation.']
  redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the promotional code was successfully redeemed by the player.']
  referral_code varchar [note: 'The referral or affiliate code associated with this promotional redemption, if the promo was distributed through a referral program.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason why the promotional discount was reversed (e.g., order refund, chargeback, fraud detection, policy violation). Null if not reversed.']
  reversal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the promotional discount was reversed. Null if the redemption has not been reversed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system that originated this promotional redemption record (e.g., Steamworks, Epic Games Store SDK, SAP S/4HANA, proprietary billing platform).']
  terms_accepted_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the player explicitly accepted the promotional terms and conditions at redemption time.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this promotional redemption record was last modified in the billing system.']
  usage_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The sequential usage count for this promotional code by this player (e.g., 1 for first use, 2 for second use), enabling multi-use promo tracking.']
  validation_error_code varchar [note: 'The system error code returned if the promotional code failed validation (e.g., EXPIRED, INVALID_CODE, USAGE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED, REGION_RESTRICTED). Null if validation succeeded.']
  validation_error_message varchar [note: 'Human-readable error message explaining why the promotional code redemption failed validation. Null if validation succeeded.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a promotional code or discount voucher being redeemed by a player on a real-money purchase. Captures redemption timestamp, player reference, promo code reference, order reference, discount amount applied, redemption channel (storefront, in-game store, web), and redemption status (applied, reversed). Enables tracking of promotional discount utilization and ROI measurement.'
}

Table billing.billing_account {
  billing_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the billing account. Primary key for the billing account entity.']
  licensee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensee. Business justification: Licensees (publishers, developers, middleware vendors) maintain billing accounts for royalty payments, license fees, and revenue shares. Enables licensee financial management, payment term enforcement']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player identity who owns this billing account. Links the billing account to the player domain master record.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique account number for customer service and billing correspondence. Format: BA followed by 10 digits.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing account. Active accounts can transact; suspended accounts are temporarily blocked; closed accounts are permanently terminated; under-review accounts are flagged for fraud investigation; pending-activation accounts await verification; delinquent accounts have overdue payments.']
  account_tier varchar [note: 'Segmentation tier based on lifetime spend and engagement. Standard represents typical players; premium represents moderate spenders; VIP represents high-value players; whale represents top-tier spenders with dedicated account management.']
  activated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the billing account was activated and became eligible for transactions. May differ from creation timestamp if verification was required.']
  auto_renew_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether automatic renewal is enabled for recurring subscriptions on this account. False requires manual renewal by player.']
  balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current store credit or wallet balance available for purchases. Positive balance represents prepaid funds; negative balance represents outstanding debt. Denominated in preferred currency.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for billing correspondence and tax jurisdiction determination.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for apartment, suite, or unit number.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City name for billing address.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for billing address. Determines applicable tax rules, payment methods, and regulatory requirements.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for billing address. Critical for tax jurisdiction and fraud detection.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region code for billing address. Used for sales tax calculation.']
  chargeback_count varchar [note: 'Total number of chargebacks filed against this account. High chargeback rates may result in account suspension and increased fraud scrutiny.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the billing account was permanently closed. Null for active accounts.']
  coppa_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this account is subject to COPPA restrictions (player under 13 years old in the US). True enforces parental consent requirements and restricted data collection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the billing account was first created in the system. Represents the start of the billing relationship.']
  data_retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when billing account data must be purged or anonymized per GDPR right-to-erasure or internal data retention policies. Null for active accounts with ongoing retention requirements.']
  dunning_cycle_count varchar [note: 'Number of dunning cycles (payment retry attempts) executed for the current outstanding balance. Resets to zero when balance is cleared.']
  dunning_status varchar [note: 'Current stage in the payment recovery process for failed recurring charges. None indicates no outstanding issues; soft-dunning represents initial retry attempts; hard-dunning indicates escalated recovery; final-notice is last warning before suspension; collections indicates account sent to external recovery.']
  failed_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of failed payment transactions on this account. High failure rates trigger fraud alerts and dunning workflows.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the account has been flagged for suspected fraudulent activity. True triggers additional verification steps for transactions.']
  fraud_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated fraud risk score ranging from 0.00 (lowest risk) to 100.00 (highest risk). Generated by fraud detection algorithms based on transaction patterns, device fingerprints, and behavioral analysis.']
  gdpr_consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the player provided explicit consent for processing billing and payment data under GDPR Article 6. Required for EU players.']
  last_failed_transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent failed payment transaction. Used to trigger dunning workflows and fraud alerts.']
  last_successful_transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful payment transaction on this account. Used for account activity monitoring and churn prediction.']
  lifetime_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cumulative amount spent by this billing account across all transactions since account creation. Denominated in the accounts preferred currency. Used for player segmentation and LTV (Lifetime Value) analysis.']
  marketing_opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the player has consented to receive promotional offers and marketing communications related to billing and monetization. Required for GDPR and CAN-SPAM compliance.']
  parental_consent_verified boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether parental consent has been verified for accounts subject to COPPA or similar child protection regulations. Required before processing payments for minors.']
  pci_compliance_scope varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this account stores payment card data subject to PCI DSS requirements. In-scope accounts require full compliance controls; out-of-scope accounts do not handle card data; tokenized-only accounts use vault tokens exclusively.']
  preferred_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the players preferred currency for pricing display and billing. Transactions may still settle in platform currency.']
  refund_count varchar [note: 'Total number of refunds issued to this account. Used for customer satisfaction analysis and fraud pattern detection.']
  subscription_active_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the account has at least one active recurring subscription (e.g., battle pass, premium membership). Used for churn analysis and retention campaigns.']
  successful_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of successful payment transactions on this account. Used to calculate transaction success rate.']
  tax_exemption_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued certificate number supporting tax exemption claim. Required for audit trail when tax_exemption_status is exempt or partial.']
  tax_exemption_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the tax exemption certificate expires and must be renewed. Null for permanent exemptions or non-exempt accounts.']
  tax_exemption_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the account qualifies for sales tax exemption. Exempt accounts bypass tax calculation; partial exemptions apply reduced rates; pending-verification accounts await documentation review.']
  total_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of all payment transactions (successful and failed) processed on this account since creation. Used for engagement and fraud analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to any field in this billing account record. Used for change tracking and audit trails.']

  Note: 'Master billing account record linking a player identity to their real-money financial relationship with the platform. Captures account status (active, suspended, closed, under-review), account creation date, default payment instrument reference, billing address, preferred currency, tax exemption status, lifetime spend amount, account balance (store credit), fraud risk score, and dunning status. SSOT for the player\'s billing identity — distinct from the player profile (owned by the player domain) and from virtual wallet (owned by monetization domain).'
}

Table billing.dunning_event {
  dunning_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dunning event record. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Dunning and retention costs are tracked by cost center for customer lifetime value analysis and subscription economics. Gaming finance allocates dunning campaign costs to title cost centers to measure']
  dunning_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier grouping all dunning attempts for a single payment failure incident, allowing tracking of the complete recovery workflow.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title associated with the subscription in dunning.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice that could not be collected, triggering this dunning event.']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment instrument that failed during the billing attempt.']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player whose payment failed and triggered dunning.']
  recovery_offer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retention offer presented to the player. Null if no offer was made.']
  resolved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: When automated dunning fails and escalates to manual intervention, the CS rep who resolved the payment issue (updated card, applied credit, negotiated payment plan) must be tracked for performance met']
  subscription_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.subscription_cycle. Business justification: Dunning events are triggered by failed billing cycles. While dunning_event already links to subscription (the parent), linking to the specific subscription_cycle that failed provides granular tracking']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscription that triggered this dunning event.']
  access_revoked_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether subscription access has been revoked due to unresolved payment failure.']
  access_revoked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when subscription access was revoked. Null if access has not been revoked.']
  attempt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this dunning attempt was executed.']
  attempted_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount that was attempted to be charged during this dunning event.']
  churn_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated risk score (0-100) indicating the likelihood that this player will churn due to this payment failure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this dunning event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the attempted payment amount.']
  dunning_attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of this dunning attempt within the dunning cycle (1 for first attempt, 2 for second, etc.).']
  dunning_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the dunning process (active: awaiting retry, resolved: payment succeeded, escalated: moved to manual collection, subscription_cancelled: player cancelled, grace_period_expired: access revoked, payment_updated: player updated payment method).']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this dunning case was escalated to manual collection or customer support. Null if not escalated.']
  failure_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the payment failed (insufficient funds, card expired, card declined, bank block, invalid card, fraud suspected).']
  failure_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed human-readable description of the payment failure reason provided by the payment processor.']
  gateway_response_code varchar [note: 'Raw response code returned by the payment gateway for this dunning attempt.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Total number of days in the grace period granted to the player for this dunning cycle.']
  grace_period_end_date date [note: 'Date when the grace period expires and subscription access will be revoked if payment is not resolved.']
  grace_period_start_date date [note: 'Date when the grace period began, allowing the player continued access despite payment failure.']
  is_voluntary_cancellation boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the subscription was voluntarily cancelled by the player (true) or involuntarily cancelled due to dunning failure (false).']
  ltv_at_dunning decimal(15,2) [note: 'Player\'s lifetime value at the time of this dunning event, used to prioritize recovery efforts.']
  max_retry_attempts varchar [note: 'Maximum number of automated retry attempts configured for this dunning cycle before escalation or cancellation.']
  next_retry_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next automated payment retry attempt. Null if no further retries are scheduled.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Communication channel used to notify the player about the payment failure (email, push notification, in-game message, SMS, or none if no notification sent).']
  notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dunning notification was sent to the player. Null if no notification was sent.']
  notification_template_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the message template used for player communication during this dunning attempt.']
  payment_processor varchar [note: 'Name of the payment processor or gateway that handled this transaction attempt (Stripe, Braintree, Adyen, etc.).']
  platform_code varchar [note: 'Platform where the subscription and payment failure originated (Steam, Epic Games Store, PlayStation Network, Xbox Live, Nintendo eShop, iOS App Store, Google Play, web). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: steam|epic|psn|xbox|nintendo|ios|android|web — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  player_response_action varchar [note: 'Action taken by the player in response to the dunning notification (updated payment method, contacted support, ignored notification, cancelled subscription, or no action).']
  processor_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier assigned by the payment processor for this dunning attempt.']
  recovery_offer_presented boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a special retention offer (discount, free period) was presented to the player during this dunning event.']
  resolution_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the dunning event was resolved (automatic retry succeeded, manual payment by player, player updated payment method, subscription cancelled, or written off as uncollectible).']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dunning event was resolved (payment succeeded or subscription cancelled). Null if still active.']
  retry_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between this attempt and the next scheduled retry.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this dunning event record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a dunning action taken when a recurring billing payment fails. Captures dunning attempt number, attempt timestamp, failure reason code (insufficient funds, card expired, card declined, bank block), notification channel (email, push, in-game), next retry date, dunning status (active, resolved, escalated, subscription-cancelled), and grace period expiry date. Supports automated retry and player communication workflows for GaaS subscription recovery.'
}

Table billing.credit_memo {
  credit_memo_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the credit memo. Primary key.']
  defect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.defect. Business justification: Credit memos issued for bug-related compensation track defect_id for quality cost accounting, defect impact measurement, and to report cost of quality (COQ) to finance and executive teams for quality ']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game title associated with this credit memo, if the credit is game-specific (e.g., in-game currency credit). Null if credit is account-wide.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Credit memos generate GL journal entries for revenue reversals and customer credits; audit trail requirement. Gaming companies must link credit memos to GL for financial statement accuracy and revenue']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing or promotional campaign that generated this credit memo, if applicable. Null if not campaign-related.']
  originating_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice that this credit memo is correcting or crediting against. Null if the credit is not tied to a specific invoice (e.g., goodwill credit).']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player account to which this credit memo is issued. Null if issued to a platform partner account instead.']
  primary_credit_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service agent, finance team member, or automated system process that authorized and issued this credit memo.']
  storefront_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment transaction that triggered this credit memo (e.g., a refund request, chargeback event). Null if not transaction-specific.']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.subscription. Business justification: Credit memos are issued for subscription billing issues (e.g., failed auto-renewal, service outage credits, prorated cancellations). While credit_memo already links to invoice and storefront_order, ma']
  support_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer support ticket that resulted in this credit memo being issued. Null if not ticket-driven.']
  tertiary_credit_voided_by_agent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agent who voided or cancelled this credit memo. Null if not voided.']
  applied_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary value of the credit that has been consumed (applied to purchases). Zero if not yet used.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center code responsible for the financial impact of this credit memo (e.g., customer service department, marketing department).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this credit memo record was first created in the billing system.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary value of the credit issued, in the specified currency. This is the gross credit before any application or expiry.']
  credit_memo_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the credit memo, typically formatted as CM-XXXXXXXXXX for customer and platform partner reference.']
  credit_memo_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the credit memo: issued (active and available for use), applied (fully consumed against purchases), partially_applied (some balance remaining), expired (past expiry date and no longer usable), voided (cancelled by issuing agent), pending (awaiting approval or system processing).']
  credit_memo_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the credit memo indicating the business reason category: goodwill (customer service gesture), billing_error_correction (invoice mistake), partial_refund (store credit instead of cash refund), promotional (marketing campaign credit), chargeback_reversal (chargeback won and credit issued), platform_adjustment (platform partner settlement correction).']
  credit_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized internal code representing the specific reason for issuing the credit (e.g., BILLING_ERROR, GOODWILL_GESTURE, PROMO_CAMPAIGN, CHARGEBACK_WON). Used for reporting and analytics.']
  credit_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the credit memo was issued, providing business context and details for audit and customer service reference.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the credit amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  data_retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this credit memo record is eligible for archival or deletion per data retention policies and regulatory requirements (GDPR, CCPA, tax law retention periods).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the credit memo expires and any remaining balance becomes unusable. Null if the credit has no expiration.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External system identifier or reference number from the platform partner or payment processor related to this credit memo (e.g., Steam transaction ID, Epic order ID). Used for reconciliation.']
  fully_applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit memo balance was fully consumed (remaining_balance reached zero). Null if not yet fully applied.']
  gdpr_consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the player provided consent for processing their personal data in relation to this credit memo, if applicable under GDPR. Null if not subject to GDPR or consent not required.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which this credit memo is posted for financial reporting and reconciliation.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the credit memo was officially issued and became available for use by the player or platform partner.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional internal notes, comments, or context about this credit memo for audit trail and customer service reference.']
  pci_compliance_scope varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this credit memo record is within PCI DSS compliance scope: IN_SCOPE (contains or references cardholder data), OUT_OF_SCOPE (no cardholder data), NOT_APPLICABLE (non-card transaction).']
  platform_partner_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the platform partner (Steam, Epic, console first-party, mobile app store) to which this credit memo is issued. Null if issued to a player account instead.']
  remaining_balance decimal(18,4) [note: 'Current available credit balance (credit_amount minus applied_amount). This is the amount still available for future purchases.']
  revenue_reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this credit memo triggers a revenue reversal in the general ledger (True) or is treated as a marketing expense (False).']
  storefront_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the digital storefront or platform where the original transaction occurred and where this credit will be applied (Steam, Epic Games Store, PlayStation Network, Xbox Live, Nintendo eShop, Apple App Store, Google Play, Direct Web Store). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: STEAM|EPIC|PSN|XBOX|NINTENDO|APPLE|GOOGLE|DIRECT — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the tax jurisdiction applicable to this credit memo (e.g., US-CA for California, GB for United Kingdom, EU-VAT for EU VAT area).']
  tax_treatment_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating how this credit memo is treated for tax purposes: TAXABLE (credit includes tax component), NON_TAXABLE (credit is pre-tax), TAX_EXEMPT (recipient is tax-exempt), REVERSE_CHARGE (VAT reverse charge applies).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this credit memo record was last modified (status change, balance update, etc.).']
  voided_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this credit memo was voided or cancelled. Null if not voided.']

  Note: 'Formal financial document issued to a player or platform partner representing a credit against a billing account (e.g., goodwill credit, billing error correction, partial refund as store credit). Captures credit amount, currency, credit reason, originating invoice reference, expiry date, credit status (issued, applied, expired, voided), and applying agent. Distinct from a refund (cash back) — a credit memo applies value to the billing account for future purchases.'
}

Table billing.payment_hold {
  payment_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment hold record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fraud analyst or automated system agent assigned to review this payment hold. Null for unassigned holds.']
  fraud_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific fraud prevention rule or model that triggered the hold. Enables rule performance analysis and optimization.']
  original_payment_hold_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payment_hold (original_payment_hold_id)']
  payment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment transaction that is subject to this hold. Links to the payment record under review.']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_instrument. Business justification: Payment holds are placed on specific payment instruments during fraud review or high-value purchase verification. The hold is instrument-specific (e.g., a specific credit card is flagged). This FK ena']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player whose payment is being held for review. Enables player-level fraud analysis and velocity checks.']
  sanctions_screening_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening_result. Business justification: Payment holds often result from sanctions screening hits (OFAC, export control). Gaming companies must link holds to screening results for AML compliance and hold resolution workflows. Critical for re']
  storefront_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storefront order associated with this payment hold. Links the hold to the original purchase transaction.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Authorization code returned by the payment processor when the hold was placed. Used to reference the authorization during capture or void operations.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166 country code of the billing address associated with the payment method. Used for geo-mismatch fraud detection.']
  capture_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the held payment authorization was captured and settled. Null if the hold was declined, voided, or expired.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment hold record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the held payment amount. Essential for multi-currency fraud analysis and settlement.']
  decline_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason the payment hold was declined and voided. Used for fraud pattern analysis and player communication.']
  decline_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the payment hold was declined. Provides context for player support and dispute resolution.']
  device_fingerprint varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the device used to initiate the payment. Used for device-based fraud detection and velocity checks.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment hold was escalated to senior fraud analysts or management for additional review. True if escalated.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the payment hold required escalation beyond standard fraud review procedures.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment hold was escalated for senior review. Null if never escalated.']
  fraud_rule_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the fraud rule or model that was active when the hold was triggered. Critical for audit trails and rule effectiveness analysis.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical fraud risk score assigned to the payment at the time of hold. Higher scores indicate greater fraud risk. Typically ranges from 0 to 100.']
  fraud_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'The fraud score threshold that was exceeded, triggering the payment hold. Used to evaluate and tune fraud prevention rules.']
  gateway_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier assigned by the payment gateway for this authorization hold. Used for reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  hold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount of the payment authorization that is being held pending fraud review. Represents the value at risk.']
  hold_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment hold was initially placed. Marks the start of the authorization hold period.']
  hold_expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment authorization hold will expire if not captured or voided. Typically 7-30 days from creation depending on card network rules.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique identifier for the payment hold, used for tracking and reference in fraud review workflows and customer support interactions.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason the payment was placed on hold. Used for categorization and reporting of fraud prevention triggers.']
  hold_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed human-readable explanation of why the payment was placed on hold. Provides context for fraud analysts and support agents reviewing the case.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment hold in the fraud review and settlement workflow. Tracks progression from initial hold through final resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending_review|approved_for_capture|declined|expired|escalated|cancelled|captured|voided — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  hold_trigger_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fraud prevention mechanism that triggered the hold. Distinguishes between automated rules, ML models, and manual interventions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fraud_score_threshold|velocity_check|geo_mismatch|first_purchase|high_value|card_verification_failure|suspicious_device|manual_review|rule_engine|machine_learning_model — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the payment transaction originated. Used for geo-location analysis and fraud pattern detection.']
  payment_processor varchar [note: 'Name of the payment gateway or processor handling this authorization hold. Critical for routing capture and void requests.']
  player_contact_attempted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fraud team attempted to contact the player to verify the transaction. True if contact was attempted.']
  player_contact_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fraud team attempted to contact the player for transaction verification. Null if no contact attempted.']
  player_verification_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of player identity and transaction verification attempts during fraud review. Indicates whether the player confirmed the purchase.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the payment hold after review. Indicates whether the payment was ultimately captured, voided, or allowed to expire.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment hold was resolved with a final decision (approved, declined, expired). Marks the end of the review process.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by fraud analysts during the review process. Documents investigation findings and decision rationale.']
  review_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the hold review based on fraud risk, transaction value, and player history. Determines review queue ordering.']
  review_started_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when fraud review of the held payment began. Used to measure review queue wait times and SLA compliance.']
  transaction_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166 country code derived from the IP address or device location at transaction time. Used for geo-mismatch analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment hold record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.']
  void_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the held payment authorization was voided and released. Null if the hold was captured or expired.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a payment authorization hold placed during fraud review or high-value purchase verification. Captures hold amount, currency, hold reason (fraud score threshold, velocity check, geo-mismatch, first-purchase, high-value), authorization reference, hold status (pending_review, approved_for_capture, declined, expired, escalated), review agent or automated rule reference, hold creation timestamp, expiry timestamp, resolution timestamp, and capture/void outcome. Critical for managing the gap between payment authorization and settlement in gaming where digital goods fraud rates are elevated.'
}

Table billing.dunning_cycle {
  dunning_cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for dunning_cycle']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or system account that created this dunning cycle configuration.']
  modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or system account that last modified this dunning cycle configuration.']
  platform_holder_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the platform holder (Steam, Epic, Sony, Microsoft, Apple, Google) for platform-specific dunning cycles that comply with first-party billing policies.']
  previous_dunning_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dunning_cycle (previous_dunning_cycle_id)']
  auto_update_payment_method boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dunning cycle includes automatic payment method update attempts via account updater services (Visa Account Updater, Mastercard Automatic Billing Updater).']
  cancellation_threshold varchar [note: 'Number of failed attempts after which subscription or service is automatically cancelled if payment is not resolved.']
  chargeback_protection_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dunning cycle includes chargeback prevention measures such as pre-dispute notifications or representment workflows.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance framework this dunning cycle adheres to (e.g., PCI DSS, GDPR, CCPA, regional consumer protection laws).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dunning cycle configuration was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for dunning cycles that are currency-specific due to payment processor or regulatory requirements.']
  cycle_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the dunning cycle, used for external reference and reporting.']
  cycle_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the dunning cycle (e.g., Standard Retry, Aggressive Recovery, Soft Reminder).']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dunning cycle strategy (standard, aggressive, soft, custom, platform_specific for console/mobile stores, regulatory for compliance-driven cycles).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the dunning cycle strategy, business rationale, and intended use cases.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this dunning cycle configuration expires or is superseded. Null for open-ended cycles.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this dunning cycle configuration becomes active and applicable to payment retry logic.']
  escalation_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether failed payments in this cycle are escalated to collections, account management, or alternative recovery processes.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market this dunning cycle applies to, allowing region-specific retry strategies based on local payment behaviors and regulations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NAM|EUR|APAC|LATAM|MEA|CHN|JPN|KOR — promote to reference product]']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the first retry attempt is initiated after initial payment failure, allowing customer self-resolution.']
  hard_decline_action varchar [note: 'Action taken when a hard decline (permanent failure like stolen card, closed account) occurs: immediate suspension, notification only, request new payment method, or escalate to support.']
  max_retry_attempts varchar [note: 'Maximum number of payment retry attempts allowed within this dunning cycle before escalation or account suspension.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dunning cycle configuration was last modified.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Primary communication channel used for dunning notifications to customers (email, SMS, push notification, in-app message, or multi-channel).']
  notification_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated customer notifications (email, SMS, in-app) are sent during this dunning cycle.']
  payment_method_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment instrument this dunning cycle applies to. Specific cycles may be configured for different payment methods due to varying retry success rates.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this dunning cycle for resource allocation and processing queue management.']
  retry_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between consecutive payment retry attempts in this dunning cycle.']
  revenue_recovery_rate_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of failed payment revenue this dunning cycle aims to recover, used for performance measurement and optimization.']
  soft_decline_retry_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether soft declines (temporary failures like insufficient funds, issuer timeout) trigger immediate retry attempts within this cycle.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dunning cycle configuration.']
  subscription_tier varchar [note: 'Subscription tier this dunning cycle applies to, enabling differentiated retry strategies for high-value vs. low-value customers.']
  suspension_threshold varchar [note: 'Number of failed attempts after which account or subscription is automatically suspended pending payment resolution.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for dunning_cycle. Referenced by dunning_cycle_id.'
}

Table billing.fraud_rule {
  fraud_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fraud_rule']
  superseded_fraud_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fraud_rule (superseded_fraud_rule_id)']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Automated action taken when the rule is triggered: block transaction, route to manual review, flag for investigation, challenge user with additional authentication, or notify fraud team.']
  audit_trail_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether detailed audit logging is enabled for this rule (true) or not (false). Required for high-severity rules and compliance purposes.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance framework this rule supports (e.g., PCI DSS, GDPR, CCPA, regional gaming regulations). Multiple frameworks separated by commas.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fraud rule record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the fraud pattern this rule detects and the rationale for its configuration.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this rule was deactivated or deprecated. Null for currently active rules.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this rule became active in the fraud detection system.']
  execution_order varchar [note: 'Sequence order in which this rule is evaluated in the fraud detection pipeline. Lower numbers execute first. Used to optimize performance and dependency management.']
  false_positive_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Historical false positive rate (0.0000 to 1.0000) for this rule, calculated as the ratio of incorrectly flagged legitimate transactions to total flagged transactions. Used for rule tuning.']
  is_machine_learning_based boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rule uses machine learning models (true) or deterministic logic (false) for fraud detection.']
  last_tuned_date date [note: 'Date when the rule parameters (thresholds, weights, logic) were last adjusted based on performance analysis.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the machine learning model or rule logic (semantic versioning format: major.minor.patch). Null for non-ML rules.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this fraud rule.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fraud rule record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, tuning history, or special considerations related to this fraud rule.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Communication channel used for fraud alerts when this rule is triggered: email, SMS, Slack, PagerDuty, or webhook integration.']
  notification_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time notifications are sent to the fraud team when this rule is triggered (true) or not (false).']
  owner_team varchar [note: 'Name of the business team or department responsible for maintaining and tuning this fraud rule (e.g., Fraud Operations, Risk Management, Payment Security).']
  payment_method_filter varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of payment method types (credit_card, debit_card, paypal, wallet, bank_transfer) to which this rule applies. Null indicates all payment methods.']
  region_filter varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes to which this rule applies. Null indicates all regions.']
  risk_score_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric weight (0.00 to 100.00) assigned to this rule in the composite fraud risk scoring model. Higher weights indicate greater fraud risk contribution.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'Business category of fraud the rule is designed to detect: payment fraud, account takeover, chargeback fraud, refund abuse, bonus abuse, or identity theft.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the fraud rule, used for external reference and system integration.']
  rule_logic_expression varchar [note: 'Technical expression or pseudocode defining the rule logic. May reference transaction attributes, user behavior patterns, or external data sources. Confidential to prevent fraud circumvention.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the fraud detection rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fraud rule by detection methodology: velocity-based, threshold-based, pattern matching, geolocation analysis, device fingerprinting, or behavioral analysis.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Application scope of the rule: global (all transactions), storefront-specific (Steam, Epic, console), region-specific, payment method-specific, or user segment-specific.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of fraud events detected by this rule, determining response priority and escalation path.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fraud rule: active (in production), inactive (disabled), testing (pilot phase), deprecated (scheduled for removal), or suspended (temporarily disabled).']
  storefront_filter varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of storefront codes (Steam, Epic, PSN, Xbox, AppStore, GooglePlay) to which this rule applies. Null indicates all storefronts.']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the threshold value: currency (USD, EUR), transaction count, percentage, or velocity rate (per hour, per day).']
  threshold_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric threshold value that triggers the rule. Interpretation depends on threshold_unit (e.g., dollar amount, transaction count, velocity rate).']
  time_window_minutes varchar [note: 'Time window in minutes over which the rule evaluates activity. Used for velocity and pattern-based rules (e.g., 5 transactions in 60 minutes).']
  true_positive_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Historical true positive rate (0.0000 to 1.0000) for this rule, calculated as the ratio of correctly identified fraudulent transactions to total actual fraud cases. Measures rule effectiveness.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number of this rule configuration. Incremented with each modification to support change tracking and rollback.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this fraud rule.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for fraud_rule. Referenced by fraud_rule_id.'
}

Table billing.bank_account {
  bank_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for bank_account']
  payment_provider_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment service provider or payment gateway that manages this bank account (e.g., Stripe, PayPal, Adyen).']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player or user who owns this bank account for payouts, refunds, or withdrawals.']
  primary_bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on bank_account (primary_bank_account_id)']
  account_holder_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the individual or entity that holds the bank account, as registered with the financial institution.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The full bank account number. Stored in encrypted or tokenized form per PCI DSS requirements.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the bank account in the gaming platform billing system.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'The classification of the bank account (checking, savings, business, prepaid).']
  added_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the bank account was first added to the gaming platform. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  bank_branch_name varchar [note: 'The name of the specific branch where the account is registered, if applicable.']
  bank_country_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the bank is domiciled (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'The name of the financial institution or bank where the account is held.']
  closure_reason varchar [note: 'The reason why the bank account was closed or deactivated.']
  closure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the bank account was closed or deactivated by the player or system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the account (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date when the bank account record expires or needs re-verification, if applicable. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bank account has been flagged for suspected fraudulent activity. True if flagged, False otherwise.']
  iban varchar [note: 'The International Bank Account Number used for international transactions, primarily in Europe and other regions. Up to 34 alphanumeric characters.']
  is_payout_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bank account is enabled for receiving payouts (e.g., tournament winnings, creator revenue share). True if enabled, False otherwise.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary bank account for the player for payouts and refunds. True if primary, False otherwise.']
  is_refund_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bank account is enabled for receiving refunds. True if enabled, False otherwise.']
  last_four_digits varchar [note: 'The last four digits of the bank account number, used for display and identification purposes without exposing the full account number.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the bank account record was last updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this bank account was last used for a transaction (payout, refund, or withdrawal). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes, comments, or special instructions related to this bank account (e.g., manual review notes, customer service remarks).']
  payment_token varchar [note: 'The tokenized representation of the bank account provided by the payment gateway (e.g., Stripe bank account token, PayPal billing agreement ID). Used for PCI DSS compliance to avoid storing raw account details.']
  pci_compliance_level varchar [note: 'The PCI DSS compliance level applicable to this bank account record based on transaction volume and risk assessment.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A calculated risk score (0.00 to 100.00) for this bank account based on fraud detection algorithms, transaction history, and verification status.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'The routing transit number (RTN) or sort code used to identify the financial institution. Format varies by country (e.g., ABA routing number in USA, sort code in UK).']
  swift_code varchar [note: 'The SWIFT/BIC code identifying the bank for international wire transfers. 8 or 11 alphanumeric characters.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify the bank account (e.g., micro-deposit, instant verification via third-party service like Plaid, manual review).']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'The status of the bank account verification process (micro-deposit verification, instant verification via Plaid/Stripe, etc.).']
  verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the bank account was successfully verified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for bank_account. Referenced by bank_account_id.'
}

Table billing.settlement_batch {
  settlement_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for settlement_batch']
  previous_settlement_batch_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on settlement_batch (previous_settlement_batch_id)']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the gaming platform or storefront that originated this settlement batch (Steam, Epic Games Store, PlayStation Network, Xbox Live, Apple App Store, Google Play Store).']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of manual adjustments, corrections, or promotional credits applied to the settlement batch.']
  batch_generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the platform holder generated this settlement batch report.']
