  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this test case was formally approved by the QA lead and transitioned to active status. Supports audit trails for test case governance and compliance reporting.']
  automation_framework varchar [note: 'Name of the automated testing framework or tool used to execute this test case when is_automated is True (e.g., \'Selenium\', \'Appium\', \'Unity Test Framework\', \'Unreal Automation Tool\', \'pytest\', \'Robot Framework\'). Null when the test case is manual.']
  automation_script_path varchar [note: 'File path or repository reference (Perforce Helix Core depot path or Git URL) to the automation script that implements this test case. Enables traceability between the test case catalog and the version-controlled test code.']
  build_version_introduced varchar [note: 'The game build version or software version in which this test case was first introduced into the test library (e.g., \'1.0.0\', \'2.3.1-beta\'). Enables historical analysis of test coverage growth across build versions.']
  certification_standard varchar [note: 'The regulatory or platform certification standard that this test case validates compliance against. TRC/TCR are console manufacturer technical requirements; ESRB/PEGI/USK/CERO/GRAC are content rating body requirements; GDPR/COPPA are data privacy regulations; PCI DSS covers payment processing; WCAG covers accessibility. None indicates a non-compliance test case. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TRC|TCR|ESRB|PEGI|USK|CERO|GRAC|GDPR|COPPA|PCI_DSS|WCAG|none — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this test case record was first created in the QA system of record (Jira). Serves as the authoritative record creation audit timestamp for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'The total number of active defects (Jira bugs) currently linked to this test case. Provides a quick indicator of the test case\'s defect discovery effectiveness and the current quality health of the associated game component. Not a calculated aggregate — sourced directly from Jira linkage metadata.']
  estimated_execution_mins varchar [note: 'The estimated time in minutes required to execute this test case manually or via automation. Used for test planning, sprint capacity estimation, and QA resource allocation. Supports release timeline forecasting.']
  execution_environment varchar [note: 'The target environment in which this test case is designed to be executed. Dev is the development sandbox; QA is the dedicated testing environment; staging mirrors production for pre-release validation; cert is the platform certification environment; production is for live-service monitoring tests.']
  expected_result varchar [note: 'The precise, observable outcome that must occur for the test case to be considered passed. Defines the acceptance criterion against which actual test execution results are compared. Essential for pass/fail determination in both manual and automated QA runs.']
  game_component varchar [note: 'The specific game system, module, or feature area that this test case targets (e.g., \'In-Game Store\', \'Matchmaking\', \'Player Inventory\', \'FTUE\', \'Anti-Cheat\', \'Physics Engine\', \'NPC AI\'). Enables component-level quality reporting and defect trend analysis across the game title.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test case is executed by an automated testing framework (True) or requires manual execution by a QA engineer (False). Drives CI/CD pipeline integration decisions and automation coverage reporting.']
  is_certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test case must pass as a mandatory requirement for platform certification submission (True). Certification-required tests are blocking for console, PC storefront, or mobile store submissions and must achieve a pass result before Gold Master sign-off.']
  is_regression_candidate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test case should be included in the standard regression test pack (True) for every build or patch release. Regression candidates are high-value tests that protect core game functionality and are executed as part of every CI/CD pipeline run.']
  jira_epic_key varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent Jira Epic that this test case is associated with, enabling traceability from test coverage back to feature epics and OKRs. Supports release readiness reporting at the epic level.']
  jira_issue_key varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique identifier for this test case as tracked in Jira (e.g., QA-1042). Serves as the business-facing reference across QA, development, and release management teams. Maps to the Jira Test issue type in the project management system of record.']
  last_executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this test case was most recently executed. Used to identify stale test cases that have not been run recently and to track test execution cadence across QA cycles.']
  last_execution_result varchar [note: 'The outcome of the most recent execution of this test case. Pass indicates the expected result was achieved; fail indicates a defect was found; blocked indicates execution was prevented by a dependency or environment issue; skipped indicates intentional exclusion; not_run indicates no execution has occurred yet.']
  last_passed_build varchar [note: 'The most recent game build version in which this test case achieved a pass result. Used to identify stale or flaky tests and to assess the current health of test coverage relative to the active build.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the test case within its authoring and maintenance lifecycle. Draft indicates the test case is being authored and not yet approved for execution; active indicates it is approved and available for test runs; deprecated indicates it has been superseded by a newer version; archived indicates it is retained for historical reference only; under_review indicates it is pending QA lead approval.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the QA engineer, lead, or team responsible for authoring, maintaining, and reviewing this test case. Provides accountability and a point of contact for test case clarifications and updates.']
  owner_team varchar [note: 'The QA team or functional group responsible for this test case (e.g., \'Core Gameplay QA\', \'Platform Certification Team\', \'Performance QA\', \'Compliance QA\', \'UAT Team\'). Enables team-level quality metrics and workload distribution reporting.']
  pass_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The historical pass rate of this test case expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00), calculated as the ratio of pass executions to total executions over the test case\'s lifetime. Identifies flaky or consistently failing tests for prioritized maintenance. Sourced from execution history aggregation in the QA system.']
  platform_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of target platforms for which this test case is applicable (e.g., \'PC,PS5,Xbox Series X,iOS,Android,Switch\'). Determines which platform-specific test runs should include this test case. Aligns with Steamworks, Console SDKs, and mobile store certification requirements.']
  preconditions varchar [note: 'Describes the required system state, environment configuration, player account state, or data setup that must exist before the test case can be executed (e.g., \'Player must be logged in with a level 10 character and have 500 in-game currency\'). Critical for reproducibility and automation setup.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business-assigned priority level indicating the importance of executing this test case relative to others. Critical tests must pass before any release gate; high-priority tests cover core gameplay loops and monetization flows; medium covers secondary features; low covers edge cases and cosmetic validations. Drives test execution ordering and triage decisions.']
  release_milestone varchar [note: 'The game release milestone or sprint to which this test case is associated (e.g., \'Alpha\', \'Beta\', \'Gold Master\', \'Patch 1.2\', \'DLC Drop 3\'). Links test coverage to the release planning lifecycle and quality gate checkpoints.']
  requirement_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or reference to the business or technical requirement that this test case validates (e.g., a Jira story key, a GDD section reference, or a platform TRC/TCR requirement ID). Ensures full traceability from requirements through test coverage to defects.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes added by the QA lead or reviewer during the test case review and approval process. Captures feedback, clarifications, or conditions for approval that are not captured in structured fields.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Indicates the potential impact on the game or platform if this test case fails. Blocker failures prevent release; major failures impact core gameplay or monetization; minor failures affect non-critical features; trivial failures are cosmetic or low-impact. Used in release quality gate assessments.']
  test_case_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose, scope, and intent of the test case. Provides context for QA engineers and stakeholders on what business functionality or technical behavior is being validated.']
  test_case_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable title of the test case that clearly identifies the scenario being validated (e.g., \'Verify player inventory persists after server restart\'). Used as the primary display label in QA dashboards and test reports.']
  test_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification of the test case by its scope and coverage level within the QA process. Smoke tests validate basic build stability; integration tests verify cross-system interactions; localization tests validate regional language and cultural content; security tests verify DRM and anti-cheat integrity; accessibility tests validate compliance with platform accessibility guidelines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: smoke|sanity|integration|unit|end_to_end|localization|security|accessibility — promote to reference product]']
  test_steps varchar [note: 'Step-by-step instructions for executing the test case, written in a numbered or structured format. Each step describes a discrete action the tester or automation script must perform. Sourced from Jira test management fields and serves as the authoritative execution guide.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the test case by its testing methodology and objective. Functional tests validate game features; regression tests ensure existing functionality is not broken by new changes; performance tests measure FPS, load times, and stability benchmarks; compliance tests verify ESRB/PEGI/GDPR/COPPA adherence; certification tests validate platform TRC/TCR requirements for console submission.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this test case record was most recently modified. Tracks the recency of test case maintenance and supports change detection in ETL pipelines from Jira to the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the test case following semantic versioning conventions (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.3\', \'1.0.1\'). Incremented when test steps, expected results, or preconditions are materially changed. Supports change management and audit traceability.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all test cases used across QA, UAT, performance, regression, and certification testing. Captures test case name, description, test type (functional, regression, performance, compliance, certification), preconditions, test steps, expected results, platform applicability, associated game component, automation flag, and ownership. Serves as the authoritative test case library for all QA activities.'
}

Table quality.test_plan {
  test_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the QA test plan record in the Gaming lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  audit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_schedule. Business justification: Test plans align with scheduled compliance audits (annual COPPA review, GDPR audit). Real business process: audit preparation and compliance validation planning.']
  content_patch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.patch. Business justification: Test plans validate patch content before deployment (patch regression test plan, hotfix validation plan). Patch-scoped test planning enables targeted QA for incremental releases.']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_release. Business justification: Test plans scope to specific content drops for validation (seasonal event test plan, DLC test plan, patch test plan). Release-scoped test planning enables targeted QA resource allocation and release g']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the QA lead or test manager responsible for owning and executing this test plan. Supports accountability tracking and escalation routing in release gate reviews.']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Test plans consume approved QA budgets; finance tracks planned vs actual QA spend for cost control, and test plan approval gates reference budget availability. Essential for project cost management an']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the development studio responsible for the game build under test. Supports studio-level QA reporting and accountability.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title this test plan is associated with. Links QA planning to the specific game product under test.']
  jira_sprint_id varchar [note: 'The Jira sprint identifier associated with the QA execution work for this test plan. Links test plan execution to the agile sprint cadence for capacity and velocity reporting.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Test plans validate KPI-related features (monetization flows, retention mechanics); linking ensures test coverage aligns with business metrics and KPI measurement is validated.']
  milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.milestone. Business justification: Test plans scope to milestones (alpha, beta, gold master); QA planning aligns with development milestones. Milestone exit criteria include test plan completion. No existing candidate; creating milesto']
  platform_cert_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.cert_submission. Business justification: Test plans scope to specific cert submissions; plan execution gates submission readiness and validates TRC/TCR coverage before platform holder review.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Test plans scope compliance validation activities (age rating, privacy, platform TRCs). Real business process: release compliance planning and regulatory checkpoint definition.']
  release_gate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the release gate or quality milestone that this test plan must satisfy before the game build can progress to the next production stage (e.g., Alpha Gate, Gold Master Gate, Cert Submission Gate).']
  seasonal_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.seasonal_event. Business justification: Test plans scope to seasonal events for validation (Halloween event test plan, holiday event test plan). Event-scoped test planning enables targeted QA for time-limited content.']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.storefront. Business justification: Test plans scope to specific platforms for certification cycles; each platform has distinct entry/exit criteria and compliance frameworks requiring separate planning.']
  title_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.title_release. Business justification: Test plans validate specific releases for go/no-go decisions. Release-level test coverage tracking is required for launch readiness reviews and post-launch defect attribution. Replaces string release_']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.tournament. Business justification: Tournament-specific test plans (pre-event certification, patch validation for LAN events) must reference the tournament for scheduling coordination, stakeholder sign-off, and release gate approval tie']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'The actual date on which test execution concluded. Used to calculate test cycle duration and compare against planned schedule for QA velocity reporting.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'The actual date on which test execution commenced. Compared against planned_start_date to measure QA schedule adherence and identify planning variance.']
  cert_submission_ready boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the game build has passed all platform certification readiness checks within this test plan and is cleared for submission to first-party certification (e.g., Sony, Microsoft, Nintendo, Apple, Google). Sourced from Steamworks/Console SDK certification checklists.']
  coppa_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the game has been tested and verified for compliance with COPPA requirements, particularly relevant for titles rated for children or with online features accessible to minors.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this test plan record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting data lineage, compliance reporting, and change history tracking.']
  current_cycle_build_version varchar [note: 'The specific game build version being tested in the current active cycle. May differ from target_build_version if a hotfix build was submitted mid-cycle. Sourced from Perforce Helix Core.']
  current_cycle_number varchar [note: 'The ordinal number of the currently active or most recently completed test cycle within this plan (e.g., 1 = first cycle, 2 = re-test cycle). Tracks iteration progression.']
  current_cycle_status varchar [note: 'Execution status of the currently active test cycle. Values: planned (not yet started), active (execution in progress), completed (cycle finished), aborted (cycle terminated early due to blocking defects or build failure).']
  cycle_count varchar [note: 'Total number of test execution cycles (iterations) defined under this test plan. Each cycle targets a specific build and has its own start/end dates, pass/fail results, and defect counts.']
  defect_count_critical varchar [note: 'Number of open or unresolved defects classified as Critical (P1) severity in the current cycle. Critical defects are showstoppers that block release. Sourced from Jira bug tracking.']
  defect_count_major varchar [note: 'Number of open or unresolved defects classified as Major (P2) severity in the current cycle. Major defects significantly impact gameplay or platform certification. Sourced from Jira bug tracking.']
  defect_count_minor varchar [note: 'Number of open or unresolved defects classified as Minor (P3) severity in the current cycle. Minor defects have limited gameplay impact and may be deferred post-launch. Sourced from Jira bug tracking.']
  defect_count_trivial varchar [note: 'Number of open or unresolved defects classified as Trivial (P4) severity in the current cycle. Cosmetic or negligible issues typically deferred to a future patch. Sourced from Jira bug tracking.']
  entry_criteria varchar [note: 'Conditions that must be met before test execution can begin (e.g., \'Build deployed to cert environment, smoke test passed, no P1 blockers open\'). Enforces release gate discipline.']
  esrb_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the game content tested in this plan has been verified as compliant with ESRB rating requirements for North American distribution. Required for all titles targeting North American retail and digital storefronts.']
  exit_criteria varchar [note: 'Conditions that must be satisfied to declare the test plan complete and approve progression to the next release gate (e.g., \'100% critical test cases executed, pass rate ≥ 95%, zero P1 defects open\').']
  jira_epic_key varchar [note: 'The Jira epic key (e.g., QA-1042) that anchors this test plan within the project management system. Enables bidirectional traceability between the QA test plan and Jira sprint/backlog planning.']
  objectives varchar [note: 'Stated quality objectives for this test plan (e.g., \'Validate all critical gameplay paths on PS5 prior to Gold Master submission\'). Drives prioritization of test cases and defect triage decisions.']
  pass_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current observed pass rate across all executed test cases in the active or most recently completed cycle (e.g., 97.50%). Compared against pass_threshold_pct to determine release gate eligibility.']
  pass_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum required pass rate (as a percentage, e.g., 95.00) for this test plan to satisfy its exit criteria and unlock the associated release gate. Defined at plan authoring time.']
  pegi_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the game content tested in this plan has been verified as compliant with PEGI rating requirements for European distribution. Required for all titles targeting European storefronts.']
  performance_fps_target decimal(6,2) [note: 'The minimum acceptable frames per second (FPS) performance benchmark that the game build must sustain during performance testing within this plan (e.g., 60.00 FPS for console, 30.00 FPS for mobile). Drives performance test pass/fail determination.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this test plan across QA tooling, Jira, and release management systems (e.g., TP-GAME2401). Serves as the business identifier for cross-system reference.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the QA test plan (e.g., \'PC Gold Master Regression — v2.4.1\'). Used as the primary display label in QA dashboards and release gate reports.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the test plan. Controls workflow transitions and visibility in release gate dashboards. Values: draft (being authored), active (execution in progress), on_hold (paused), completed (all cycles finished), cancelled (abandoned).']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the test plan by testing methodology. Drives which test suites, entry/exit criteria templates, and reporting dashboards apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regression|smoke|functional|performance|certification|uat|compliance|integration|localization|accessibility — promote to reference product]']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'The scheduled date on which test plan execution is planned to complete. Drives release gate scheduling and milestone dependency management.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'The scheduled date on which test plan execution is planned to begin. Used for QA capacity planning, sprint scheduling in Jira, and release milestone alignment.']
  platform_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of target platforms covered by this test plan (e.g., \'PC,PS5,XSX,iOS,Android\'). Determines which platform-specific TRC/TCR certification checklists apply. Sourced from Steamworks/Console SDK certification requirements.']
  qa_team_name varchar [note: 'Name of the QA team or squad assigned to execute this test plan (e.g., \'Console Cert Team\', \'Mobile QA Squad\', \'Performance Lab\'). Supports capacity planning and team-level reporting.']
  release_gate_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test plan has satisfied all exit criteria and the associated release gate has been formally approved (True) or not yet approved (False). Drives downstream build promotion workflows.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of what is in scope and out of scope for this test plan. Captures game features, systems, and platforms explicitly included or excluded from testing coverage.']
  target_build_version varchar [note: 'The specific game build version string targeted by this test plan (e.g., \'2.4.1.8823\'). Sourced from Perforce Helix Core version control labels. Ensures QA results are traceable to an exact build artifact.']
  test_environment varchar [note: 'The infrastructure environment in which testing is executed. Determines data isolation, build deployment pipeline, and certification validity. Values: dev, staging, cert (certification), production, sandbox.']
  total_test_case_count varchar [note: 'Total number of test cases included in this test plan across all test suites. Baseline denominator for pass rate, coverage, and execution progress calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this test plan record was most recently modified. Supports incremental ETL processing, audit trails, and change detection in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']

  Note: 'Master record for structured QA test plans and their execution cycles (iterations) associated with a specific game title, build, release milestone, or platform certification. Captures plan name, scope, objectives, entry/exit criteria, test environment, assigned QA team, and supports multiple cycle iterations each with their own start/end dates, target build, platform scope, total test case count, pass/fail thresholds, defect count by severity, cycle status (planned, active, completed, aborted), pass rate, and release gate linkage. Serves as both the planning artifact and execution container for all QA testing iterations. Links to Jira epics and sprint planning.'
}

Table quality.quality_test_execution {
  quality_test_execution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual test execution record within the QA system. Primary key for the test_execution data product.']
  ab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ab_experiment. Business justification: Test executions in experimental builds must track which experiment variant was tested to ensure proper test coverage and isolate variant-specific bugs before rollout.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Test runs target individual assets for validation (load time, format compliance, platform compatibility). Performance and certification testing requires asset-level test result tracking.']
  asset_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset_version. Business justification: Test runs validate specific asset versions in build pipeline (automated asset validation on commit). Version-specific test results enable CI/CD quality gates and promotion decisions.']
  build_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game build version under test. Ties execution evidence to a precise software build for release gate qualification.']
  build_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.build_submission. Business justification: Test runs execute against specific cert submission builds; results determine submission pass/fail status and gate cert approval.']
  defect_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary defect or bug raised as a result of this execution when the result is a failure. Maps to the Jira bug ticket created during QA. Nullable when execution passes.']
  drm_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.drm_entitlement. Business justification: Test runs validate specific entitlement configurations; DRM test results required for cert submission and platform holder approval.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the QA engineer or playtester who performed this execution. Used for workload tracking, defect attribution, and tester performance analytics.']
  game_server_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.game_server. Business justification: Test executions (automated and manual) run on specific game servers. Test result validation requires correlation with server build version, configuration, and performance metrics (FPS, latency) for ac']
  iap_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.iap_transaction. Business justification: Test executions for IAP flows generate real or sandbox transactions (test purchases, refund flows, receipt validation) that must be traceable for validation, debugging, and platform certification evid']
  level_map_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.level_map. Business justification: Test runs target individual levels for validation (playthrough testing, performance profiling, accessibility checks). Level-specific test results enable per-level quality gates and release readiness a']
  ml_model_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ml_model_registry. Business justification: ML models predict test outcomes or prioritize test cases; test executions validate model predictions and provide training data for model improvement and test optimization.']
  network_region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.network_region. Business justification: Test executions target specific regions for geo-specific validation (latency SLAs, content availability, compliance). Certification testing requires regional execution tracking for platform-holder and']
  parent_execution_quality_test_execution_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the original execution record when this record represents a rerun or re-execution. Enables lineage tracking of execution attempts within a test cycle.']
  sprint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.sprint. Business justification: Test executions performed within sprint boundaries; QA velocity and sprint health metrics require sprint-level aggregation. Sprint retrospectives analyze test execution pass rates. Creating new sprint']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.storefront. Business justification: Test runs execute against specific platform builds; results feed platform-specific cert submissions and release gates. Replaces string platform_code with proper FK.']
  telemetry_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.telemetry_event. Business justification: Automated test runs generate telemetry events; linking enables correlation of test failures with specific telemetry patterns for root cause analysis and test result validation.']
  test_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific test case that was executed in this run. Links to the test case definition containing expected results, preconditions, and test steps.']
  test_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.test_cycle. Business justification: Test executions occur within test cycles. test_execution has test_cycle_name (STRING) but no FK to test_cycle. Adding test_cycle_id FK allows joining to get cycle details. Cardinality: N test_executio']
  test_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the test plan or test cycle under which this execution was performed. Organises executions into release-gate or sprint-level QA campaigns.']
  test_suite_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.test_suite. Business justification: quality_test_execution currently has test_suite_name as STRING (denormalized). Normalizing by adding test_suite_id FK to test_suite master reference table. The test_suite_name STRING column becomes re']
  tester_player_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to player.player_account. Business justification: When players participate in beta testing or crowdsourced QA programs (increasingly common in gaming), test executions performed by players (not employees) need player_account link for access control (']
  actual_result_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the actual observed outcome during test execution, contrasted against the expected result defined in the test case. Primary evidence field for defect reproduction and release gate review.']
  automation_framework varchar [note: 'Name of the automated testing framework or tool used to execute this test (e.g., \'Selenium\', \'Appium\', \'Unity Test Framework\', \'Unreal Automation Tool\', \'pytest\'). Populated only when is_automated is True.']
  automation_run_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier of the CI/CD pipeline run or automated test job that triggered this execution (e.g., Jenkins build number, GitHub Actions run ID). Enables traceability from execution record back to the pipeline run.']
  build_version varchar [note: 'Human-readable version string of the game build under test (e.g., 1.4.2.8731-rc1). Captured directly on the execution record for traceability without requiring a join to the build table in release gate reports.']
  crash_occurred boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the game application crashed during this test execution (True). A crash is a certification-blocking event on all first-party platforms and triggers mandatory defect creation and escalation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this test execution record was first persisted in the system. Distinct from execution_timestamp which captures the real-world event time.']
  device_model varchar [note: 'Hardware model or device identifier of the machine used for this execution (e.g., \'PS5 DevKit CFI-7000\', \'iPhone 15 Pro\', \'Xbox Series X Dev Mode\'). Supports hardware-specific defect reproduction and certification evidence.']
  evidence_attachment_url varchar [note: 'URL or path to supporting evidence artefacts for this execution such as screenshots, video captures, log files, or crash dumps stored in the asset management system. Required for defect reproduction and certification submission packages.']
  execution_duration_sec varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds from execution start to completion. Used for performance benchmarking of test suites, identifying slow automated tests, and estimating QA cycle time for release planning.']
  execution_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this test execution was completed or terminated. Used in conjunction with execution_timestamp to derive execution duration for performance benchmarking.']
  execution_key varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable identifier for this execution record (e.g., QE-0001234). Used in QA reports, release notes, and cross-team communication as the business-facing execution reference.']
  execution_status varchar [note: 'Current outcome or lifecycle state of this test execution. \'pass\' indicates all assertions met; \'fail\' indicates one or more assertions failed; \'blocked\' indicates a prerequisite prevented execution; \'skipped\' indicates intentional omission; \'not_run\' indicates pending execution; \'in_progress\' indicates active execution.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which this test execution was initiated. Represents the real-world business event time and is the primary temporal anchor for release gate evidence and QA audit trails.']
  execution_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this execution was performed manually by a QA engineer or playtester, executed by an automated test framework (e.g., CI/CD pipeline), or a hybrid semi-automated approach requiring human validation steps.']
  fps_observed decimal(6,2) [note: 'Frames per second (FPS) recorded during this execution, applicable to performance and stability test cases. Used to validate performance benchmarks against target FPS thresholds defined in release quality gates.']
  game_title_code varchar [note: 'Short code identifying the game title under test (e.g., \'GAME_ALPHA\', \'TITLE_X\'). Enables cross-title QA reporting and defect density benchmarking across the studio portfolio.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test execution was triggered and run by an automated test framework (True) or performed manually by a QA engineer or playtester (False). Key metric for automation coverage reporting.']
  is_cert_blocking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a failure in this execution constitutes a platform certification blocker (True) per TRC/TCR requirements from first-party console manufacturers (Sony, Microsoft, Nintendo). Drives escalation workflows for certification readiness.']
  is_regression boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this execution is part of a regression test pass (True) verifying that previously passing functionality has not been broken by new changes, versus a new feature test (False).']
  jira_issue_key varchar [note: 'The Jira ticket key of the defect or task raised as a result of this execution (e.g., \'GAME-4521\'). Provides a direct cross-system link to the bug tracking system for defect lifecycle management.']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'IETF BCP 47 locale code representing the language and region setting active during this execution (e.g., \'en-US\', \'ja-JP\', \'de-DE\'). Required for localisation QA tracking and PEGI/ESRB/USK/CERO regional compliance testing.']
  memory_usage_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Peak memory consumption in megabytes observed during this execution, applicable to performance test cases. Validated against platform memory budget thresholds for certification readiness.']
  os_version varchar [note: 'Version of the operating system or firmware on the test device at the time of execution (e.g., \'Windows 11 22H2\', \'iOS 17.2\', \'PS5 FW 7.00\'). Critical for platform compatibility defect triage and certification evidence.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level of the test case at the time of execution, inherited from the test case definition. Drives triage ordering for blocked or failed executions and determines release gate blocking criteria.']
  release_version varchar [note: 'The game release or patch version this execution is qualifying (e.g., \'v2.5.0\', \'Patch 1.4.2\'). Used to aggregate execution evidence per release for go/no-go release gate decisions.']
  rerun_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this specific test case has been re-executed within the same test cycle or plan. A high rerun count on a failing test indicates a persistent defect or flaky automated test requiring investigation.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the defect or failure identified during this execution. \'blocker\' indicates a showstopper preventing release; \'critical\' indicates a severe functional failure. Nullable when execution result is pass or skipped.']
  steps_executed_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual test steps executed within this test case run. Used to identify partial executions (e.g., blocked mid-step) and to calculate step-level pass rates for detailed QA reporting.']
  steps_passed_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual test steps within this execution that produced a passing result. Enables granular step-level pass rate calculation alongside steps_executed_count.']
  test_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the test type executed. \'certification\' maps to TRC/TCR platform compliance tests; \'playtesting\' captures structured player experience QA; \'uat\' denotes User Acceptance Testing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: functional|regression|smoke|performance|certification|uat|playtesting|security|localization|accessibility|compatibility — promote to reference product]']
  test_environment varchar [note: 'The deployment environment in which the test was executed. \'cert\' denotes a platform certification environment (e.g., Sony DevKit, Xbox Dev Mode). Determines the validity of execution evidence for release gates.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording the most recent modification to this test execution record, such as a status update, defect linkage, or result note amendment.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each individual execution of a test case within a test plan or test cycle. Records execution timestamp, tester identity, build version tested, platform, environment, execution result (pass, fail, blocked, skipped, not run), actual result notes, defect references raised, execution duration, and automation run flag. Primary source of QA execution evidence for release gates.'
}

Table quality.test_cycle {
  test_cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the test cycle record. Primary key for the test_cycle data product in the quality domain.']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_release. Business justification: Test cycles validate specific content releases before go-live (pre-launch regression cycle, patch validation cycle). Release gate testing workflow requires cycle-to-release linkage for approval decisi']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the QA lead or test manager assigned as the accountable owner of this test cycle. Used for escalation routing and cycle ownership reporting.']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the development studio responsible for the build under test. Enables studio-level QA performance reporting and accountability tracking.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title (IP) that this test cycle is scoped to. Links the cycle to the specific game product under test.']
  jira_sprint_id varchar [note: 'The Jira sprint identifier associated with this test cycle, linking QA execution to the development sprint cadence. Enables sprint-level quality reporting and velocity correlation.']
  platform_cert_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.cert_submission. Business justification: Test cycles validate cert submission candidates; cycle completion gates submission approval and provides evidence for platform holder certification.']
  release_gate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal release gate or milestone record that this test cycle is linked to. Connects the test cycle execution results to the release decision workflow.']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.storefront. Business justification: Test cycles organized per platform for certification readiness; each platform has separate gold master approval workflows. Replaces string platform_scope with proper FK.']
  test_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.test_plan. Business justification: Test cycles execute test plans. test_cycle has cycle_type, cycle_objective but no FK to test_plan. Cycles are iterations of plan execution. Cardinality: N test_cycles : 1 test_plan. Adding test_plan_i']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.tournament. Business justification: Tournament-specific test cycles (pre-event regression, patch validation, LAN environment testing) must reference the tournament for scheduling coordination, gate approval, and competitive readiness si']
  abort_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason this test cycle was aborted before completion (e.g., \'Critical P1 blocker in build rendering all test cases invalid — new build required\'). Populated only when status = \'aborted\'. Supports root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when test cycle execution actually concluded (completed or aborted). Used to calculate cycle duration and compare against planned schedule.']
  actual_pass_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The actual pass rate percentage achieved in this test cycle, calculated as (test_cases_passed / test_cases_executed) * 100. Compared against pass_rate_threshold_pct to determine release gate pass/fail status.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when test cycle execution actually commenced. Compared against planned_start_date to measure schedule adherence and QA process efficiency.']
  certification_body varchar [note: 'The external certification or rating authority whose requirements are being validated in this test cycle (e.g., ESRB, PEGI, Sony TRC, Microsoft TCR). Applicable for certification cycle types. Null for internal regression or UAT cycles. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ESRB|PEGI|USK|CERO|GRAC|Apple|Google|Sony|Microsoft|Nintendo — promote to reference product]']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'The regulatory or compliance framework being validated in this test cycle (e.g., \'GDPR\', \'COPPA\', \'PCI DSS\', \'ESRB Content Rating\', \'ISO/IEC 27001\'). Applicable for compliance-type test cycles. Supports regulatory reporting and audit trail requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this test cycle record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage, compliance, and change tracking purposes.']
  cycle_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the test cycle (e.g., \'Sprint 42 QA Regression\', \'Platform Certification Cycle — PS5 v2.1.0\', \'UAT Cycle — Season 3 Launch\'). Used for identification in dashboards and reports.']
  cycle_objective varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the primary business and quality objectives for this test cycle (e.g., \'Validate all P1/P2 regression scenarios for Season 3 content drop prior to live ops deployment\'). Provides context for stakeholders reviewing cycle results.']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the test cycle by its testing methodology and purpose. Drives which test suites, entry/exit criteria, and reporting templates apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regression|certification|uat|smoke|performance|compliance|playtesting — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  defect_count_p1 varchar [note: 'Number of open or total P1 (Critical/Blocker) severity defects identified during this test cycle. P1 defects represent game-breaking issues that prevent core functionality and are hard blockers for release. Tracked separately per severity for release gate assessment.']
  defect_count_p2 varchar [note: 'Number of open or total P2 (Major) severity defects identified during this test cycle. P2 defects represent significant functional issues that degrade player experience but have workarounds. Tracked for release gate threshold evaluation.']
  defect_count_p3 varchar [note: 'Number of open or total P3 (Minor) severity defects identified during this test cycle. P3 defects represent low-impact issues that do not block release but are tracked for quality trending and backlog management.']
  entry_criteria varchar [note: 'Defined conditions that must be met before the test cycle can begin execution (e.g., \'Build deployed to QA environment, smoke test passed, all P0 blockers resolved\'). Enforces quality gates at cycle initiation.']
  exit_criteria varchar [note: 'Defined conditions that must be satisfied for the test cycle to be declared complete and eligible for release gate sign-off (e.g., \'95% pass rate, zero open P1 defects, all certification test cases executed\'). Drives release readiness decisions.']
  gate_waiver_justification varchar [note: 'Documented business justification for overriding a failed release gate (populated only when release_gate_status = \'waived\'). Records the rationale, risk acceptance, and authorizing stakeholder for audit and compliance purposes.']
  is_gold_master_candidate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the build under test in this cycle is a Gold Master (GM) candidate — the final release-ready build submitted for platform certification and manufacturing. Flags cycles that are part of the final pre-launch quality gate process.']
  is_regression_baseline boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test cycle has been designated as the official regression baseline for the associated game title and build version. Baseline cycles serve as the reference point for future regression comparisons and stability benchmarking.']
  jira_cycle_key varchar [note: 'The externally-known Jira issue or sprint key that uniquely identifies this test cycle in the Jira project management system (e.g., QA-1042). Serves as the cross-system business identifier linking the test cycle to its Jira sprint or epic.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for QA leads to capture additional context, observations, or stakeholder communications relevant to this test cycle that are not captured in structured fields (e.g., environment anomalies, scope changes, external dependency impacts).']
  pass_rate_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum required pass rate percentage (e.g., 95.00) that must be achieved for this test cycle to satisfy the release quality gate. Defined at cycle planning time based on cycle type and platform requirements.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'The scheduled date on which test cycle execution is planned to conclude. Used for release gate scheduling and QA timeline commitments.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'The scheduled date on which test cycle execution is planned to begin. Used for capacity planning, resource scheduling, and release timeline management.']
  qa_team_size varchar [note: 'Number of QA testers assigned to execute this test cycle. Used for capacity planning, workload distribution analysis, and QA resource utilization reporting.']
  release_gate_status varchar [note: 'The outcome of the release quality gate assessment for this test cycle. \'pending\' = gate evaluation not yet performed; \'passed\' = all exit criteria and thresholds met; \'failed\' = one or more exit criteria not met; \'waived\' = gate failure acknowledged and overridden by authorized stakeholder with documented justification.']
  target_build_version varchar [note: 'The specific game build version (e.g., \'2.1.0.4521\') that this test cycle is scoped to test. Aligns with the build label in Perforce Helix Core version control. Critical for traceability between defects and the build under test.']
  test_cases_blocked varchar [note: 'Number of test cases that could not be executed due to a blocking defect, environment issue, or dependency. Blocked cases are excluded from pass rate calculations and flagged for root cause analysis.']
  test_cases_executed varchar [note: 'Number of test cases that have been executed (run) within this test cycle as of the latest update. Tracks execution progress against the total assigned scope.']
  test_cases_failed varchar [note: 'Number of executed test cases that have resulted in a failure within this test cycle. Drives defect creation workflows and release gate blocking decisions.']
  test_cases_passed varchar [note: 'Number of executed test cases that have achieved a passing result within this test cycle. Used as the numerator for pass rate calculation and release gate assessment.']
  test_cycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the test cycle. Drives workflow routing, reporting visibility, and release gate eligibility. \'planned\' = scheduled but not started; \'active\' = execution in progress; \'completed\' = all test cases executed and results recorded; \'aborted\' = terminated before completion; \'on_hold\' = paused pending external dependency.']
  test_environment varchar [note: 'Name or description of the QA environment configuration used for this test cycle (e.g., \'QA-ENV-03 — PS5 DevKit Firmware 8.0, Build Server B\'). Ensures reproducibility and traceability of test results to a specific environment state.']
  total_test_cases varchar [note: 'Total number of test cases assigned to this test cycle at the time of planning. Represents the full scope of testing work planned for the cycle. Used for capacity planning and pass rate denominator calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this test cycle record was most recently modified. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Master record representing a planned and executed iteration of testing activity (e.g., sprint QA cycle, regression cycle, certification cycle, UAT cycle). Captures plan scope, objectives, entry/exit criteria, test environment, assigned QA team, start/end dates, target build, platform scope, total test cases assigned, pass rate, defect count by severity, pass/fail thresholds, release gate linkage, and cycle status (planned, active, completed, aborted). Serves as both the planning artifact and execution container for all QA testing iterations.'
}

Table quality.defect_triage {
  defect_triage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the defect triage event record in the quality domain Silver layer.']
  compliance_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_incident. Business justification: Triage sessions can identify defects that constitute compliance incidents (privacy breach, age-gate failure). Real business process: compliance escalation during defect triage.']
  defect_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent defect record that this triage event is associated with. Maps to the defect entity in the quality domain.']
  experiment_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.experiment_assignment. Business justification: Triage teams need to know if defect reporters were in experiments to assess if bug is experiment-induced; critical for determining root cause and experiment safety.']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the development studio responsible for the game title associated with this defect. Enables studio-level quality KPI reporting.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title against which this defect triage is recorded. Enables cross-title defect quality benchmarking and release readiness reporting.']
  primary_defect_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or QA lead who chaired the triage session and is accountable for the triage outcome decision. Maps to the workforce/employee entity.']
  tertiary_defect_reporter_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the QA tester, player support agent, or community manager who originally reported the defect. Maps to the workforce/employee entity. Corresponds to the Jira \'Reporter\' field.']
  affected_game_area varchar [note: 'The functional area or system of the game affected by the defect (e.g., \'FTUE\', \'PvP Matchmaking\', \'In-Game Store/MTX\', \'NPC AI\', \'Audio\', \'UI/HUD\', \'Save System\'). Sourced from Jira component field. Enables area-level defect density analysis.']
  affected_game_version varchar [note: 'The game build version string in which the defect was first observed (e.g., 1.4.2.1087). Aligns with the version label tracked in Perforce Helix Core and Jira fix version fields.']
  affected_platform varchar [note: 'The target platform(s) on which the defect is reproducible (e.g., PS5, Xbox Series X, PC, iOS, Android, Nintendo Switch). Sourced from Jira custom field aligned to Steamworks/Console SDK platform identifiers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PC|PS5|Xbox_Series_X|Xbox_Series_S|Nintendo_Switch|iOS|Android|PS4|Xbox_One — promote to reference product]']
  certification_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this defect poses a risk to platform certification submission (TRC/TCR compliance for console platforms such as PlayStation, Xbox, or Nintendo). True = certification risk identified during triage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this triage record was first created in the system, corresponding to the Jira workflow transition that initiated the triage state. Audit trail field for data lineage.']
  duplicate_of_jira_key varchar [note: 'When triage_outcome is \'duplicate\', this field records the Jira issue key of the canonical (master) defect that this issue duplicates. Enables duplicate chain analysis and defect deduplication reporting.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this defect has been escalated beyond the standard triage process (e.g., to executive leadership, platform holder, or external certification body). True = escalated; False = standard triage path.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of why the defect was escalated, such as platform certification risk, player safety concern, legal/regulatory exposure, or executive visibility requirement. Populated only when escalation_flag is True.']
  estimated_fix_effort varchar [note: 'T-shirt size estimate of the engineering effort required to resolve the defect, as assessed during triage. Used for sprint capacity planning in Jira and release schedule risk assessment.']
  jira_epic_key varchar [note: 'The Jira epic key to which this defect is linked, enabling roll-up of defect counts by feature area or release milestone epic.']
  jira_issue_key varchar [note: 'The externally-known Jira issue key (e.g., QA-1042) that uniquely identifies the defect in the Jira project management system. Serves as the business-facing identifier for cross-team communication and triage meeting references.']
  live_ops_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the defect affects live service operations such as GaaS event scheduling, in-game store/MTX transactions, matchmaking, or DAU/MAU tracking. True = live ops team must be notified. Critical for games operating under a Game-as-a-Service (GaaS) model.']
  monetization_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the defect directly impacts in-game monetization flows including IAP, MTX, virtual currency, or the in-game store. True = revenue risk; requires immediate escalation to monetization and finance teams.']
  player_facing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the defect is visible or impactful to end players (True) versus being an internal/backend-only issue (False). Influences prioritization, player communication decisions, and NPS/CSAT risk assessment.']
  post_triage_priority varchar [note: 'The official fix priority assigned during triage, reflecting urgency relative to release schedule and business impact. \'blocker\' indicates the defect must be resolved before any release can proceed; used to drive sprint backlog ordering in Jira.']
  post_triage_severity varchar [note: 'The official defect severity level confirmed or reassigned by the triage panel after deliberation. Severity reflects the impact of the defect on game functionality, player experience, or platform certification compliance (e.g., critical = game-breaking crash or TRC/TCR failure).']
  pre_triage_severity varchar [note: 'The defect severity level assigned by the reporter or QA engineer prior to the formal triage session. Captured to enable comparison with post-triage severity and measure triage calibration accuracy.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this defect has regulatory or legal compliance implications (e.g., GDPR data handling, COPPA age-gating, ESRB/PEGI rating content, PCI DSS payment flow). True = compliance review required.']
  release_gate_impact varchar [note: 'Classification of how this defect impacts the release quality gate decision. \'blocks_release\' means the defect must be fixed before any release; \'blocks_certification\' means it prevents platform submission; \'watch_list\' means it is monitored but does not block; \'no_impact\' means it does not affect release readiness.']
  reproduction_rate decimal(18,2) [note: 'The frequency with which the defect can be reproduced under the documented steps, as assessed during triage. Informs fix prioritization and test case design.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the defect root cause as determined during triage. \'third_party_sdk\' covers issues originating from Unity, Unreal Engine, or platform SDKs. \'platform_specific\' covers hardware/OS-level issues. Drives root cause trend analysis for process improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: code_defect|asset_error|design_issue|environment|third_party_sdk|platform_specific|data_issue|unknown — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_fix_sprint varchar [note: 'The Jira sprint name or identifier to which the defect fix has been assigned during triage (e.g., \'Sprint 42 - Gold Candidate\'). Enables sprint-level defect burn-down reporting.']
  target_fix_version varchar [note: 'The planned game release version in which the defect fix is scheduled to be delivered, as assigned during triage. Drives sprint planning in Jira and release readiness gate tracking.']
  triage_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the formal triage event occurred. Used for scheduling, SLA tracking, and release readiness reporting.']
  triage_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded during the triage session capturing discussion context, decision rationale, open questions, or conditions attached to the triage outcome. Sourced from Jira comment or triage meeting minutes.']
  triage_outcome varchar [note: 'The formal disposition decision reached during triage. \'accepted\' means the defect is confirmed and scheduled for fix; \'rejected\' means it is not a valid defect; \'deferred\' means fix is postponed to a future release; \'duplicate\' means it duplicates an existing issue; \'by_design\' means the behavior is intentional; \'needs_more_info\' means additional reproduction data is required before a decision can be made.']
  triage_participant_count varchar [note: 'The number of stakeholders who participated in the triage session for this defect (e.g., QA lead, producer, engineer, platform compliance officer). Supports governance and quorum validation for triage decisions.']
  triage_participant_roles varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of functional roles represented in the triage session (e.g., \'QA Lead, Producer, Platform Engineer, Compliance Officer\'). Captures governance breadth without storing individual PII.']
  triage_sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the triage event occurred outside the defined SLA window for the defect\'s severity level (e.g., critical defects must be triaged within 4 hours). True = SLA was breached. Supports QA process compliance reporting.']
  triage_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the triage event itself. \'pending\' indicates triage is scheduled but not yet conducted; \'in_progress\' indicates the triage session is active; \'completed\' indicates a final disposition has been recorded; \'cancelled\' indicates the triage was abandoned.']
  triage_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the triage decision was recorded in Jira. Distinct from triage_date which captures day-level scheduling; this field captures the exact workflow transition moment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this triage record was last modified, capturing any post-triage updates such as severity reassignment, escalation changes, or fix version updates. Audit trail field for data lineage.']
  workaround_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a player-facing or operational workaround exists for the defect, as determined during triage. True = a workaround has been identified and documented, which may influence deferral decisions.']
  workaround_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the available workaround for the defect. Populated only when workaround_available is True. Used by player support (Zendesk) and community management teams to communicate interim guidance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the formal triage event for a defect, including triage date, triage outcome (accepted, rejected, deferred, duplicate, by-design), assigned severity and priority post-triage, triage participants, target fix version, and escalation flag. Tracks the decision-making lifecycle of defects from discovery through disposition. Sourced from Jira workflow transitions and triage meeting records.'
}

Table quality.playtest_session {
  playtest_session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each formal playtesting session record in the quality domain. Primary key for this transactional entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  ab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ab_experiment. Business justification: Playtests often evaluate experimental features; linking enables analysis of playtest feedback by experiment variant to inform rollout decisions and variant selection.']
  age_verification_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.age_verification_event. Business justification: Playtests with minors require age verification events for COPPA compliance. Real business process: minor participant compliance and parental consent tracking.']
  build_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.build_submission. Business justification: Playtests validate specific cert submission candidates; feedback determines submission readiness and identifies blocking issues before platform holder review.']
  certification_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.certification_checklist. Business justification: Playtests validate TRC/TCR compliance (age ratings, accessibility features); findings map to checklist requirements for cert readiness assessment.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the QA lead or researcher who facilitated and was responsible for conducting this playtesting session. The primary party reference for this transaction.']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Playtest sessions incur direct costs (participant compensation, facilities, equipment) charged to marketing or research budgets. Finance tracks playtest spend for cost control and capitalization decis']
  game_mode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.game_mode. Business justification: Playtest sessions evaluate specific game modes (multiplayer, campaign, battle royale) for balance, fun, and retention. Mode-level feedback aggregation drives design iteration. Replaces string game_mod']
  game_server_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.game_server. Business justification: Playtest sessions run on dedicated game servers. Session feedback (fun rating, usability issues) must be correlated with server performance metrics (FPS, latency, crash count) for build quality assess']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the development studio responsible for the game build under test. Enables quality benchmarking and defect trend analysis across studios within the enterprise.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title under playtesting. Links the session to the specific intellectual property (IP) being evaluated, enabling quality trend analysis by game title.']
  milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.milestone. Business justification: Playtests scheduled around milestone reviews (vertical slice, alpha, beta); studios track which milestone a playtest validates. Milestone sign-off requires playtest feedback incorporation. Creating ne']
  network_region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.network_region. Business justification: Playtest sessions target specific regions for player experience validation. Regional performance benchmarking and certification readiness assessment require region context for latency, content availab']
  participant_player_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to player.player_account. Business justification: Playtest sessions recruit specific player accounts (often from targeted segments like high-engagement or VIP players) to participate in closed alpha/beta tests. Recruitment tracking, NDA enforcement, ']
  platform_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.platform_sku. Business justification: Playtests validate specific SKU configurations (edition features, regional content); feedback is SKU-specific for edition differentiation validation.']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the target platform on which the game build was tested (e.g., PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X, PC, iOS, Android, Nintendo Switch). Platform-specific quality metrics and TRC/TCR compliance are tracked at this level.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.tournament. Business justification: Tournament patch playtests (pre-event validation with pro players, balance testing for competitive meta) must reference the tournament for feedback routing, patch approval decisions, and competitive r']
  accessibility_evaluated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accessibility features (e.g., colorblind modes, subtitle options, motor accessibility controls) were formally evaluated during this session. Supports compliance with platform holder accessibility requirements and IGDA accessibility guidelines.']
  avg_fps_recorded decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average frames per second (FPS) measured across the session when performance_evaluated is true. A primary technical quality metric for game stability and player experience. Compared against platform-specific FPS targets (e.g., 30 FPS, 60 FPS).']
  bugs_logged_count varchar [note: 'Total number of defects and bugs formally logged in Jira as a direct result of this playtesting session. Provides a quantitative measure of session productivity and build quality.']
  build_label varchar [note: 'Human-readable label or codename assigned to the game build in Perforce Helix Core (e.g., \'Alpha-Sprint-42\', \'RC1-Candidate\', \'Gold-Master-Candidate\'). Complements build_version with a descriptive milestone identifier used in QA communications.']
  build_version varchar [note: 'Specific software build version of the game tested during this session (e.g., 1.4.2.1087). Sourced from Perforce Helix Core version control. Critical for correlating defects and findings to a precise code baseline and for regression tracking.']
  certification_readiness_status varchar [note: 'Assessment of the build\'s readiness for submission to platform holder certification (TRC/TCR compliance for PlayStation, Xbox, Nintendo, Apple, Google). Distinct from general release readiness — specifically tracks platform certification gate status.']
  consent_collected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether informed consent was collected from all participants prior to the session, including consent for data recording, observation, and use of feedback. Mandatory for GDPR and COPPA compliance when participant data is collected.']
  crash_count varchar [note: 'Total number of game crashes or critical application failures observed across all participants during the session. A key stability metric for release readiness gates and platform certification (TRC/TCR) compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this playtesting session record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  critical_bugs_count varchar [note: 'Number of bugs logged from this session classified as Critical or Blocker severity in Jira. Critical bugs are blockers for release and are tracked separately for executive QA dashboards and release quality gates.']
  csat_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Customer Satisfaction Score (CSAT) collected from session participants, typically on a 1–5 or 1–10 scale. Measures overall satisfaction with the game experience during the session. Complements NPS with a direct satisfaction measure.']
  difficulty_rating decimal(4,2) [note: 'Participant-reported perceived difficulty rating for the game content tested, typically on a 1–10 scale. Used to calibrate game balance, difficulty curves, and onboarding design. Critical input for game design iteration.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual elapsed duration of the playtesting session in minutes, derived from start and end timestamps and stored for direct reporting use. Key metric for QA capacity planning and session effectiveness analysis.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the playtesting session concluded. Combined with start_timestamp to derive actual session duration for QA reporting and capacity planning.']
  esrb_content_flags varchar [note: 'Content descriptors or flags identified during the session that are relevant to ESRB (Entertainment Software Rating Board) or PEGI content rating compliance (e.g., \'Violence\', \'Language\', \'In-Game Purchases\'). Supports rating submission accuracy and regulatory compliance.']
  ftue_evaluated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the First-Time User Experience (FTUE) flow was a specific evaluation objective of this playtesting session. FTUE quality is a critical KPI for player acquisition and D1 retention.']
  fun_rating decimal(4,2) [note: 'Participant-reported fun/enjoyment rating for the game experience, typically on a 1–10 scale collected via post-session survey. A game-industry-specific satisfaction metric distinct from CSAT, capturing the core entertainment value proposition.']
  jira_epic_key varchar [note: 'Jira Epic identifier linking this playtesting session to the overarching QA epic or release milestone in the project management system (e.g., \'QA-1042\'). Enables traceability between playtesting activity and sprint/release planning.']
  key_findings_summary varchar [note: 'Structured narrative summary of the most significant findings, observations, and insights from the playtesting session. Authored by the facilitator and reviewed by the QA lead. This is the primary qualitative output of the session record.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the playtesting session was conducted on-site at a studio facility, fully remote (participants at home), hybrid, or at a third-party testing lab. Affects logistics, data collection methods, and NDA compliance requirements.']
  minor_participants_present boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any participants under the age of 18 (or 13 for COPPA) were present in the session. Triggers enhanced data protection protocols, parental consent requirements, and COPPA compliance procedures.']
  nda_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether participants in this session were required to sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) prior to participation. Relevant for pre-release builds and external/focus group sessions where IP protection is required.']
  nps_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Net Promoter Score (NPS) collected from session participants via post-session survey, ranging from -100 to 100. Measures participant likelihood to recommend the game. A key player satisfaction KPI tracked from playtesting through live operations.']
  participant_count_actual varchar [note: 'Number of participants who actually attended and completed the playtesting session. Compared against participant_count_planned to assess session execution quality and statistical validity of findings.']
  participant_count_planned varchar [note: 'Number of participants originally scheduled to attend the playtesting session. Used to measure session fill rate and plan QA resource capacity.']
  performance_evaluated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether performance metrics (FPS, load times, memory usage) were formally measured and evaluated during this playtesting session. Distinguishes performance-focused sessions from pure gameplay/usability sessions.']
  recommendations varchar [note: 'Actionable recommendations generated from the session findings, intended for the development team. Includes suggested design changes, bug fix priorities, and areas requiring follow-up testing. Feeds into Jira backlog grooming.']
  release_readiness_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator set by the QA facilitator or lead indicating whether the build tested in this session meets the quality gate criteria for progression to the next release milestone (e.g., Alpha to Beta, RC to Gold Master). A critical input to the release quality gate process.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the playtesting session is planned to occur. Used for QA scheduling, resource allocation, and milestone tracking against the game development roadmap.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for the playtesting session (e.g., PLT-2024-000123). Used in Jira tickets, QA reports, and cross-team communications to reference a specific session without exposing the surrogate key.']
  session_objectives varchar [note: 'Structured description of the primary goals and test objectives for this playtesting session (e.g., evaluate FTUE flow, test PvP balance, assess accessibility features, validate performance on target hardware). Sourced from the Jira epic or QA test plan.']
  session_recording_url varchar [note: 'Secure URL to the recorded video or screen capture of the playtesting session stored in the digital asset management system. Confidential as it may contain unreleased game footage and participant interactions. Access controlled per IP protection policy.']
  session_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the playtesting session, tracking its workflow from scheduling through completion or cancellation. Drives QA pipeline reporting and release readiness dashboards.']
  session_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the playtesting session by methodology and participant profile. Internal sessions use studio staff; external sessions use recruited players; focus groups target specific demographics; blind tests exclude prior game knowledge; accessibility sessions evaluate inclusive design; UAT (User Acceptance Testing) validates release criteria; regression sessions verify bug fixes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: internal|external|focus_group|blind_test|accessibility|uat|regression — promote to reference product]']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the playtesting session actually began. The principal real-world business event timestamp for this transaction. Used to calculate actual session duration and compare against scheduled time.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this playtesting session record was most recently modified. Supports incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail requirements.']
  usability_issues_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct usability issues identified during the session, separate from technical bugs. Usability issues relate to UI/UX clarity, control intuitiveness, and player comprehension failures that impact player experience without being code defects.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each formal playtesting session conducted during game development. Captures session date, type (internal, external, focus group, blind test, accessibility), game title/build, platform, participant count, duration, facilitator, objectives, key findings summary, usability issues, FTUE feedback flags, and satisfaction scores (NPS/CSAT). SSOT for playtesting activity scheduling and aggregate session outcomes.'
}

Table quality.playtest_feedback {
  playtest_feedback_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual playtest feedback record captured during a playtesting session. Primary key for this entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Playtesters report issues with specific assets (broken texture on character model, missing audio cue, low-res icon). Direct asset feedback loop for content quality improvement.']
  battle_pass_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.battle_pass. Business justification: Playtest feedback on battle pass UX (progression pacing, reward satisfaction, tier skip pricing, premium value perception) must reference the specific pass instance being evaluated for design iteratio']
  build_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game build version under test at the time of feedback collection. Critical for correlating feedback to a specific code and asset state in version control (Perforce Helix Core).']
  compliance_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_incident.
