Business justification: Player feedback can reveal compliance incidents (minor data exposure, inappropriate content shown to minors). Real business process: incident discovery and escalation from playtest findings.']
  defect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.defect. Business justification: Playtest feedback can result in defect creation. playtest_feedback has jira_issue_key (STRING) and is_actioned (BOOLEAN) but no FK to defect. When feedback is actioned, it may create a defect. Cardina']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.dev_project. Business justification: Playtest feedback actionable at project level; product owners prioritize feedback into project backlogs. Project roadmap adjustments based on playtest insights. Creating new dev_project_id FK.']
  experiment_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.experiment_assignment. Business justification: Feedback from players in experiments needs assignment context to attribute feedback to correct variant; enables variant-specific feedback analysis for experiment decision-making.']
  game_mode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.game_mode. Business justification: Playtest feedback must be categorized by game mode for design iteration and balance tuning. Mode-level sentiment analysis drives live ops priorities. Replaces string game_mode with FK for structured r']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title under test when this feedback was collected. Enables cross-title quality benchmarking and release readiness tracking.']
  iap_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.iap_transaction. Business justification: Playtesters report monetization UX issues (confusing pricing, checkout friction, unclear value propositions) during actual purchase attempts. Linking feedback to the transaction provides purchase cont']
  level_map_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.level_map. Business justification: Playtesters report level-specific issues (stuck spots, confusing layout, performance drops, unclear objectives, visual glitches). Direct level feedback loop for environment quality improvement and pla']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensed_ip. Business justification: Playtest feedback on licensed characters, brands, or middleware features is tracked per IP asset for licensor reporting, brand compliance validation, and quality assurance. Real business need for bran']
  localization_string_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.localization_string. Business justification: Playtesters report translation errors, cultural inappropriateness, text overflow in UI, or confusing phrasing. Direct string feedback loop for localization quality improvement and cultural adaptation.']
  ml_model_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ml_model_registry. Business justification: ML models analyze sentiment or predict feedback impact; linking enables model validation against actual feedback and automated feedback prioritization for QA action.']
  npc_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.npc_definition. Business justification: Playtesters report NPC issues (broken AI, missing dialogue, incorrect loot, unbalanced combat, poor pathfinding). Direct NPC feedback loop for AI tuning and gameplay balance improvement.']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Anonymized identifier for the playtesting participant who submitted this feedback item. Anonymized to comply with GDPR and COPPA requirements; no direct PII is stored at this level.']
  player_behavior_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.player_behavior_segment. Business justification: Playtest feedback from different behavioral segments has different business weight; segment context enables prioritized action on feedback from high-value or at-risk segments.']
  playtest_session_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.playtest_session. Business justification: Playtest feedback is collected during playtest sessions. playtest_feedback currently has session_id pointing to player.session (wrong domain). This feedback is specifically from quality.playtest_sessi']
  seasonal_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.seasonal_event. Business justification: Playtesters report event issues (broken mechanics, unclear rules, reward bugs, poor balance, confusing UI). Direct event feedback loop for live ops quality improvement and player engagement optimizati']
  session_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the playtesting session during which this feedback item was collected. Links feedback to its parent session context including date, build, and test plan.']
  telemetry_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.telemetry_event. Business justification: Playtest feedback references specific gameplay moments captured in telemetry; analysts link feedback to telemetry to validate player claims and measure impact of reported issues.']
  accessibility_concern boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this feedback item identifies an accessibility issue (e.g., colorblind mode, subtitle readability, motor accessibility). Supports compliance with accessibility standards and inclusive design commitments.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'The disposition or resolution action taken on this feedback item by the QA or development team. Captures the outcome of the triage process and aligns with Jira resolution types used in bug tracking workflows.']
  actioned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this feedback item was first actioned (triaged, assigned, or converted to a Jira issue) by the QA team. Used to measure triage cycle time and QA team responsiveness.']
  content_rating_concern boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this feedback item raises a concern related to game content rating compliance (e.g., unexpected mature content, violence level, language). Flags items for review by the compliance team against ESRB, PEGI, USK, CERO, and GRAC rating standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this playtest feedback record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail timestamp for data lineage and GDPR data subject request processing.']
  device_model varchar [note: 'The specific hardware device model used during the playtesting session (e.g., PlayStation 5 Standard, iPhone 15 Pro, NVIDIA RTX 4090 PC). Enables hardware-specific performance and compatibility analysis.']
  feedback_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the feedback item by game experience domain. Drives routing to the appropriate development team and enables category-level quality trend analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gameplay|ui_ux|ftue|difficulty|performance|audio|visual|narrative|controls|accessibility — promote to reference product]']
  feedback_reference_code varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable business identifier for this feedback item (e.g., PF-20240101). Used in QA reports, stakeholder communications, and cross-system traceability. Distinct from the surrogate PK.']
  feedback_source_type varchar [note: 'The method or channel through which the feedback was collected during the playtesting session (e.g., written form, verbal debrief, in-game feedback tool, structured survey, facilitator observation).']
  feedback_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this feedback item within the QA workflow, from initial capture through triage, actioning, resolution, and closure. Drives QA team workqueue management and release readiness reporting.']
  feedback_subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing finer granularity within the feedback category (e.g., within \'gameplay\': combat_mechanics, progression_system, economy_balance). Enables detailed root-cause analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: varies by category — promote to reference product]']
  feedback_text varchar [note: 'The raw, verbatim feedback text as provided by the playtesting participant. Captures unstructured qualitative input for NLP analysis, sentiment classification, and QA team review. Classified confidential as it may contain unreleased game content details.']
  feedback_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) when this feedback item was recorded during the playtesting session. The principal business event timestamp for this transaction record.']
  fps_at_event decimal(6,2) [note: 'The measured Frames Per Second (FPS) recorded on the test device at the moment the feedback event occurred. Key performance metric for identifying frame rate drops correlated with player experience complaints.']
  game_component varchar [note: 'The specific game system, feature, or module associated with this feedback item (e.g., inventory_system, matchmaking, character_controller, hud, tutorial_flow). Maps to development team ownership for routing and accountability.']
  is_actioned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this feedback item has been reviewed and actioned by the development or QA team (e.g., converted to a Jira ticket, acknowledged, or formally deferred). Core operational flag for feedback triage workflow management.']
  is_ftue_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this feedback item is specifically related to the First-Time User Experience (FTUE) flow. FTUE feedback is prioritized for pre-launch analysis as it directly impacts Day 1 (D1) retention metrics.']
  is_release_blocker boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this feedback item has been classified as a release blocker that must be resolved before the game can pass the release quality gate. Directly feeds into the release readiness dashboard.']
  jira_issue_key varchar [note: 'The Jira issue key (e.g., QA-1234) linked to this feedback item when it has been converted into a tracked defect, task, or story in the Jira project management system. Null if not yet actioned into Jira.']
  level_or_area varchar [note: 'The specific game level, map, zone, or area within the game where the feedback event occurred (e.g., level_01_tutorial, zone_darkwood_forest, hub_main_menu). Enables spatial analysis of player experience issues.']
  milestone_target varchar [note: 'The release milestone by which this feedback item must be resolved (e.g., Alpha, Beta, Gold Master, Day-One Patch). Directly supports release quality gate management and Gold Master readiness tracking.']
  os_version varchar [note: 'The operating system version running on the test device at the time of feedback collection (e.g., Windows 11 23H2, iOS 17.2, Android 14). Used for OS-level compatibility and regression analysis.']
  participant_skill_level varchar [note: 'Self-reported or assessed skill level of the playtesting participant. Enables difficulty calibration analysis by correlating feedback sentiment and severity with player skill level, particularly for difficulty tuning.']
  platform varchar [note: 'The gaming platform on which the playtesting session was conducted when this feedback was collected. Critical for platform-specific QA analysis and Technical Requirements Checklist (TRC) / Technical Certification Requirements (TCR) compliance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pc|ps5|xbox_series|nintendo_switch|ios|android|vr — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority_rating varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this feedback item by the QA lead or product owner, indicating urgency of resolution relative to the release schedule. Distinct from severity: a cosmetic bug may be low severity but high priority near launch.']
  qa_analyst_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes added by the QA analyst during triage or review of this feedback item. May include reproduction steps, environmental context, or cross-reference to related defects. Confidential internal working notes.']
  reproducibility varchar [note: 'Assessment of how consistently the issue or experience described in the feedback can be reproduced by the QA team. Critical for defect prioritization: always-reproducible issues are prioritized for immediate investigation.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the issue raised by this feedback item was confirmed as resolved by the QA team. Used to calculate resolution cycle time and track quality improvement velocity across sprints.']
  sentiment varchar [note: 'Sentiment classification of the feedback item as determined by QA analyst review or automated NLP processing. Indicates the emotional tone of the participant\'s response to the game experience.']
  sentiment_confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Confidence score (0.0000 to 1.0000) associated with the automated sentiment classification, indicating the reliability of the NLP model\'s prediction. Used to flag low-confidence classifications for manual QA analyst review.']
  session_elapsed_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'The elapsed time in minutes from the start of the playtesting session to the moment this feedback item was recorded. Enables temporal analysis of when in a session players encounter issues, particularly for First-Time User Experience (FTUE) analysis.']
  severity_rating varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the feedback item indicating the impact on player experience and release readiness. Critical and major items are escalated as release blockers. Aligned with Jira severity taxonomy used in QA bug tracking.']
  sprint_target varchar [note: 'The Jira sprint identifier in which the resolution of this feedback item is planned (e.g., Sprint_42). Enables sprint-level quality planning and velocity tracking for QA-driven development work.']
  suggested_fix varchar [note: 'Optional free-text field capturing the participant\'s or QA analyst\'s suggested resolution or improvement for the identified issue. Provides actionable input to the development team during sprint planning.']
  target_audience_segment varchar [note: 'The player audience segment that the playtesting participant represents. Enables segmented analysis of feedback to understand how different player demographics experience the game, informing targeted design decisions.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of playtesting activity during which this feedback was collected. Distinguishes internal QA, external beta, User Acceptance Testing (UAT), focus groups, alpha testing, and platform certification testing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this playtest feedback record was last modified in the data platform. Supports incremental data processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer pipeline and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing individual feedback items collected during a playtesting session. Records participant identifier (anonymized), feedback category (gameplay, UI/UX, FTUE, difficulty, performance, audio, visual, narrative), verbatim feedback text, sentiment classification, severity rating, associated game component or level, and whether the feedback was actioned. Enables granular analysis of player experience issues pre-launch.'
}

Table quality.cert_finding {
  cert_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each certification finding, failure, or formally waived quality exception record. Primary key for the cert_finding data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete quality governance event with a full lifecycle from identification through clearance or risk acceptance.']
  ab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ab_experiment. Business justification: Certification findings in experimental builds need experiment tracking to determine if issues are variant-specific or baseline, critical for safe experiment rollout to production.']
  asset_bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset_bundle. Business justification: Certification findings on bundle size exceeding platform limits, missing encryption, or non-compliant compression format. TRC/TCR bundle requirements validation for platform submission.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Platform certification findings cite specific assets violating TRC/TCR (oversized texture exceeds memory budget, audio format non-compliant, missing age-appropriate content flag). Asset-level certific']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Cert findings discovered during external platform audits become formal audit findings. Real business process: platform audit integration and finding remediation tracking.']
  capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_project. Business justification: Certification findings trigger rework affecting capitalization decisions; finance must determine if certification-driven costs are capitalizable or expensed. Findings delaying go-live affect amortizat']
  content_patch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.patch. Business justification: Certification findings on patch submissions (patch fails TRC, hotfix requires expedited certification). Patch-level certification tracking enables submission planning and deployment blocking.']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_release. Business justification: Certification findings block specific content releases (patch rejected by Sony TRC, DLC fails Xbox TCR). Release-scoped certification tracking enables submission planning and deployment blocking.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title (IP) against which this certification finding was raised. Enables cross-title quality analytics and release readiness tracking.']
  iap_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.iap_transaction. Business justification: Platform certification findings related to IAP compliance (pricing display requirements, refund flow validation, parental control enforcement, receipt handling) reference specific test transactions su']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.ip_agreement. Business justification: Platform certification findings often stem from licensed content restrictions, middleware integration issues, or trademark usage violations defined in IP agreements. Certification teams must verify co']
  localization_string_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.localization_string. Business justification: Certification findings on age-inappropriate text, missing required translations, or accessibility non-compliance (missing subtitle timing, no screen reader support). String-level certification trackin']
  milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.milestone. Business justification: Certification findings tie to submission milestones; studios track resolution deadlines by milestone. Milestone payment gates require cert finding closure. No existing candidate column; creating new m']
  platform_cert_submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the platform certification submission under which this finding was raised. Links the finding to the specific build submission package sent to a first-party platform (e.g., Sony, Microsoft, Nintendo) or internal release gate.']
  primary_cert_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal team member or QA engineer assigned as the accountable owner responsible for resolving or formally accepting risk on this finding.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Platform cert findings often map to specific regulatory requirements (privacy policies, content rating compliance, data handling). Real business process: cert remediation tracking and regulatory align']
  remediation_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.remediation_action. Business justification: Cert findings require remediation actions to achieve platform approval. Real business process: cert remediation tracking and platform re-submission.']
  title_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.sku. Business justification: Certification findings are SKU-specific (platform+region+storefront). First-party cert processes require tracing findings to exact SKUs for storefront compliance and resubmission workflows. Critical f']
  affected_feature_area varchar [note: 'The game system, feature, or functional area where the finding manifests (e.g., online multiplayer, trophy/achievement system, DLC entitlement, save/load, UI/UX, audio, crash/stability, FTUE). Used for defect trend analysis and QA resource allocation.']
  age_rating_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding has potential impact on the game\'s age rating classification (ESRB, PEGI, USK, CERO, GRAC). True = finding may affect content rating; False = no rating impact. Triggers mandatory escalation to the ratings compliance team when True.']
  build_version varchar [note: 'The specific game build version string (e.g., 1.4.2.8831) in which this finding was identified. Enables correlation with Perforce Helix Core changelists and Unity/Unreal Engine build pipelines for root-cause analysis.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'The regulatory or platform compliance framework category under which this finding is classified. Determines the applicable governing body and reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: trc|tcr|esrb|pegi|usk|cero|grac|gdpr|coppa|pci_dss|app_store|internal_gate — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The UTC timestamp when this certification finding record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage, SOC 2 compliance, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  finding_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this certification finding was formally identified and logged, either by a first-party platform reviewer or by an internal QA release gate evaluation. Principal business event date for this transaction.']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the certification finding or defect, including observed behavior, expected behavior, and the specific game scenario or feature area affected. Authored by the QA engineer or platform reviewer who identified the issue.']
  finding_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable business identifier for this finding, formatted as CF-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used in communications with first-party platform holders, internal release committees, and audit trails.']
  finding_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the finding origin: cert_failure (first-party platform rejection), release_gate_failure (internal quality gate block), waived_exception (formally risk-accepted defect), advisory (non-blocking platform note), regression (previously passing requirement now failing).']
  fix_build_version varchar [note: 'The game build version string in which the fix for this finding was implemented and verified. Null until the finding is resolved. Enables traceability between defect resolution and specific Perforce Helix Core changelists.']
  is_blocker boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding is classified as a hard release blocker that must be resolved or formally waived before the title can proceed to launch. True = blocks release; False = non-blocking. Drives release readiness gate logic.']
  is_regression boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding represents a regression — a requirement or behavior that previously passed certification or a quality gate but has failed in the current build. True = regression; False = new finding. Critical for root-cause analysis of build pipeline quality.']
  jira_issue_key varchar [note: 'The Jira project issue key (e.g., QA-4821) that tracks this finding in the bug tracking and project management system. Provides traceability between the certification finding record and the engineering defect workflow.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for supplementary context, workaround descriptions, platform holder correspondence summaries, or internal release committee commentary that does not fit structured fields. Supports QA team collaboration and audit documentation.']
  performance_metric_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum or maximum required threshold value for the performance metric as defined by the platform certification standard or internal quality gate (e.g., minimum 30 FPS, maximum 5 seconds load time). Null for non-performance findings.']
  performance_metric_type varchar [note: 'For performance-related findings, identifies the specific performance metric that failed the certification or quality gate threshold (e.g., FPS below minimum, load time exceeding limit, crash rate above threshold). \'none\' for non-performance findings.']
  performance_metric_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The observed numeric value of the performance metric at the time the finding was identified (e.g., 24.3 FPS, 8.7 seconds load time, 0.023 crash rate). Null for non-performance findings. Paired with performance_metric_type for full context.']
  platform varchar [note: 'The distribution platform or console hardware for which this certification finding applies. Determines which first-party Technical Requirements Checklist (TRC) or Technical Certification Requirements (TCR) standard governs the finding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PlayStation|Xbox|Nintendo_Switch|PC_Steam|PC_Epic|iOS|Android|PC_GOG|PC_Microsoft — promote to reference product]']
  platform_reviewer_ref varchar [note: 'The first-party platform holder\'s internal case or reviewer reference number associated with this finding (e.g., Sony Lotcheck case ID, Microsoft CERT ticket number). Enables direct correspondence tracking with platform certification teams.']
  post_launch_monitoring varchar [note: 'Formal description of the post-launch monitoring plan committed to as a condition of waiver approval, specifying metrics to be tracked (e.g., crash rate thresholds, player complaint volume, telemetry alerts) and the monitoring duration. Required for all approved waivers.']
  privacy_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding has potential impact on player data privacy compliance (GDPR, COPPA). True = finding may expose a privacy risk; False = no privacy impact. Triggers mandatory escalation to the Data Protection Officer when True.']
  reproduction_rate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Qualitative frequency with which the finding can be reproduced following the documented reproduction steps: always (100%), frequent (>50%), intermittent (10-50%), rare (<10%), unable_to_reproduce. Informs severity assessment and fix prioritization.']
  reproduction_steps varchar [note: 'Step-by-step instructions required to reliably reproduce the finding on the specified platform and build version. Critical for engineering triage, fix verification, and regression testing. Sourced from Jira defect records.']
  requirement_code varchar [note: 'The specific platform certification requirement identifier (e.g., TRC R4000, TCR CERT-0123, App Store Guideline 4.2) that this finding violates. Provides direct traceability to the governing platform standard or internal quality gate rule.']
  requirement_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the platform certification requirement or internal quality gate rule that was violated, as defined by the governing platform holder or internal QA standard. Provides context for engineers and release managers without requiring lookup of external documentation.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the finding\'s resolution workflow: open (newly identified), in_progress (fix underway), resolved (fix verified), waived (risk formally accepted), closed (cleared by platform or gate), deferred (pushed to future release). SSOT for release readiness dashboards.']
  resolved_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the finding was formally resolved, verified as fixed, or cleared by the platform holder. Null for open or in-progress findings. Used to calculate time-to-resolution KPIs and release gate compliance.']
  risk_assessment varchar [note: 'Formal risk level assigned to this finding as part of the waiver governance process, reflecting the potential player impact, regulatory exposure, or commercial risk if the finding ships unresolved: low, medium, high, critical. Informs waiver approval authority level.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the finding per platform certification and internal QA standards: critical (submission blocker, must fix before release), major (significant functional defect), minor (low-impact issue), advisory (informational, no fix required). Drives prioritization and release gate decisions.']
  submission_round varchar [note: 'The sequential certification submission attempt number (1, 2, 3…) during which this finding was raised. Tracks how many resubmission cycles a title has undergone, a key KPI for release efficiency and QA process maturity.']
  target_release_date date [note: 'The planned commercial release or patch launch date for the game title at the time this finding was logged. Provides business urgency context for prioritization — findings raised close to the target release date carry higher commercial risk.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The UTC timestamp when this certification finding record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports incremental data pipeline processing, audit trails, and change detection for downstream analytics.']
  waiver_approved_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the waiver approver formally granted approval for the risk-acceptance waiver. Null when waiver is pending, denied, or not applicable.']
  waiver_expiry_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the approved waiver expires, after which the finding must be re-evaluated, fixed, or a new waiver requested. Enforces time-bounded risk acceptance and prevents indefinite deferral of quality issues.']
  waiver_justification varchar [note: 'Formal written justification provided by the resolution owner explaining why the finding cannot be fixed prior to release and why the associated risk is acceptable. Required field for all waiver requests; subject to audit by release governance committees.']
  waiver_requested_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which a formal risk-acceptance waiver was requested for this finding, initiating the waiver governance workflow. Null when no waiver has been requested.']
  waiver_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the waiver governance process for this finding: not_requested, pending (submitted, awaiting approval), approved, denied, expired (approval period lapsed), withdrawn (requester cancelled). SSOT for waiver compliance reporting.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each certification finding, failure, or formally waived quality exception identified during platform certification review, release gate evaluation, or defect risk-acceptance process. Captures finding date, associated submission, violated requirement, description, severity, reproduction steps, platform, build version, resolution owner, resolution status, and comprehensive waiver governance details (waiver request date, justification, risk assessment, approver identity, approval date, expiry date, post-launch monitoring commitment, waiver status lifecycle). SSOT for the full lifecycle of certification blockers and all formal quality waivers from identification through clearance or risk acceptance.'
}

Table quality.release_gate {
  release_gate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each formal release quality gate checkpoint record in the game release pipeline.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who formally approved or rejected this release gate, serving as the accountable sign-off authority.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement. Business justification: Release gates are audited during regulatory/platform audits. Real business process: audit scope definition and release readiness verification.']
  build_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game build version under review at this gate, sourced from Perforce Helix Core or the build pipeline.']
  capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_project. Business justification: Release gate approval determines go-live date, triggering capitalization-to-amortization transition. Finance depends on gate sign-off to start amortizing capitalized development costs and close asset-']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_release. Business justification: Release gates approve/reject specific content drops for production deployment. Gate-to-release linkage enables go/no-go decision tracking and release readiness reporting.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title associated with this release gate checkpoint.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Release gates include KPI thresholds (crash rate, performance benchmarks); linking formalizes which KPIs gate releases and enables automated gate evaluation against KPI targets.']
  platform_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.platform_sku. Business justification: Release gates approve specific SKUs for launch; each edition/region has separate approval workflows and content validation requirements.']
  primary_release_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or team lead who owns and is accountable for this release gate checkpoint and its outcome.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Release gates enforce regulatory compliance checkpoints (GDPR, COPPA, age rating, platform TRCs). Real business process: go/no-go decision based on regulatory readiness.']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the target platform (e.g., PlayStation, Xbox, PC, iOS, Android, Nintendo Switch) for which this release gate applies.']
  actual_fps decimal(6,2) [note: 'The measured Frames Per Second (FPS) achieved by the build under review during performance testing, compared against target_fps to determine benchmark compliance.']
  actual_review_date date [note: 'The date on which the release gate review was actually conducted. Compared against target_review_date to measure schedule adherence.']
  blocking_defect_critical_count varchar [note: 'Count of open Critical severity defects that are blocking release at the time of this gate review. Critical defects are showstoppers that prevent the game from functioning.']
  blocking_defect_major_count varchar [note: 'Count of open Major severity defects that are blocking release at the time of this gate review. Major defects significantly impair gameplay or platform compliance.']
  blocking_defect_minor_count varchar [note: 'Count of open Minor severity defects flagged as blocking at the time of this gate review.']
  condition_resolution_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which all conditional approval conditions must be resolved and verified. Applicable only when gate disposition is Conditional Approve.']
  conditional_approval_conditions varchar [note: 'Textual description of the mandatory conditions that must be fulfilled when the gate disposition is Conditional Approve. Null when disposition is not conditional.']
  coppa_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether COPPA compliance requirements for children\'s privacy protection have been verified for this build at this gate. Required for titles accessible to players under 13 in the US.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this release gate record was first created in the system, providing the audit creation trail.']
  criteria_evaluated_count varchar [note: 'Total number of release readiness criteria evaluated during this gate review, providing a completeness measure for the review process.']
  criteria_failed_count varchar [note: 'Number of release readiness criteria that received a failing outcome during this gate review, directly informing the gate disposition decision.']
  criteria_passed_count varchar [note: 'Number of release readiness criteria that received a passing outcome during this gate review.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'The formal final decision outcome of the release gate review: Approve (release cleared), Reject (release blocked), Conditional Approve (release cleared with mandatory follow-up actions), Defer (gate postponed to a later iteration), Escalate (decision elevated to senior leadership).']
  disposition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the final gate disposition decision was recorded, providing an authoritative audit timestamp for the release readiness decision.']
  escalation_item_count varchar [note: 'Number of items escalated to senior leadership or executive stakeholders during this gate review for decision-making beyond the gate owner\'s authority.']
  escalation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of escalation items raised during the gate review, capturing the nature of the escalation and any decisions made by senior stakeholders.']
  esrb_rating_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ESRB content rating has been confirmed and is consistent with the build under review at this gate. Required for North American distribution.']
  gate_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the release quality gate checkpoint (e.g., \'Alpha Gate\', \'Beta Gate\', \'Gold Master Gate\', \'Day-1 Patch Gate\').']
  gate_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing additional context, observations, or commentary from the gate review session not captured in structured fields.']
  gate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the release gate review. Scheduled: gate is planned but not yet started. In Review: active review in progress. Approved: gate passed and release is cleared. Rejected: gate failed and release is blocked. Conditional Approved: gate passed with conditions that must be resolved. Cancelled: gate was voided.']
  gate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the release gate stage in the game release pipeline. Alpha indicates early feature-complete review; Beta indicates pre-launch stability review; Gold Master (GM) indicates final ship-ready certification; Patch Release and Hotfix indicate post-launch update gates; RC indicates Release Candidate. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: alpha|beta|gold_master|patch_release|hotfix|rc|uat|certification — promote to reference product]']
  gdpr_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GDPR compliance requirements (data privacy, consent mechanisms, data subject rights) have been verified for this build at this gate. Required for EU distribution.']
  jira_project_key varchar [note: 'The Jira project key associated with this release gate, enabling traceability to the Jira release planning board and sprint backlog.']
  next_review_target_date date [note: 'The planned date for the next review iteration of this gate, applicable when the current disposition is Reject or Conditional Approve and a re-review is required.']
  pci_dss_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether PCI DSS compliance for in-game payment processing and In-App Purchase (IAP) flows has been verified at this gate. Required for titles with real-money transactions.']
  pegi_rating_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PEGI content rating has been confirmed and is consistent with the build under review at this gate. Required for European distribution.']
  performance_benchmark_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the build met all defined performance benchmarks (e.g., target Frames Per Second (FPS), load times, memory usage) at the time of this gate review.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Textual explanation of why the release gate was rejected. Populated only when gate disposition is Reject. Provides the formal rationale for blocking the release.']
  review_iteration_number varchar [note: 'Sequential iteration count for this gate review, supporting multiple review cycles per gate (e.g., iteration 1 = initial review, iteration 2 = re-review after defect remediation).']
  review_participants varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of employee identifiers or names of stakeholders who participated in the gate review session, forming part of the review event audit trail.']
  sign_off_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the required stakeholder sign-off for this gate has been completed. Pending: awaiting sign-off. Signed Off: all required approvals obtained. Waived: sign-off formally waived with justification. Not Required: gate type does not require formal sign-off.']
  stability_crash_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The measured crash rate (crashes per session or per hour of play) for the build under review, used as a key stability benchmark for release readiness decisions.']
  target_fps decimal(6,2) [note: 'The target Frames Per Second (FPS) performance threshold defined for this release gate, against which the actual build performance is evaluated.']
  target_review_date date [note: 'The planned date on which the release gate review is scheduled to occur, used for release milestone tracking and project planning.']
  trc_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the build has passed the platform manufacturer\'s Technical Requirements Checklist (TRC) or Technical Certification Requirements (TCR) at the time of this gate. Compliant: all TRC/TCR items passed. Non-Compliant: one or more TRC/TCR items failed. Waived: non-compliance formally accepted. Not Applicable: TRC/TCR not required for this gate type.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this release gate record was last modified, supporting audit trail and change tracking requirements.']
  waived_defect_count varchar [note: 'Number of defects that were formally waived (accepted risk) during this gate review, allowing the gate to proceed despite known issues.']

  Note: 'Master record for each formal release quality gate checkpoint in the game release pipeline (Alpha, Beta, Gold Master, Patch Release gates). Captures gate name, type, associated game title/build, target and actual review dates, gate owner, and full review event audit trail including review participants, criteria evaluated with pass/fail outcomes, blocking defect count by severity, defect waiver decisions, escalation items, final disposition (approve, reject, conditional approve), approver identities, and sign-off status. Supports multiple review iterations per gate. SSOT for release readiness decisions, their review evidence, and stakeholder approvals.'
}

Table quality.release_gate_review {
  release_gate_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each formal release gate review event. Primary key for this transactional record. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — captures a discrete quality gate decision event with its own lifecycle.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement. Business justification: Release gate reviews are subject to audit during regulatory engagements. Real business process: audit evidence collection and release readiness verification.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the QA lead or release manager who chaired and is accountable for this release gate review session. Serves as the primary party reference for this transaction.']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the development studio whose game build is under review at this release gate. Enables studio-level quality metrics aggregation and release readiness benchmarking.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title under review at this release gate. Links the review event to the specific game product being evaluated for release readiness.']
  milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.milestone. Business justification: Release gate reviews are milestone-specific (alpha gate, beta gate, gold master); each milestone has defined gate criteria. Milestone progression requires gate review approval. Creating milestone_id F']
  release_gate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.release_gate. Business justification: Release gate reviews are conducted at release gates. release_gate_review has gate_type (STRING) but no FK to release_gate. Reviews are events that evaluate gates. Cardinality: N release_gate_reviews :']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the target distribution platform (e.g., PlayStation, Xbox, PC/Steam, iOS, Android) for which this release gate review is being conducted. Platform-specific certification requirements (TRC/TCR) vary by platform.']
  actual_fps decimal(6,2) [note: 'The measured average Frames Per Second (FPS) achieved by the build during QA performance testing, recorded at the time of this gate review. Compared against target_fps to determine performance gate pass/fail.']
  assigned_rating varchar [note: 'The content rating assigned by the applicable rating body (e.g., ESRB: E, T, M, AO; PEGI: 3, 7, 12, 16, 18). Null if rating has not yet been assigned. Critical gate criterion for commercial release compliance.']
  build_version varchar [note: 'The specific game build version string (e.g., 1.4.2.8831) submitted for this release gate review. Corresponds to the Perforce Helix Core changelist label or Unity/Unreal Engine build artifact version.']
  conditional_approval_conditions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific conditions that must be satisfied for a conditional_approve recommendation to convert to a full approval. Populated only when recommendation is conditional_approve. Conditions are tracked to closure before release.']
  content_rating_body varchar [note: 'The regulatory body that issued or is processing the content rating for this release (e.g., ESRB, PEGI, USK, CERO, GRAC). Relevant to the target market(s) for this release. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ESRB|PEGI|USK|CERO|GRAC|IARC — promote to reference product]']
  content_rating_status varchar [note: 'Status of the game\'s content rating submission and approval with applicable rating bodies (ESRB, PEGI, USK, CERO, GRAC) at the time of this gate review. A valid content rating is typically a hard gate requirement for commercial release.']
  coppa_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether COPPA (Children\'s Online Privacy Protection Act) compliance requirements have been verified as part of this release gate review. Mandatory gate criterion for games accessible to players under 13 in the US market.']
  crash_rate_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The measured crash rate of the build as a percentage of sessions, recorded at the time of this gate review. A key stability benchmark used to assess release readiness. Typically sourced from QA playtesting telemetry or GameAnalytics/Amplitude crash reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this release gate review record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and record provenance tracking.']
  criteria_failed_count varchar [note: 'The number of release gate criteria items that received a failing outcome during this review. Non-zero values typically trigger rejection or conditional approval outcomes.']
  criteria_passed_count varchar [note: 'The number of release gate criteria items that received a passing outcome during this review. Used alongside total_criteria_evaluated to compute pass rate for release readiness reporting.']
  criteria_waived_count varchar [note: 'The number of release gate criteria items that were formally waived (bypassed with documented justification) during this review. Waivers require explicit stakeholder sign-off and are tracked for audit and compliance purposes.']
  defect_waiver_count varchar [note: 'The total number of individual defect waivers formally approved during this gate review session. Each waiver represents a documented risk acceptance decision allowing a known defect to ship.']
  escalation_item_count varchar [note: 'The number of issues or decisions escalated to senior leadership or cross-functional stakeholders during this gate review. Escalations indicate items that could not be resolved within the standard review authority.']
  escalation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of items escalated to senior leadership or cross-functional stakeholders during this gate review. Documents the nature of each escalation, the escalation target, and expected resolution timeline. Populated when escalation_item_count > 0.']
  gdpr_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) compliance requirements for player data handling have been verified as part of this release gate review. Mandatory gate criterion for releases targeting EU markets.']
  jira_release_ticket varchar [note: 'The Jira issue key (e.g., REL-4821) for the release epic or release planning ticket associated with this gate review. Provides traceability back to the Jira project management system of record for release planning.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The date scheduled for the follow-up gate review, applicable when the current review results in a rejection or conditional approval requiring re-evaluation. Used for release milestone planning.']
  open_critical_defect_count varchar [note: 'The number of open critical-severity (P0/P1) defects in Jira at the time of this gate review. Critical defects are typically blocking for release approval and must be resolved or waived before gate passage.']
  open_major_defect_count varchar [note: 'The number of open major-severity (P2) defects in Jira at the time of this gate review. Major defects may be conditionally waived with documented risk acceptance.']
  open_minor_defect_count varchar [note: 'The number of open minor-severity (P3/P4) defects in Jira at the time of this gate review. Minor defects are typically tracked but do not block gate passage.']
  participant_count varchar [note: 'The total number of stakeholders who participated in this release gate review session. Includes QA leads, producers, studio directors, platform compliance officers, and any other attendees.']
  pci_dss_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard) compliance requirements for in-game payment processing (IAP/MTX) have been verified as part of this release gate review. Required for titles with in-app purchase (IAP) or microtransaction (MTX) monetization.']
  performance_benchmark_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the build met all defined performance benchmarks (e.g., target FPS (Frames Per Second), load times, memory usage, crash rate thresholds) as evaluated during this gate review. Performance benchmarks are a standard gate criterion for release quality.']
  platform_cert_readiness varchar [note: 'Assessment of the build\'s readiness for submission to first-party platform certification (e.g., Sony TRC, Microsoft TCR, Nintendo Lot Check). Tracks whether the build meets all Technical Requirements Checklist (TRC) / Technical Certification Requirements (TCR) criteria required by the platform holder.']
  recommendation varchar [note: 'The formal recommendation issued by the review lead at the conclusion of this gate review session. Approve indicates the build is cleared to proceed; reject indicates the build must be remediated and re-reviewed; conditional_approve indicates approval is granted subject to documented conditions being met.']
  regression_suite_pass_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of automated regression test suite cases that passed for this build at the time of the gate review. Sourced from CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment) pipeline test results. A key quality gate metric.']
  release_target_date date [note: 'The planned commercial release date for the game title at the time of this gate review. Used to assess schedule risk and urgency of gate decisions. May differ from the actual release date if the gate results in a delay.']
  review_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this release gate review event, used in Jira, release management communications, and audit documentation (e.g., RGR-TITLE01-20240315). Aligns with Jira release planning ticket references.']
  review_date timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world timestamp when the formal release gate review session was conducted. This is the business event timestamp, distinct from record audit timestamps. Used for release timeline tracking and audit trail.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative notes captured during the gate review session, documenting key discussion points, risk observations, escalation context, and any decisions not captured in structured fields. Provides qualitative audit trail context.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this release gate review event. Approved indicates all gate criteria passed; rejected indicates blocking issues remain; conditional_approved indicates approval granted subject to specified conditions; deferred indicates the review has been postponed.']
  scheduled_review_date date [note: 'The originally planned calendar date for this release gate review, as recorded in Jira release planning. Used to measure schedule adherence and release milestone tracking against planned dates.']
  sign_off_status varchar [note: 'Current status of stakeholder sign-off collection for this gate review. Complete indicates all required approvers have signed off; partial indicates some approvers have signed but others are outstanding; pending indicates sign-off collection has not yet begun.']
  sign_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when all required stakeholder sign-offs were collected and the gate review was formally closed. Null if sign-off is not yet complete. Used for release timeline and audit trail.']
  target_fps varchar [note: 'The target Frames Per Second (FPS) performance threshold defined for this release gate. Used as the benchmark against which actual build performance is measured during the review.']
  total_criteria_evaluated varchar [note: 'The total count of release gate criteria items formally evaluated during this review session. Used to measure review scope and completeness of the gate assessment.']
  uat_completion_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of User Acceptance Testing (UAT) test cases that have been executed and closed at the time of this gate review. A key gate criterion indicating test coverage completeness before release.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this release gate review record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each formal review event conducted at a release gate checkpoint. Records review date, participants, gate criteria evaluated, criteria pass/fail outcomes, defect waiver decisions, escalation items, final recommendation (approve, reject, conditional approve), and sign-off status. Provides the audit trail for release gate decisions and stakeholder approvals.'
}

Table quality.perf_test_run {
  perf_test_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each performance test run record. Primary key for the perf_test_run data product in the quality domain.']
  build_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.build. Business justification: Performance test runs validate specific builds; studios track which build passed performance benchmarks for release gate decisions. Build certification requires perf test passage. Replaces denormalize']
  build_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.build_submission. Business justification: Performance tests validate cert submission builds against TRC benchmarks; results gate submission approval and identify performance regressions.']
  executed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Performance test execution requires tracking which QA engineer ran the test for accountability, scheduling conflict resolution, and expertise matching. Critical for capacity planning and SLA complianc']
  game_mode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.game_mode. Business justification: Performance benchmarks (FPS, memory, latency) vary significantly by game mode (64-player vs 4-player). Mode-specific performance SLAs are required for certification and release gate approval. Critical']
  game_server_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.game_server. Business justification: Performance test runs execute on specific game servers. Benchmarking requires correlation with server hardware configuration, resource utilization (CPU, GPU, memory), and network conditions for accura']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the game title under test. Links this performance test run to the specific game IP being evaluated.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Performance test runs measure specific KPIs (FPS, latency); linking enables tracking of KPI compliance over builds and automated performance regression detection.']
  level_map_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.level_map. Business justification: Performance tests measure FPS, memory usage, load times per level for benchmarking and optimization. Level-specific performance profiling enables targeted optimization and certification readiness vali']
  network_region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.network_region. Business justification: Performance tests validate region-specific latency and throughput targets. Certification gate validation requires regional SLA compliance verification (max latency, target FPS) for platform-holder app']
  stability_benchmark_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.stability_benchmark. Business justification: Performance test runs validate against stability benchmarks. perf_test_run has fps_benchmark_threshold and memory_budget_mb but no FK to stability_benchmark. Benchmarks define approved thresholds. Car']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the target platform on which the performance test was executed (e.g., PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X, PC, iOS, Android, Nintendo Switch). Aligns with Steamworks/Console SDK platform taxonomy.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.tournament. Business justification: Performance benchmarking for tournament builds (LAN server capacity, spectator mode stability, broadcast delay testing) must reference the tournament for infrastructure planning, capacity validation, ']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or display name of the QA lead or release manager who approved this performance test run result for the release gate. Null when release_gate_approved is False or approval is pending.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the release gate approval was granted for this performance test run. Null when release_gate_approved is False or approval is pending. Used for release timeline tracking and audit compliance.']
  avg_cpu_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean CPU utilization percentage recorded across all active cores during the test run. Used to assess CPU headroom, identify bottlenecks, and validate that the build does not exceed platform CPU budget thresholds.']
  avg_fps decimal(6,2) [note: 'The mean Frames Per Second (FPS) recorded across the full duration of the test run. Primary rendering performance KPI used in release gate pass/fail evaluation and regression comparison.']
  avg_frame_time_ms decimal(8,3) [note: 'The mean time in milliseconds to render a single frame during the test run. Complements FPS metrics by providing a direct measure of rendering latency. Target thresholds vary by platform (e.g., 16.67ms for 60 FPS, 33.33ms for 30 FPS).']
  avg_gpu_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean GPU utilization percentage recorded during the test run. Used to assess GPU headroom and rendering pipeline efficiency. High GPU utilization with low FPS indicates a GPU bottleneck.']
  avg_memory_usage_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mean RAM consumption in megabytes across the full duration of the test run. Used alongside peak memory to assess memory stability and detect memory leaks during soak tests.']
  avg_network_latency_ms decimal(8,2) [note: 'Mean round-trip network latency in milliseconds measured during the test run. Relevant for online multiplayer (PvP, MMO, GaaS) titles. High latency degrades player experience and is a key quality gate metric for live service games.']
  ccu_achieved varchar [note: 'The actual number of Concurrent Users (CCU) successfully simulated or observed during the test run. For CCU/PCU simulation scenarios, this represents the peak concurrent load achieved before performance degradation or failure. Key metric for GaaS live operations capacity planning.']
  ccu_target varchar [note: 'The target number of Concurrent Users (CCU) defined for this test run scenario. Compared against ccu_achieved to determine if the build meets the required concurrency capacity for release.']
  cert_readiness_status varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether this build\'s performance results meet platform certification requirements (TRC/TCR). \'not_assessed\' = certification review not yet performed; \'ready\' = performance meets all TRC/TCR thresholds; \'not_ready\' = performance fails one or more TRC/TCR requirements; \'waiver_pending\' = exception waiver submitted to platform holder.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance test run record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  game_mode_tested varchar [note: 'The specific game mode or scenario exercised during the performance test (e.g., \'PvP_Arena_64Player\', \'PvE_OpenWorld\', \'MainMenu\', \'FTUE_Tutorial\', \'MMO_Hub_Zone\'). Enables performance comparison across game modes.']
  hardware_config_profile varchar [note: 'Label or identifier of the hardware configuration profile used during the test run (e.g., \'PS5-Standard\', \'PC-MinSpec\', \'PC-RecommendedSpec\', \'iOS-iPhone14\'). Captures the device/hardware tier to contextualize performance results.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this performance test run was executed by an automated CI/CD pipeline (True) or manually triggered by a QA engineer (False). Used to segment automated regression suites from manual exploratory performance tests in reporting.']
  is_regression boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test run detected a performance regression compared to the prior build\'s baseline results. True = regression detected (performance degraded vs. prior build); False = no regression detected. Triggers mandatory investigation before release gate approval.']
  jira_issue_key varchar [note: 'The Jira issue key (e.g., QA-4821) associated with this performance test run. Enables traceability from the test run back to the Jira sprint, epic, or release planning ticket that initiated the test.']
  min_fps decimal(6,2) [note: 'The lowest Frames Per Second (FPS) value recorded during the test run. Critical for identifying frame rate drops, stutters, and worst-case rendering performance that impact player experience.']
  overall_result varchar [note: 'The overall pass/fail determination for this performance test run against all defined benchmarks. \'pass\' = all benchmarks met; \'fail\' = one or more benchmarks not met; \'conditional_pass\' = passed with documented exceptions approved by QA lead; \'inconclusive\' = run completed but results are insufficient for a determination.']
  p99_frame_time_ms decimal(8,3) [note: 'The 99th percentile frame time in milliseconds, representing worst-case frame rendering latency excluding the top 1% of outliers. Used to detect hitching and micro-stutter patterns that degrade player experience even when average FPS appears acceptable.']
  peak_cpu_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum CPU utilization percentage observed at any point during the test run. Identifies CPU spike events that may cause frame drops or game freezes under peak load conditions.']
  peak_fps decimal(6,2) [note: 'The highest Frames Per Second (FPS) value recorded during the test run. Used to assess maximum rendering throughput and identify performance ceiling for the build on the target platform.']
  peak_gpu_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum GPU utilization percentage observed at any point during the test run. Identifies GPU saturation events that may cause rendering bottlenecks or thermal throttling on target hardware.']
  peak_memory_usage_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum RAM consumption in megabytes observed during the test run. Critical for platform certification compliance — console and mobile platforms enforce strict memory budgets (e.g., TRC/TCR memory limits). Exceeding this threshold is a certification blocker.']
  peak_network_latency_ms decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum round-trip network latency in milliseconds observed during the test run. Identifies worst-case latency spikes that may cause rubber-banding, desync, or disconnection events in online multiplayer scenarios.']
  prior_build_version varchar [note: 'The build version used as the regression baseline for comparison in this test run. Populated when is_regression evaluation is performed. Enables direct traceability to the specific prior build whose metrics were used as the performance baseline.']
  regression_delta_fps decimal(6,2) [note: 'The difference in average FPS between this build and the prior build baseline (current avg_fps minus prior avg_fps). Negative values indicate FPS regression. Null when no prior baseline exists or regression comparison was not performed.']
  release_gate_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this performance test run result has been reviewed and approved as part of the release quality gate process. True = approved by QA lead/release manager; False = not yet approved or approval denied. Required before build promotion to certification or production.']
  run_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for QA engineers to document observations, anomalies, environmental conditions, or context relevant to this test run (e.g., \'Server under maintenance during run\', \'Thermal throttling observed on device\'). Supports qualitative analysis alongside quantitative metrics.']
  run_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique code assigned to this performance test run (e.g., PTR-FTUE-20240315-0042). Used in Jira tickets, QA reports, and release gate documentation to reference a specific run.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the performance test run. \'queued\' = scheduled but not started; \'in_progress\' = actively executing; \'completed\' = finished with results captured; \'aborted\' = terminated before completion; \'failed\' = run encountered a fatal error preventing result capture.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the performance test run was initiated. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction record.']
  test_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds from test run start to completion or abort. Used to validate that soak tests ran for the required duration and to normalize performance metrics across runs of different lengths.']
  test_environment varchar [note: 'The deployment environment in which the performance test was executed. \'dev\' = development; \'qa\' = QA lab; \'staging\' = staging/integration; \'pre_prod\' = pre-production; \'cert\' = platform certification environment (e.g., Sony TRC, Microsoft TCR).']
  test_scenario_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the performance test scenario executed. \'load_test\' = normal expected load; \'stress_test\' = beyond normal capacity; \'soak_test\' = sustained load over time; \'fps_benchmark\' = Frames Per Second (FPS) measurement; \'latency_test\' = network latency measurement; \'ccu_simulation\' = Concurrent Users (CCU)/Peak Concurrent Users (PCU) simulation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance test run record was last modified. Tracks post-run updates such as result annotation, pass/fail override, or regression flag changes.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each performance test run executed against a game build. Captures run date, game title and build version, platform, test scenario (load test, stress test, soak test, FPS benchmark, latency test, CCU/PCU simulation), environment configuration, peak FPS, average FPS, frame time, CPU/GPU utilization, memory usage, network latency, CCU achieved, pass/fail against defined benchmarks, and regression flag vs. prior build. SSOT for game performance stability data.'
}

Table quality.stability_benchmark {
  stability_benchmark_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the stability benchmark record. Primary key for the stability_benchmark data product in the quality domain.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual or role (e.g., QA Director, Technical Director, Studio Head) who formally approved this benchmark as the accepted standard for release readiness evaluation.']
  game_mode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.game_mode. Business justification: Stability benchmarks define mode-specific performance targets (campaign vs multiplayer have different FPS/memory SLAs). Release gates and certification require mode-level benchmark compliance for appr']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title for which this stability benchmark is defined. Links the benchmark to the specific game product under evaluation.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Stability benchmarks ARE KPI definitions for technical metrics (crash rate, FPS); this is a definitional relationship linking quality standards to business KPIs.']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the target platform (e.g., PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X, PC, iOS, Android) for which this benchmark applies. A benchmark is platform-specific as performance thresholds differ by hardware.']
  superseded_by_stability_benchmark_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer stability_benchmark record that replaced this one when it was superseded. Enables version chain traceability. Null if this benchmark is the current active version.']
  applies_to_build_type varchar [note: 'Specifies which build lifecycle stage(s) this benchmark applies to. \'gold_master\' = final release build; \'release_candidate\' = near-final candidate; \'beta\' = public/closed beta; \'alpha\' = early development; \'live_patch\' = post-launch update; \'all\' = applies universally.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this benchmark was formally approved by the approving authority and became the active reference standard. Marks the transition from \'pending_approval\' to \'active\' status.']
  benchmark_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this benchmark within QA tooling and Jira project tracking (e.g., \'SB-PC-ULTRA-2024-V2\'). Used as the business identifier in cross-system references.']
  benchmark_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name identifying this benchmark record (e.g., \'PC Ultra Settings Gold Master Benchmark v2.1\', \'PS5 Launch Stability Baseline\'). Serves as the primary identity label for QA teams and stakeholders.']
  benchmark_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the benchmark record. \'draft\' = under construction; \'pending_approval\' = awaiting sign-off; \'active\' = approved and in use as the reference standard; \'superseded\' = replaced by a newer version; \'retired\' = no longer applicable.']
  benchmark_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the benchmark by its primary quality concern. \'performance\' = FPS/frame time/load time targets; \'stability\' = crash rate/memory targets; \'compliance\' = platform TRC/TCR requirements; \'certification\' = first-party certification readiness; \'regression\' = baseline for regression detection.']
  benchmark_version varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of this benchmark definition (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.3\', \'1.0.1\'). Incremented when threshold values or scope are revised. Enables traceability of which version was active at any point in time.']
  ccu_capacity_target varchar [note: 'Target number of Concurrent Users (CCU) the game server infrastructure must support without degradation. Used to validate server-side stability benchmarks for Game as a Service (GaaS) and online multiplayer titles.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stability benchmark record was first created in the system. Audit trail field capturing the initial record creation event in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this benchmark version is binding and must be used as the reference standard for evaluating performance test runs. May differ from approval_date if a future effective date was specified.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date on which this benchmark version ceases to be the active reference standard, typically when superseded by a newer version. Null indicates the benchmark is open-ended and currently active.']
  game_build_version varchar [note: 'The game build version or milestone (e.g., \'Alpha\', \'Beta\', \'Gold Master\', \'v1.2.0\') against which this benchmark was defined. Anchors the benchmark to a specific development stage or release candidate.']
  graphics_quality_preset varchar [note: 'Graphics quality setting level at which this benchmark was defined and must be evaluated (e.g., \'ultra\', \'high\', \'medium\', \'low\', \'platform_default\'). PC titles may have multiple benchmarks per quality tier.']
  hardware_config varchar [note: 'Description of the reference hardware configuration used to establish this benchmark (e.g., \'PS5 Retail Unit\', \'PC - Intel i7-12700K / RTX 3080 / 16GB RAM\', \'iPhone 14 Pro\'). Ensures benchmark thresholds are interpreted in the correct hardware context.']
  is_certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether passing this benchmark is a mandatory prerequisite for first-party platform certification submission (e.g., Sony TRC, Microsoft TCR, Nintendo Lot Check). True = certification gate; False = internal quality gate only.']
  is_release_gate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benchmark constitutes a hard release quality gate that must be passed before a build can be promoted to Gold Master or submitted for distribution. True = blocking gate; False = advisory/informational benchmark.']
  jira_epic_key varchar [note: 'The Jira Epic key associated with the QA initiative or release milestone that owns this benchmark (e.g., \'QA-1042\'). Enables traceability between the benchmark definition and the project management backlog in Jira.']
  max_cpu_usage_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable CPU utilization percentage during standard gameplay scenarios. Sustained CPU usage above this threshold may cause frame drops, audio glitches, or instability on target hardware.']
  max_crash_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable crash rate expressed as a percentage of sessions (e.g., 0.5000 = 0.5%). Builds exceeding this threshold fail the stability gate and are blocked from release. A key Quality Assurance (QA) release readiness criterion.']
  max_disk_io_mbps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable disk read/write throughput in megabytes per second during asset streaming and level loading. Critical for open-world titles and platforms with SSD-based streaming architectures (e.g., PS5 SSD).']
  max_frame_time_ms decimal(8,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable frame render time in milliseconds. Exceeding this threshold indicates frame drops or hitches that degrade player experience. Complementary to FPS as it captures frame time variance (e.g., 33.33ms for 30 FPS target).']
  max_gpu_usage_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable GPU utilization percentage during standard gameplay scenarios. Sustained GPU usage above this threshold may indicate rendering inefficiency or thermal throttling risk on target hardware.']
  max_hang_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable application hang or freeze rate expressed as a percentage of sessions. Hangs are distinct from crashes and represent unresponsive states that require player intervention. A key stability Quality Assurance (QA) gate.']
  max_load_time_sec decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable level load or initial boot time in seconds as defined by the Service Level Agreement (SLA). Exceeding this threshold constitutes a performance failure. Applies to the primary load scenario (e.g., main menu to gameplay).']
  max_memory_usage_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable RAM consumption in megabytes during gameplay. Builds exceeding this ceiling risk out-of-memory crashes on target hardware. Platform-specific as console and mobile hardware have fixed memory budgets.']
  max_network_latency_ms decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable network round-trip latency in milliseconds for online/multiplayer game modes. Exceeding this ceiling degrades Player versus Player (PvP) and Massively Multiplayer Online (MMO) gameplay quality. Not applicable for offline-only titles.']
  max_texture_memory_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable VRAM/texture memory consumption in megabytes. Exceeding this ceiling causes texture pop-in, Level of Detail (LOD) degradation, or out-of-memory crashes on GPU-constrained platforms.']
  measurement_duration_sec decimal(8,2) [note: 'Duration in seconds over which performance metrics must be sustained to satisfy the benchmark. Ensures thresholds are evaluated over a representative gameplay window rather than a momentary snapshot (e.g., 300 seconds = 5-minute sustained test).']
  min_fps decimal(6,2) [note: 'The minimum acceptable Frames Per Second (FPS) threshold that a game build must sustain during performance testing. Builds falling below this value fail the benchmark. Measured in frames per second.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for QA engineers and technical directors to document rationale for threshold values, known exceptions, platform-specific caveats, or context about how this benchmark was derived.']
  percentile_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical percentile at which FPS and frame time thresholds must be met (e.g., 95.00 = 95th percentile, meaning 95% of frames must meet the FPS floor). Distinguishes between average-case and worst-case performance requirements.']
  resolution varchar [note: 'Target display resolution at which this benchmark applies (e.g., \'1920x1080\', \'2560x1440\', \'3840x2160\', \'1080p\', \'4K\'). Performance thresholds vary significantly by resolution, particularly for GPU-bound scenarios.']
  target_fps decimal(6,2) [note: 'The desired optimal Frames Per Second (FPS) target for the game on this platform (e.g., 30, 60, 120). Represents the intended player experience quality level, distinct from the minimum acceptable floor.']
