  test_scenario varchar [note: 'Description of the specific gameplay scenario or test case used to measure performance against this benchmark (e.g., \'Open World Stress Test - 100 NPCs\', \'PvP Match - 64 Players\', \'Main Menu to Gameplay Load\'). Ensures reproducibility of benchmark conditions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this stability benchmark record. Updated on any change to threshold values, status, or metadata. Supports audit trail and change detection in the Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the approved stability and performance benchmarks for a game title on a specific platform. Captures benchmark name, game title, platform, minimum acceptable FPS, maximum frame time, memory ceiling, crash rate threshold, load time SLA, network latency ceiling, CCU capacity target, benchmark version, approval date, and approving authority. Serves as the reference standard against which perf_test_run results are evaluated.'
}

Table quality.crash_report {
  crash_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each crash report record in the quality domain. Primary key for the crash_report data product.']
  asset_bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset_bundle. Business justification: Crashes during bundle download, decompression, or streaming (corrupted bundle, CDN timeout, memory overflow during decompression). Bundle-level crash analysis for live ops stability.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Crashes frequently caused by malformed/corrupted assets (invalid mesh data, missing texture mipmap, corrupted audio stream). Root cause analysis links crash signature to specific asset for artist reme']
  assigned_to_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Crash triage workflow assigns reports to specific engineers for investigation and resolution. Essential for workload balancing, SLA tracking, and escalation management. Role-prefixed to distinguish fr']
  build_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game build version that produced the crash. Enables regression tracking across build iterations managed in Perforce Helix Core.']
  build_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.build_submission. Business justification: Crashes discovered during cert review linked to specific submissions; crash rate determines submission outcome and triggers resubmission.']
  changelist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.changelist. Business justification: Crash reports traced to specific code changes; studios track which changelist introduced crash for rollback decisions. Hotfix prioritization requires changelist-level crash attribution. Creating new c']
  compliance_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_incident. Business justification: Crashes exposing player data or violating platform policies trigger compliance incidents. Real business process: incident reporting and regulatory notification for data breaches.']
  defect_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated Jira defect/bug ticket created or linked from this crash report. Enables traceability between crash telemetry and the defect management workflow.']
  game_server_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.game_server. Business justification: Crash reports from live gameplay should reference the specific game server where the crash occurred. This enables debugging server-side issues, correlating crashes with server health metrics, and iden']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title that experienced the crash. Links the crash report to the specific intellectual property (IP) under test or in live operations.']
  iap_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.iap_transaction. Business justification: Crashes during IAP flows (payment processing, receipt validation, entitlement grant) must link to the transaction in progress to diagnose monetization-critical failures and prevent revenue loss from a']
  level_map_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.level_map. Business justification: Crashes in specific levels due to geometry issues, streaming failures, or asset loading problems. Level-scoped crash analysis enables environment artist remediation and streaming optimization.']
  live_ops_signal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.live_ops_signal. Business justification: Crash spikes trigger live ops signals for emergency response; linking enables automated incident response and crash-to-mitigation traceability for post-incident analysis.']
  ml_model_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ml_model_registry. Business justification: ML models predict crash likelihood or classify crash types; crash reports provide ground truth for model training and validation of crash prediction systems.']
  network_region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.network_region. Business justification: Crash reports include region context for geo-specific issues (network conditions, CDN performance, regional build variants). Crash triage prioritizes fixes based on regional player population, CCU, an']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to player.player_account. Business justification: Production crash reports must link to the player who experienced the crash for customer support ticket creation, compensation decisions (premium currency, apology items), pattern analysis by player se']
  player_behavior_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.player_behavior_segment. Business justification: Crash impact varies by segment (crashes affecting high-value players are higher priority); segment linking enables impact-weighted prioritization and targeted fixes.']
  player_prediction_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.player_prediction_record. Business justification: Crash predictions (which players will crash) need validation against actual crash reports; enables model accuracy tracking and proactive player support for crash-prone users.']
  playtest_session_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.playtest_session. Business justification: Crashes can occur during playtest sessions. crash_report has environment (STRING) but no FK to playtest_session. Linking crashes to playtest sessions provides context for crash analysis. Cardinality: ']
  quality_test_execution_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.test_execution. Business justification: Crashes can occur during test execution. crash_report has environment (STRING) but no FK to test_execution. Linking crashes to the specific test execution provides traceability. Cardinality: N crash_r']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the target platform (console, PC, mobile) on which the crash occurred. Supports platform-specific stability analysis and Technical Requirements Checklist (TRC) / Technical Certification Requirements (TCR) compliance tracking.']
  telemetry_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.telemetry_event. Business justification: Crash reports originate from telemetry events; the telemetry event captures the crash signature before the crash report is generated. Direct causal relationship for crash investigation.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.tournament. Business justification: Crashes during live tournament matches must reference the tournament for incident tracking, post-mortem analysis, competitive integrity investigations, and potential match replay decisions required by']
  uat_session_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.uat_session. Business justification: Crashes can occur during UAT sessions. crash_report has environment (STRING) but no FK to uat_session. Linking crashes to UAT sessions provides context for acceptance testing issues. Cardinality: N cr']
  affected_game_component varchar [note: 'The high-level game system or feature area where the crash occurred (e.g., Rendering, Physics, Networking, UI, Audio, AI/NPC, Matchmaking, In-App Purchase). Used for component-level crash rate dashboards and sprint planning in Jira.']
  affected_session_count varchar [note: 'The number of distinct game sessions impacted by this crash signature. Distinct from occurrence_count; a single session may produce multiple crash events. Used for session-level crash rate calculation.']
  build_configuration varchar [note: 'The compilation configuration of the game build that crashed. Debug builds include additional logging; Development builds are pre-release with some optimizations; Release builds are production-optimized; Gold Master is the final certified release candidate.']
  build_version varchar [note: 'Human-readable semantic version string of the game build at the time of the crash (e.g., 1.4.2.1023). Complements build_id for display in QA dashboards and certification reports. Sourced from Perforce Helix Core build metadata.']
  cpu_model varchar [note: 'The CPU model identifier on the device where the crash occurred (e.g., Apple M3, Snapdragon 8 Gen 3, Intel Core i9-14900K). Used for CPU-specific crash pattern analysis and performance testing correlation.']
  crash_signature_hash varchar [note: 'SHA-256 hash of the normalized crash stack trace, used as a unique fingerprint to group and deduplicate crash reports with the same root cause. Enables crash clustering, frequency analysis, and regression detection across builds.']
  crash_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (UTC) when the crash event occurred on the device. This is the principal real-world event time used for crash rate trending, session correlation, and stability regression detection.']
  crash_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the crash event by failure mode. Hard crash = process termination; Soft crash = handled exception with recovery; ANR (Application Not Responding) = UI thread timeout; Freeze = unresponsive state without termination; Out of Memory = memory allocation failure; Watchdog Timeout = system-level process kill. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hard_crash|soft_crash|anr|freeze|out_of_memory|watchdog_timeout — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this crash report record was first ingested into the data platform. Distinct from crash_timestamp; used for data pipeline auditing and SLA tracking.']
  device_manufacturer varchar [note: 'The manufacturer of the hardware device on which the crash occurred (e.g., Apple, Samsung, Sony, Microsoft). Enables manufacturer-level crash rate analysis for hardware compatibility reporting.']
  device_model varchar [note: 'The hardware model identifier of the device on which the crash occurred (e.g., iPhone 15 Pro, Samsung Galaxy S24, ASUS ROG Phone 8). Used for device-specific crash clustering and hardware compatibility analysis.']
  environment varchar [note: 'The operational environment in which the crash was captured. QA = internal quality assurance; Playtesting = external or internal playtest sessions; UAT (User Acceptance Testing) = pre-release validation; Live = production/live operations; Staging = pre-production mirror; Certification = platform certification submission build.']
  faulting_module varchar [note: 'The name of the game engine module, DLL, or shared library where the crash originated (e.g., UnrealEngine-Core, libgame.so, GameplayAbilitySystem). Used to attribute crashes to specific engine or game code components.']
  fps_at_crash decimal(6,2) [note: 'The Frames Per Second (FPS) rendering rate recorded immediately before the crash event. Used to correlate performance degradation with crash onset and identify freeze/ANR patterns linked to frame rate drops.']
  game_mode varchar [note: 'The game mode active at the time of the crash (e.g., PvP, PvE, Campaign, Tutorial, Lobby, Matchmaking, Spectator). Enables crash analysis by gameplay context to isolate mode-specific stability issues. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pvp|pve|campaign|tutorial|lobby|matchmaking|spectator|co_op|battle_royale — promote to reference product]']
  gpu_model varchar [note: 'The GPU model identifier on the device where the crash occurred (e.g., NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090, Apple GPU M3, Adreno 750). Critical for graphics-related crash analysis, Level of Detail (LOD) rendering issues, and Frames Per Second (FPS) stability investigations.']
  is_certification_blocker boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this crash is classified as a blocker for platform certification submission (TRC/TCR compliance). Certification blockers must be resolved before a Gold Master build can be submitted to first-party platform holders.']
  is_first_session boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the crash occurred during the player\'s very first game session (First-Time User Experience / FTUE). FTUE crashes have disproportionate impact on Day 1 (D1) retention and player acquisition metrics.']
  is_regression boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this crash represents a regression — a defect that was previously resolved but has reappeared in a newer build. Regression flags trigger escalated QA review and block release quality gates.']
  map_level_name varchar [note: 'The name of the in-game map, level, or scene that was loaded at the time of the crash. Used to identify level-specific asset loading or streaming issues contributing to crashes.']
  memory_usage_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total memory consumption (in megabytes) of the game process at the moment of the crash. Key quantitative metric for diagnosing out-of-memory crashes and validating memory budget compliance against platform Technical Requirements Checklist (TRC).']
  network_condition varchar [note: 'The network connectivity state of the device at the time of the crash. Used to isolate network-dependent crashes in online multiplayer (MMO, PvP) and Game-as-a-Service (GaaS) live operations contexts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for QA engineers to record reproduction steps, workarounds, environmental observations, or additional context not captured in structured fields. Supports root cause analysis and knowledge transfer.']
  occurrence_count varchar [note: 'The number of times this specific crash signature (crash_signature_hash) has been observed across all sessions within the reporting window. Used for crash rate trending and identifying high-frequency stability regressions.']
  os_version varchar [note: 'The operating system version string on the device where the crash occurred (e.g., Windows 11 23H2, Android 14, iOS 17.4). Critical for identifying OS-specific regression patterns and platform certification compliance.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Fix priority assigned by the QA or development team, reflecting urgency relative to release schedule. P1 = immediate fix required; P2 = fix before next milestone; P3 = fix in upcoming sprint; P4 = backlog. Aligned with Jira priority taxonomy.']
  ram_gb decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total RAM (in gigabytes) available on the device at the time of the crash. Used to identify out-of-memory crash patterns and minimum hardware specification compliance.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable identifier for the crash report, formatted as CR-{YYYY}-{SEQUENCE}. Used in QA communications, Jira ticket references, and platform certification submissions.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the QA tester, automated crash reporter system, or live operations monitoring agent that first reported this crash. Distinguishes manual QA reports from automated telemetry ingestion.']
  reproduction_frequency varchar [note: 'QA-assessed reproducibility of the crash: Always = 100% reproducible; Often = >50% of attempts; Sometimes = 10-50%; Rarely = <10%; Once = single occurrence, not reproduced. Informs defect prioritization and release quality gate decisions.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the crash report in the defect management lifecycle. Drives QA release quality gate decisions and Jira backlog prioritization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|in_progress|resolved|closed|wont_fix|duplicate — promote to reference product if additional states are needed]']
  resolved_in_build_version varchar [note: 'The build version string in which the crash was confirmed as fixed. Enables fix verification tracking and regression detection if the crash reappears in subsequent builds.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the crash was marked as resolved in the defect management workflow. Used to calculate mean time to resolution (MTTR) for QA KPI reporting and release quality gate tracking.']
  session_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'The length of the game session in seconds at the point of crash. Used to identify early-session crashes (First-Time User Experience / FTUE impact) versus late-session crashes, and to correlate with memory leak patterns.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Business impact severity of the crash, aligned with QA defect severity taxonomy. Critical = game-breaking, blocks release; High = major feature impaired; Medium = partial feature impaired; Low = cosmetic or rare edge case. Drives release quality gate decisions.']
  stack_trace_summary varchar [note: 'Truncated or summarized top-of-stack trace text identifying the faulting module, function, and line number. Provides human-readable crash context for QA engineers without storing the full raw stack trace in the Silver layer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this crash report record was last modified, such as when resolution status, severity, or defect linkage was updated by the QA team.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each crash event captured during QA testing, playtesting, or live operations monitoring. Records crash timestamp, game title and build version, platform, device/hardware profile, OS version, crash type (hard crash, soft crash, ANR, freeze), crash signature/stack trace hash, reproduction frequency, affected game component, severity, associated defect reference, and resolution status. Enables crash rate trending and stability regression detection.'
}

Table quality.uat_session {
  uat_session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each UAT session record in the quality domain. Primary key for the uat_session data product. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — entity represents a discrete QA business event with a defined lifecycle.']
  ab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ab_experiment. Business justification: UAT sessions may test experimental features; tracking experiment context ensures proper validation coverage and acceptance criteria are met for each variant before launch.']
  age_rating_cert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.age_rating_cert. Business justification: UAT validates content against age rating requirements before cert submission. Real business process: pre-cert validation ensuring content matches submitted rating application.']
  build_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game build version submitted for UAT. Corresponds to the build artifact tracked in Perforce Helix Core or the CI/CD pipeline.']
  build_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.build_submission. Business justification: UAT validates specific cert submission candidates; sign-off required before submission to platform holder for certification review.']
  content_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_release. Business justification: UAT sessions validate specific content releases with stakeholders (publisher UAT for DLC, marketing UAT for seasonal event). Release acceptance workflow requires session-to-release linkage for sign-of']
  dlc_bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.dlc_bundle. Business justification: DLC UAT sessions validate expansion content, narrative, and monetization before launch. DLC-level acceptance testing is required for commercial sign-off and DLC-specific go/no-go decisions.']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: UAT sessions incur costs (participant time, environment hosting, third-party testers) charged to project budgets. Finance tracks UAT spend for cost control and determines capitalization eligibility. E']
  game_edition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.game_edition. Business justification: UAT sessions must validate edition-specific content bundles (Deluxe vs Standard), entitlements, and DLC inclusions. Business acceptance testing requires edition-level traceability for SKU matrix valid']
  game_server_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.game_server. Business justification: UAT sessions run on production-like game servers. Acceptance testing requires production environment parity validation (server configuration, build version, performance characteristics) for launch app']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title under UAT evaluation. Links the session to the specific intellectual property (IP) being tested.']
  milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.milestone. Business justification: UAT sessions validate milestone deliverables; acceptance criteria are milestone-specific. Milestone payment triggers require UAT sign-off. No existing candidate column; creating milestone_id FK.']
  network_region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.network_region. Business justification: UAT sessions validate region-specific deployment readiness. Regional launch approval gates require UAT validation in target regions for compliance (GDPR, data residency), performance (latency SLAs), a']
  participant_player_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to player.player_account. Business justification: UAT sessions often include selected player accounts (alpha testers, community council members, content creators) alongside internal stakeholders. Tracking which player accounts participated is essenti']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the target platform (e.g., PlayStation, Xbox, PC, iOS, Android) on which the UAT session is conducted. Relevant for platform certification readiness per TRC/TCR requirements.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.tournament. Business justification: Tournament UAT sessions (pre-event acceptance testing with league officials, referee tool validation) must reference the tournament for sign-off documentation, readiness validation, and compliance wit']
  acceptance_criteria_defined_count varchar [note: 'Total number of acceptance criteria defined for this UAT session prior to execution. Establishes the baseline scope of quality gates that must be evaluated.']
  acceptance_criteria_met_count varchar [note: 'Number of defined acceptance criteria that were fully satisfied during the UAT session. Used to calculate pass rate and inform the UAT decision.']
  build_version varchar [note: 'Human-readable version string of the game build under test (e.g., \'2.4.1.0\'). Complements the build_id FK with a display-friendly identifier for reports and sign-off documentation.']
  conditional_acceptance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the conditions, remediation commitments, or waivers associated with a conditionally_accepted UAT decision. Null when decision is accepted or rejected outright.']
  content_rating_compliance_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether content rating compliance (ESRB, PEGI, USK, CERO, GRAC) was verified as part of this UAT session. Required for release readiness in regulated markets.']
  coppa_compliance_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether COPPA compliance requirements (parental consent, age-gating, data collection restrictions for under-13 players) were validated during this UAT session. Required for titles with potential child audiences in North America.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the UAT session record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this TRANSACTION_HEADER. Populated automatically at record insertion.']
  critical_issues_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical or blocker-severity defects identified during the UAT session. Critical issues typically trigger an automatic rejection decision and must be resolved before re-submission.']
  environment_type varchar [note: 'The deployment environment in which the UAT session was conducted: dev (development environment), staging (pre-production staging), cert (platform certification environment), production (live environment for soft launch UAT).']
  ftue_validated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the First-Time User Experience (FTUE) flow was specifically validated during this UAT session. FTUE quality is a critical retention driver and is tracked separately from general test coverage.']
  gdpr_compliance_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GDPR data protection requirements (consent flows, data subject rights, privacy notices) were validated during this UAT session. Mandatory for releases targeting EU markets.']
  jira_project_key varchar [note: 'The Jira project key associated with this UAT session, enabling traceability to the sprint, epic, and backlog items in the project management system of record.']
  outstanding_issues_count varchar [note: 'Number of defects or issues identified during the UAT session that remain unresolved at the time of the UAT decision. Key input to the conditional acceptance or rejection determination.']
  participant_count varchar [note: 'Total number of participants enrolled in the UAT session. Used to assess coverage breadth and normalize defect density metrics.']
  participant_group_name varchar [note: 'Name or label of the participant cohort involved in the UAT session (e.g., \'Internal QA Team\', \'Partner Studio Beta Group\', \'Community Beta Cohort A\'). Identifies the testing audience for segmentation and reporting.']
  participant_group_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the participant group by relationship to the studio: internal (employees/contractors), external_partner (publisher or co-dev partners), beta_community (registered community testers), press (media/influencer preview), first_party (console manufacturer certification team). Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE classification for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  performance_benchmarks_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the game build met defined performance benchmarks (e.g., target FPS, load times, memory usage) during the UAT session. Performance failures are a common cause of conditional acceptance.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Originally scheduled end date for the UAT session as defined in the release plan. Used for release gate scheduling and milestone tracking.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Originally scheduled start date for the UAT session as defined in the release plan. Enables variance analysis between planned and actual execution timelines.']
  platform_certification_scope varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this UAT session includes platform certification readiness testing and which certification framework applies: trc (Technical Requirements Checklist — PlayStation), tcr (Technical Certification Requirements — Xbox), lotcheck (Nintendo), app_store_review (Apple), google_play_review (Google), not_applicable (no certification scope). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: trc|tcr|lotcheck|app_store_review|google_play_review|not_applicable — promote to reference product]']
  regression_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of regression testing included in this UAT session: full (complete regression suite), partial (targeted regression on changed areas), smoke (basic sanity checks only), none (no regression testing performed).']
  release_milestone varchar [note: 'The release milestone or gate this UAT session is associated with (e.g., \'Alpha\', \'Beta\', \'Gold Master\', \'Soft Launch\', \'Hard Launch\'). Provides context for the session within the overall release lifecycle.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the UAT session, used in cross-system references, stakeholder communications, and Jira ticket linkage. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  session_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the UAT session formally concluded. Used to calculate session duration and assess testing throughput.']
  session_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title assigned to the UAT session (e.g., \'Title X Closed Beta UAT — PC Build 2.4.1\'). Used for identification in reports and dashboards.']
  session_notes varchar [note: 'General free-text notes or observations recorded by the QA lead or session facilitator during or after the UAT session. Captures qualitative context not represented in structured fields.']
  session_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the UAT session formally commenced. Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this TRANSACTION_HEADER, representing the real-world start of the testing event.']
  session_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the UAT session. Tracks workflow progression from planning through execution to completion or cancellation. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  sign_off_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role title of the individual or body with authority to formally approve the UAT decision (e.g., \'QA Director\', \'Executive Producer\', \'Platform Certification Lead\'). Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE for the authorizing party on this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  sign_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sign-off authority formally rendered the UAT decision and signed off on the session outcome. Critical for release audit trails and compliance documentation.']
  target_fps varchar [note: 'The target Frames Per Second (FPS) performance threshold defined for this UAT session. Used to assess whether the build meets the performance acceptance criterion for the target platform.']
  test_cases_blocked_count varchar [note: 'Number of test cases that could not be executed due to blocking defects or environmental issues. Indicates incomplete coverage and may affect the validity of the UAT decision.']
  test_cases_failed_count varchar [note: 'Number of test cases that failed during the UAT session. Drives defect triage prioritization and re-test planning.']
  test_cases_passed_count varchar [note: 'Number of test cases that passed successfully during the UAT session. Used alongside total_test_cases_count to compute the test pass rate.']
  total_test_cases_count varchar [note: 'Total number of test cases executed during the UAT session. Provides the denominator for pass/fail rate calculations and coverage reporting.']
  uat_decision varchar [note: 'Formal outcome decision of the UAT session: accepted (all criteria met, cleared for release), conditionally_accepted (minor outstanding issues with agreed remediation plan), rejected (critical issues prevent release), pending (decision not yet rendered).']
  uat_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the UAT session by participant scope and release phase: internal_uat (internal stakeholders only), closed_beta (invited external participants), open_beta (broad external access), soft_launch_uat (limited geographic or platform release validation).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the UAT session record was last modified. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for this TRANSACTION_HEADER. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail maintenance.']

  Note: 'Master record for each User Acceptance Testing session conducted with internal stakeholders, external partners, or beta participants. Captures session name, UAT type (internal UAT, closed beta, open beta, soft launch UAT), game title and build, platform, participant group, session start/end dates, acceptance criteria defined, acceptance criteria met count, outstanding issues count, UAT decision (accepted, conditionally accepted, rejected), and sign-off authority.'
}

Table quality.compliance_test_result {
  compliance_test_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each compliance test result record. Primary key for the compliance_test_result data product in the quality domain.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Compliance test failures can trigger audit findings during regulatory reviews. Real business process: audit evidence trail linking test results to formal findings.']
  certification_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.certification_checklist. Business justification: Compliance results validate specific checklist requirements; test evidence organized by requirement_id for cert audits and submission documentation.']
  content_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.content_rating. Business justification: Compliance test results must link to official content rating records (ESRB, PEGI) for audit trails and rating authority submissions. Required for regulatory compliance documentation and rating re-eval']
  defect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.defect. Business justification: Compliance test failures can result in defect creation. compliance_test_result has jira_issue_key (STRING) and remediation_status but no FK to defect. When compliance issues are identified, defects ma']
  drm_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.drm_entitlement. Business justification: Compliance tests validate DRM implementation against platform requirements; entitlement test evidence required for cert and TRC compliance.']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the development studio responsible for the game title under test. Supports studio-level compliance reporting and accountability.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title under compliance testing. Enables filtering and reporting of compliance results by game product.']
  iap_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.iap_transaction. Business justification: Compliance tests for IAP regulations (COPPA age gates, GDPR consent, platform policy adherence, loot box disclosure) execute test purchases that must be traceable as evidence for certification and reg']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensed_ip. Business justification: Compliance testing validates licensed IP usage against age rating requirements, content descriptors, and privacy regulations. QA teams test specific licensed characters, brands, or middleware for regu']
  milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.milestone. Business justification: Compliance tests (COPPA, GDPR, age rating) are milestone gates; cannot ship without compliance sign-off. Milestone payment and platform submission require compliance passage. Creating new milestone_id']
  platform_cert_submission_id varchar [note: 'The platform certification submission identifier (e.g., Sony submission ID, Microsoft certification ID, Apple review ID) to which this compliance test result is attached. Links compliance evidence to the formal platform submission package. Sourced from Steamworks/Console SDKs.']
  platform_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.platform_sku. Business justification: Compliance tests validate SKU-specific content (regional age ratings, pricing compliance); results are per-SKU for regulatory approval.']
  primary_compliance_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the QA tester or compliance analyst who executed this compliance test. Supports accountability tracking and workload reporting within the QA team.']
  regulatory_disclosure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.regulatory_disclosure. Business justification: Compliance test results (COPPA, GDPR, age rating) provide evidence for regulatory filings. Finance references QA compliance documentation in SEC filings, annual reports, and platform disclosures for m']
  remediation_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.remediation_action. Business justification: Failed compliance tests trigger remediation actions. Real business process: compliance remediation tracking and regulatory requirement closure.']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'The specific requirement, clause, or control identifier within the compliance framework being tested (e.g., GDPR Article 13, PCI DSS Requirement 6.4, WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.3). Provides granular traceability to the exact regulatory obligation tested.']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.storefront. Business justification: Compliance tests validate platform-specific TRC/TCR requirements; results map directly to cert submission checklists. Replaces string platform with proper FK.']
  test_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent test plan under which this compliance test was executed. Links the result to the overarching QA test plan governing the release cycle.']
  automated_test_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance test was executed via an automated testing tool or pipeline (True) versus manual testing by a QA analyst (False). Supports test coverage analysis and CI/CD pipeline integration reporting.']
  build_version varchar [note: 'The specific game build version (e.g., 1.4.2.8731) against which this compliance test was executed. Critical for traceability — ensures compliance evidence is tied to the exact software artifact submitted for platform certification or regulatory review.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'The regulatory or standards framework under which this test was conducted. Identifies the governing body or standard being validated, such as age rating bodies (ESRB, PEGI, USK, CERO, GRAC), data privacy (GDPR, COPPA), payment security (PCI DSS), accessibility (WCAG), or information security (ISO 27001, SOC 2). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ESRB|PEGI|USK|CERO|GRAC|GDPR|COPPA|PCI_DSS|WCAG|ISO27001|SOC2|APPLE_APP_STORE|GOOGLE_PLAY — promote to reference product]']
  content_descriptor_flags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of content descriptor flags identified during age rating compliance testing (e.g., Violence, Blood, Language, Gambling, In-Game Purchases, User Interaction). Required for ESRB, PEGI, and other rating body submissions. Null for non-age-rating tests.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this compliance test result record was first created in the system. Audit trail field required for data lineage and regulatory evidence integrity.']
  evidence_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL pointing to the compliance evidence artifact (e.g., screenshot, log file, test report, video capture) stored in the digital asset management system (Perforce Helix Core) or document repository. Required for platform submissions and legal audits.']
  evidence_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the compliance evidence artifact type. Indicates whether the evidence is a screenshot, video recording, log file, formal test report, document, or automated scan output. Supports evidence management and audit readiness.']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the compliance finding, including what was observed, how it deviates from the requirement, and the specific game feature or content area affected. Required for remediation teams and regulatory auditors.']
  framework_version varchar [note: 'The specific version or edition of the compliance framework applied during testing (e.g., WCAG 2.1, PCI DSS v4.0, GDPR 2018). Ensures test results are traceable to the exact standard revision in force at the time of testing.']
  jira_issue_key varchar [note: 'The Jira issue key (e.g., COMP-4521) associated with this compliance test result. Links the compliance finding to the defect or task tracked in Jira for remediation workflow management. Sourced from Jira (Project Management and Bug Tracking).']
  privacy_data_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the personal data category involved in this privacy compliance test. Populated for GDPR and COPPA tests to identify whether the test covers general personal data, children\'s data (under 13/16), payment data, behavioral/analytics data, or biometric data. Null for non-privacy frameworks.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region for which this compliance test applies. Regulatory requirements differ by region (e.g., ESRB for North America, PEGI for Europe, CERO for Japan, GRAC for South Korea). Drives which framework and rating body governs the test.']
  release_gate_blocking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance test result (specifically a fail or unresolved finding) is currently blocking the release gate for the associated game title and platform. True means the title cannot proceed to platform submission or launch until this finding is resolved.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the compliance finding must be remediated. Drives escalation workflows and release gate decisions. Typically aligned with the platform submission deadline or regulatory reporting deadline.']
  remediation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the remediation approach, actions taken, or rationale for waiver. Provides context for re-test verification and audit review.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of remediation activity for a compliance finding. Not_required applies to passing tests; open indicates a new unaddressed finding; in_progress indicates active remediation; resolved indicates the fix has been implemented; verified indicates the fix has been re-tested and confirmed; waived indicates a formal risk acceptance has been granted.']
  requirement_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the specific compliance requirement or control being tested. Provides context for auditors and reviewers without requiring reference to the external standard document.']
  result_code varchar [note: 'Externally referenceable business identifier for this compliance test result record, formatted as CTR-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX. Used in audit trails, platform submissions, and legal correspondence.']
  retest_date date [note: 'The date on which the compliance retest was or is scheduled to be performed following remediation. Null if retest has not yet been scheduled or is not required.']
  retest_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance finding requires a formal retest after remediation before the release gate can be cleared. True for all fail and conditional_pass results that are not waived.']
  retest_result_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the compliance retest performed after remediation. Null if no retest has been conducted. Pass confirms the finding is resolved; fail indicates the remediation was insufficient and further action is required.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the compliance finding when the test result is a fail or conditional pass. Critical findings block platform submission; major findings require remediation before release; minor findings are tracked but may not block release; informational findings are advisory only.']
  sign_off_date date [note: 'The date on which the compliance test result was formally signed off by the QA lead or compliance officer. Required for platform submission packages and regulatory audit evidence.']
  sign_off_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the compliance test result by the designated QA lead or compliance officer. Pending indicates awaiting review; approved indicates the result has been formally accepted; rejected indicates the result requires rework; escalated indicates the finding has been raised to senior management or legal.']
  test_category varchar [note: 'High-level category classifying the type of compliance test performed. Supports filtering and dashboard segmentation across age rating (ESRB/PEGI/USK/CERO/GRAC), data privacy (GDPR/COPPA), payment security (PCI DSS), accessibility (WCAG), platform certification (TRC/TCR), and content compliance testing.']
  test_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the compliance test was executed. Principal real-world event date for this compliance test result, used for audit trail and regulatory submission timelines.']
  test_environment varchar [note: 'The environment in which the compliance test was executed. Cert (certification) environment is the standard for platform submission compliance tests; staging is used for pre-cert validation; production tests may be required for live service compliance audits.']
  test_result_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the compliance test execution. Pass indicates full compliance; fail indicates a violation requiring remediation; conditional_pass indicates compliance with noted caveats; not_applicable indicates the requirement does not apply to this title/platform; blocked indicates testing could not be completed; in_review indicates result is pending sign-off.']
  test_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the compliance test was executed. Provides sub-day precision for audit logs and automated testing pipelines.']
  test_tool varchar [note: 'Name and version of the tool or framework used to execute this compliance test (e.g., OWASP ZAP 2.14, axe-core 4.7, custom PCI scanner, manual). Supports reproducibility and audit evidence quality assessment.']
  tester_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the QA tester or compliance analyst who executed this compliance test. Retained for audit trail readability without requiring a join to the employee table.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this compliance test result record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail completeness, and Silver layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  wcag_level varchar [note: 'The WCAG conformance level targeted by this accessibility compliance test (A, AA, or AAA). Populated only for WCAG accessibility tests. Null for non-accessibility frameworks. AA is the standard target for most gaming platforms.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing outcomes of regulatory and standards compliance testing: age rating compliance (ESRB/PEGI/USK/CERO/GRAC), data privacy (GDPR/COPPA), payment security (PCI DSS), and accessibility (WCAG). Records test date, compliance framework, requirement tested, result, evidence reference, remediation status, and sign-off. SSOT for regulatory compliance testing evidence required for platform submissions and legal audits.'
}

Table quality.defect_waiver {
  defect_waiver_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each defect waiver record. Primary key for the defect_waiver data product in the quality domain.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Defect waivers may be issued in response to audit findings requiring remediation. Real business process: audit remediation and risk acceptance documentation.']
  capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_project. Business justification: Waivers allowing known defects to ship affect capitalization and impairment decisions. Finance assesses whether waived defects indicate impairment of capitalized development costs or create contingent']
  cert_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.cert_finding. Business justification: Defect waivers can be granted for certification findings. defect_waiver has cert_finding_reference (STRING) but no FK to cert_finding. Waivers allow known cert findings to ship. Cardinality: N defect_']
  defect_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific defect or quality issue record for which this waiver is being requested. Links to the defect management system of record.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title for which this defect waiver is granted. Links the waiver to the specific game product under quality governance.']
  ip_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.ip_agreement. Business justification: Defect waivers for licensed content issues (middleware bugs, brand usage violations) require licensor approval and are tracked against specific IP agreements. Real approval workflow for risk managemen']
  milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.milestone. Business justification: Defect waivers granted for specific milestones when fix cannot be completed in time; studios track waiver expiry by milestone. Milestone ship decisions require waiver approval. Creating milestone_id.']
  primary_defect_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual (QA lead, producer, or executive) who formally approved or rejected this waiver. Provides the governance audit trail for accountability.']
  release_gate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the release quality gate at which this waiver was evaluated and applied. Ties the waiver to a specific milestone or certification checkpoint.']
  approval_conditions varchar [note: 'Any conditions or stipulations attached to the waiver approval by the approver (e.g., mandatory hotfix within 30 days, player communication required, specific monitoring thresholds must be met). Null for unconditional approvals or rejected waivers.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the waiver was formally approved or rejected by the designated approver. Null until a decision is made. Used for governance audit trail and approval cycle time reporting.']
  build_version varchar [note: 'The specific game build version in which the defect was identified and against which the waiver is being granted. Provides traceability to the exact code state at the time of waiver request.']
  coppa_compliance_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the waived defect could impact COPPA compliance, particularly for games with under-13 player populations. True requires legal review before waiver approval.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the defect waiver record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for governance and compliance reporting.']
  escalation_threshold varchar [note: 'Defined threshold value for the monitoring KPI that, if exceeded post-launch, triggers mandatory escalation and emergency patch consideration (e.g., crash rate > 0.5%, support tickets > 500/day). Expressed as a string to accommodate varied metric formats.']
  esrb_compliance_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the waived defect has any potential impact on ESRB rating compliance (e.g., unintended content exposure, age-gating bypass). True requires ESRB notification review before waiver approval.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the approved waiver expires. After this date, the waiver is no longer valid and the defect must be re-evaluated. Typically aligned with the fix_target_date or a specific post-launch patch milestone.']
  fix_target_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date by which the waived defect is committed to be resolved in a post-launch patch or update. Used to track remediation SLA compliance and waiver expiry alignment.']
  fix_target_version varchar [note: 'The planned game build version or patch release in which the waived defect is scheduled to be resolved post-launch. Establishes the post-launch remediation commitment tied to the waiver approval.']
  gdpr_compliance_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the waived defect could impact GDPR compliance, such as data exposure, consent mechanism failure, or player data handling issues. True requires Data Protection Officer review before waiver approval.']
  is_cert_blocking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the waived defect was originally classified as a platform certification blocker (TRC/TCR blocking finding). True signals that platform holder approval or exception was required in addition to internal waiver approval.']
  jira_issue_key varchar [note: 'The Jira ticket key (e.g., QA-1234) corresponding to the defect or certification finding for which this waiver is granted. Provides direct traceability to the bug tracking system of record.']
  monitoring_kpi varchar [note: 'Specific KPI or telemetry metric used to monitor the waived defect post-launch (e.g., crash rate threshold, defect reproduction rate per session, support ticket volume). Triggers escalation if breached.']
  pegi_compliance_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the waived defect has any potential impact on PEGI rating compliance in European markets. True requires PEGI authority review before waiver approval.']
  platform_code varchar [note: 'The target platform(s) on which the waived defect is present and accepted for release. Aligns with platform certification bodies (Sony, Microsoft, Nintendo) and distribution channels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PC|PS5|PS4|XBSX|XBONE|NSW|IOS|ANDROID|STADIA|LUNA|VR — promote to reference product]']
  platform_holder_acknowledgment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the relevant platform holder (Sony, Microsoft, Nintendo, Apple, Google) has formally acknowledged or accepted the certification finding waiver. Required for cert-blocking issues before release approval.']
  player_impact_scope varchar [note: 'Estimated breadth of player population affected by the waived defect. Informs risk level and post-launch monitoring intensity. Ranges from all players (systemic issue) to single player (isolated edge case).']
  post_launch_monitoring_plan varchar [note: 'Description of the monitoring commitment made as a condition of waiver approval. Specifies telemetry signals, crash reporting thresholds, player support ticket triggers, and escalation criteria that will be tracked post-launch via GameAnalytics/Amplitude and Zendesk.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Formal explanation provided by the approver when a waiver request is rejected. Documents the rationale for denial and any conditions under which a revised waiver request may be resubmitted. Null for approved or pending waivers.']
  release_version varchar [note: 'The planned release version (e.g., v1.0.0, v2.3.1) in which the waived defect will ship to players. Used to scope the waiver to a specific product release and track post-launch remediation.']
  reproduction_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate at which the defect can be reproduced under standard test conditions (0.00–100.00). Low reproduction rates strengthen the waiver justification; high rates require stronger risk mitigation commitments.']
  request_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the defect waiver was formally submitted by the requestor. Marks the start of the waiver review process and is used to measure approval cycle time.']
  risk_assessment varchar [note: 'Formal risk assessment narrative documenting the potential player impact, business impact, and probability of the defect manifesting in production. Includes assessment of affected player population, reproduction rate, and workaround availability.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Categorical risk rating assigned to the waiver based on the formal risk assessment. Determines the approval authority level required (QA Lead for low/medium, Studio Head for high, Executive for critical).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the defect waiver record. Tracks changes to waiver status, approval decisions, conditions, and monitoring commitments for audit trail completeness.']
  waiver_category varchar [note: 'Functional area of the game affected by the waived defect. Used for trend analysis and quality reporting across release gates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gameplay|audio|visual|performance|stability|ui_ux|network|security|accessibility|localization|monetization — promote to reference product]']
  waiver_code varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable unique code for this waiver (e.g., WVR-2024-0042). Used in Jira comments, release notes, and certification submissions to cross-reference the formal waiver document.']
  waiver_justification varchar [note: 'Formal written justification provided by the requestor explaining why the defect cannot be resolved before release and why the risk is acceptable. This is the primary governance narrative for the waiver and must address business impact, technical constraints, and mitigation plans.']
  waiver_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the defect waiver. Drives release gate decisions and audit reporting. Pending = awaiting approval decision; Approved = risk accepted and waiver granted; Rejected = waiver denied, defect must be resolved; Expired = waiver approval window has passed; Withdrawn = requestor cancelled the waiver before decision.']
  waiver_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the waiver by the nature of the quality exception being accepted. Distinguishes between defect waivers, platform certification finding waivers (TRC/TCR), performance benchmark waivers, compliance waivers, and regression waivers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: defect_waiver|cert_finding_waiver|performance_waiver|compliance_waiver|regression_waiver|uat_waiver — promote to reference product]']
  workaround_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a player-facing or internal workaround exists for the waived defect. A documented workaround reduces risk level and strengthens the waiver justification.']
  workaround_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the available workaround for the waived defect, if one exists. May be communicated to players via support knowledge base or patch notes. Null when workaround_available is false.']

  Note: 'Master record for each formal waiver granted to allow a known defect or certification finding to ship without resolution. Captures waiver request date, associated defect or cert finding, waiver justification, risk assessment, approver identity, approval date, waiver expiry date, post-launch monitoring commitment, and waiver status (pending, approved, rejected, expired). Provides the governance audit trail for risk-accepted quality exceptions at release gates.'
}

Table quality.test_suite {
  test_suite_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for test_suite']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this test suite record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the QA lead or team member responsible for maintaining and managing this test suite.']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game title or product this test suite is associated with.']
  modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this test suite record.']
  parent_suite_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent test suite if this suite is a sub-suite or component of a larger testing hierarchy.']
  parent_test_suite_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on test_suite (parent_test_suite_id)']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Degree of test automation within the suite indicating the balance between automated and manual test execution.']
  blocking_for_release boolean [note: 'Indicates whether failure of this test suite blocks game release or deployment to production.']
  build_version_target varchar [note: 'Target game build version or version range that this test suite is designed to validate (e.g., \'1.2.0\', \'2.0.x\', \'Release Candidate 3\').']
  certification_body varchar [note: 'Name of the external certification authority or platform holder that requires this test suite (e.g., \'Sony Interactive Entertainment\', \'Microsoft Xbox\', \'Nintendo\', \'ESRB\', \'PEGI\').']
  certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test suite is required for platform certification or regulatory compliance approval.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry compliance framework that this test suite addresses (e.g., \'COPPA\', \'GDPR\', \'WCAG 2.1\', \'Platform TRC\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this test suite record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the test suite purpose, scope, coverage areas, and testing objectives.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL link to detailed documentation, test plans, or wiki pages for this test suite.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date when this test suite became or will become active and available for execution in the QA pipeline.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Date when this test suite is scheduled to be deprecated or retired. Null for suites with indefinite lifecycle.']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Expected time in minutes required to complete full execution of all tests in the suite.']
  execution_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled or triggered frequency at which this test suite should be executed in the QA pipeline.']
  is_baseline boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test suite represents a baseline or master suite used as a reference for quality benchmarking.']
  jira_project_key varchar [note: 'Jira project key where test cases and defects related to this suite are tracked.']
  last_executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent execution of this test suite.']
  last_execution_result varchar [note: 'Overall result status of the most recent test suite execution.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this test suite record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the test suite execution, maintenance, or known issues.']
  pass_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage of test cases that must pass for the suite execution to be considered successful and meet release quality gates.']
  platform_target varchar [note: 'Target gaming platform(s) for which this test suite is designed (e.g., \'PlayStation 5\', \'Xbox Series X\', \'PC\', \'Mobile iOS\', \'Nintendo Switch\', \'Cross-Platform\').']
  prerequisites varchar [note: 'Required setup, configurations, data, or environmental conditions that must be in place before executing this test suite.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the test suite indicating its importance in the overall QA strategy and release gates.']
  suite_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the test suite used across QA systems and reporting (e.g., \'TS-MP-001\', \'CERT-PS5-2024\').']
  suite_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the test suite (e.g., \'Multiplayer Stress Test\', \'UI Regression Suite\', \'Platform Certification Suite\').']
  suite_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the test suite indicating its operational state and availability for execution.']
  suite_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the test suite by testing methodology (functional, regression, performance, security, compliance, certification).']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of tags or labels for categorization, filtering, and organization of test suites (e.g., \'multiplayer, stress-test, critical-path\').']
  test_category varchar [note: 'Testing level category indicating the scope and granularity of tests within the suite (unit, integration, system, acceptance, smoke, sanity).']
  test_environment varchar [note: 'Target execution environment for the test suite indicating where tests should be run.']
  test_framework varchar [note: 'Testing framework or tool used to implement and execute the test suite (e.g., \'Selenium\', \'Unity Test Framework\', \'Unreal Automation Tool\', \'Custom Framework\').']
  test_scope varchar [note: 'Specific game features, systems, or components covered by this test suite (e.g., \'Multiplayer Matchmaking\', \'In-Game Economy\', \'Graphics Rendering\', \'Audio System\').']
  total_test_cases varchar [note: 'Total number of individual test cases included in this test suite.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the test suite indicating revisions and updates to test coverage or methodology (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.3.1\').']

  Note: 'Master reference table for test_suite. Referenced by validation_test_suite_id.'
}

Table workforce.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the employee record. Primary key sourced from SAP S/4HANA HR module personnel number.']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the game studio to which the employee is assigned. Links to the studio master data for organizational hierarchy, project assignment, and capacity planning.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_profile. Business justification: Employee is classified under a standardized job profile. Job profile is the master definition of role family, competencies, and career track. Removing job_family from employee as it is defined in job_']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the direct manager or supervisor. Establishes reporting hierarchy for organizational structure, performance management, and approval workflows.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: Employee occupies an authorized position. Position is the headcount slot; employee is the person filling it. This is a fundamental HR relationship. FK will be nullable (employee may be between positio']
  annual_salary decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base annual salary for the employee in local currency. Used for payroll processing, compensation analysis, and budget planning. Excludes bonuses and equity.']
  compensation_grade varchar [note: 'Internal compensation band or grade level assigned to the employee. Used for salary benchmarking, promotion planning, and pay equity analysis.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code to which the employee\'s compensation and expenses are allocated. Used for budgeting, financial reporting, and project cost tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employee record was first created in the HR system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle tracking.']
  data_retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the employee record is eligible for archival or deletion per data retention policies. Calculated based on termination date and regulatory retention requirements.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the employee. Used for age verification, benefits eligibility, retirement planning, and COPPA compliance for employees under 18.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department to which the employee belongs (e.g., Game Development, Live Operations, Platform Engineering, Player Support). Used for cost center allocation and organizational reporting.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Corporate email address assigned to the employee. Primary communication channel for internal collaboration, project management in Jira, and business correspondence.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee\'s designated emergency contact person. Used for critical incident response and employee safety protocols.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the employee\'s designated emergency contact. Used for critical incident response and employee safety protocols.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the employee within the organization. Determines access rights, payroll processing, and benefits eligibility.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of employment arrangement. Determines benefits eligibility, work hour expectations, and compensation structure. Supports studio capacity planning for game development production scheduling.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name of the employee as recorded in HR system. Used for payroll, benefits administration, and official communications.']
  gdpr_consent_date date [note: 'Date when the employee provided GDPR consent. Used for consent audit trail and compliance verification.']
  gdpr_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee has provided explicit consent for processing of personal data under GDPR requirements. Required for EU-based employees and data processing compliance.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee officially started employment with the organization. Used for tenure calculations, benefits vesting, anniversary recognition, and retention analytics.']
  igda_member boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is a member of the International Game Developers Association. Used for professional development tracking and industry engagement metrics.']
  is_remote_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee\'s role is eligible for remote work arrangements. Used for workforce planning, office space allocation, and flexible work policy administration.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title of the employee as recorded in HR system. Reflects role, seniority, and functional area (e.g., Senior Game Designer, Lead Programmer, QA Tester).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified the employee record. Used for audit trail and change accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employee record was last updated. Used for change tracking, data freshness assessment, and audit compliance.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name (surname) of the employee as recorded in HR system. Used for payroll, benefits administration, and official communications.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal performance review. Used to track review cycles and ensure timely performance management processes.']
  national_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued national identification number (e.g., Social Security Number in USA, National Insurance Number in UK). Used for tax reporting, payroll processing, and legal compliance.']
  office_location varchar [note: 'Physical office or campus location where the employee is based (e.g., Seattle HQ, Tokyo Studio, London Office). Used for facilities management, travel planning, and regional workforce analytics.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Most recent annual performance review rating. Used for merit increase decisions, promotion eligibility, performance improvement plans, and talent management.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the employee. Used for emergency contact, business communication, and HR outreach.']
  primary_skill varchar [note: 'The employee\'s primary area of expertise or specialization (e.g., Gameplay Programming, Character Art, Level Design, QA Testing). Used for resource allocation and project matching.']
  salary_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the employee\'s salary. Supports multi-currency payroll processing for global workforce. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CAD|AUD|KRW|CNY — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  seniority_level varchar [note: 'Career level indicating experience, responsibility, and decision-making authority. Used for compensation bands, reporting structure, and succession planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: junior|mid|senior|lead|principal|director|vp|c_level — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  skill_tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of technical and functional skills possessed by the employee (e.g., Unity, Unreal Engine, C++, Game Design, QA Automation). Used for developer skill matrices, project staffing, and talent pool analysis.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s employment ended. Null for active employees. Used for offboarding, final payroll processing, benefits termination, and turnover analytics.']
  work_location varchar [note: 'Primary work arrangement indicating where the employee performs their duties. Used for facilities planning, collaboration tool provisioning, and workforce distribution analytics.']
  work_permit_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the employee\'s work permit or visa. Null for citizens and permanent residents. Used for proactive renewal management and compliance risk mitigation.']
  work_permit_status varchar [note: 'Legal work authorization status for the employee in their work location country. Used for compliance tracking, visa renewal management, and international mobility planning.']
  years_of_experience varchar [note: 'Total years of professional experience in the gaming industry. Used for seniority assessment, compensation benchmarking, and skill level estimation.']

  Note: 'Master record for all full-time and part-time employees across all studios and corporate functions. Captures personal details, employment status, hire date, termination date, employment type, work location, studio assignment, job family, seniority level, IGDA membership status, and GDPR/COPPA consent flags. Single source of truth for employee identity across the gaming enterprise. Primary source system: SAP S/4HANA HR.'
}

Table workforce.contractor {
  contractor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the contractor record. Primary key.']
  game_studio_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the game studio to which this contractor is currently assigned.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_profile. Business justification: Contractor engagement is based on a job profile defining the role requirements and competencies. Skill_specialization on contractor is kept as it may be more specific than job_profile technical_skills']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the full-time employee who serves as the contractor\'s direct manager or project lead.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Contractor is assigned to an organizational unit within the studio. Contractors work within org units just like employees. No columns removed as contractor table doesn\'t have redundant org unit fields']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: Contractor can fill an authorized position when position.is_contractor_eligible = true. This links the contractor engagement to the formal headcount position they are filling. FK will be nullable (not']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of background verification process for the contractor, if required by studio policy or project sensitivity.']
  benefits_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contractor is eligible for any company benefits. Typically false for contractors, distinguishing them from employees.']
  clearance_level varchar [note: 'Security clearance level granted to the contractor for access to confidential game development assets, source code, and unreleased content.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contractor engagement is scheduled to end. May be extended through contract amendments.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contractor engagement officially begins and the contractor is authorized to perform work.']
  contract_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the contractor engagement for fixed-price or capped contracts.']
  contracting_agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the staffing agency or contracting firm providing the contractor, if applicable. Null for direct freelancers.']
  contractor_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the contractor, typically assigned by HR or procurement systems. Format: CTR-########.']
  contractor_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contractor engagement. Active contractors have system access and can be assigned to projects.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contractor record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for contractor payment (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  daily_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Daily billing rate for the contractor in the contract currency. Used for day-rate engagements.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for contractor communication and system access provisioning.']
  engagement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contractor engagement model. Determines compliance obligations and payment processing rules.']
  equipment_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether company equipment (laptop, development kit, peripherals) has been issued to the contractor.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the contractor as it appears on contracts and tax documents.']
  hourly_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hourly billing rate for the contractor in the contract currency. Used for time-and-materials engagements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contractor record was most recently updated.']
  nda_signed_date date [note: 'Date when the contractor signed the non-disclosure agreement protecting confidential game development information and unreleased content.']
  onboarding_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the contractor completed all onboarding requirements including training, compliance certifications, and system access setup.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment schedule and terms for the contractor (e.g., Net 30, Bi-Weekly, Monthly, Upon Milestone Completion).']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Most recent performance assessment rating for the contractor, used for contract renewal and future engagement decisions.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the contractor.']
  rehire_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contractor is eligible for future re-engagement based on past performance and conduct.']
  remote_work_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location from which the contractor performs work, if remote. Used for tax nexus and compliance tracking.']
  skill_specialization varchar [note: 'Primary skill area or discipline for which the contractor was engaged (e.g., 3D Artist, QA Tester, Level Designer, Gameplay Programmer, Audio Engineer, Localization Specialist).']
  statement_of_work_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the statement of work document governing this contractor engagement.']
  system_access_provisioned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contractor has been granted access to required development systems, tools, and platforms (Perforce, Jira, game engines, SDKs).']
  tax_classification varchar [note: 'Tax treatment classification for the contractor determining withholding and reporting obligations.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number for the contractor (SSN, EIN, VAT number, or equivalent based on jurisdiction).']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for contractor engagement termination, if applicable (e.g., contract completion, project cancellation, performance issues, policy violation).']
  time_tracking_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contractor must submit timesheets for billing and project cost tracking purposes.']
  work_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the contractor performs work. Determines labor law compliance requirements.']

  Note: 'Master record for external contractors, freelancers, and contingent workers engaged by studios for game development, QA playtesting, art production, and live operations. Tracks engagement type, contracting agency, statement-of-work reference, clearance level, studio assignment, skill specialization, and contract period.
